The review is a summary of publicly available datasets and commonly used signals and features (EDA, ECG, and PPG), metrics of reported evaluation, and most critical gaps pertaining to validation procedures, robustness, and real-world implementation. The survey gives a very concise overview of datasets, signals, methods, metrics and open challenges to physiological stress detection.

title: A 0.5 V 2.4 GHz Digital PLL with a Sub-Feedback Loop Technique
authors: Wen-Hsuan Cheng, Bo-Jun Huang, Jen-Chieh Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents a digital bang-bang phase-locked loop (BBPLL) for low-supply-voltage applications. The proposed BBPLL utilizes a five-stage ring oscillator, which incorporates a forward-interpolation sub-feedback loop to achieve a higher output frequency under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply. A 4-bit secondorder delta-sigma modulator (DSM) is integrated into the digitally controlled oscillator (DCO) to enhance time resolution and reduce jitter. The prototype was implemented in TSMC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 - n m}$</tex> CMOS technology and occupied a core area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$59 \mu ~\mathrm{m} \times 110 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. Measurement results showed that the BBPLL operated at 2.4 GHz with a peak-to-peak jitter of 23.46 ps and an RMS jitter of 2.68 ps, while consuming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$283.4 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex> from a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply.

title: Dynamic Parameter Extraction for Battery Discharge Characteristics Using Intelligent Algorithms Under Real Measurement
authors: DEGLA Aicha, REHOUMA Youssef, DANOUNE Mohamed Bilal, MEZIANE Fares, BOUMECHETA Samer, HAZIL Omar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Accurate modeling and simulation of lead-acid batteries are essential for understanding their internal behavior and optimizing battery-based systems. However, the scarcity of realistic models in the literature remains a significant limitation. This study proposes the biogeography-based heterogeneous cuckoo search (BHCS), a hybrid metaheuristic algorithm that combines heterogeneous cuckoo search (CS) with biogeography-based optimization (BBO). The BHCS is designed to accurately estimate the unknown parameters of the battery's equivalent circuit by effectively balancing exploration and exploitation. Its performance is assessed on a lead-acid battery and benchmarked against four advanced algorithms: IJAYA, Slime Mould Algorithm (SMA), Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA), and conventional CS. Statistical analysis demonstrates that BHCS provides the most precise modeling, achieving a discrepancy of less than 0.034 A between predicted and experimental data.

title: Integrating Generative AI Into Total Quality Management for Public Health Improvement
authors: Astri Indriyati, Wikan Danar Sunindyo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Ensuring service quality and satisfaction in public healthcare remains challenging due to limited resources and fragmented data utilization. This study proposes an AI-Enhanced Total Quality Management (TQM) framework that integrates Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Explainable AI (XAI) to support predictive and evidence-based quality improvement. Using an exploratory sequential mixed-methods design, qualitative insights on organizational readiness and ethics were combined with quantitative survey data and machine learning models. Synthetic data generated via GANs were employed for privacy-preserving analytics, while Random Forest, XGBoost, and Artificial Neural Networks were evaluated using cross-validation and statistical significance testing. Results from a public dental hospital case study show that XGBoost achieved the highest predictive performance, and explainability analysis highlights leadership commitment and employee involvement as key determinants of satisfaction. The findings demonstrate that integrating GenAI and XAI into TQM enables transparent, data-driven, and human-centered quality governance in public healthcare.

title: Lightning AutoML Agent: An Offline Framework for Automated Machine Learning
authors: Gold Sharon R, Khalid Abdul Jaleel, Nivi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The high level of manual knowledge to preprocess data, engineer features, select with the use of models, and tune hyperparameters makes training dependable machine learning models difficult. These steps also tend to slow down, be inconsistent and hard to scale once it comes to converting raw data into a deployable model. We present Lightning AutoML Agent, an AutoML agent which is fully automated to resolve these challenges by simplifying the entire machine learning pipeline. At this base, the system consists of a Large Language Model (LLM)-powered cognitive engine that is able to study datasets and comes up with task-specific medical preprocessing and feature engineering plans. This intelligence layer is paired with a high-performance backend implementation based on modular PyTorch implementations of the training and background hyperparameter optimization based on Optuna. The framework favors the supervised learning (classification and regression), and the unsupervised tasks (clustering and the association rule mining). The main contribution of this work is the combination of the decision-making process guided by LLM and scalable AutoML architecture which leads to less manual intervention and less adverse model behavior. Reproducibility and accessibility are enforced by an extensive reporting, open-ended preprocessing and generation of leaderboards, which are the result of automated generation. In general, the Lightning AutoML Agent makes machine learning much more accessible to high-quality due to both strategic and computational intervals being automated.

title: Review of Person Identification Techniques Using Bronchial Sound
authors: Anagha Ramnath Kadve, Grantej V. Otari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The concept of sound-based person identification (SPID) refers to a process of identifying or recognizing an individual's identity through their bronchial sounds, footsteps, human stride, as well as breathing sounds. For sound-based identification, pattern recognition, and acoustic signature analysis remain core technologies, where these technologies effectively conform the individual identity and are utilized in several domains such as surveillance, banking, healthcare, and law enforcement. Besides, various approaches have been installed recently to recognize the person based on human-sounding activities, in which the conventional models are labour-intensive, which maximizes the computational cost, and the variability associated with human labour. The study examined about 25 research papers for effective recognition in order to obtain a better understanding of bronchial sound-based PID. Eventually, the main objective of this research is to find the research gaps in the conventional models and aim for an effective bronchial sound-based PID in the future.

title: Bluetooth Controlled Robot with Real-Time Obstacle Detection and Live Video Feedback
authors: Sumukh A V, Nagarathna S, Nanda S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a cost-effective, Bluetooth-controlled mobile robot intended for industrial inspection and surveillance tasks, which integrates real-time obstacle detection and live video feedback. The system is built around an Arduino Mega 2560 microcontroller, which manages a servo-mounted HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor and an ESP32-CAM module for live video streaming. Unlike conventional teleoperated robots that rely on a single data stream for situational awareness, this platform incorporates a systematic 180° ultrasonic scanning mechanism to generate a proximity map, which significantly enhances obstacle detection reliability and complements visual data. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a 95% obstacle detection accuracy within a 40 cm range, a stable video stream at 15 frames per second (FPS), and a system latency of 200ms. The Bluetooth connection proved robust up to 10 meters indoors. The system's modular, cost-effective design (under 150 $) facilitates enhanced operator perception in hazardous environments where fully autonomous solutions are often impractical. Compared to existing designs, this approach offers superior multi-sensor fusion capabilities in a low-cost package, which improves navigation safety during teleoperation.

title: Time-Frequency Domain Based Glaucoma Detection Using Electroretinography
authors: Jenefa A V, Jemsina Banu S, Gogila Devi K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Glaucoma is a chronic eye disorder characterized by the gradual damage of retinal ganglion cells, which most often leads to irreversible vision loss if not detected at an early stage. The conventional diagnostic techniques, namely tonometry and visual field analysis, have their own setbacks due to the subjective nature and late-stage detection capability. In this context, this work proposes a Convolutional Neural Network-based framework for automated glaucoma detection using Electroretinogram signals. These ERG signals are processed by noise filtration, baseline correction, and signal normalization to improve signal quality. The preprocessed signals are then transformed into two-dimensional spectrograms by Time-Frequency Transformation, which allows the CNN to learn both time and frequency-based variations related to glaucomatous changes. These complex features are learned autonomously by the model to classify patients as normal or glaucomatous with high precision. Experimental analysis shows that the proposed CNN-based algorithm outperforms the conventional machine learning models in terms of accuracy and robustness. The approach offers a reliable, objective, and non-invasive diagnostic tool for early detection and better clinical management of glaucoma.

title: Towards Autonomous Spectral Mapping for Soil Analysis: Development and Validation of a Robot-Deployable DIY Spectrophotometer
authors: Ghouti Abdellaoui, Anisse Chiali, Fayçal Dergal, Mohamed Ramzi Bousmaha, Maissara Bellouati
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper presents the complete design, implementation, and experimental validation of a DIY spectrophotometer embedded on an autonomous mobile robot specifically developed for in-situ soil analysis. The system employs accessible components including Raspberry Pi, USB camera, and diffraction grating, integrated with a custom-designed mobile robotic platform featuring articulated suspension for agricultural terrain navigation. A comprehensive Python-based software framework was developed to automate image acquisition, spectral extraction, and analysis of absorbance and transmittance curves based on Beer-Lambert law principles. Experimental validation against a commercial Horiba LabRAM HR Evolution Raman spectrometer demonstrated the system's capability to detect characteristic spectral peaks of aspirin, silica, and malachite green with 90-97% qualitative agreement. The integrated system enables automated georeferenced spectral mapping, providing a robust and costeffective solution for precision agriculture and environmental monitoring applications.

title: Usage of Hashed Time-Frequency Constellation Analysis in Real-Time Network Traffic Fingerprinting for Anomaly Detection
authors: M. Uğur Öney, M. Mert Güler, O. Orçun Ada
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The real-time identification and classification of network traffic is a fundamental challenge in network management and cybersecurity. Traditional methods like Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) are often resource-intensive and struggle with encrypted traffic, while signature-based systems can be evaded by novel threats. This paper presents a novel, lightweight system for network traffic fingerprinting inspired by the principles of audio fingerprinting systems [1]. By transforming statistical features of network flow into a time-series signal, applying frequency analysis to identify dominant patterns, and generating robust hashes, the system can effectively identify and classify recurring traffic patterns in real time. This approach enables the rapid detection of known behaviors and the flagging of new, potentially anomalous activities, offering a powerful tool for network monitoring and cybersecurity applications.

title: Distributional Information Embedding: A Framework for Multi-Bit Watermarking
authors: Haiyun He, Yepeng Liu, Ziqiao Wang, Yongyi Mao, Yuheng Bu
publish: 2025 Asia Pacific Workshop on Data Science and Information Theory (APWDSIT)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel problem, distributional information embedding, motivated by the practical demands of multi-bit watermarking for large language models (LLMs). Unlike traditional information embedding, which embeds information into a pre-existing host signal, LLM watermarking actively controls the text generation process-adjusting the token distribution-to embed a detectable signal. We develop an information-theoretic framework to analyze this distributional information embedding problem, characterizing the fundamental trade-offs among three critical performance metrics: text quality, detectability, and information rate. In the asymptotic regime, we demonstrate that the maximum achievable rate with vanishing error corresponds to the entropy of the LLM's output distribution and increases with higher allowable distortion. We also characterize the optimal watermarking scheme to achieve this rate. Extending the analysis to the finite-token case with non-i.i.d. tokens, we identify schemes that maximize detection probability while adhering to constraints on false alarm and distortion.

title: Automatic Detection of Central Serous Retinopathy Using Faster R-CNN
authors: Thokala Nikhitha, Katkam Himabindu, Jatoth Rahul, Kachi Anvesh, E Ravi Kumar, Muni Sekhar Velpuru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Central Serous Retinopathy (CSR) refers to a retinal disorder marked by the presence of fluid beneath the retina, which, if not identified at the initial stages, can cause distorted vision and permanent vision loss. Inappropriate identification of the disease depends entirely upon the interpretation of the images, which are time-consuming. To deal with this issue, this study removes this limitation by applying an image recognition tool called the Faster Region-Based Convolutional Neural Network (Faster R-CNN) to detect the location of the disease. In this study, the method aims to detect lesions by determining the bounding box of the lesions, which are influenced by the Central Serous Retinopathy disease. To begin with, images from publicly accessible sources are pre-processed. In pre-processing, the images are passed through resizing, normalization, and techniques involving the adjustment of image contrasts. Bounding boxes, which are annotated with the help of professionals, are used to mark the regions to be identified. Using the Faster R-CNN method, the regions can be precisely identified because the ResNet50 algorithm assists the model to learn features. Performance assessment shows the method to be relevant, reporting that it has an accuracy of 89%, Mean Average Precision of 0.83, and an F1-score of 0.83, which shows the efficiency of the method.

title: Bi-Fluid Synergy: Unlocking Unprecedented Efficiency in Hybrid Photovoltaic/Thermal Collectors via Numerical Simulation
authors: Chaima Goubar, Mama Bouchaour, Laarej Merad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This study presents a detailed numerical analysis of the performance of a hybrid photovoltaic/thermal (PV/T) collector that simultaneously uses two heat transfer fluids: air and water. The objective is to evaluate and optimize the overall energy conversion efficiency of the system through a comprehensive thermal model implemented in Python. The model is based on steady-state energy balances applied to each component of the collector. Simulations demonstrate that the dual-fluid configuration significantly enhances both thermal and electrical efficiencies compared to single-fluid systems, due to more effective heat extraction and improved cooling of the photovoltaic cells. Results show that thermal efficiency reaches up to 58.6% with water, while electrical efficiency increases to 13.1% at high flow rates. The combined airwater configuration achieves a maximum overall efficiency of 71.7%, confirming the benefits of dual-fluid cooling in hybrid solar systems. This work highlights the strong potential of bi-fluid PV/T systems as a sustainable energy solution, particularly in regions with high solar irradiance.

title: Forecasting Regional Oil-Hazardous Waste Generation in the Philippines Through Arima Modeling
authors: Mayolito III Gulleban, Lheann Cabase, Roel Andrei Carreon, John Archilles Deduyco, Florence Jean Talirongan, Roseclaremath Caroro, Phoebe Claire Hermione Gation, Jazzen Keithly Yusop, Jon Kristian Luste, Earl RJ Hocoy, Femarie Sumile, Prince Rainier Lomo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The improper management of oil-hazardous waste presents escalating environmental and public health risks in the Philippines amid rapid industrialization and urban expansion. This study clustered regional oil waste data and forecasted waste generation trends across Philippine regions from 2025 to 2029 using k-means clustering and an Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model. Kmeans clustering using 2020 and 2024 data to identify similar patterns resulted in 3 regions in the first cluster and 14 in the second cluster. Moreover, in the forecasting results, critical regional disparities were revealed: Region X and Region XII showed persistent increases due to industrial expansion, while National Capital Region, Cordillera Administrative Region, Region III, and Region VII exhibited decreasing patterns. Ten regions displayed volatile patterns with fluctuations in 2025, followed by alternating trends that eventually stabilized or showed slight upward trends from 2027 to 2029. The Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) was excluded from the forecasting analysis only due to the lack of sufficient historical data required for the ARIMA model. The study features the significance of data-driven forecasting models in supporting evidence-based policymaking and environmental planning strategy for maintainable waste management in the Philippines.

title: Assessing Vulnerability of Deepfake Detection Models to Noise-Masked Attacks
authors: Artyom Galimov, Artem Chirkovskiy
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Audio deepfake detection models perform reliably in clean audio conditions, but may struggle in noisy environments. Moreover, fraudsters may enhance deepfake samples with genuine background noise to fool an audio deepfake model. This paper introduces a novel metric to quantify the robustness of the model against noise-masked attacks. Using the In-the-Wild (ITW) dataset as a foundation, we construct two large-scale benchmarks consiting more than 380,000 samples each, spanning four SNR levels (6-24 dB) and diverse noise categories from MSSNSD and DEMAND noise datasets. Unlike standard evaluation protocols that re-optimize thresholds per condition, our proposed metric employs a fixed threshold calibrated on clean data to measure three complementary aspects: overall degradation (noise vulnerability), class asymmetry (noise tolerance), and integrated robustness (noise influence). We evaluated three state-of-the-art open source models to test the proposed metric. Our results showed, that the metric is able to capture vulnerability of the models to noise-masked attacks and to measure it. The proposed metric provides a standardized framework for assessing model resilience, revealing that single-source evaluation yields incomplete and potentially misleading conclusions.

title: Deep Segmentation of Retinal Layers and Fluid in Diabetic Retinopathy OCT with UU-MAMBA
authors: Rolf Alexander Klæboe, Raju Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of retinal layers and pathological fluid regions in Spectral-Domain Optical Coherence Tomography (SD-OCT) images is critical for early diagnosis and monitoring of retinal diseases. However, this task is challenged by severe class imbalance, low contrast boundaries, and complex anatomical variations. In this paper, we propose a modified UU-Mamba architecture tailored for fine-grained retinal layer and fluid segmentation on the Duke SD-OCT dataset. Our approach integrates gated Mamba blocks for efficient global context modeling and introduces a customized Uncertainty-Aware Loss (UAL) function that strategically combines Tversky loss, class-specific Focal loss, and Cross-Entropy loss to mitigate class imbalance and enhance minority class sensitivity. We further employ Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) during training to improve generalization across underrepresented structures. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance, surpassing human expert segmentation, with an average Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) score of 0.85 compared to the human expert's 0.81. Extensive ablation studies validate the individual contributions of each proposed component, confirming the efficacy of our tailored loss design and regularization strategy in enhancing segmentation quality and improving precision for challenging structures like fluid regions.

title: Development and Testing of an RFID-Based Library Circulation System Using ESP32 and MySQL
authors: Adi Wijaya, Resa Pramudita, Andre Reyvaldo Iglesias, Ibnu Hartopo, Jaja Kustija
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: This study introduces the development and evaluation of an RFID-based library circulation system using the ESP32 microcontroller and a MySQL database to optimize book lending and returning processes. Conventional library systems often face challenges, such as time-consuming manual data entry, limited accuracy, and inefficiencies in managing book inventories. To address these issues, the proposed system integrates Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for automatic book and user identification, coupled with a web-based database for real-time monitoring and record management. The hardware prototype was built using ESP32, RFID readers, LCD displays, and supporting components, while the software was implemented using Arduino IDE, PHP, and MySQL. Testing included functional verification, RFID performance measurements, system integration testing (SIT), and user acceptance testing (UAT). Results demonstrated that all hardware and software components performed as intended, with RFID successfully reading various tags within practical distances. System integration allowed seamless data transfer between hardware and database, while UAT confirmed usability and reliability from the user perspective. The findings highlight that RFID-based systems can significantly reduce staff workload, minimize errors, and provide faster, user-friendly self-service options in library operations. This research contributes to modernizing library infrastructure and demonstrates the practical benefits of combining microcontroller technology with database management.

title: Head Reconstruction Using 3D Keypoint Constraints
authors: Wanshu Feng, Gang Zhang, Mingjian Gao
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Head reconstruction remains a fundamental challenge in computer vision, especially when only limited geometric cues are available for guiding the recovery of finegrained facial structure. Considering the limitations of using 2D keypoints constraints, this paper proposes a head reconstruction method that introduces 3D keypoints as constraints. The FLAME parametric model is used as an auxiliary model, transforming the head reconstruction task into the estimation of model parameters. An encoder-decoder network is used to estimate the shape, pose, and expression parameters. During head mesh optimization, the 3D keypoints are used as constraints to guide the reconstruction process, and the loss function is designed to account for the difference between the ground-truth and predicted 3D keypoints. Experimental results demonstrate that, due to the use of 3D keypoint constraints, the proposed method obtains the alignment error 1.34 and is superior to methods using 2D keypoint constraints.

title: Enhancing AI Chatbot Responsiveness Through Design of a Multimodal Emotion Detection
authors: Yashraj Sah, Atharva Padole, Mahesh Dongre, Gitanjali Shinde, Grishma Bobhate, Sonal Fatangare
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The research objective focuses on building emotion-based chatbots using artificial intelligence. Text-based bots fail to detect emotions which makes them incapable of producing suitable responses. Wav2Vec 2.0 NLP and Vision Transformer analysis enable the system to identify voice tones which solves this issue. The system utilizes real-time observation for monitoring many interviewees at once and measuring classroom engagement of students while identifying AI-generated tests. This research combines multimodal attention functions with reinforcement learning algorithms to control cultural and unknown user preferences and provides limited ethical protection during execution. This chatbot performs emotional detection at a human standard while adhering to ethical guidelines in AI practice. Complex systems require integrated systems of deep learning and neuro-symbolic artificial intelligence to attain ethical protection characteristics during their decision-making processes.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Leadership in Small Businesses: A Hawaiˋi-Based Perspective with Broader Implications
authors: Eduard O. Merc, Michael Rose
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study examines how artificial intelligence (AI) is being adopted by small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Hawaii and explores the cultural, organizational, and leadership factors that shape this adoption. Drawing on survey responses from 87 SME leaders, the research identifies clear patterns in how AI is currently used, with adoption strongest in marketing analytics and customer service automation and considerably lower in leadership-oriented applications, consistent with global SME trends [1], [2], [3]. The findings show that AI integration in Hawaii is not driven solely by technological readiness but is deeply influenced by cultural values emphasizing trust, interpersonal relationships, and community reputation [4], [5]. Leaders consistently expressed strong support for AI as an augmentation tool, particularly for improving insight, forecasting, and decision quality, aligning with established research on AIassisted leadership rather than AI-driven automation [6], [7], [8], [9]. Barriers including cost constraints, limited technical expertise, and data security concerns further restrict adoption, reflecting widely documented challenges in SME digital transformation [1], [2], [10], [11]. Overall, the study underscores the importance of culturally informed, human-centered AI strategies for SMEs operating in relationship-based or geographically isolated contexts. These insights contribute to a broader understanding of how AI can be integrated responsibly and effectively within culturally distinct leadership systems.

title: A New Scheme for Continuous and Discrete States Estimation Under MDADT Constraints
authors: Rafika El Harabi, Manel Atitallah, Mohamed Naceur Abdelkrim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper aims to design a new robust estimator scheme dedicated to switched linear systems subject to unknown disturbances and noises. This algorithm is able to estimate both continuous and discrete behaviors owing to the unknown synchronous switching signal estimator block consisting of eventtriggered mechanism and the unknown input observer to estimate the system states directly. Using linear matrix inequalities (LMIs), novel stability criteria is formulated taking into account the mode-dependent average dwell time (MDADT) constraints. This later is able to reduce the conservativeness of stability results. Finally, a numerical example is proved to illustrate the applicability of the proposed framework.

title: MelSpectrogram-Based Voice Disorder Classification Using Bayesian-Optimised Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: M. Kalaiselvi, T. Jayasree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The prevalence of voice disorders is increasing annually, highlighting the need for accurate and cost-effective diagnostic tools. In this work, we developed an automated framework for detecting and classifying structural voice disorders, including papilloma, fibroma, cysts, and polyps. Voice samples were gathered from the Saarbrucken Voice Database (SVD) and the Voice Database. To precisely capture the time-frequency characteristics of diseased voices, each audio sample was converted into a Mel-spectrogram representation. The four disorders were then classified using a Bayesianoptimised Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The suggested solution outperformed current methods in terms of performance and computational efficiency, achieving 96% accuracy on the publicly accessible SVD dataset. By enabling timely and economical remote evaluation, the method provides a practical tool for preliminary clinical screening of vocal disorders.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Based Model for Early Parkinson's Disease Detection
authors: Rafael Sotelo, Carlos Perez, José Santisteban
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Early detection of Parkinson's disease remains challenging due to the absence of accessible biomarkers and objective diagnostic tools. This study presents a hybrid machine learning model that combines structured data and graphical biomarkers through a multimodal late-fusion approach. The proposed method combines Gradient Boosting and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models, achieving improved interpretability and computational efficiency compared to attention-based fusion models. Results show that Gradient Boosting achieved 95.25% accuracy, while CNN achieved 97.40% accuracy. Their adaptive late fusion attained 98.27% accuracy and a 96.6% F1-score. A 5-fold crossvalidation confirmed robustness and absence of overfitting, demonstrating potential for cost-effective and reproducible early diagnosis of Parkinson's disease in clinical contexts across Latin America.

title: EV Charging Scheduling Optimization Using PSO Algorithm for Cost Efficiency and Network Load Management
authors: Saiful Karim, Syafaruddin, Ansar Suyuti, Anuar Bin Mohamad
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The rapid rise of electric vehicles (EVs) is transforming the transportation sector, driving innovation and sustainability efforts worldwide. However, this growth also places considerable pressure on existing power systems, particularly during periods of high demand. A major challenge lies in managing the surge in peak electricity demand and the resulting increase in charging costs, which can strain both utilities and consumers. To address these challenges, effective scheduling strategies are required to balance grid stability while ensuring efficient and cost-effective energy utilization. In this study, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is employed as a computational method to optimize EV charging schedules. The approach focuses on identifying cost-saving patterns and improving charging behavior by distributing the charging load more evenly across time. The proposed model is designed to lower operational and charging expenses. Simulation results indicate that applying PSO can reduce costs by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11-18 \%$</tex> compared to conventional scheduling methods, while also improving energy efficiency and enhancing overall load distribution. These outcomes highlight the potential of PSO as a practical and effective tool for supporting the sustainable integration of EVs into modern power systems and promoting a more reliable, resilient, and economically efficient energy infrastructure.

title: Multivariate Spatial Modelling of Tree Species Distribution Using Adaptive Sparse Group Lasso: Case Study in Barro Colorado Island Plot
authors: Refki Assadiky, Achmad Choiruddin, Santi Puteri Rahayu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Multivariate spatial point patterns have gained significant popularity as technology enables large-scale data collection. This study focuses on modeling tree species distribution in the Barro Colorado Island (BCI) forest plot, considering 9 tree species and 13 environmental covariates. To understand species co-occurrence with their environment and each other, we apply the inhomogeneous multivariate Poisson point process (IMPP) model. The Random Quadrature (RQ) method is used for efficient parameter estimation, aligning with generalized linear models while minimizing redundant computations. Given the high density of covariates, we apply the adaptive sparse group lasso (ASGL) for variable selection, focusing on the most influential environmental variables per species. The model using ASGL shows superior performance, with an AIC value of 158849.6, indicating a better fit compared to the model without ASGL. The study identifies key environmental factors such as elevation, slope, potassium content, phosphorus content, and solar radiation that significantly influence species distribution. Finally, hierarchical clustering identifies three distinct habitat groups based on their estimated environmental responses. The clustering results reveal three distinct habitat groups. These findings offer valuable insights for afforestation and reforestation efforts.

title: Power Factor Correction in Low Voltage Distribution System Using Capacitor Bank in Educational Facilities
authors: Devinda Rizky Putri Azroi, Abdul Syakur, Susatyo Handoko
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The Power factor in low voltage distribution systems has declined due to the increasing use of inductive devices such as single-phase motors, fluorescent lamps, welding machines, and grinders. A low power factor leads to higher current flow, greater energy losses, and penalties for excessive reactive power demand. This study investigates the application of a capacitor bank in a single-phase low voltage network at Vocational School Islam Sudirman Ungaran to improve power factor and overall efficiency. Field measurements at a critical load point showed a power factor of 0.75, below the recommended threshold of 0.90. The system was modeled and simulated using Electrical Transient and Analysis Program (ETAP) version 19.0.1 to evaluate electrical performance before and after capacitor bank installation. The correction method involved injecting leading reactive power to counteract the lagging effect of inductive loads. Using optimal capacitor placement, a 100 kVAR bank was installed at Bus 3, supplying 97.4 kVAR and reducing the reactive power burden by 96.8%. This increased the power factor to 0.95 and reduced line current by 20.1%. These results confirm that targeted capacitor bank placement is a reliable and cost-effective strategy to enhance power quality, minimize technical losses, and ensure voltage stability in educational electrical systems.

title: SDFUECR: Software-Defined and Fuzzy-Based Unequal Clustering Energy-Efficient Cluster Routing
authors: Thi Thuy Dung Tran, Ying Liang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) face energy imbalance due to the “hotspot problem,” where cluster heads near the sink drain energy faster. This paper aims to solve this by proposing a new protocol named SDFUECR (Software-Defined Fuzzy Unequal Energy-efficient Cluster Routing), to mitigate this issue. Traditional routing protocols impose high computational loads on sensor nodes and result in uneven energy distribution. SDFUECR addresses these shortcomings through a synergistic integration of Software-Defined Networking (SDN) and fuzzy logic. The SDN controller provides centralized, network-wide orchestration for global optimization, while the fuzzy logic system executes adaptive cluster head selection and unequal clustering based on local node parameters like residual energy, distance to the sink, and node density. Simulation results demonstrate that SDFUECR significantly outperforms existing protocols, achieving network lifetime improvements of 54.5%, 46.4%, and 9.9% compared to the Fuzzy-Based Unequal Clustering Algorithm (FUCA), Hybrid Unequal Energy-Efficient Clustering (HEEC), and Fuzzy-based Multi-hop Unequal Cluster Routing (FMUCR) schemes, respectively. It also achieves more balanced energy consumption and reduced control overhead.

title: Optimized IIR Filter Design Using Genetic Algorithm and Theoretical Methods with Symmetric Coefficients
authors: Sidi Mohammed Meriah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This article presents a novel approach to designing IIR filters by combining theoretical methods for deriving filter coefficients with an optimization-based approach. The denominator coefficients are constrained to be symmetrical, and only half of them are optimized using Genetic algorithm (GA). This reduces the computational complexity of the optimization process while maintaining filter performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves comparable or superior performance to traditional design techniques, such as Butterworth, Chebyshev Type II, and elliptic filters, with significant savings in computational resources.

title: AI Tutor Study: Linking Student Questioning Patterns to Learning Gains Through Bloom's Taxonomy and Intent Analysis
authors: Tsun Chow
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper investigates whether patterns in student questioning within an AI-powered tutoring system can predict learning outcomes. In a small exploratory pilot (Phase 1), a prototype tutor for principal component analysis (PCA) was used with ten graduate students in Business Analytics; six complete dialogue sessions were analyzed. Questions were coded along Bloom's taxonomy and communicative intent categories by ChatGPT, and validated by the author manually. Learners who engaged across a broader range of Bloom levels and displayed diverse intent types tended to achieve higher gains on a post-quiz. These observations informed three hypotheses for Phase 2: (1) that cognitive breadth predicts learning gain; (2) that diversity of question intents also predicts learning; and (3) that their interaction offers the strongest model of engagement. Phase 2 will involve a larger, naturalistic dataset, automated tagging validation, and regression modelling to test these hypotheses. Together, the study aims to provide a rigorous, data-driven approach to assessing cognitive engagement in AI-supported learning environments.

title: Unraveling Parkinson's Progression with AI: Multi-Model Predictive Analysis and Explainable Diagnostic Biomarker Insight
authors: Aarya Apte, Vibhav Gogate
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder whose clinical progression remains difficult to model due to its heterogeneous symptomatology and limitations in existing tracking methods. This study evaluates the effectiveness of supervised machine learning (ML) techniques for forecasting PD progression over a 3-year time window using high-dimensional, longitudinal biomarker data from the Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI) dataset. The nine predictive models are trained and compared, including linear regression, ridge regression, random forests, gradient boosting, and neural networks on curated input features and clinically validated progression scores. Ensemble models such as Random Forest and Gradient Boosting achieve the highest performance, yielding prediction accuracies between 94.8% and 96.4%. In contrast, deeper models exhibit no performance benefit, highlighting the suitability of simpler architectures for structured clinical data. To enable interpretability, SHAP and LIME are applied, which identify baseline clinical scores, age, REM Sleep Behavior Disorder metrics, and Body Mass Index as the most influential features across both global and patient-specific contexts. These results demonstrate that accurate PD progression prediction is feasible using standard ML methods trained on well-structured biomarker data. Moreover, the integration of model-agnostic explanation techniques facilitates biomarker-level insight, providing a basis for early intervention and individualized treatment planning.

title: Ghost-YOLOv8-S: A Lightweight Network Design for Real-Time Traffic Light Detection
authors: Yuelin Tong, Yong Dai
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: With the development of autonomous driving technology, traffic light detection has become a core part of vehicle environment perception. However, existing detection models often face high computational costs and large numbers of parameters when deployed on embedded devices. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight improved model called Ghost-YOLOv8-S and optimizes it in the following two ways. First, the standard convolution layers in YOLOv8's backbone are replaced with lightweight GhostNet modules. These modules generate feature maps using low-cost linear operations, which significantly reduce the model's complexity and number of parameters. Second, based on the knowledge that traffic lights are usually small or medium-sized objects, the model removes the detection scale for large objects and keeps the scales for small and medium objects. This adjustment makes the network structure more suitable for the task. Experiments on a self-collected traffic light dataset show that Ghost-YOLOv8-S reduces model size by 41.2% and increases inference speed by 18.3% compared to the baseline YOLOv8n model, achieving a good balance between accuracy and efficiency. This study provides an effective solution for deploying perception systems on edge devices in autonomous vehicles and has broad application potential.

title: Experimental Study of QoS Parameters in CasaOS and Nextcloud on Ubuntu 22.04
authors: Hane Yorda Dinata, Galura Muhammad Suranegara, Ahmad Fauzi
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital data has increased the demand for efficient and reliable cloud-storage solutions. This study presents a comparative experimental evaluation of two open-source platforms, CasaOS and Nextcloud, deployed on Ubuntu 22.04. The primary objective is to assess Quality of Service (QoS) performance measured by throughput, latency, jitter, and packet loss. Experiments involved file transfers of 1 MB, 10 MB, 100 MB, 500 MB, and 1000 MB executed under controlled virtualized conditions; each scenario was repeated five times and the reported values are averages. Network traffic was captured and analyzed with Wireshark. Results indicate that CasaOS provides more stable throughput (2.4-4.0 Mbps) with negligible packet loss (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq \text{0. 6 \%}$</tex>), albeit with higher latency and jitter (up to 24 ms and 25 ms). Nextcloud exhibits lower and more consistent latency and jitter (7-14 ms) while showing more variable throughput; importantly, no packet loss (0%) was observed across all Nextcloud scenarios. These findings suggest CasaOS is better suited for reliable large-file transfers, whereas Nextcloud is preferable for latency-sensitive applications. The study offers empirical guidance for selecting open-source cloud-storage platforms according to operational priorities.

title: Decentralized Energy Management in Residential Microgrids: Simulation and Optimization for Homes Grouped by Proximity
authors: Fatma Ben Youssef, Yassine Ben Salem, Nihel Chekir, Ali Snoussi, Houcine Bendaoud
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: The deployment of distributed renewable resources in residential microgrids calls for efficient energy management strategies. This paper compares two decentralized control approaches applied to a 20 -house microgrid equipped with photovoltaic (PV) generation and battery storage. In the first method, energy is shared globally among all households, whereas in the second, the system is organized into four clusters of five, enabling intra-cluster exchanges prior to inter-cluster coordination. A MATLAB-based Energy Management System (EMS) is developed to simulate power flows, battery dynamics, and energy transfers. Performance is assessed using standard indicators such as self-consumption, self-production, and surplus utilization. Results show that clustering reduces energy losses, improves demand-generation matching, and enhances overall system resilience, demonstrating its effectiveness for sustainable residential microgrids.

title: Real-Time and Energy-Efficient VLC Indoor Localization Using Dimming-Aware Modulation and Lightweight Machine Learning
authors: Edo Lutfi Mahanani, Aminah Indahsari Marsuki, M. Imansyah Basudewa
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time and energy-efficient indoor localization system that leverages Visible Light Communication (VLC) for both data transmission and positioning. To address the challenges of illumination control and accurate localization, the proposed method integrates a dimming-aware Variable Pulse Position Modulation (VPPM) scheme with a lightweight Random Forest (RF) machine learning model. The system is evaluated using a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 6 2 5}$</tex> samples with 20 features, providing a solid basis for classification and regression tasks. Experimental results show that the proposed framework achieves a zone classification accuracy of 97.93% and mean localization error of 1.20 m. Beyond classification, regression-based estimation significantly enhances precision, reducing the mean error to 0.0835 m and the median error to 0.0669 m. Real-time simulations further confirm the stability of the approach, with an overall mean error of 0.214 m and a final accuracy of 98%, demonstrating reliable performance under practical conditions. In addition to its accuracy, the system exhibits extremely low energy consumption, requiring only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0 \times 10^{-11} \mathrm{J}$</tex> per symbol and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0 \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{J}$</tex> per localization for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=10$</tex>. These results highlight the potential of combining dimming-aware modulation with machine learning to achieve practical, energy-efficient, and high-accuracy indoor localization, making the proposed system a strong candidate for next-generation VLC-based smart environments.

title: Predictive Modeling of Groundwater Depletion Using Ensemble Machine Learning and Geospatial Data
authors: K. SenthilVadivu, G.P. Berathsaran, R K. Kiruthikvarshan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Accelerated urbanization, climate change, and unsustainable water usage practices are making groundwater issues more serious. Conventional monitoring methods usually lack a predictive component, are less accurate, and take a lot of time. The suggested system predicts groundwater levels using machine learning techniques based on conductivity, pH, temperature, and other environmental and water quality factors. The model is trained on historical data using XGBoost, a highperformance implementation of the gradient boosting technique. A straightforward Stream lit web application is then used to host the trained machine learning model, providing a user-friendly interface for real-time prediction access. This prediction algorithm offers a quick and reliable way to manage water resources and issue early warnings. Additionally, the technology can assist decision-makers in developing conservation and sustainable water usage plans. Compared to manual monitoring, the predictive model will be more accurate and scalable. Additionally, the approach is affordable, necessitates little infrastructure, and makes long-term planning of water resources and use easier. All things considered, this initiative will make it possible to make informed forecasts that will support sustainable groundwater management.

title: Real-Time Markerless Hand and Object Tracking with Occlusion Awareness for Augmented Reality
authors: Amulya Sakhamuru, Sandeep Vasireddy, Bhavsingh Maloth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) applications demand precise and real-time markerless tracking of hands and objects to enable natural interaction. Existing markerless approaches, while effective under controlled conditions, struggle with occlusion, leading to performance degradation in dynamic and cluttered environments. Additionally, many deep learning-based models are computationally heavy, limiting deployment on mobile or edge AR devices. This work aims to design a lightweight, occlusion-aware tracking framework that achieves high accuracy and robustness while maintaining real-time performance on edge platforms. The proposed system introduces an Occlusion-Aware Perception module, which explicitly integrates segmentation, depth ordering, and visibility reasoning to handle partial and severe occlusions. A hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture fuses spatial and temporal features with visibility cues for robust markerless tracking. Training is performed using the Rendered Hand Pose Dataset (RHD) for pretraining and validated on DexYCB and EgoGesture datasets. Optimization through quantization and pruning ensures real-time inference on AR hardware. Experimental evaluations demonstrate a reduction in tracking error by up to 35% under high occlusion compared to CNN-only, LSTM-only, and Transformer-only baselines. The framework sustains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>30$</tex> FPS with latency below 35 ms on Jetson Nano and HoloLens 2 devices. Case studies in AR-assisted surgery and AR-retail validate practical usability, achieving over 90% accuracy. The proposed framework delivers a compact, robust, and deployable solution for real-time occlusion-aware markerless tracking, bridging algorithmic innovation with practical AR applications.

title: Adversarial Multitask Learning for Accurate Change Detection in Multi-Temporal Satellite Images
authors: Tolulope Ale, Bayu Adhi Tama, Vandana Janeja
publish: ACM Transactions on Spatial Algorithms and Systems
abstract: None

title: Staged Training for Robust Crop and Weed Detection Under Temporal Domain Shift Using YOLO26
authors: Dmitriy Poleshchenko, Ilia Mikhailov
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This paper investigates temporal domain shift in crop and weed detection from aerial imagery during the growing season using the PhenoBench dataset. We demonstrate that the YOLO26m model, trained exclusively on an early phenological stage, experiences significant degradation in mean Average Precision (mAP) on subsequent stages due to changes in plant morphology and increased scene complexity. To improve robustness, we propose a staged training strategy that sequentially trains the detector on temporal domains corresponding to different acquisition dates within the same field and crop. Experimental results show that staged training exceeds the baseline (training only on the initial date) by +22.3 p.p. in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P}$</tex> at late-season stages and approaches the oracle scenario trained on all dates. Quantitative analysis of domain differences via Frechet Distance in YOLO26m's feature space demonstrates that temporal domain shift magnitude correlates with observed detection quality degradation, highlighting the effectiveness of staged training for accounting for phenological scene variability.

title: Modeling and Optimizing Dynamic Coalitions in Energy Markets Using Game Theory
authors: Diogo Ribeiro, José Baptista, Tiago Pinto, Adelaide Cerveira, Tiago Soares
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study provides a comprehensive review of how game theory can be applied to model and optimize dynamic coalitions in contemporary energy markets. With the increasing decentralization of energy systems driven by technologies such as solar photovoltaics, home energy storage, and electric vehicles, consumers have begun to play a more active and influential role in the market. In this new context, where cooperative and collective decision-making is gaining importance, game theory emerges as a valuable tool for analyzing and structuring these interactions. The primary objective of this work is to systematically review existing models, assess their methodological strengths and limitations, and identify open research gaps that hinder their applicability to real-world settings. By synthesizing the current state-of-the-art, this study aims to highlight pathways toward the development of more realistic and effective models that capture the dynamic and interdependent behaviors of energy consumers and the coalitions they form. Ultimately, this review seeks to provide an updated overview of this growing field, serving both as a basis for future research and as a foundation for the design of solutions that promote fairer, more efficient, and more participatory energy markets, especially for small-scale consumers, who now have greater voice and power of choice.

title: Implementation of Sharding to Improve the Performance of Blockchain
authors: Divyesh Bhatnagar, Anil Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Blockchain is used in many number of industries such as supply chain, healthcare and cryptocurrency because it is having decentralised Ledger. [1] [2] Blockchain is reliable, but as the users' increases, the blockchain faces scalability issues, such as storing nodes and validating transactions. It can be solved by sharding. [3] In sharding large blockchain network is divided into smaller nodes, which allows parallel processing. [4] This makes the system faster, but there can be security issues if too many shards are used in the node. By this study, we can analyses the security of sharded blockchain. The study has been conducted on the database, which is extracted from a blockchain network, to simulate real-world node and structural patterns. The result of this study gives information about optimising transaction speed in a sharding-based blockchain system. Our findings will conclude how sharding can be effectively implemented within blockchain network which will improve overall performance and solve the problem in the transactional blockchain system.

title: Research on Confirmation Mechanisms in Spatial Links
authors: Yushuo Liu, Lishi Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: In space links, due to sudden drops in communication quality on the forward link channel, reverse link congestion and low forward link throughput occur. Therefore, traditional transmission control protocols (TCP) cannot meet the communication requirements of space links. To address this issue, this paper focuses on the acknowledgment mechanism and proposes the TACK-N(TACK-NEW) mechanism based on the concept of the TACK(Tamed Acknowledgment) mechanism. The aim is to improve the shortcomings of the TACK mechanism by adjusting the frequency and quantity of returned acknowledgment information. This enhances the reverse link's congestion resistance and increases the forward link's throughput. Simulations conducted on the OMNET++ platform demonstrate that the TACK-N mechanism achieves an average throughput improvement of approximately 155% compared to the traditional TACK mechanism under high bit error rate scenarios, effectively alleviating reverse link congestion. This research provides a novel technical approach for efficient and reliable transmission over space links, holding significant application value for deep-space communications and satellite networks.

title: A Multiple-Decomposed Recursive Generalized Extended Least Squares Algorithm for High-Order MISO CARARMA Systems
authors: Roya Fathi Sabri, Munya Ali Arwin, Nasar Aldian Ambark Shashoa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper concerns the identification challenge in high-order system Controlled Autoregressive Autoregressive Moving Average (CARARMA) systems. Utilizing a decomposition-based approach, a novel multiple-decomposed recursive generalized extended least squares (RGELS) algorithm is used to estimate system parameters efficiently. A key difficulty in this process arises from the presence of unknown variables within the noise information vector. To overcome this, the algorithm replaces these unknowns with their estimated counterparts, leading to the formulation of the multiple-decomposed RGELS method. Compared to the conventional RGELS algorithm, the proposed technique offers superior computational efficiency by partitioning the system's covariance matrix into multiple smaller sub-matrices. Ultimately, the outcomes of the simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm has higher estimation accuracy under low noise variance, and model validation methods illustrates that the Best Fit Criterion and the Histograms indicating a high level of algorithm performance.

title: Enhancing Access to Urban Primary Health Care Through Digital General Practitioner Model: An Initiative for Women Health Inclusion Towards Achieving SDG
authors: Refat Ahmed Bhuiyan, Farhana Sarker, Margub Aref Jahangir, Chandrasagar Soloman, Syed Abdul Hamid, Khondaker A. Mamun
abstract: Primary healthcare in urban areas is scarce due to the lack of infrastructure and availability of structured healthcare services for urban dwellers, especially in slum areas. When it comes to the below-property population, particularly women, basic healthcare is inadequate, which creates vulnerability to women's health, such as maternal complications. To address this issue, we propose implementing a new General Practitioner (GP) model that emphasizes affordability and easy accessibility for primary healthcare (PHC). In the pilot phase, we served 1,24,926 people, 65% being women (mean age: 28, SD: 18.7). The model drew attention in women and raised awareness about primary healthcare (PHC), proven by the growing utilization of services. The model represents a significant advancement in women's healthcare and promotes equitable and inclusive care.

title: Saukhyam: A Sustainable Solution to the Sanitary Napkin Menace
authors: Minnie Diana A J, B Siva Kumar, S Giridhar Reddy
abstract: The most popular feminine hygiene product utilized during menstruation is sanitary napkins or menstrual pads. These are specialized textile products designed for women to hygienically absorb menstrual fluids during their menstrual cycle. They are crafted from various natural or synthetic materials. However, menstrual hygiene products utilized by women are predominantly composed of non-biodegradable plastic materials, and it takes between 500 to 800 years for a single sanitary napkin to decompose as per the Menstrual Health Alliance India. The exponential rise of markets for these menstrual products due to rising demand pose a serious environmental risk. The urgent demand for sanitary napkins that decompose and pose no danger to biodiversity without compromising hygiene and performance can be met by biodegradable substitutes. This research explores the development of a biodegradable alternative to the plastic backsheets found in conventional disposable sanitary napkins and reusable napkins. Kraft lignin and gelatin, both renewable biopolymers, are employed in a solution casting method to create films that can be enhanced further to replace the non-biodegradable leak proof layer in menstrual pads. The study aims to optimize the ligningelatin ratio for effective film formation while maintaining mechanical integrity.

title: Residual-Attention CNN for Dysgraphia Detection Using Synthesized Offline Handwriting from Online Traces
authors: Manabu Okawa
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Workshop on Computational Intelligence and Applications (IWCIA)
abstract: Handwriting serves not only as a means of communication and biometric security but also as a valuable source of diagnostic information for neurological and learning disorders. Dysgraphia, a writing disorder mostly prevalent among children, often goes undetected due to the limitations of traditional diagnostic methods, which heavily rely on subjective expert evaluation. To address this limitation, we propose a machine learning (ML)-based end-to-end framework for early dysgraphia screening using handwriting data collected from digital tablets. Our approach synthesizes offline handwriting images from online input by incorporating spatial features such as pen pressure and velocity, thereby enhancing visual fidelity while preserving handwriting dynamics. A convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture, augmented with residual connections and squeeze-and-excitation blocks, is employed to classify handwriting samples. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves the highest classification accuracy, outperforming the baseline CNN and traditional ML approaches on a public dataset. This framework enhances interpretability by integrating attention mechanisms that reveal the model's decision process, and bridges the modality gap between online and offline handwriting, providing a scalable and objective solution for dysgraphia detection in educational settings.

title: Socio-Economic Impacts of Clean Energy Transition in Bangladesh
authors: Firuz Ahamed Nahid, Khadiza Akter, S. M. A. Motakabber, AHM Zahirul Alam
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This study utilizes the PyPSA framework to evaluate the capacity expansion and socio-economic implications of Bangladesh's shift to a clean energy system, addressing its heavy reliance on fossil fuels (69 % of electricity from gas and coal). Three scenarios are modeled: 30% renewable energy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2030,50 \%$</tex> by 2040, and 100 % clean energy by 2050. The analysis uses high-resolution national official data to quantify capacity expansion, employment, land requirements, and investment. The 2030 scenario projects 65.58 GW capacity (23.57 GW solar), generating 59.63 TWh of renewables, 885,440 jobs, and requiring € 77.03 billion. By 2040, an optimal capacity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$137.19 \text{GW}(69.28 \text{GW}$</tex> solar) delivers 156.92 TWh, creating <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2,824,362$</tex> jobs with € 136.56 billion investment. The 2050 scenario achieves 474.9 TWh from 298.7 GW (176.58 GW solar, 62.81 GW wind) installation, yielding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7,074,651$</tex> jobs and needing € 254.43 billion. This transition eliminates fossil fuel dependence, enhances energy affordability, and promotes equitable economic growth. Land use escalates to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,300.41 ~\text{km}^{2}(2.23 \%$</tex> of Bangladesh's total land) by 2050, posing challenges in a land-constrained nation. Strategic policies promoting solar and wind incentives, innovative land-use solutions like rooftop and floating solar, and green financing mechanisms are essential to ensure the clean energy transition, energy affordability, and drive inclusive economic growth, positioning Bangladesh as a leader in sustainable development.

title: Software Diversity in Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
authors: Kush Dave, Ian G. Harris
abstract: Resource-constrained embedded devices often lack the security measures against Return-Oriented Programming (ROP) attacks, like Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR), due to overhead restrictions and device constraints. If a malicious actor were to get hold of this firmware, reverse engineer it and craft a ROP attack for a device and the firmware, all similar devices would immediately become vulnerable to this attack. Moreover, it has been shown that compared to general purpose devices, IoT and embedded devices are far slower to receive the patch for vulnerabilities, leaving many of them still vulnerable up to multiple months even after a patch has been pushed out. In this paper, we present a tool to generate logically diverse, but functionally equivalent firmware for resource constrained embedded devices. Our system is context aware and prevents the uncertain performance or large overheads usually associated with diversifying software due to addition of extra instructions. Our security analysis shows that by diversifying firmware, we are able to generate variants that are secure against generalized attacks that involve using ROP gadgets.

title: Embedded Tutorial: Design for EMC and Trust in Integrated Systems – Combining Robustness, Innocuity and Security Concerns
authors: Régis Leveugle
abstract: The main goal of this tutorial is to highlight similarities and complementarities between electromagnetic compatibility and some hardware security concerns. When designing integrated systems, these two categories of design requirements are tackled separately and different scientific communities deal with the two types of objectives. However, the evolution of the global context leads to a convergence that would justify more communication and cross-fertilization to foster improved practices on both sides.

title: Enhancing the Lifetime of STT-MRAM in IoT Applications by Current Balancing
authors: Somayeh Sadeghi-Kohan, Fatemeh Akbari, Mahta Mayahinia, Mehdi B. Tahoori, Sybille Hellebrand, Hans-Joachim Wunderlich
abstract: Spin-Transfer Torque Magnetic Random Access Memory (STT-MRAM) combines non-volatility, high endurance, and fast access with CMOS compatibility, making it an attractive technology for next-generation memory systems. However, depending on the data patterns, large write currents on the signal lines can cause electromigration, which may considerably shorten the lifetime of the memory. This paper targets applications with structured data patterns and presents a technique based on systematic data transformation to reduce current stress without modifying the core array structure or data semantics. A simulation study for a 7 nm STT-MRAM shows that the bitwise inversion of every second data word in image and audio data streams can greatly reduce the current density on the signal lines and enhance STT-MRAM lifetime.

title: Assessment of 5G Performance in Philippines Using Mobility Tests
authors: Therdpong Daengsi, Mongkol Siriwong, Phisit Pornpongtechavanich, Tipmanee Rompun, Zool H Ismail, Paramate Horkaew
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This study investigates the real-world performance of 5G networks in the Philippines through mobility testing across two scenarios: urban commuting and indoor environments. Two leading mobile network operators, anonymized as MNO-X and MNO-Y, were evaluated using G-NetTrack Pro as a tool to measure key radio metrics: signal strength (RSRP), signal quality (RSRQ), and signal clarity (SNR). Drive tests were conducted along the MRT-3 line in Metro Manila to simulate dynamic, realworld commuting conditions, while walk tests were performed in Terminal 3 of the Ninoy Aquino International Airport to capture low-mobility, indoor usage. The results show that MNO-Y outperforms MNO-X in mobile environments, delivering higher average RSRP, RSRQ, and SNR values. Conversely, MNO-X demonstrated superior performance in stationary indoor conditions, suggesting more effective optimization for enclosed spaces. These findings underscore the critical need to evaluate Quality of Service (QoS) based on varied user environments and highlight that broad 5G coverage alone does not guarantee consistent or high-quality user experience.

title: Downscaling Method for Ground Flash Density in Transmission Line Corridors Considering Terrain Effects
authors: Kuntan Zhang, Tao Yuan, Wenxia Sima, Jia Shi, Yueyue Wang, Yongli Yi
abstract: Ground Flash Density (GFD) serves as a critical parameter for evaluating the lightning strike risk of transmission lines.  It is typically derived from the statistical analysis of data monitored by Lightning Location Systems. However, constrained by the limited sample size of ground flashes and the detection accuracy of Lightning Location Systems, the spatial resolution of GFD is relatively low. To enhance the spatial resolution of GFD along transmission line corridors, this paper proposes a downscaling method of GFD based on the Monte Carlo method considering micro-terrain conditions. For a given original grid area, considering the influence of terrain on lightning, the lightning strike point is determined by the lightning leader fractal model, thereby generating a simulated dataset of ground flash samples dataset. The original grid is further divided into multiple subunits, and the GFD of subunit is calculated by combining the grid average ground flash density statistics, ultimately yielding the high spatial resolution ground flash density. The proposed method is used to calculate GFD in the actual grid area and the effectiveness of the method is verified.

title: Improving Out-of-Distribution Generalization on Point Clouds with Cross-Domain Adversarial Distillation
authors: Bowei Xing, Xianghua Ying, Ruibin Wang
abstract: Point cloud is an important data format to represent 3D objects and real world scenes, which is widely used in many fields such as virtual reality and autonomous driving. In recent years, various methods have been proposed for point cloud recognition and achieved great performance. However, since these methods are always under the i.i.d. assumption, the trained model inevitably suffers drastic performance degradation when evaluated on out-of-distribution (OOD) data. To address the above problem, in this paper, we propose a framework named cross-domain adversarial distillation, with the goal of learning a model that generalizes well on OOD point clouds. Concretely, we initiate by dynamically generating adversarial point cloud examples that deviate from original domain and exhibit diversification, so as to progressively broaden the training distribution. Subsequently, we implement a bidirectional distillation scheme to ensure semantic consistency between the generated novel-domain example and source domain example, which facilitates the exchange of cross-domain knowledge through teacher-student cooperation. By iteratively alternating between the above processes, the model is encouraged to learn more generalizable representations when encountering point clouds from novel distributions, leading to improved performance on the unseen target domain. We carry experiments on two typical datasets, ModelNet40-C for corrupted point clouds and Sim-to-Real for real-world point clouds, implemented with different 3D recognition models. The experimental results show that our method achieves better performance on these two benchmarks, which demonstrate the effectiveness of our method for improving point cloud OOD generalization.

title: HSD: Hough-Based Structure-Aware Detection of B-Lines in Lung Ultrasound
authors: Tuo Liu, Hao Zhou, Jia-Hao Wang, Yu Zhang, Chen Chen, Yang Chen, Guang-Quan Zhou, Lin Li, Xu-Feng Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: B-lines are artifacts produced by the interaction of the ultrasound with the small air-liquid interface, which often serve as crucial biomarkers for evaluating lung pathology, such as the presence of liquid. However, due to the reverberation phenomenon, B-lines manifest as blurred, strip-like comet tails perpendicularly originating from the pleural line, making their automatic identification in speckle-noisy ultrasound images particularly challenging. This study proposes a Hough-based structure-aware detection framework, dubbed HSD, which leverages structural priors and the intrinsic relationship between the pleural line and B-lines to enhance B-line detection in ultrasound images. First, the proposed method adopts the shared encoder and two collaborative decoders to improve B-lines identification with the auxiliary pleural line detection, ensuring effective representation learning of linear structural features under inherent prior constraints. Specifically, one decoder incorporates Hough-based regression to reinforce the modeling of the global linear nature for B-line detection, alleviating the appearance influences of the fuzzy comet-tail. Simultaneously, another pathway enhances the exploration of the slender, curved morphology by integrating semantic context learning with linear heatmap regression, thereby facilitating the detection of the pleural line for calibration of B-lines. Second, we introduce a position-aware rectification module to ensure the consistency of the pleural line and its perpendicular alignment with B-lines. This post-processing module reduces the influence of ambiguous pixels, improving the robustness of B-line detection. Extensive experimental results on an in-house ultrasound dataset demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach, which achieves a precision of 0.743, a recall of 0.953, and an F-measure of 0.837, substantially ahead of other methods, suggesting its potential for detecting pathological indicators in lung ultrasound.

title: AttriReBoost: A Gradient-Free Propagation Optimization Method for Cold-Start Mitigation in Attribute Missing Graphs_supp1-3681613.pdf
authors: Ronghui Zhang
abstract: In real-world graphs, node attributes are often incomplete due to acquisition costs or privacy restrictions, reducing representation quality and harming downstream predictions in graph neural networks (GNNs). A common remedy is feature-propagation-based imputation. However, cold-start effects arising from attribute resetting and low-degree nodes impede effective propagation and convergence in these methods. To address these challenges, we propose AttriReBoost (ARB), a propagation-based method that mitigates cold-start issues in attribute-missing graphs. ARB enhances global feature propagation (FP) by redefining initial boundary conditions and strategically integrating virtual edges, thereby improving node connectivity and ensuring stable and efficient convergence. The method supports gradient-free attribute reconstruction with low computational overhead, and we provide a rigorous convergence analysis. Extensive experiments on several real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of ARB, achieving an average accuracy improvement of 5.11% over state-of-the-art methods. In addition, ARB exhibits remarkable computational efficiency, processing a large-scale graph with 2.44 million nodes in just 16s on a single GPU. Our code is available at https://github.com/limengran98/ARB

title: A Review of Active Thermal Control for Power Devices: Advances, Challenges, and Applicability in Intelligent Power Modules
authors: Xiaolong Lin, Zhaokang Wu, Weiwei Wei, Guoqing Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The reliability of power devices and integrated intelligent power modules (IPMs) is critical for power electronics systems, where thermal stress remains a predominant failure mechanism. Effective thermal control is essential to achieve reliable and cost-effective power conversion. This paper focuses on reliability-oriented active thermal control (ATC) and presents a systematic review of multi-level ATC strategies, spanning from device switching characteristics and algorithmic regulation to system-level energy management.
