                    Bilayer Gate Dielectric
authors: Xuanming Zhang, Yunsong Xu, Jiachen Duan, Zifeng Qu, Haotian Ji, Chengmurong Ding, Hao Tian, Jiangmin Gu, Ivona Mitrovic, Harm van Zalinge, Sen Huang, Jie Zhang, Ang Li, Wen Liu
abstract: This article reports the monolithic integration of gallium nitride (GaN)-based complementary logic (CL) circuits using MIS-gated <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-FETs on a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-GaN/UID-GaN/AlGaN/GaN platform. By employing a bilayer gate dielectric stack (2 nm PEALD AlON/18 nm ALD Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{3}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, the MIS-gated <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-FET achieves enhanced gate breakdown and negligible leakage over a wide <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> range (−2 to 10 V). Time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB)-based lifetime modeling predicts a maximum operating <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {GS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 6.75V for 10-year reliability. Furthermore, the optimized <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-FET exhibits a high <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${i} _{\mathrm {off}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ratio exceeding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{{8}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, an enhancement-mode (E-mode) threshold voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {TH}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of −0.5 V, and a peak <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 10.6 mA/mm. The resulting GaN-based CL inverter demonstrates a large 11-V gate swing, rail-to-rail output, and well-matched <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-FET current density, offering a robust solution for GaN-based power integrated circuit (IC) integration.

title: Multiwall Carbon Nanotubes-Integrated Fiber Microsphere Probe Light Intensity Sensor with Harmonic Vernier Effect
authors: Ying Wan, Sheng Zhang, Chen Jiang, Jianxin Ren, Jingkun Xu, Jianxiang Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Reliable and highly sensitive fiber-based light intensity sensors are increasingly demanded for integrated and miniaturized optical sensing platforms. In this work, we demonstrate a highly sensitive all-fiber light intensity sensor based on a hollow microsphere Fabry–Pérot (FP) cavity probe coated with multiwalled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs). Light intensity detection is realized through the photothermal conversion of MWCNTs and is further enhanced by a harmonic Vernier mechanism. The harmonic order of the FP cavity can be flexibly controlled by adjusting the air cavity length and the diameter of the microsphere. Microsphere FP cavities operating at the traditional, first-order harmonics, and second-order harmonics Vernier effect are fabricated experimentally. Temperature response measurements reveal that sensitivity increases significantly with the harmonic order. For the same harmonic order, a larger envelope-free spectral range of the Vernier spectrum leads to higher temperature sensitivity. A maximum temperature sensitivity of 0.392 nm/°C is achieved for the probe operating at the second order harmonic. By further functionalizing the probe with MWCNTs to realize photodetection, a light intensity sensitivity as high as 2.5 nm/mW is achieved. These results demonstrate that the hollow microsphere structure provides an excellent platform for realizing high-order harmonic Vernier effects, while the MWCNTs coating enhances the light intensity response, enabling a highly sensitive and integrated all-fiber light intensity sensor.

title: A Novel Prescribed-Time Control Approach Under Unknown Control Gain and Mismatched Disturbance
authors: Guangtai Tian, Mehdi Golestani, Bin Li, Yongduan Song, Guangren Duan
abstract: In this article, a prescribed-time output feedback controller is proposed for a class of uncertain nonlinear systems with unknown control coefficients and mismatched nonvanishing disturbances. Both unknown control coefficients and mismatched disturbances are tricky to address by the existing prescribed-time output feedback control frameworks. Differently, a novel prescribed-time control criterion in conjunction with Nussbaum functions is proposed, and prescribed-time stability is achieved. Furthermore, design methods for a state observer and a prescribed-time output feedback controller are developed. With the proposed control design, both the system output and observer errors are rigorously proved to converge to zero within a prescribed time. Moreover, the unified prescribed performance (UPP) of the system output and the satisfaction of output constraints are simultaneously achieved. Numerical simulations and experiments are provided to illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed control design.

title: Development of a Realistic physical Phantom for laparoscopic and robotic Assisted Sacrocolpopexy Training and Associated_supp1-3684747.pdf
authors: Rosanna Maria Viglialoro

title: Directivity Enhancement of Movable Antenna Arrays with Mutual Coupling
authors: Wei Xu, Lipeng Zhu, Wenyan Ma, An Liu, Rui Zhang
abstract: In conventional antenna arrays, mutual coupling between antenna elements is often regarded as detrimental. However, under specific conditions, it can be harnessed to enhance the far-field directivity (i.e., beamforming gain). Theoretically, the directivity of an N-antenna superdirective array over the endfire direction can reach N2, significantly exceeding the directivity of a traditional uncoupled array which is N over all directions. This paper investigates the potential of mutual coupling effects in movable antenna (MA) arrays for directivity enhancement. A low-complexity algorithm called Greedy Search and Gradient Descent (GS-GD) is proposed to optimize the antenna positions for maximizing the array directivity over any given direction, where the antenna positions are first selected sequentially from discrete grid points and then continuously refined through gradient descent (GD) optimization. Numerical results demonstrate that the optimized MA array design by exploiting the antenna coupling achieves significant directivity gains compared to the conventional uniform linear array (ULA) without antenna coupling over all directions. Additionally, the proposed GS-GD algorithm is shown to approach the global optimum closely in most directions.

title: The AI Research Assistant: Promise, Peril, and a Proof of Concept_supp1-3683758.pdf
authors: Tan Bui-Thanh
abstract:
Can artificial intelligence truly contribute to creative mathematical research, or does it merely automate routine calculations while introducing risks of error? We provide empirical evidence through a detailed case study: the discovery of novel error representations and bounds for Hermite quadrature rules via systematic human-AI collaboration.

Working with multiple AI assistants, we extended results beyond what manual work achieved, formulating and proving several theorems with AI assistance. The collaboration revealed both remarkable capabilities and critical limitations. AI excelled at algebraic manipulation, systematic proof exploration, literature synthesis, and LaTeX preparation. However, every step required rigorous human verification, mathematical intuition for problem formulation, and strategic direction. We document the complete research workflow with unusual transparency, revealing patterns in successful human-AI mathematical collaboration and identifying failure modes researchers must anticipate. Our experience suggests that, when used with appropriate skepticism and verification protocols, AI tools can meaningfully accelerate mathematical discovery while demanding careful human oversight and deep domain expertise.

title: Energy-Efficient Air-Ground Cooperative Detection for UAV-Enabled ISAC Systems
authors: Pengfei Du, Siyu Zhao, Haotong Cao, Ziye Jia, Lei Liu, Jianbo Du, Celimuge Wu
abstract: With the exponential proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), the “black-fly” UAVs pose a severe threat to the public safety. Fortunately, the UAV-enabled integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system exploits the high mobility, line-of-sight (LoS) dominant links, and multi-antenna arrays to construct omnidirectional sensing fields and reliable communication links, enabling multi-view joint detection and tracking of black-fly UAVs, thus guaranteeing the low-altitude safety. However, the existing UAV-enabled ISAC systems suffer from limited beamforming gain due to small uniform linear arrays (ULAs), degraded extended Kalman filter (EKF) performance under strong nonlinear noise and time-varying multipath, and energy inefficiency stemming from sensing-communication resource competition. To deal with these challenges, we investigate the ground-air cooperative detection problem for UAV-enabled ISAC systems, where a ground base station (BS) cooperates with multiple cellular networks connected UAVs (CUAVs) to detect the black-fly UAVs. Specifically, an EKF-Neural Network (NN) based cooperative sensing data fusion algorithm to fuse the sensing results obtained from the BS and the UAVs for realizling high accurate, long-range, and continuous sensing of back-fly UAVs. Based on the fusion sensing results, we propose an energy-efficient joint beamforming and trajectory optimization algorithm to minimize the total ISAC energy consumption of the CUAVs under the stringent communication rate and sensing beampattern gain constraints, which has a positive feedback to the sensing data fusion. Abundant numerical results demonstrate the proposed strategy obtains the 18.4%–24.1% energy savings, 9.43% higher average communication rate and 27.3% improved sensing beam pattern gain compared with the benchmark schemes.

title: Stress Field Engineering for Ultra-Low Birefringence in Trench-Assisted Multi-Core Fibers
authors: Gustavo Ocampo, Kunimasa Saitoh
abstract: We propose and numerically demonstrate a method to achieve ultra-low birefringence in trench-assisted (TA) multi-core fibers (MCFs) by engineering the thermally induced stress field through the incorporation of doped silica stress rods. The approach is evaluated in two structures: a 4-core TA-MCF employing a single boron-doped rod at the cladding center, and an 8-core TA-MCF using one central and four peripheral fluorine-doped rods. By optimizing the rod positions, radii, and dopant concentrations, the method identifies parameter sets that reduce birefringence into the ultra-low regime by counteracting the stress asymmetries induced by neighboring cores. The optimized designs exhibit birefringence beat lengths exceeding 10 m and are expected to yield polarization-mode dispersion (PMD) on the order of 0.01 ps/√km with strong resilience to external perturbations. Moreover, the same stress balancing mechanism is expected to mitigate inter-core crosstalk (IC-XT) in certain MCF designs, thereby addressing the two critical impairments of PMD and IC-XT with a single approach.

title: GranSSG: Correlating Volumetric Granularities for 3D Semantic Scene Graph Prediction
authors: Kaixiang Huang, Qifeng Zhang, Jin Wang, Jingru Yang, Yang Zhou, Jiao Yi, Guodong Lu, Shengfeng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Predicting 3D Semantic Scene Graphs (3DSSG) is vital for understanding complex scenes by constructing structured representations. Current methods struggle with significant granularity discrepancies among instances, often relying on features at a single scale, which hampers their ability to perceive and interact with differently sized instances. To tackle this challenge, we introduce GranSSG, a novel approach that integrates volumetric granular awareness into 3DSSG prediction. Central to GranSSG is the Volumetric Pooling block, which aggregates features from multiple instance volumes, enhancing the representation of instance patterns across different granularities. Complementing this, the Granularity Transformer block dynamically directs attention to instance features across various network layers, ensuring precise perception of instances regardless of their granularity. Furthermore, the Cross-Granularity Correlation Transformer block mitigates performance degradation in instance pair relationship prediction by adaptively fusing hybrid features from different granularities, providing a comprehensive representation of instance pairs. Extensive evaluations on the challenging 3DSSG benchmark demonstrate that GranSSG significantly enhances prediction performance, setting a new state-of-the-art in 3DSSG prediction.

title: AdaptiController: VR-Enhanced Fine Motor Assistance Through Finger Pressure Modulation
authors: Hangyu Zhou, Haotian Mao, Zixuan Guo, Yushi Wei, Yan Zhang, Xubo Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This paper explores finger pressure as a continuous implicit input modality to enhance interaction precision in virtual reality (VR). While motion controllers are widely adopted, their limitations in delicate operations remain a critical challenge. We investigate whether finger pressure signals from conventional VR controllers could offer advantages over traditional kinematic metrics for precision interaction.Through empirical studies, we demonstrate a robust relationship between pressure dynamics and task precision requirements, leading to a lightweight sigmoid-based model that leverages detected pressure to infer desired control granularity. In a comparative evaluation of video-scrubbing tasks, our adaptive method outperforms static sensitivity baselines in both task performance and subjective preference, without elevating cognitive load. Further validation via a VR sketching application demonstrates that our technique maintains task performance while reducing mental demand compared to manual control. Our findings reveal the untapped potential of pressure-based input to bridge coarse and fine-grained VR interactions, offering a path toward more versatile and intuitive input systems.

title: HiPo-Gait: Hierarchical Pose model decoupling for Gait Recognition
authors: Nicolas Cubero, Francisco M. Castro, Nicolas Guil, Manuel J. Marin-Jimenez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Gait recognition has traditionally relied on silhouetteor appearance-based representations to analyze walking patterns. While silhouettes capture rich movement information, they are highly dependent on body shape and contours, which are potentially irrelevant to gait analysis. The human pose emerges as a more robust and semantically meaningful alternative. However, pose-based models have typically underperformed compared to silhouette-based approaches. To enhance gait feature extraction from pose representations, this paper proposes HiPo-Gait, a novel hierarchical architecture that analyzes isolated limbs of the body pose and groups them at different levels using a new local attention-based fusion module (LAF) to produce a robust hierarchical descriptor of the walking pattern. Moreover, instead of using point coordinates as input, we use heatmaps, a richer representation of the body pose. Our experimental results indicate that a) the hierarchical decoupling extracts richer features from every individual limb and b) the fusion approach optimally aggregates the limbs, outperforming the classical fusion operations. Finally, our approach achieves a percentage point increase of 3.3% over the top pose-based state-of-the-art model in CASIA-B, 21.9%, and 25.6%, respectively, in CCPG and SUSTech1K, and 1.7% improvement over the top model in the FVG-B dataset. Source code is available at https://github.com/Nico-Cubero/HiPo-Gait.

title: TechRxiv: Share Your Preprint Research with the World!
authors: N/A

title: Control-Aware Two-Stage Collision Screening for Real-Time Path Planning via MPC-Based Pose Correction and Certified Tube Inflation
authors: Jonghyup Lee
abstract: Local path planning for autonomous driving often generates multiple candidates and selects a collision-free one based on geometric clearance over a finite prediction horizon. However, such geometric screening implicitly assumes ideal execution, and the resulting decision can be inconsistent with real closed-loop behavior when tracking deviations and model–plant mismatch occur. This paper proposes a control-aware planning framework that incorporates the closed-loop tracking envelope of a tube-based MPC tracker into collision decision making. At each step, the planner generates kinematically feasible quintic-polynomial candidates from an anchor on the previously selected path and discretizes them on the same prediction grid used by the controller. To enable real-time operation, we adopt a two-stage screening architecture. Stage 1 performs fast geometric gating using a bounding-box-based collision check and forwards a reduced set of promising candidates to the next stage using a lightweight proxy cost that promotes goal compliance and temporal consistency. Stage 2 then performs control-aware verification for each remaining candidate by solving a nominal tracking MPC with the candidate as the reference, using the candidate-conditioned predicted lateral deviation to shift the ego footprint toward the expected trajectory center (pose correction), and inflating the footprint by a certified, speed-parametric tube radius derived from the robust invariant set underlying tube MPC to cover bounded residual effects. The resulting shift-and-inflate screening rejects candidates that would collide under realistic closed-loop deviations while avoiding overly conservative symmetric margins. High-fidelity CarSim/Simulink simulations with static obstacles and dynamic cut-in scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method improves closed-loop safety and robustness compared to purely geometric screening and static-margin baselines under aggressive, high-speed maneuvers.

title: On Maximum Correntropy GM-PHD Filtering
authors: Lin Gao, Jingling Li, Chaoqun Yang, Yao Zhou, Guobing Qian
abstract: Multi-target tracking (MTT) in real-world environments often faces the challenge of outlier measurements, which severely degrades the performance of standard MTT algorithms. This paper integrates the maximum correntropy criterion (MCC) into the Gaussian mixture probability hypothesis density (GM-PHD) filter, an implicit data association and a highly efficient MTT algorithm. The MCC provides a localized similarity measure that is inherently resilient to impulsive outliers. We embed an iterative fixed-point measurement update for the GM-PHD filter, and an adaptive kernel size design strategy is also devised. The proposed MCC-GM-PHD filter effectively suppresses the influence of large measurement residuals, while maintaining a closed-form Gaussian mixture representation. The performance of the proposed MCC-GM-PHD filter is verified via simulations.

title: Impact of Electromagnetic Interference From Consumer Electronics and Bluetooth Crowding on GNSS-Based Performance in Mobile Devices
authors: Luca Morichi, Iman Ebrahimi Mehr, Mikhael Russo, Cristian Carallo, Riccardo Tommasi, Andrea Nardin
publish: IEEE Journal of Indoor and Seamless Positioning and Navigation
abstract: This paper explores the impact of electromagnetic interference (EMI) from consumer electronics and common household appliance on the performance of GNSS receivers in smartphones. As GNSS signals are weak and susceptible to external noise and interference, consumer appliances can introduce spurious emissions within GNSS frequency bands, leading to signal degradation. To investigate this, we conducted a series of controlled experiments simulating typical smartphone usage in proximity to different sources of EMI among household appliances (i.e., a microwave oven) and consumer electronics (i.e., Wi-Fi and Bluetooth devices), ultimately focusing on a dense Bluetooth environment. Our analysis reveals that the microwave oven can induce severe GNSS signal degradation, with substantial AGC and C/N<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula> drops (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.3\,$</tex-math></inline-formula>dB-Hz) and significant satellite availability loss (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$18 \%$</tex-math></inline-formula>), particularly in close-range configurations, leading to positioning RMSE increases exceeding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7\,$</tex-math></inline-formula> m. Additionally, Wi-Fi interference resulted in moderate reductions in signal quality, with positioning RMSE increases of less than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\,$</tex-math></inline-formula> m in most scenarios. In contrast, Bluetooth exhibited negligible effects under tested conditions. These findings suggest potential implications for indoor navigation applications and underscore the importance of further improvements in interference mitigation for consumer GNSS devices.

title: TacMan-Turbo: Proactive Tactile Control for Robust and Efficient Articulated Object Manipulation_supp6-3683863.mp4
authors: Zihang Zhao
abstract: Adept manipulation of articulated objects is essential for robots to operate successfully in human environments. Such manipulation requires both effectiveness—reliable operation despite uncertain object structures—and efficiency—swift execution with minimal redundant steps and smooth trajectories. Existing approaches struggle to achieve both objectives simultaneously: methods relying on predefined kinematic models lack robustness when encountering structural variations, while the tactile-informed approach achieves robust manipulation but sacrifices efficiency through reactive, step-by-step execute-and-recover cycles. To address this challenge, this paper introduces TacMan-Turbo, a proactive tactile control framework that unifies the cycles into a continuous control loop. Our key insight is to interpret tactile signals temporally in addition to spatially: instead of treating contact deviations merely as instantaneous error signals requiring immediate compensation, we analyze sequential tactile observations to reveal local kinematic information. This temporal perspective enables our controller to predict future object states and proactively modulate manipulation velocities, eliminating the previous execute-and-recover cycle. In evaluations across 200 diverse simulated articulated objects and real-world experiments, our approach maintains a near-perfect performance while significantly enhancing time efficiency, action efficiency, and trajectory smoothness (all p-values < 0.0001). These results demonstrate that tactile feedback serves a dual purpose: providing not only spatial error signals for reactive control but also critical temporal structural information that enables proactive prediction and control. By enabling robots to manipulate articulated objects both reliably and efficiently, this work advances robot capabilities for seamless operation in dynamic, human-centric environments.

title: Dynamic Event-Triggered Target Encirclement Control for Heterogeneous UAV/UGV Swarm Based on Finite-Time Distributed Target Observation
authors: Shiqi Gong, Haibin Duan, Lingchen You
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Target encirclement by heterogeneous swarms composed of uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) and uncrewed ground vehicles (UGVs) represents a significant application in multi-agent cooperative operations. With the unified kinematic model for the air-ground heterogeneous multi-agent system (HMAS) established, a finite-time distributed target observer (FTDTO) is first developed to address the issue that not all individuals in the swarm can obtain complete target information. An extended state observer (ESO) is then designed to simultaneously estimate both the agent’s own state and external disturbances, effectively handling uncertainties arising from sensor measurement noise and nonlinear model dynamics. Furthermore, considering the inherent communication challenges and bandwidth limitations in complex air-ground cooperative networks, a dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) is employed. Based on this DETM, an HMAS encirclement control law is developed with adaptive radius contraction capability to prevent target escape. Theoretical analysis demonstrates the stability of the proposed ESO, FTDTO, and control law, and shows that Zeno behavior can be avoided. Simulation experiments are conducted to compare the proposed method with existing approaches, verifying the effectiveness of the proposed method and the target tracking capability with adaptive encirclement formation.

title: Verification of Resilient Current-State Opacity Against Dynamic Sensor Enabling Attacks
authors: Ziyue Ma, Yin Tong, Xiang Yin
abstract: In this paper we study the problem of verifying resilient opacity in discrete-event systems in the presence of dynamic sensor attacks. The system contains several secret states, each assigned a secret level. The system is vulnerable to sensor enabling attacks by potential intruders: such attacks allow the intruder to make certain originally unobservable events temporarily observable. We propose the notion of resilient current-state opacity (R-CSO) which is a property that ensures an intruder cannot definitively determine that the system is currently in a secret state with a secret level $k$ by performing fewer than $k$ attacks. To verify R-CSO in a given system, we propose a novel type of bipartite transition system (BTS), called the R-CSO BTS, which models the interaction between the system and the intruder. Based on R-CSO BTS, we then derive a necessary and sufficient condition for verifying R-CSO. Finally, we prove that if the sensor mask is distinct, i.e., the intruder has the capability to differentiate between originally observable and attackable events, then the R-CSO verification can be simplified by only encodes binary attack decisions into the R-CSO BTS.

title: Multi-Taper FFT-based Full-Spectrum Phase Demodulation for High-Precision GaAs F-P Temperature Sensors
authors: Zhiyuan Chen, Xingyu Zhang, Xuanwei Xiong, Yunjie Shi, Xingliang Shen, Tianyu Yang, Ronghui Xu, Huanhuan Liu, Shi Liu, Tao Tao, Yuming Dong
abstract: Conventional demodulation methods for fiber-optic Fabry–Pérot (F-P) temperature sensors are limited either by the spectral resolution of wavelength-drift approaches or by spectral leakage in FFT-based phase demodulation. To address these issues, this study proposes a multi-taper FFT full-spectrum phase demodulation method (Multi-Taper FFT-FSPD) for high-precision GaAs F-P temperature sensors. In the proposed dual-band cooperative scheme, the absorption-edge shift of GaAs in the 880 nm band is used for coarse temperature localization and free spectral range (FSR) counting, while high-precision phase demodulation is performed in the 1550 nm band using multi-taper FFT for fine temperature compensation. By combining one Hamming window and two mutually orthogonal DPSS windows through coherent averaging in the complex spectrum domain, the proposed method effectively suppresses spectral leakage and improves dominant-frequency phase extraction. Experimental results show that the proposed sensor achieves a phase sensitivity of 0.235 rad/°C and a demodulation error of ±0.0984 °C. Compared with the traditional wavelength-drift method and the multi-peak average wavelength-drift method, the proposed method improves demodulation accuracy by 63.6% and 38.8%, respectively. These results demonstrate the strong potential of the proposed method for high-precision real-time temperature monitoring.

title: Frequency-Guided Lightweight Network for Unsupervised Spectral Demosaicing
authors: Yaohang Wu, Renwei Dian, Shutao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multispectral filter array (MSFA) cameras enable snapshot spectral data acquisition but require effective demosaicing algorithms to recover spatial-spectral information. Existing methods either rely on iterative optimization with limited high-frequency recovery, or require large-scale paired data and sophisticated network architectures that introduce considerable computational overhead. Moreover, insufficient frequency awareness in both spectral image initialization and the training process often leads to biased representations and unstable optimization under unsupervised settings. To address these issues, we propose a lightweight unsupervised spectral demosaicing framework, termed Frequency-Guided Lightweight Network (FGLN). First, we propose the Frequency-Parameterized Convolution (FPC) as the spectral initialization strategy, enhancing frequency coverage by explicitly embedding frequency-aware parameterization into convolutional weights. Second, we develop a frequency-aware unsupervised training framework based on equivariant imaging to regulate the optimization dynamics and suppress degenerate solutions. Finally, the Lightweight Spectral Demosaicing Unit (LSDU) improves reconstruction quality while maintaining low computational complexity. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that FGLN achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art methods. The code will be uploaded at https://github.com/Matsuri247/FGLN.

title: Existence of asynchronous and synchronous behaviour in a neuromorphic Josephson Junction network
authors: E. Müller, F. Feldhoff, H. Toepfer.
abstract: Josephson Junction based neuron models form a basis for biologically realistic whole brain simulations with immensely energy efficient and fast superconducting hardware. To leverage the potential of neurons in Josephson Junction architecure, it is necessary to enable the assembly of networks and show that the dynamical properties of micro-circuits in the human brain can be replicated by ensembles of neurons. To achieve these goals, we provide a framework for simulating Josephson Junction based neurons in network configurations using common SPICE software tools. We provide a circuit design for the transmission of signals from one presynaptic neuron to arbitrary many postsynaptic neurons. Further, we demonstrate the feasibility of simulating such networks up to a size of 1000 neurons and investigate the existence of dynamical states such as synchronous and asynchronous firing through quantitative measures such as the coefficient of variance and the Kuramoto order parameter. Finally, we show where these network states exist and how their existence depends on the network connectivity.

title: An Enhanced High-Gain Cubic Step-Up DC-DC Converter With Low Voltage Stress and Continuous Input Current for DC Microgrid Applications
authors: Mahendra Chand Bade, Sankar Peddapati, S. V. K. Naresh, Rambabu Kallelapu
abstract: In this work, a cubic step-up DC-DC converter (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>SUC) is configured in three stages to achieve high gain with reduced voltage stress on the switching elements. Also, the proposed topology highlights the feature of providing continuous input current and establishing a common ground between the input and output. Additionally, the proposed work offers an extremely high voltage gain while enabling the switches to operate at a lower duty ratio, resulting in improved efficiency. A detailed comparative description of works related to the state-of-the-art of high-gain topologies is presented. Moreover, the operating modes, small-signal modeling for stability, practical voltage gain, and efficiency as a function of duty cycle and various power levels of the proposed converter are presented. Further, a hardware prototype with a 300 W power rating and a 400 V output voltage has been implemented in the laboratory. The results obtained are in line with the theoretical findings, exhibiting a peak efficiency of 96.2% and a voltage stress of switching devices lower than the output voltage. For validation, the microcontroller TMS320f28379D is used to test the converter in both open-loop and closed-loop modes, yielding satisfactory results under the dynamic changes applied to the proposed cubic converter.

title: Gaze Authentication: Factors Influencing Authentication Performance
authors: Dillon J. Lohr, Michael J. Proulx, Mehedi Hasan Raju, Oleg V. Komogortsev
abstract: This paper examines the key factors that influence the performance of state-of-the-art gaze-based authentication. Experiments were conducted on a large-scale, in-house dataset comprising 8,849 subjects collected with Meta Quest Pro equivalent hardware running a video oculography-driven gaze estimation pipeline at 72 Hz. State of the neural network architecture was employed to study the influence of the following factors on authentication performance: eye tracking signal quality, various aspects of eye tracking calibration, and simple filtering on estimated raw gaze. This report provides performance results and their analysis.

title: Environmentally Responsive Ag/BaTiO/TiO Nanocomposite for Selective Gas Sensing and Advanced Piezo-Photocatalysis_supp1-3678665.docx
authors: Deepshikha Rathore
abstract: This article reports the environmental sensitivity including piezophotocatalytic activity and gas sensitivity of the Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite toward ethanol, toluene, and isopropanol. The structural properties and crystalline morphology of Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite were determined by analysing peak positions of XRD pattern, high-resolution TEM images including 2D faceted, alongside Moiré fringes, and SAED patterns. In gas sensing trials, it showcased n-type semiconductor behaviour, with rapid response times of 3-5 s and recovery times of 3-7 s toward each test gas, showing enhanced sensitivity with increasing flow rate and temperature with best response for ethanol. Further, 70% degradation of ARS dye was achieved in photocatalytic activity, which was enhanced to 90% via piezo-photocatalytic activity with Ag/BaTiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> nanocomposite as catalyst. Consequently, these results present a promising new avenue for the development of advanced, efficient gas sensors and catalysts.

title: Topological Data Analysis for Breast Pathology Classification
authors: Aleksei Pakhomov, Anatoly Khamukhin
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Thermographic images of the mammary gland exhibit highly variable temperature distributions and low pathological contrast, complicating informative feature extraction. Conventional texture features rely on local information often dominated by task-irrelevant details. This paper investigates the use of topological data analysis (persistent homology) to capture global image structure. Although widely adopted, topological data analysis is mainly applied to homogeneous, well-formalized datasets; its effectiveness for heterogeneous thermographic data remains underexplored. The proposed method employs weighted persistence entropy for feature representation and gradient boosting for classification, and introduces a visualization technique to identify image regions contributing most to the decision process.

title: Improving Sentiment Classification of Product Reviews in Bahasa Indonesia Using Kernel-Based SVM
authors: Tuga Mauritsius, Aldi Rifaldi Saputra, Amelia Ludia Kafiar, Gabriella Gayle Monica, Astari Retnowardhani, Eka Miranda
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Sentiment analysis of user-generated reviews has become an important tool for understanding consumer opinions in e-commerce platforms. However, sentiment datasets in online marketplaces are often highly imbalanced, and limited research has examined how kernel selection in Support Vector Machine (SVM) models interacts with imbalance handling strategies in Indonesian-language text classification. This study presents a comparative analysis of Support Vector Machine (SVM) kernel functions for sentiment classification of Indonesian-language product reviews. A dataset comprising 5,495 reviews of Kahf skincare products was collected from Tokopedia and Shopee platforms and manually labeled as positive, neutral, or negative. Text preprocessing included normalization, tokenization, stopword removal, and TF-IDF vectorization, enabling unstructured text to be systematically transformed into structured numerical features for analytical modeling. Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed on the numerical feature representations (TF-IDF vectors) of the training data to mitigate class imbalance, while preserving the integrity of the test set. Three SVM kernelsLinear, Radial Basis Function (RBF), and Polynomial-were evaluated both with and without SMOTE balancing. Performance was assessed using accuracy, F1-score, and macroaveraged AUC. Results indicate that the Linear SVM with SMOTE achieved the most balanced and robust performance, attaining an AUC of 0.84 and outperforming the non-linear kernels. The findings demonstrate that a linear decision boundary, combined with oversampling, is well suited for sentiment analysis of high-dimensional Indonesian-language text in imbalanced scenarios.

title: Coal-Gangue Recognition Technology Based on Bimodal Data Fusion
authors: Yaohui Wang, Xiaoli Han, Xiaohu Sun, Yihang Hua, Bo Pan, Haoran Zhang, Zheng Wang, Hua Li, Lingbo Li, Qing Yang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Accurate online recognition of coal and gangue during top-coal caving is a key technology for improving coal quality, enhancing recovery efficiency, and realizing intelligent mining. This paper proposes a deep learning-based recognition method founded on bimodal signal-image fusion, in which sound and vibration signals are innovatively integrated into a unified composite image representation after standardizing the dimensions of the single-modality images from both sources. Experimental results show that using only acoustic signals yields an accuracy of 68.52%, while using only vibration signals achieves 74.4%. When both modalities are combined, the recognition accuracy on the test set increases by more than 10% compared to any single-modality model, reaching 83.7%. This study not only verifies the great potential of multisource information fusion in coal-gangue recognition but also provides a new technological pathway for intelligent separation in complex industrial environments. Furthermore, it offers a simple and effective approach for multimodal fusion of physical quantities such as vibration and sound in other application fields.

title: ARDUINO Based Smart Water Quality Monitoring System
authors: Sunaina Tiwari, Sapna Kumari, Arpita Ghosh, Sauraj Sen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This work proposes a Smart Water Quality Monitoring System implemented utilizing pH, Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensor, temperature sensor and ATmega328P microcontroller. The system provides real-time data acquisition and analysis, interfaced with an Arduino UNO microcontroller for data processing and transmission to a central monitoring unit. The implementation includes sensor calibration, data processing algorithms, and a user-friendly interface. Prototype testing shows reliable and accurate measurements, offering a cost-effective solution for maintaining water quality standards and ensuring public health.

title: Blockchain Technologies for Traceability and Efficiency in Service-Sector Supply Chains
authors: Joel Curado, Ana L Martins, Joao C. Ferreira
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Liquids-focused service-sector supply chains (healthcare cold chain, maritime documentation, luxury spirits) face concurrent challenges in traceability, privacy, and throughput. We present a modular blockchain architecture that combines a permissioned core with zeroknowledge proofs (zk-SNARKs) for selective disclosure, Layer-2 batching for scale, and IoT edge ingestion for realtime monitoring. Using Design Science Research, we design, prototype, and simulate representative workloads. Under WAN latencies typical of port operations, L2 batching reduces end-to-end verification latency relative to a Fabriconly baseline; in cold-chain scenarios, proof cadence tuning maintains privacy with bounded overhead. We discuss oracle and interoperability risks and provide a cost model. Results and artifacts are intended to guide practitioners balancing privacy, performance, and compliance in regulated logistics.

title: Standardized Communication Protocols for Digital Twin Integration in Discrete Manufacturing: From Machine to Plant
authors: Mohsin Raza, Naeem Ayoub, Arne Bilberg, Ali Ahmad Malik, Anis Assad Neto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Bringing together simulation models and real manufacturing systems is central to creating effective digital twins (DTs) and cyber-physical production systems. This study emphasis on the communication standards that make such integration possible, showing how information can move smoothly from a single machine to an entire production cell and, ultimately, the whole plant. The analysis is based on academic sources and global standards. We look at the structure, semantic modeling, time performance, and how well standardized communication protocols work for system integration. The research demonstrates that these standards collectively facilitate a seamless exchange of information between digital and physical realms. Drawing from existing literature, it formulates an integrated framework for communication spanning from the machine level to the cell and plant levels, grounded in these standards. It concludes that standardized communication is what makes the ISO 23247 digital twin framework operate and is what makes it possible to have scalable, secure, and interoperable manufacturing environments.

title: Anti-Cheating Online Exam Through Hand Gesture Answering Using Webrtc
authors: Rafli Zaki Rabbani, Siscka Elvyanti, Nurul Fahmi Arief Hakim
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Shorter exam formats, such as Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs), which are preferable for online exams has amplified vulnerabilities for exam dishonesty. Online exams easily exploited through search engines or current state of AIdriven platforms such as ChatGPT. Existing proctoring approaches, ranging from authentication, facial monitoring to browser lockdowns, remain limited by requirements and evolving cheating strategies. This research proposes a system designed to prevent remote cheating on online exams, particularly in MCQ exams, by requiring no additional software installation and hardware beyond a built-in camera, enforcing gesture-only answering. A dual-detection algorithm combined hand orientation validation with angle features classification, supported by delay buffers to reduce false inputs, with real-time video streaming and processing was facilitated through WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication). Experimental evaluation demonstrated that the system achieved robust performance and low latency, which maintained approximately 15 FPS (Frames Per Second). Offering a reliable and practical complement to conventional proctoring methods, enhancing the integrity of online MCQ examinations.

title: Agro E-Commerce Platform with AI-Based Disease Outbreak Prediction
authors: K. Vanitha, K.B. Jayanthi, Harish V, Kavyaa S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces persistent challenges such as soil nutrient degradation, crop disease outbreaks, and inefficient utilization of agricultural resources, which collectively reduce productivity and increase operational costs. To address these challenges, this paper presents an IoT-enabled agro e-commerce platform integrated with an AI-driven disease outbreak prediction framework. The system continuously monitors critical soil parameters, including moisture, pH, and nutrient levels, through distributed IoT sensors, while enabling real-time assessment of crop health. A machine learning-based multi-modal prediction model analyzes plant images, environmental sensor data, and weather information to forecast potential disease occurrences at an early stage, facilitating timely preventive interventions. The platform further incorporates an intelligent chatbot interface that delivers context-aware recommendations for fertilizer application, crop management, and disease mitigation. To enhance practical usability, an integrated e-commerce module allows farmers to directly procure recommended seeds, fertilizers, and agroinputs. Experimental results demonstrate a disease prediction accuracy of 92% and a statistically significant crop yield improvement of approximately 15 %, along with improved efficiency in soil resource utilization. The proposed platform provides a secure, scalable, and data-driven solution that supports sustainable and resilient agricultural practices.

title: Probabilistic Virtual Sensor Modeling for Building Digital Twins Using Generative Inverse Models
authors: Dagimawi D. Eneyew, Girma T. Bitsuamlak, Miriam A.M. Capretz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Building digital twins can reduce energy consumption and emissions by virtually replicating buildings' static and dynamic characteristics, enabling proactive decision-making to optimize operations. A key feature of building digital twins is their continuous evolution with the physical building, enabled by real-time sensor data. However, buildings often have sensing limitations, including difficulty measuring certain variables, insufficient redundancy, and absent or malfunctioning sensors. This study proposes a Probabilistic Virtual Sensor (PVS) modeling framework to address such limitations. Specifically, Conditional Variational Autoencoders (CVAE) and Conditional Invertible Neural Networks (cINN) were employed as generative inverse models for multi-variable sensing with uncertainty quantification. These models achieved less than 20% Mean Absolute Percentage Error in predicting zone-level lighting and less than 15% for plug loads. In uncertainty quantification, the cINN-based model reduced calibration error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.3 \%-99.9 \%$</tex>, outperforming the CVAE-based model. These results showed that PVS models address physical-sensing limitations while ensuring reliable decision-making.

title: DLR: A Unified Framework for Semi-Supervised Learning with Large Language Models
authors: Imran Ahmad, Khalid N. Alhayyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper introduces Distill, Label, and Refine (DLR), a teacher-student framework for semi-supervised learning (SSL) designed to address label scarcity in specialized domains by leveraging the capabilities of large language models (LLMs). In this framework, an LLM teacher first generates weak labels and explanatory rationales for a large corpus of unlabeled data. A compact student model then learns from these signals using robust SSL techniques, including confidence thresholding and distribution alignment. To mitigate the effects of noisy labels and confirmation bias, the framework incorporates a refinement stage in which pseudo-labels are audited and filtered through retrievalconsistency checks, critic-LLM reviews, and privileged feedback. By synthesizing state-of-the-art methods such as task-adaptive pre-training (TAPT) and retrieval-validated self-training, DLR provides a cohesive and practical methodology for developing specialized models under significant data and compute constraints. We demonstrate DLR's effectiveness on medical text classification and present preliminary cross-domain evidence on general-domain benchmarks, with comprehensive multi-domain evaluation reserved for future work.

title: Big Data of TikTok Shop Consumer Reviews for Mapping Service Quality Dissatisfaction Aspect Using ABSA Method
authors: Nanda Ardi Pradita, Gunawan Wang
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The rapid expansion of social commerce, particularly through TikTok Shop, has generated a massive volume of unstructured consumer feedback that meets Big Data characteristics. Although such data contain valuable insights into service quality, traditional sentiment analysis provides only document-level polarity and fails to identify the specific factors driving user dissatisfaction. This study aims to automatically map and rank the most influential service quality aspects contributing to negative sentiment by applying Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA). More than 29,000 consumer reviews were collected and processed through rigorous text preprocessing, including normalization, text cleaning, and stopword removal, before being vectorized using TF-IDF. Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Logistic Regression were employed for sentiment and aspect classification, achieving strong performance with an overall sentiment accuracy of 83%. Using ABSA, the study successfully identified the key aspects shaping consumer dissatisfaction. Results indicate that the delivery aspect represents the most dominant source of negative feedback, accounting for 76.63% of all negative reviews. These findings offer actionable, data-driven insights that can assist TikTok Shop management in prioritizing strategic service improvements and enhancing customer retention in a highly competitive e-commerce environment.

title: Brain Tumor MRI Interpretation and Classification using Swin Transformer Framework with Multi-Method Explainable AI
authors: Ch. Rajendra Prasad, Ramu Moola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The accurate and interpretable classification of brain tumors in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is crucial for supporting clinical diagnosis and improving patient outcomes. The given paper introduces an end-to-end deep learning pipeline with the help of Swin Transformer architectures to classify tumors by their types using an upscaled brain MRI dataset. Class imbalance is managed via more advanced training algorithms and built in explainability modules with attention rollout, Grad-CAM, Integrated Gradients, SHAP, LIME, and occlusion sensitivity extend the concept of interpretability of predictions of a machine with a multi-perspective view. Experimental evidence on a community standard indicates the high classification precision, as well as detailed visual explanations, which enhance transparency of the model and clinical confidence. This paper indicates the importance of multi-channel XAI to contemporary vision transformers in medical imaging processes.

title:
High-Performance Activated Carbon Derived from Palm Petiole Using Microwave-Assisted H
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    P O
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    Activation for Methylene Blue Adsorption
authors: Nesrine Abderrahim, Amira Hrichi, Mariem Ahbil, Hedi Ben Amor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Activated carbon (AC) was synthesized from palm petiole (PP), an abundant agricultural waste, using phosphoric acid activation followed by microwave-assisted impregnation and carbonization. The material was applied for the adsorption of Methylene Blue (MB) from water-based solutions. Important operational factors, such as pH solution, contact time, initial dye concentration and adsorbent dosage, were systematically exanimated. Adsorption was optimal at neutral pH and an adsorbent dose of 0.24 g/L. The equilibrium data were best represented by the Langmuir isotherm, whereas the adsorption kinetics were described by a pseudo-second-order model with a strong correlation coefficient (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). With a specific surface area of 895 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/g and a maximum adsorption capacity of 352.6 mg/g, the produced AC showed promise as an effective and long-lasting dye removal adsorbent.

title: XGBoost-Based Web Application for Optimizing Credit Evaluation in Peruvian Loan Fintechs
authors: Rodrigo Salvador Gonzales Ysla, Kenny Alexander Ayme Cuba, Jymmy Stuwart Dextre Alarcon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The rapid growth of the fintech sector in Peru has created an urgent need for more accurate and automated credit evaluation systems, as traditional approaches often fail to consider external or alternative data sources. This paper proposes the development of a web application based on the XGBoost algorithm to optimize credit risk prediction in Peruvian fintech companies offering personal loans. The study followed four stages: data collection and preparation, model training, web application construction, and validation. A dataset of 59,682 historical loan applications provided by a Peruvian fintech company was used, after applying data cleaning, normalization, and categorical encoding techniques. The XGBoost model was compared against Random Forest and Logistic Regression using classification metrics such as accuracy, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC. Results show that XGBoost achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.965 and an F1score of 0.977, offering a balanced trade-off between accuracy and computational efficiency. When integrated into the web platform, the model reduced the average credit evaluation time by over 60 percent and increased accuracy by 13.2 percentage points compared to the manual process. Overall, the proposed system demonstrates that using XGBoost in a web-based environment significantly improves the automation, consistency, and speed of credit evaluation processes, contributing to more reliable financial decision-making in Peruvian fintechs.

title: Transforming MRI Data Into Latent Space Embeddings Using Multi-Head Learning to Understand Alzheimer's Progression
authors: Manasvini Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a fatal neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions, with one in nine people over 65 years of age diagnosed, and AD-related care costs projected to reach one trillion dollars by 2050. This research focuses on visualizing the progression of AD by leveraging latent space embeddings to depict a continuous trajectory of disease stages, transitioning from cognitively normal (CN) to AD with smooth bridges connecting intermediate states. MRI data from the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) were preprocessed using FreeSurfer for brain segmentation, and thresholding was applied to reduce input size while preserving key structural features. Several models were evaluated, with ResNet101 achieving the highest F1 score of 0.39. A multi-modal feedforward neural network (FNN) was then introduced to aggregate slice-level predictions, improving the F 1 score to 0.42. A multi-head ResNet101 variant integrating patient metadata (age, sex, and APOE genotype) yielded more distinct and interpretable latent space visualizations. While class overlap and misclassification remained challenges, the multi-head model revealed enhanced class separability and provided a clearer visual continuum of disease progression. Overall, this work presents a data-driven framework that transforms high-dimensional MRI scans into low-dimensional latent space embeddings, enabling visual exploration of Alzheimer's progression through interpretable maps that reveal the continuous transition from healthy to diseased states, offering a visual analytics perspective on neurodegenerative change.

title: LockEase- Smart Door Security System
authors: Pallavi Nayak, Gunnikaa P Gupta, Vipul Raj, Manishka Nayaka, Sushma Shree H S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The Smart locking system is of advanced technology for an access control system implemented for residential and commercial security, where the traditional locking systems are based on physical keys, whereas RFID tags and password authentication access, which are prone to loss, duplication or unauthorized access which has an impact on the security. To overcome these limitations, this idea revolves around a smart locking system that combines biometrics, mobile app-based unlocking, biometric verification, remote access control, and live surveillance using the ESP32, and integrates radio frequency identification for access control. Every attempt is recorded and timestamped, and the activity log also enhances real-time communication by enabling remote control, allowing users to see and verify, after which they can grant access to the protected system safely. The system can also be accessed and monitored through a web application with instant notifications, which is reliable and scalable. The system can be installed and used as a security system in any environment, like houses, apartments (commercial and private), wardrobes, safe boxes, gates and small businesses. Compared to a traditional locking system, the smart door locking is customizable to an extent, making it versatile in use and easily implemented.

title: Prediction Comparison in Educational Data using Hybrid Time Series SARIMA-XGBoost Model
authors: Gede Bramanta Pandya Wisesa, Muhammad Zarlis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Accurate student enrollment forecasting is a cornerstone of institutional budgeting and resource allocation. The present study proposes and assesses a hybrid time-series model for forecasting monthly new-student enrollments at the Bali Institute of Design and Business. The methodology utilizes a two-stage, residual-learning framework. Initially, a Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) model is fitted to capture the primary linear trends and seasonality in the data. Secondly, an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model is trained on the resulting residuals to learn the remaining non-linear patterns, reconciling statistical structure with flexible non-linear function approximation. Two distinct sets of engineered features were tested to optimize the XGBoost component, incorporating multiple lag variables and rolling mean windows to capture short, medium, and long term dynamics. The model's performance was evaluated using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (sMAPE). The model was trained using data from 2021–2024 and tested using data from 2025. The findings substantiate that the hybrid model, particularly when endowed with a comprehensive feature set, exhibits a marked superiority in performance over the standalone SARIMA model, achieving an 18% reduction in MAE, thereby furnishing a more precise forecasting instrument for institutional planning.

title: ML-Based Analysis of Crop Recommended System
authors: Rahul Bhandari, Suresh Kaswan, Daulat Sihag, Reshma Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This study is interested in assisting farmers to select the right crop on their fields using the latest methods to enhance crop management system. The current study uses a Kaggle dataset of 2,200 inputs and outputs: eight inputs (see Figure 3), i.e., N, P, K, rainfall, pH, temperature, humidity, and labels of 22 crops to fit and test predictive models. Five classification algorithms, i.e., Decision Tree (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and K-Nearest Neighbour (KNN), were used and performance evaluated by Precision, Recall, F1-score and Mean Squared error. The findings suggest that the Random Forest algorithm is both the most accurate and predictive algorithm, whereas Logistic Regression performs the worst.

title: Performance Analysis of OFDM, OTFS, and AFDM Modulation Over Doubly Selective Channels
authors: Amani Bouguila, Abdelhakim Khlifi, Mohamed Yahia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation is a novel technique designed to address the challenges of communication over doubly selective channels, where both time and frequency variations occur due to high mobility. Affine Frequency-Time Modulation (AFDM) extends the concept of time-frequency processing by applying an affine transformation to ensure orthogonality in both domains, making it suitable for combating time and frequency selectivity. In contrast, Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) modulates data onto orthogonal subcarriers in the frequency domain. This work compares OTFS, AFDM, and OFDM in terms of their structure, performance, and resilience under doubly selective channels, with a focus on bit error rate (BER) performance across varying signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). This paper aims to offer valuable insights into the performance of modern wireless systems under various real-world conditions, providing practical recommendations for designing robust communication systems that can adapt to diverse channel environments.

title: Edge-Efficient Crop Health Monitoring using Selfsupervised Shufflenet and Attention-Based Multimodal Fusion
authors: P. Vanathi, R. Nithees Kumar, K. Gokul, S. Giritharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: A major concern of sustainable agriculture is to ensure crop health, especially with rising climate variability and low natural resources. The majority of approaches to crop monitoring are based on the traditional approach or deep neural networks that necessitate a lot of manual inspections and are computationally complex, which is incompatible with real-time monitoring of large fields. The following paper will suggest an efficient and smart crop health monitoring system, which will be implemented as a lightweight ShuffleNet-driven deep neural network, with heavy emphasis on high-precision and low-computational-overhead. Self-supervised learning is used to perform initial training on a large number of unlabeled crop images to extract robust and domain-invariant features and avoid the need to annotate a large number of Paris crop images manually. In order to further improve stress detection performance, the visual data is fused with the measurements of the environmental sensors, such as soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and nutrient levels, using an attention-based multimodal fusion mechanism that adaptively focuses on the most effective biotic and abiotic stress indicator. Also, an edge cloud collaborative inference architecture is presented with lightweight ShuffleNet models running real-time analysis at the edge and temporal modeling on the cloud assisting in making early predictive decisions before crops progressively lose their health. Experimental findings also indicate that the suggested framework is more precise and generalizes better and incurs less inference latency than the traditional approaches based on supervised convolutional neural networks. In general, the system is scalable, energy efficient and suitable to be applied to next generation precision agriculture uses.

title: A Deep Federated Learning Approach for Secure Malware Detection in Cloud Computing
authors: B. Chermadurai, S. Singaravelan, P. Gopalsamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: In this paper, a privacy-preserving malware detection framework for cloud infrastructures is presented using federated learning and deep neural networks. The proposed system is designed to address the growing challenges of data privacy, distributed cloud environments, and evolving malware threats. Federated learning is employed to enable collaborative model training across multiple cloud clients while ensuring that sensitive data remain on local devices and are never centrally shared. Advanced deep learning (DL) architectures, namely Transformer-based and GRU-enhanced models, are integrated to capture both long-range contextual dependencies and temporal behavioral patterns in cloud network traffic. An ensemble learning strategy is adopted to combine the strengths of both models, resulting in improved detection accuracy and robustness. The system is evaluated using a synthetic dataset that simulates real-world cloud traffic scenarios, and performance is assessed through standard metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC, and confusion matrix analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high detection performance while maintaining strong privacy guarantees. The findings confirm that the integration of federated learning with hybrid DL models provides a scalable and effective solution for secure malware detection in cloud environments, making the framework suitable for deployment in privacy-sensitive and large-scale distributed systems.

title: Command-Based Speech Recognition with LSTM for Visually Impaired Users
authors: Gabryelle Ninna Deffanya Siahaan, Annisa Fadhillah Pulungan, Rossy Nurhasanah
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Visually impaired individuals face significant barriers in accessing text-based information, which impacts their independence. To address this, this research presents the implementation of a voice command system for a mobile application designed to assist blind users in reading physical documents. A custom dataset consisting of seven specific Indonesian voice commands was collected from 53 speakers and was augmented to ensure model robustness. MelFrequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) were extracted as features from the preprocessed audio signals. The LSTM architecture was optimized through a series of experiments with different hyperparameters and layer combinations to achieve the best performance. The evaluation of the final model demonstrates high efficacy, achieving a classification accuracy of 96.6% and a macro-averaged F1-Score of 0.966. Furthermore, the system exhibited excellent real-time processing capabilities, with a Real-Time Factor (RTF) of 0.0314. The results confirm that a simple LSTM architecture is highly effective for recognizing a limited set of voice commands in the Indonesian language. This work provides a foundational component for an assistive mobile application aimed at enhancing the independence of visually impaired users, contributing to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by reducing inequalities.

title: Design and Construction of ELCB Practical Equipment as Touch Voltage Protection
authors: Sunarto, Toto Tohir, Yudi Pranahikmat, Sudrajat
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The tools and practicums required in the Low Voltage Protection course of the D3 Electrical Engineering Study Program, Department of Electrical Engineering, Politeknik Negeri Bandung include a practicum tool for testing leakage current protection components using Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker (ELCB). The availability of these practicum tools is essential to increase the practicum facilities for the Low Voltage Protection course. This practicum tool can be used for ELCB testing as protection against direct and indirect touch voltage disturbances. Direct touch voltage occurs when humans directly touch the phase part. At the same time, indirect touch voltage occurs when there is insulation damage so that there is a leakage current to the equipment body, which can cause a touch voltage hazard. Based on these problems, this research was conducted to improve the performance and services of the Electrical Installation Laboratory of Politeknik Negeri Bandung as the main facility for learning and research. In low-voltage systems, the ELCB protection component is a very important protection component to prevent the occurrence of touch voltage hazards. As touch voltage protection, MCB has a very important role in safety in the world of electrical system work. The method used in this research is the engineering research method. The result of this research is a single-unit laboratory tool, specifically an ELCB testing tool for touch voltage protection. The ELCB component used in the testing is a single-phase 30 mA ELCB.

title: Advanced IOT-Based Real-Time Fire Detection and Smart Home Environmental Monitoring System
authors: Khakam Ma'ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Desi Ayu Kumalasari, Nur Azizah, Darmono, Nur Evirda Khosyiati
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The implementation of an early fire warning system is essential to mitigate substantial losses and provide individuals with sufficient time to evacuate. This study develops an Internet of Things (IoT)-based fire detection system designed to effectively monitor environmental conditions in residential settings. The system integrates an infrared sensor, MQ-2 gas sensor, and DHT11 humidity sensor to detect potential fire hazards in realtime. Utilizing the ESP32 microcontroller, the system processes sensor data and transmits notifications via the Blynk application to users' smartphones. Experimental results indicate that the infrared sensor achieves 73.3% accuracy in detecting flames, while the MQ-2 sensor and DHT11 sensor record 86.67% accuracy in detecting hazardous gas concentrations and humidity levels that could trigger fires, respectively. This study underscores the importance of continuous environmental monitoring to mitigate fire risks. By employing this system, users receive early warnings, enabling swift action in emergency situations. The IoTbased fire detection system proposed in this research is expected to enhance home safety and can be adapted for industrial and smart building applications.

title: A Smart Electricity Meter Using IoT for Real-Time Monitoring and Remote Control
authors: Linga Reddy Gari Kavyanjali, K. Amaresh Srujan Gupta, Jonnavaram Gireesh Reddy, Markapuram Lokesh Kumar, Kavyashree I Pattan, Nandini K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Traditional electricity meters leave consumers with nothing but information on their consumption, giving them extremely little real-time feedback on the usage of energy. Thus, consumers cannot manage electricity utilization effectively, and unscheduled energy utilization and surprise bills are experienced. With growing needs for electricity, there is an urgent requirement for smart energy management systems that are consumer-friendly and utility-friendly as well. In this paper, an IOT based smart electricity meter system design, with a focus on real-time monitoring, enhanced accuracy of bill estimation, and better control over the utilization of energy by the users, has been described. The system has been implemented on an ESP8266 micro controller with a voltage and a current sensor and is webenabled with a web interface, where electricity statistics of a user can be checked on any device connected to the internet. The most prominent features are real-time units of usage, history of usage, and prediction, which assist the user in identifying which appliance is drawing more electricity. Users can set personal consumption levels and are notified when they reach near these levels in order to utilize energy in the most optimum manner. All these enhancements lead to improved utilization of energy distribution. Remote monitoring and control of energy eliminate the necessity for manual readings of meters and therefore maximize operations and minimize inefficiencies. With the use of data analytic and IOT, such a smart metering solution is a costsaving, scalable solution for electricity management in the present day. The suggestion is that such a system's operation can instill increased efficiency in household electricity utilization, cut costs, and head towards a greener and smarter grid.

title: Hybrid Blockchain-Based Presidential Election System to Increase Vote Efficiency and Security
authors: Inayatulloh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: The presidential election is a process of voting by the people to elect the president and vice president of a country. This process involves citizens actively participating in determining the national leader who will lead the government for a given period. The purpose of the presidential election is to realize democracy and ensure that state power comes from the sovereignty of the people by electing leaders through majority votes. Several countries have used information technology to support presidential elections, making the process easier. The problems arising from conventional information technologybased election systems include data manipulation and nontransparent vote-counting processes, which cast doubt on the presidential election system. Several studies offer solutions using blockchain technology. Blockchain technology offers several advantages, such as peer-to-peer validation, which ensures transparency in the vote-counting process, and distributed storage, which provides security for vote-counting data. However, research on applying existing blockchain technology is limited to conceptual models that are difficult to understand and implement. Therefore, this research aims to build a prototype of a presidential election using Ganache. Selecting a hybrid blockchain for research enables the government-owned presidential election system to be used for blockchain-based presidential elections. Research uses a qualitative approach through literature review to study the weaknesses of conventional information technology-based and blockchain technology-based presidential election systems. The latest research is a prototype of a presidential election using a hybrid blockchain and simulation with Ganache.

title: Ml and Sentinel-2 Data for Crop Prediction: RF and KNN Performances
authors: Salah Benmahmoud, Olfa Charfi, Raef Maali, Yasmine Zghal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This study evaluates the temporal dynamics and the apparent change in Land Cover (LC), focusing on the region of El Houareb, in the governorate of Kairouan located in center of Tunisia country where arid climate prevails. By using the June 2019 dataset, we predicted the LC properties and highlight their changes by using two Machine Learning (ML) methods Random Forest (RF) and the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), for the dates of: November 2023, February 2024 and May 2024. To achieve this goal, we used the TCI (True Color Image) Sentinel2A satellite images obtained from the Copernicus hub, coupled with onsite ground truth data of June 2019 which representing the diverse vegetation and LC of the terrain: Arboriculture, Bare soil, Olives, Fallow, Cereals and Market Garden. These ML classifiers showcase a results with accurate and a visually appealing classification reaching an overall accuracy values more than 0.72 %. However, this study highlights the distinctive disparity between the two ML algorithms in LC.

title: An Improved MPPT Performance Using Horse Herd Optimization Algorithm for PV-Battery Charge Controller Application
authors: Rizky Ajie Aprilianto, Subiyanto, Mario Norman Syah, Dwi Bagas Nugroho
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
