title: Defending against Patch-Based and Texture-Based Adversarial Attacks with Spectral Decomposition
authors: Wei Zhang, Xinyu Chang, Xiao Li, Yiming Zhu, Xiaolin Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Adversarial examples present significant challenges to the security of Deep Neural Network (DNN) applications. Specifically, there are patch-based and texture-based attacks that are usually used to craft physical-world adversarial examples, posing real threats to security-critical applications such as person detection in surveillance and autonomous systems, because those attacks are physically realizable. Existing defense mechanisms face challenges in the adaptive attack setting, i.e., the attacks are specifically designed against them. In this paper, we propose Adversarial Spectrum Defense (ASD), a defense mechanism that leverages spectral decomposition via Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to analyze adversarial patterns across multiple frequency scales. The multi-resolution and localization capability of DWT enables ASD to capture both high-frequency (fine-grained) and low-frequency (spatially pervasive) perturbations. By integrating this spectral analysis with the off-the-shelf Adversarial Training (AT) model, ASD provides a comprehensive defense strategy against both patch-based and texture-based adversarial attacks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ASD+AT achieved state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance against various attacks, outperforming the APs of previous defense methods by 21.73%, in the face of strong adaptive adversaries specifically designed against ASD.

title: A Novel Design of Pulsed Magnet for Repetitive Magnetic Field
authors: Yihang Huang, Huidong Shang, Shaobo Liu, Aoming Ge, Shuang Wang, Liang Li, Yiliang Lv, Tao Peng
abstract: Repetitive pulsed magnetic fields play a critical role in neutron diffraction, room-temperature magnetic refrigeration, and pulsed terahertz gyrotron. In this article, a novel design combining flux concentrator (FC) and coil with cooling channels is proposed. This technique mitigates the thermal load generated in the coil by transferring part of it to the FC, thus enhancing both the magnetic field strength and the repetition rate. Comprehensive simulations of the electromagnetic, thermal, and mechanical behaviors were conducted, showing that the maximum temperature is 430 K and the maximum stress is 57.7MPa. A 10-T pulsed magnetic field at a repetition rate of 5 Hz was successfully generated.

title: Learning When and How to Update Memory for Video Object Segmentation
authors: Shengye Qiao, Changqun Xia, Yanjie Liang, Xiaowu Chen, Jia Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Recent progress in semi-supervised video object segmentation has largely hinged on memory-based methods. However, when faced with increasingly tough challenges emerging in complex scenarios, such as fundamental semantic transformations and severe spatial deformations, the fixed-interval memory update mechanism usually adopted in these memory-based methods is insufficient to align with the pivotal moments of object changes. This inflexible mechanism motivates us to design an adaptive memory update mechanism in response to the semantic-spatial changes of target objects. To this end, we propose a novel Change-Sensitive Network (CSNet) to learn when and how to update memory to effectively address intricate challenges in complex scenarios. Specifically, wefirst design an Adaptive Perception-Capture module with a hierarchical contrastive learning loss to determine when to update memory moments by measuring the extent of object changes, thus dividing entire videos into different object-change clips. To further extract and highlight object changes to assist in the segmentation of frames after changes occur, we construct Dynamic Memory Update modules to redefine how to update memory by smoothly retaining the object prototypes within clips and dynamically amplifying the object variations across clips. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed CSNet exhibits clear superiority when evaluated on eight datasets covering three kinds: common, complex and long-video datasets.

title: The Sliding Truncation Matched Filter for Periodic Pulse Interference Mitigation in Spaceborne SAR
authors: Jieshuang Li, Mingliang Tao, Lei Cui, Yujuan Li, Yanyang Liu, Ling Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In practical applications, periodic pulse radio frequency interference (PRFI) is often detected in spaceborne SAR echoes. The current suppression methods for such interference mostly adopt a three-step processing flow: “time-domain detection, notch filtering, and missing data repair”. However, most of these methods focus on improving time-domain detection performance while ignoring the impact of the interference-to-signal pulse repetition frequency ratio (ISPRFR) on the effectiveness of data repair algorithms. To this end, this paper proposes a sliding truncation matched filter (STMF) that dynamically constructs a range-dependent azimuth reference function by adaptively truncating the matched filter according to the range-variant Doppler centroid. This enables coherent azimuth compression while suppressing ghost targets induced by periodic signal loss. The effectiveness of the STMF is validated through simulations and real-measured data experiments.

title:
CGM
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Net: Cloud-Guided Mamba-Based Multitask Network for Multimodal Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation and Cloud Removal
authors: Jianhao Li, Jue Wang, Hao Shi, Shan Dong, Wenchao Liu, Liang Chen, Wei Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Semantic segmentation and cloud removal via optical-Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) fusion are crucial tasks in Earth observation. Existing works have proposed multitask architectures to simultaneously address segmentation and cloud removal, but they lack targeted modeling of under-cloud features, leading to blurred ground-object reconstruction and poor fine-grained recognition performance in thick-cloud regions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Cloud-Guided Mamba-based Multitask Network (CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net) for end-to-end multimodal segmentation and cloud removal. Specifically, CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net utilizes a dual-stream encoder and integrates three key modules at each stage: cloud-guided feature restoration (CGFR) module, which, under cloud-mask-guided gating, exploits spatial- and channel- wise cross-modal correlations to reconstruct sub-cloud optical features and suppress SAR speckle; cloud-guided semantic interaction (CGSI) module, which performs mask-aware state-space modulation to enable selective, region-aware cross-modal semantic exchange; and Mamba-Based Fusion (MF) module, which adaptively fuses enhanced multimodal features to fully exploit modal complementary information. Two task-specific decoders synergistically optimize segmentation and cloud removal, thereby promoting mutual enhancement between the semantic prior and visual restoration. Experiments on M3M-CR and LuojiaSET-OSFCR demonstrate that CGM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Net achieves state-of-the-art performance on both tasks. Ablation studies and feature visualizations further validate the complementary roles and effectiveness of the proposed modules. The source code will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/1lee1338/CGM2Net</uri>.

title: PrivTI: Efficient End-to-End Privacy-Preserving Inference for Transformer-based Models in MLaaS
authors: Mingshun Luo, Haolei He, Wenti Yang, Meng Li, Longfei Wu, Zijian Zhang, Zhitao Guan
abstract: Machine Learning as a Service (MLaaS) offers convenient access to high-quality transformer-based model inference without local deployment, but it requires uploading sensitive data to the server, raising significant privacy concerns. Recent advances in homomorphic encryption (HE) and secure multi-party computation (MPC) have shown great potential for enabling privacy-preserving inference. However, existing schemes face challenges with transformer-based models due to massive matrix multiplications and complex non-linear functions. To address these challenges, we propose PrivTI, an efficient end-to-end privacy-preserving inference framework for transformer-based models. Specifically, we design an HE-based single-instruction multiple-data (SIMD) encoding scheme to perform matrix multiplication efficiently. To handle non-linear functions, we develop MPC-friendly protocols for core functions like GELU, Softmax, and LayerNorm, and further extend support to SiLU and RMSNorm. Moreover, PrivTI supports conversions between HE and MPC, enabling end-to-end privacy-preserving inference. Experimental results demonstrate that PrivTI achieves speedups 1.9-45.2× in matrix multiplication and 1.1-18.9× in non-linear function evaluation compared to baseline schemes IRON, BOLT, and NEXUS under the Local Area Network (LAN) setting. Additionally, PrivTI reduces communication overhead by 1.1-4898.7×. Moreover, end-to-end benchmarks show 3.3-9.8× improvements in computation speed and 1.6-6.2× reductions in communication overhead.

title: A 95.3% Efficiency APT/AET/SPT Multimode Multiband CMOS/GaN Envelope Tracking for 6G-Oriented Systems
authors: Chun-Yuan Chen, Yi-Chun Huang, Ke-Horng Chen, Ying-Hsi Lin, Shian-Ru Lin, Tsung-Yen Tsai
abstract: This article proposes an envelope-tracking (ET) supply modulator (SM) that is scalable for sixth-generation (6G) communication systems. The design leverages a cost-efficient CMOS process for the power converters and a high-performance GaN process for the high-frequency power amplifier (PA) and the depletion-mode-only GaN-based amplifier. The proposed ET supply modulator (ETSM) supports average power tracking (APT), Analog ET (AET), and symbol power tracking (SPT) operation modes, achieving 400 MHz ET bandwidth and up to 95.3% efficiency, while also enabling fast mode transitions. Parasitic-induced voltage ringing in SPT mode is effectively mitigated by activating the depletion-mode-only GaN-based class-AB amplifier as an active snubber, maintaining performance despite parasitic variations. This solution overcomes the limitations of existing techniques and provides robust performance across multimode-multiband (MMMB) operation, spanning 2G to 5G FR2 and beyond. Experimental results demonstrate high efficiency, rapid mode transitions, and substantial mitigation of parasitic voltage ringing.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Low Loss and EMI Noise Split-Gate Trench IGBT with Self-Powered Voltage Source
authors: Lian Gan, Jinping Zhang, Zhengyu Lang, Bo Zhang
abstract: This letter presents simulations on a low loss and electromagnetic interference (EMI) noise split-gate (SG) trench IGBT with self-powered voltage source (SPSG-TIGBT). Combined with the self-powered circuit composed of the integrated polysilicon diode and external capacitor, the floating P-body (FP) in the dummy region anticipated to work as an integrated voltage source to charge the SG during turn-off process. This reduces the on-state voltage drop (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub>), while maintaining the advantage of SG structure on the gate capacitance and charge. Numerical simulation results show that compared with the Con. TIGBT and Con. SG-TIGBT, the proposed device is expected to offer better trade-off between turn-off loss (E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub>) and V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub>. At the same V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ceon</sub> of 1.18 V, the E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub> of the SPSG-TIGBT is 29.90% and 55.58% lower than that of the Con. TIGBT and Con. SG-TIGBT, respectively. Moreover, its high SG potential significantly reduces the turn-on loss (E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>) and enables a better trade-off between E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub> and surge current (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">surge</sub>) of the device, thereby effectively mitigating EMI noise.

title: Covert Communication in Federated Learning
authors: Sang Wu Kim, Chenyu Xu
abstract: This letter presents a covert communication frame-work for federated learning (FL) systems, in which the central server embeds hidden messages into global model updates that naturally exhibit a moving-target property, thereby complicating adversarial detection and tracking. The proposed embedding strategy preserves the statistical characteristics of standard updates, ensuring covertness via statistical indistinguishability. We analytically characterize the decoding error probability and the achievable covert transmission rate under Rayleigh fading channels, and evaluate the resulting expressions through experiments on standard FL benchmarks. The results demonstrate that covert message embedding introduces negligible impact on learning performance, with model accuracy converging to baseline levels after sufficient training rounds.

title: WFCDCLIP: A CLIP-Based Framework for Weakly Supervised Farmland Change Detection
authors: Zhaoxiang Cao, Yuchun Huang, Longlong Ma, Yibo Zhou, Peng Zhou, Wenxuan Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Farmland change detection is of significant importance for food security, land management, and precision agriculture. However, farmland scenarios generally exhibit high texture similarity and complex background noise, which not only makes manual annotation costly and easily disturbed but also complicates the automatic identification of fine-grained changes. Although weakly supervised methods based on image-level labels can significantly reduce annotation costs, they still face three major limitations under fine-grained changes and noisy environments: semantic mismatch between images and text descriptions, instability of pseudo labels due to noise, and insufficient depth of multimodal information fusion. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a CLIP-based weakly supervised farmland change detection framework, WFCDCLIP, whose core objective is to comprehensively enhance the modeling and discrimination of fine-grained changes through a multimodal semantic alignment mechanism. Specifically, based on the CLIP architecture, this study introduces an attribute-anchored averaged text prompt (AAATPrompt), which anchors text prompt embeddings to class-level change/no-change semantic prototypes and allows only residual adaptation to alleviate semantic mismatch between appearance variations and true semantic changes. A Monte Carlo sampling-based CAM improvement method (MC-CAM) is designed to suppress background interference through multiple samplings and weighted aggregation, thereby improving the stability of pseudo labels in noisy environments. Simultaneously, a text–vision bidirectional interaction mechanism (TVS) is incorporated at the decoder stage to deeply optimize the interaction between images and text, overcoming the insufficient fusion problem of existing methods under complex backgrounds. Finally, a local dense contrastive loss ( LLEA ) is proposed to enhance the discriminability of multimodal features in local regions, enabling better capture of fine-grained changes in farmland scenarios. These modules collectively form a multi-level multimodal alignment enhancement framework, which explicitly enforces semantic consistency between textual change concepts and fine-grained visual differences, thereby improving the reliability of weakly supervised farmland change detection. Experimental results show that our method significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches on the HI-CNA and CLCD datasets, especially in terms of Intersection over Union (IoU) and F1 score.

title: From a Glance to a Boundary: Uncertainty-Aware Distillation for Glance-Supervised Video Moment Localization
authors: Yupeng Hu, Hao Liu, Kun Wang, Ruping Cao, Yinwei Wei, Liqiang Nie
abstract: Glance-supervised video moment localization, which requires only single-frame annotations within target moments, offers a promising balance between annotation efficiency and localization precision. However, existing methods suffer from a fundamental limitation: the inherent uncertainty regarding precise moment boundaries when given only glance supervision. Current approaches either assume deterministic relationships between glance annotations and boundaries or employ predefined soft-label distributions, both failing to capture the complexity of real-world boundary uncertainty. We introduce UDist, an uncertainty-aware distillation network that explicitly models boundary prediction uncertainty as learnable probability distributions. Our framework consists of two key components: 1) an Uncertainty-aware Offset Distribution (UOD) mechanism, transforming implicit boundary uncertainty into explicit probability distributions over discretized offset bins; and 2) a dual-branch distillation framework that transfers uncertainty knowledge from a teacher to a student branch through a competitive distillation process. This “certainty to uncertainty to refined certainty” paradigm converts glance annotation certainty into uncertainty representations, then distills them into precise boundary predictions. Experiments on Charades-STA, ActivityNet Captions and TACoS show UDist improves localization accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: Charge Redistribution Loss Suppression and Phase Current Balancing Control for 48-V Two-Stage Multiphase Switched-Capacitor Converter
authors: Rui Xu, Linjun Han, Yenan Chen, Dehong Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This work focuses on minimizing charge redistribution loss and maintaining phase current balance for a two-stage 48-V hybrid switched-capacitor multiphase converter. The converter integrates a 3-to-1 Dickson converter with two flying capacitors as the intermediate bus converter, and two parallel four-phase series-capacitor buck converters for regulation and high output current. To minimize charge redistribution loss, the Dickson stage operates at an optimal duty cycle. Furthermore, the inter-stage decoupling capacitor is designed to be smaller than the flying capacitors to further reduce loss. However, this leads to increased intermediate bus voltage ripple, which induces an unequal volt-second distribution across the output phase inductors. A current balancing control strategy is proposed, eliminating the need for active current sensing. The operational principles of both the loss mitigation method and the current balancing control are presented. Experimental validation using a 48-V to 1-V/460-A two-stage, 8-phase prototype confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Robust and Secure Active IRS-aided Covert Multiuser MIMO Communications with a Multi-antenna Energy-harvesting Eavesdropper
authors: Wenhao Wang, Deli Qiao, Haifeng Qian
abstract: This paper investigates robust and secure active intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-aided covert multi-user multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) communications in the existence of a multi-antenna energy-harvesting eavesdropper. In particular, an active IRS is deployed to establish a favorable wireless communication environment for improving both security and covertness of the system. We aim to maximize the total system secrecy rate by jointly optimizing the transmit beamforming and artificial noise matrices at the base station (BS) and the reflection-coefficients of the active IRS. Furthermore, we formulate the design as a non-convex optimization problem, explicitly taking into account energy harvesting capabilities of the eavesdropper, information security requirements of legitimate users, and covertness constraint against adversarial detection. To address the inherent non-convexity of the optimization design problem, we propose an effective iterative suboptimal algorithm. In particular, we first transform the original optimization problem into a tractable form by employing the weighted minimum mean-square error (WMMSE), general sign-definiteness, and S-Procedure methods. Subsequently, we exploit the block coordinate descent (BCD) approach to decompose the coupled optimization matrices. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme can substantially outperform various baseline schemes adopting existing solutions. Besides, our results highlight the superiority of integrating active IRS into MIMO communication systems for secure and covert transmission.

title: A Lightweight Hybrid Deep Learning Model with Squeeze-And-Excitation for Big Energy Data Analytics in Smart Homes
authors: Murad Khan, Mohammed Faisal, Basil Alothman
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: The exponential growth of smart home environments has led to an unprecedented influx of highresolution energy consumption data, presenting both opportunities and challenges for efficient data analytics. Traditional deep learning models used for energy disaggregation and appliance state detection often struggle with high memory overhead, slow inference, and poor generalization under constrained computing environments. Moreover, many existing approaches overlook the structural complexity of time-series energy data and fail to adaptively focus on salient temporal features, especially in imbalanced or sparse appliance usage scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs) with a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) mechanism. The CNN layers efficiently capture local temporal patterns, while GRUs model sequential dependencies. The SE blocks further enhance discriminative power by adaptively recalibrating channel-wise feature responses. The proposed architecture enables precise ON/OFF state detection and mains power prediction using only a fraction of the parameters and memory typically required by conventional models. We validate our approach on the UK-DALE dataset, a widely used benchmark for energy disaggregation. Experimental results show that our model achieves F1-scores above 0.98 for key appliances and near-perfect alignment in regression for aggregate mains consumption. Similarly, compared to state-of-the-art methods, our model significantly reduces memory usage without compromising accuracy, making it ideal for real-time deployment on edge devices.

title: Detection of Human Trafficking Risks Using Machine Learning
authors: Manas Pati Tripathi, S. Pavithra, S. Shri Meenaakshi, Hossein Shirazi, Kurunandan Jain, Indrakshi Ray, Krishnashree Achuthan
abstract: Human trafficking remains a pervasive global issue, affecting millions of individuals through various forms of exploitation and coercion. The increasing use of digital platforms by traffickers to recruit and exploit victims necessitates the development of innovative and effective solutions. This research proposes a novel approach utilizing machine learning (ML) to identify suspicious activities indicative of human trafficking on social media platforms and within online job advertisements. This research proposes a novel approach to identify suspicious job advertisements that may indicate human trafficking. By analyzing keywords and their semantic relationships, we develop a system to flag high-risk postings. A multi-lingual dataset, comprising texts in English, Hindi, and Bengali, was created and meticulously labeled to determine the presence of suspicious content. This dataset was subsequently used to train a machine learning model capable of detecting potential trafficking activities in real time. The ultimate objective is to integrate this model into a web platform or browser extension, facilitating continuous monitoring and providing alerts to users. Moreover, the study introduces a robust model designed to detect patterns in job advertisements that are indicative of potential trafficking risks. By leveraging advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques, specifically BERT embeddings, in combination with red flag detection mechanisms and sentiment analysis, this research presents a comprehensive framework for identifying and mitigating suspicious job postings. The findings underscore the critical role of leveraging advanced machine learning techniques to safeguard vulnerable populations, offering a scalable and automated solution to combat the evolving threat of human trafficking in the digital age.

title: Enhancing Wireless Guitar Audio Signal Transmission Using a Flexible Metamaterial-Based Frequency Selective Surface
authors: Omar Shendy, Abeer Elkhouly, Ayesha Wasim Qureshi, Mohd Fareq Abd Malek, Hasliza A Rahim
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and practical application of a frequency selective surface (FSS) fabricated using metamaterial fabric (ShieldIt Super) and felt as a flexible substrate. The FSS was designed and simulated in CST Studio Suite to function as a bandpass filter at 2.4 GHz, aligning with the frequency of a wireless audio transmission system. The fabricated FSS was tested in a realistic environment using an electric guitar, a wireless transmitter, and an Arturia MiniFuse 2 audio interface. Audio signals were recorded and analyzed using Audacity. When placed over the receiver, the FSS yielded a significant increase in received signal strength, demonstrating a gain of over 4 dB at both 0.4 m and 4.5 m distances, which were selected to simulate real-world usage conditions. These findings indicate that the flexible FSS structure effectively enhances transmission quality and directionality in non-controlled environments. Further research is recommended to assess its robustness under varying mechanical deformations, such as bending or folding.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Energy-Efficient Atmospheric Water Generators for Standalone Applications
authors: Ahmed Badawi, M I. Elzein, Claude Ziad El-Bayeh, Azad Ashraf, Osama Khalid, Nasser Koleed, Mohammed Al-Mass
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Water scarcity is a critical issue, especially in remote hot areas where many regions are affected by this problem. Over a period of time, the atmosphere is filled with a staggering 3400 trillion gallons of water vapor, which is equivalent to covering the entire planet with a layer of water that is 1 inch thick. In this paper, we introduce a pioneering solution to address water scarceness in areas with moderate to high humidity - Atmospheric Water Generators (AWG). This approach transforms water vapor into liquid water and are specifically created for agrarian and irrigation needs in areas with limited water resources. Two innovative concepts in research paper are introduced, one utilizing Peltier devices and the other a heat exchanger, to investigate various design possibilities. The Peltier-based concept functions by passing current through it, resulting in a temperature difference that cools and condenses the air in its vicinity. The heat exchanger operates by circulating a coolant that is cooled by the lower ground temperature. Both sustainable engineering concepts were created with the goal of minimizing energy usage and expenses, particularly when compared to the existing AWGs available in the market. This research demonstrates that our Peltier-based prototype achieves a rate of 2.5 liters per kWh at tested parameters of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \% \text{RH}$</tex> and 85° F, while energy consumption with the heat exchanger is reduced to 0.4 kWh per cubic meter of water. The annual simulations for the climate of Qatar show locally averaged harvesting rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.36 ~\mathrm{L} / \text{hr}$</tex> with a production cost of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{ 0. 1 8}$</tex> per liter. Our designs offer a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> reduction in upfront costs compared to commercial systems like Gr8water (3,500 gallons/day at around $16,000) and focus on scalable off-grid agricultural use.

title: On Addressing Bias and Fairness in Large Language Models for Responsible Innovation in Gender Contexts
authors: Varsha Shukla, Geoffrey G Parker
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become transformative tools in automating decision-making processes across critical domains such as healthcare, criminal justice, education, and social media. By enabling unprecedented advances in efficiency, insight generation, and user interaction, LLMs hold the potential to reshape societal norms and systems. However, their widespread deployment also raises significant ethical concerns, particularly regarding the amplification of gender biases embedded within their training data. Such biases not only perpetuate historical inequities but also exacerbate them, resulting in unfair treatment and representation of genderdiverse groups in high-stakes applications. This paper examines the ethical implications of gender bias in LLMs and its ripple effects across societal structures. By dissecting the sources and manifestations of these biases-including their roots in skewed training datasets, historical inequities, and feedback loops-we highlight their real-world impacts in sensitive contexts like healthcare diagnostics, predictive policing, and AIcompanionship platforms. The study proposes novel approaches to mitigate these biases, including fairness metrics such as Demographic Parity, Equal Opportunity, and the Bias Impact Score, which provide robust tools for detecting and addressing disparities. Additionally, we introduce real-time bias monitoring techniques and emotional impact metrics to ensure ethical responsiveness, particularly in applications where user well-being is at risk. Our findings demonstrate the effectiveness of these methods, including 22 % reduction in gender bias in healthcare diagnostics, 30 % improvement in equitable outcomes in predictive policing, and enhanced psychological safety in AI companionship platforms. This research underscores the critical need for interdisciplinary approaches that prioritize fairness, transparency, and accountability in LLM design and deployment. By integrating ethical considerations into every stage of AI development, from dataset curation to real-time bias mitigation, we can foster responsible AI innovation that empowers marginalized communities, challenges societal stereotypes, and promotes equitable outcomes. Through this work, we aim to pave the way for a transformative shift in AI development - one where technology serves as a force for equity and inclusivity, aligning advancements in artificial intelligence with the values of fairness, respect, and diversity.

title: Improving Patent Document Retrieval with Customized Large Language Model
authors: Junhong Wang, Masayuki Okabe
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Workshop on Computational Intelligence and Applications (IWCIA)
abstract: This study aims to enhance patent document retrieval by employing a customized large language model, Patent BERT. We introduce a retrieval framework that leverages Patent BERT for text tokenization and vector generation, followed by a ranking mechanism to compute the similarity between topics and patent documents. Experimental evaluations on the CLEF-IP 2013 dataset demonstrate the superiority of our approach, particularly when comparing the performance of two vector types generated by Patent BERT against the conventional BERT model.

title: Self-Detection of Diabetic Foot Ulcer Using Thermography and Deep Learning
authors: Hajer Sulaiman Nasser Al Wahaibi, Taqwa Ali Mohammed Alismaili, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Rawad Abdulghafor
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Diabetic foot ulcers (DFUs) are a severe complication of diabetes, necessitating early detection to mitigate morbidity and mortality. Current diagnostic methods are often manual, time-consuming, and lack real-time automated solutions. This study proposes a deep learning framework leveraging thermal imaging for real-time DFU detection. Utilizing a dataset of 1,800 high-resolution thermal images (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,440 \times 1,080$</tex> pixels) categorized into “Healthy” and “Ulcer” classes, the framework integrates preprocessing techniques—noise reduction, contrast adjustment, and resizing—to optimize input quality. The model's performance is benchmarked against Inception V3 and MobileNet V2, evaluating accuracy, precision, recall, and computational efficiency. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves 100% validation accuracy and near-zero loss (0.0006%) by epoch 10, significantly outperforming Inception V3 (86.3% accuracy) and MobileNet V2 (85.55% accuracy). The confusion matrices reveal perfect classification for the proposed model, with no misclassifications, underscoring its clinical reliability.

title: A Cryptographically-Linked Metadata Schema for Academic Credential Interoperability: The ILMEQ Model
authors: Abdulkadir Tasdelen, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Md Rafiqul Islam
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper presents ILMEQ (Interlinked Ledger Metadata for Educational Qualifications), a novel metadata schema that enables the representation of academic credentials as cryptographically verifiable digital assets. Aimed at addressing the shortcomings of current credentialing systems—such as lack of interoperability, vulnerability to fraud, and inadequate modeling of document relationships—ILMEQ introduces a JSON-LD-based structure that supports rich semantic linking and integrity validation. Unlike many existing blockchain-based approaches that rely on fully on-chain storage, ILMEQ maintains privacy and scalability by operating off-chain, anchoring only cryptographic hashes of credential metadata. The schema is designed to capture the full lifecycle and interdependencies of academic documents, including prerequisite chains, equivalency certifications, validation references, and revocation effects. It enables status-aware, hashverified validation of credentials without exposing sensitive content, ensuring both trust and confidentiality. ILMEQ was evaluated in three practical educational scenarios, ranging from simple undergraduate records to complex cross-border recognition cases. Results demonstrated successful modeling of linear and non-linear academic paths, effective revocation tracking, and logical propagation of status changes across related documents. The implementation includes a client-side tool for managing and exporting credential graphs, emphasizing modularity, offline capability, and user privacy. By providing a structured, machine-readable, and extensible model for academic credentials, ILMEQ significantly enhances the trustworthiness, portability, and semantic clarity of digital educational records, contributing toward a globally interoperable credentialing infrastructure.

title: 5G-NR Resource Management in Multi-Orbit Non-Terrestrial Systems with Multi-Connectivity
authors: Mikko Majamaa, Eva Lagunas, Abuzar B. M. Adam, Flor Ortiz, Lauri Sormunen, Timo Hämäläinen, Symeon Chatzinotas, Tommi Mikkonen
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Non-terrestrial networks (NTN) have been identified as a key component of the upcoming 5G-Advanced technology, bringing connectivity anywhere on Earth and supporting terrestrial infrastructure whenever needed. Non-geostationary orbit systems such as low-Earth orbit constellations are in the spotlight of this NTN emerging popularity, gathering significant investments in large-scale endeavors. As we move towards systems composed with multiple and dynamical orbital shells, the radio resource block assignment problem becomes challenging. In this work, we formulate the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> compatible resource management problem as a max-min fairness problem applied to the user demand satisfaction metric, also taking into account multi-connectivity where a user can be connected to more than one satellite at a time. Two solution alternatives are provided, namely an optimization-based procedure and a heuristic method. Numerical simulations show that the optimization-based algorithm can fully satisfy 74 % of the users while the heuristic algorithm can satisfy 56% of the users in the considered scenario. The key novelty of this work lies in addressing fairness simultaneously for multiple applications and multiple constellations, distinguishing it from prior approaches that primarily focus on single-application or single-constellation scenarios.

title: Reliability Analysis of IGZO Thin Film Transistor for Wearable Biosensing Applications
authors: Anchal Thakur, Samriti Sharma, Jaya Madan, Rahul Pandey
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: This article reported the impact of mechanical bending on Indium gallium zinc oxide (IGZO) thin film transistor (TFT) with single gate electrode for wearable electrics devices for its reliability analysis. The deformation caused by mechanical bending caused a strain in the active layer of IGZO in TFTs. This strain is compressive and tensile in the parallel and perpendicular direction of current conduction. Mechanical bending is induced by a change in the radius of curvature (R= 1 μm to 10 μm). For smaller radii (0 > R > 2 μm), the strain is higher for IGZO TFTs. The radius of curvature also influences the electrical performance matrices. In this paper, the charge carrier mobility, electron concentration, drain current, and threshold voltage shift are also evaluated with variations in the radius of curvature. Furthermore, the compressive strain degrades mobility, and tensile strain enhances mobility depending on the radii of curvature. Thus, the radii less than 3 μm, the degraded reliability for IGZO TFTs has been observed in terms of threshold voltage shift and I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</sub> ratio (ΔV<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sub>=-116.7% and I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</sub> =-132.9%), respectively.

title: Performance Analysis of Space Satellite Communication System Based on Single-Photon Detectors
authors: Camellia S. Mouhammad, Ziyuan Shi, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
abstract: Free-space optical (FSO) satellite communication systems face distinct challenges depending on link topology, as horizontal or inter-satellite links (ISLs) operate above the atmosphere, uplink (UL) channels experience atmospheric turbulence near the transmitter, and downlink (DL) channels suffer primarily from geometric losses. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication links employing single-photon detectors (SPDs) to address photon-starved conditions under various FSO channel impairments. We develop comprehensive channel models for DL, ISL, and UL that capture the combined effects of atmospheric turbulence, attenuation, pointing errors, angle-of-arrival (AoA) blockage, and geometric spreading. To further enhance performance in high-data-rate scenarios, we investigate multi-pixel SPD arrays and derive analytical expressions for error-rate performance that include detector dead time and dark counts. Our theoretical models are validated through extensive Monte Carlo simulations, showing strong agreement with predicted results. The findings highlight the benefits of spatial diversity in multi-pixel SPDs, demonstrating notable gains over single-pixel configurations. Finally, we provide key insights into optimizing link parameters: receiver aperture, link distance, detection efficiency, modulation order, and beam divergence for robust, high-sensitivity photoncounting FSO satellite communication.

title: Enhancing Spectral Efficiency and Resilience in SAGIN-ITS: An IRS-Assisted Semantic Offloading Framework
authors: Ali Mehrban, Hajar Moudoud, Bouziane Brik, Zakaria Abou El Houda
abstract: In dense Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) scenarios within Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks (SAGIN), connected autonomous cars (CAVs) generate a large volume of sensor data, which leads to significant bandwidth congestion. There is a risk to safety because of the poor Packet Success Rate (PSR) and dangerous transmission delays caused by this congestion. To address this issue, we introduce SemantIRS, a framework that integrates semantic offloading with Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS)-assisted links to achieve high network resilience, when compute resources permit and lower latency. SemantIRS deploys lightweight Large Language Models (LLMs) as encoders to compress massive sensor data at the edge of the SAGIN network before transmission through IRS-assisted links. Intent-Based Networking (IBN) and Federated Learning (FL) are also integrated to automate resilience policies and preserve distributed offloading privacy throughout SAGIN layers, respectively. We conduct our experiments using a 12-state hardware benchmark of three LLM encoders (Phi-2, Llama-2-7B-Chat, and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2) on four compute tiers (TPU v6e-1, A100, L4, and Intel Xeon CPU). The measured inference latencies are then fed into an IEEE 802.11p airtime simulator. Our obtained results show that, in congested network conditions, SemantIRS improves the packet success rate from 1.7% to 66.4% while reducing transmission energy by 90%.

title: The Log-∝μ Fading Model
authors: Godfred K. Tenkorang, Michel D. Yacoub, André A. Dos Anjos, Elvio J. Leonardo, Fábio C. Martins
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The paper introduces the Log-∝μ fading model, a nonlinear envelope framework that generalizes the classical ∝–μ distribution with a logarithmic transform to capture composite propagation effects with controlled complexity. Two distinct formulations (Implicit and Explicit), leading to the same particular cases, yield closed-form first-order statistics - probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF) - and second-order statistics - level crossing rate (LCR) and average fading duration (AFD) - while subsuming a broad family of canonical and new channels as special cases. Using line-of-sight (LoS) and non-line-of-sight (nLoS) measurements at 1.8 GHz, parameter estimation via nonlinear least squares reveals that Log-∝μ achieves superior fits to empirical envelopes compared to established models, accurately reproducing deep-fade behavior in the LCR while preserving analytical tractability. Moreover, the special case Log-μ—a three-parameter fading model in its normalized form—and its six reduced-complexity variants, identified as Type I, II, III, IV, V, VI, further validate the framework: the two-parameter variant model, with complexity comparable to the ∝–μ model, achieves similarly strong fitting performance, and the one-parameter variant also delivers competitive results when applied to the measured data. Interestingly, one of these variants is the Lomax distribution, which is related to the Pareto, Beta Prime, and other distributions, for which higher-order statistics are presented for the first time. Overall, the proposed framework strikes a balance between accuracy and analytical complexity, providing a unified tool for channel characterization and performance analysis.

title: An Electrolytic-Capacitorless Integrated LDC/V2L Converter for Hybrid Electric Vehicles
authors: Gyeong-Hyun Kwon, Dong-In Lee, Hong-Seong Kim, Jun-Sik Hwang, Yeonho Jeong, Han-Shin Youn
abstract: This paper presents an integrated power converter architecture targeting vehicle-to-load (V2L) functionality in hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs). In the proposed configuration, the primary-side switches of the low-voltage dc/dc converter (LDC) are integrated with the power switches and magnetic components of the boost converter used in the V2L stage. This structural integration also enables the elimination of the dc-link capacitor, typically required at the input of the inverter stage, resulting in significant reductions in component count and circuit complexity. The reliable operation of the proposed converter is experimentally validated under three operating modes, including driving, stationary V2L, and driving with V2L. The prototype hardware is designed for a total power of 3 kW, with 2 kW allocated to the LDC and 1 kW to the V2L function. The converter achieves a peak efficiency of 94.46% in the driving mode, and a peak efficiency of 96.87% in both stationary V2L and driving with V2L modes. Moreover, the electrolytic-capacitorless implementation and reduced dedicated V2L boost elements enable a compact, cost-effective, and less complex implementation.

title: Forward-Secure Identity-Based Signcryption with Equality Test for Social Recommendation on Smart IoT Devices
authors: Wenjie Yang, Yu Cai, Qunhao Ou, Futai Zhang, Anjia Yang
abstract: With the deep integration of smart IoT devices with social networks, platforms use user-interest similarity for social recommendations to connect like-minded people. Identity-based encryption with equality test (IBEET) enables equivalence testing of interest ciphertexts encrypted under different public keys and avoids costly certificate management. It is beneficial for privacy-preserving social recommendations on smart IoT devices. Nevertheless, existing IBEET schemes suffer from two critical flaws: the lack of forward security allows historical trapdoor holders to test newly generated ciphertexts, and the absence of ciphertext origin authentication impedes the legality verification of the ciphertext source. In this paper, taking into account IoT-device features like limited resources, we introduce a forward secure identity-based signcryption scheme with equality test enabling public verification (FS-IBSCET-PV) for social recommendation. It enables the platforms to leverage users’ trapdoors to perform equality tests on interest ciphertexts, accurately matching users who share common interests. Meanwhile, users can submit time-limited authorized trapdoors when needing recommendation services, thus effectively reducing the risk of trapdoor misuse. Furthermore, upon receiving recommendation results, users can publicly verify their origin without extra decryption to ensure the authenticity of the recommendations, consequently mitigating the hardware overhead. Security analysis and simulation experiments show that FS-IBSCET-PV has enhanced functionality and better testing efficiency than related schemes. It provides a secure and practical solution for privacy-preserving social recommendations on resource-limited smart IoT devices.

title: QUBO Simplification by Singular Value Decomposition and Coefficient Elimination for Ising Machines
authors: Shinnosuke Inaba, Takeru Ota, Nozomu Togawa
abstract: Ising machines, including quantum annealing machines, can efficiently solve combinatorial optimization problems by formulating them as Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO). However, it is known that the solving performance of Ising machines deteriorates as the energy landscape represented by the QUBObecomes more complex. Simplifying the coefficients of QUBOmatrix can improve the performance of solving combinatorial optimization problems using an Ising machine. In this paper, we propose QUBO simplification methods for Ising machines using singular value decomposition (SVD) and coefficient elimination based on their absolute value (ABS). The proposed SVD method simplifies QUBO by capturing the global structure of the QUBO matrix. QUBO is further simplified by combining it with the simple ABS method. The simplified QUBO is expected to have a less complex energy landscape, while maintaining the global structure of the original, enabling the Ising machine to efficiently find the (semi-)ground state.We demonstrate the numerical experiments using an Ising machine to evaluate the effectiveness of the SVD method alone, the ABS method alone, and the two methods combined.

title: Systematic Evidence of Progressive Workflow Discontinuities in CubeSat Microstrip Antenna Design: From Electromagnetic Optimization to System Integration
authors: Segundo Francisco Segura Altamirano, Carlos Leonardo Oblitas Vera, Víctor Paúl Palacios Paredes, Lucia Isabel Chaman Cabrera, Leo Danny Camizan Cabrera, Carlos Enrique Santos Tavara, Diana M. Castro Cárdenas
abstract: This systematic review of 79 CubeSat antenna studies reveals a progressive "workflow attrition cascade" (68%→70%→87%→92%) wherein electromagnetic design sophistication systematically decouples from deployment-ready system integration. While CubeSats demand reliable communication under severe constraints, our PRISMA 2020-compliant synthesis demonstrates that current research workflows rarely translate theoretical optimization into mission readiness. Specifically, 68% of studies omit critical simulation documentation, 70% rely on single-domain experimental validation inadequate for space qualification, 87% fail to achieve full thermal-mechanical integration of feeding elements, and 92% lack environmental qualification during fabrication. Rather than reflecting technical inability, this attrition highlights systemic gaps between component-level design and system-level engineering, exacerbated by academic incentives and resource constraints. Addressing these discontinuities requires a paradigm shift from isolated electromagnetic optimization toward concurrent multiphysics integration, establishing validated workflows essential for transitioning CubeSat payloads from experimental demonstrations to operational multi-year constellations.

title: Interpretable Deep Learning in Dermoscopy: An XAI-Driven Ensemble for Skin Lesion Diagnosis
authors: Bhagyashri S. Sonune, R Udaykumar, Shon G. Nemane, Dhiraj P. Tulaskar, Manish Bhaiyya, Hossam Haick
abstract: The problem of accurate and interpretable automated classification of dermoscopic skin lesions is difficult because publicly available benchmark datasets are imbalanced, diagnostically heterogeneous, and prone to image-level confounding effects. While individual techniques such as pre-trained convolutional neural networks (CNNs), ensemble learning, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) are established, their combined effectiveness is reported without controlled comparison to determine whether ensemble consensus improves robustness and explanation quality in a unified framework. In this paper, we report a controlled comparison of six pre-trained CNN architectures, namely ResNet50, Xception, EfficientNetB3, MobileNetV2, DenseNet201, and InceptionV3, on the publicly available HAM10000 dataset, as well as their soft voting ensemble. The contribution of this paper is not in proposing a new deep learning architecture but rather in presenting a harmonized evaluation framework to determine whether diversity in architectures can be exploited to improve robustness on minority classes and qualitatively better explanations in terms of spatial coherence in seven-class classification of dermoscopic images of skin lesions. To this end, we used Grad-CAM, LIME, and occlusion sensitivity methods to evaluate the interpretability of each individual CNN as well as the ensemble. The soft voting ensemble outperformed all individual architectures by achieving a macro-average ROC curve of 0.985 and accuracy of 89.37%, while simultaneously providing qualitatively better spatially coherent explanations that are lesion-centric, suggesting better trustworthiness in transparent XAI-based automated screening in dermatological studies.

title: Intra-Group Individual Item Fairness-aware Recommendation
authors: Ruijia Ma, Lin Zhang, Rongbo Qi, Chunyao Song, Tingjian Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: The recommendation system, as a widely used and effective tool to alleviate information overload, has been receiving increasing attention regarding its issues of bias and fairness. Many studies have focused on addressing fairness on the item side, targeting item fairness by minimizing exposure discrepancies of items among similar individuals. However, in real-world recommendation scenarios, many relevant items requiring similar exposure to users may exhibit certain dissimilarities while existing methods could not solve the problem. To address this, we define a broader item fairness recommendation issue aimed at improving fairness within specified groups of related individual items, which we term as “intra-group item fairness”. To solve this issue, we propose a Group-oriented Individual Fairness recommendation model called GIFRec. First, we introduce a global exposure balance module to mitigate exposure imbalances at a global level, with the help of multimodal information contained in each item. Then, at the group level, we propose a group fusion embedding representation method, allowing individual items within the same group to adaptively share group information. Additionally, as unfair training opportunities may arise for different items during model training, we propose a general fair intra-group optimization method to reduce individual training biases within the same group. Extensive experiments conducted on four real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach with an average improvement of 11.27% in accuracy and 24.91% in fair ness compared to eight SOTA methods. Our implementation and some details can be found at https://github.com/xderui/GIFRec.

title: Vegetation Health Prediction Using RGB Images from UAV with Deep Learning
authors: Masud Rana, Ram Ray, Xishuang Dong, Lijun Qian
abstract: Vegetation health prediction is one of the most important research questions in precision agriculture. The Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) is one of the most widely adopted indicators of crop vigor and stress. Traditional NDVI estimation relies on multispectral or hyperspectral imaging, which is expensive and inaccessible for small-scale farming. Recent advances in deep learning enable the prediction of NDVI from low-cost RGB imagery; however, the limited availability of paired RGB-NDVI datasets and domain discrepancies between existing sources hinder robust generalization. In this work, we construct two UAV-based RGB-NDVI paired datasets and investigate multiple deep learning architectures, including an FCNN, an RSCNN, and an ADL, for NDVI prediction. Our experimental results show that RSCNN consistently delivers the strongest regression performance across both datasets, achieving R2 values of 82.43% and 89.53%, and the lowest MAE of 1.87% and 1.57% on two of the created datasets, respectively. Furthermore, we studied transfer learning and proposed a self-learning approach that relied on pseudo-labeled target samples. The results demonstrate improved performance that varied with the chosen confidence threshold and the transfer direction.

title: Decentralized Real-Time Train-to-Grid Control by Smoothly Homotopic Trajectory Transition
authors: Zihan He, Can Wan, Hui Liu, Jinshuo Su, Haimin Xie
abstract: Electrified rail transportation possesses significant potential to support the real-time power balance of the power systems under high penetration of renewable energy, due to the high power capacity and fast response capability. However, direct power control is fundamentally incompatible with modern electric trains’ operation, as their running processes are governed by automatic train control systems via tracking predefined reference trajectories. This paper proposes a universal real-time train-to-grid control method that is compatible with the train operation as well as the designated power response command. To reflect the power response command in train trajectory, the sliding dynamics of the reference time is backward deduced based on the kinetic model of the train. To restore operations and ensure punctuality, the generated unconstrained power response trajectory is gradually deformed into the scheduled trajectory by smoothly homotopic transition. Then the transition trajectory is further rectified to ensure the compliance with the track speed limit. Benefiting from the compatibility of trajectory synthesis and power response design, simultaneous inertia and droop type frequency response from electric trains is realized for the first time. Comprehensive simulations based on various trains and railways validate the effectiveness of the proposed method and demonstrate the considerable real-time grid supporting capability of electric trains.

title: Rep-MMB: Bridging Mobile CNN and Transformer for Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition
authors: Jinsheng Liu, Lei Zhang, Xin Liu, Guangjie Chen, Zenan Fu, Wenbo Huang, Hao Wu, Aiguo Song
abstract: Lightweight CNNs and Transformers have shown great promise in sensor-based human activity recognition (HAR), yet their structural synergies remain underexplored. This paper bridges this gap by integrating the MetaFormer paradigm—a general architecture abstracted from Transformers that structurally separates token mixing (i.e., self-attention) and channel mixing (i.e., feed-forward networks)—into efficient CNN design. While MetaFormer offers a powerful inductive bias, its standard self-attention mechanism is often computationally intensive for resource-constrained HAR. To address this, we revolutionize the classic MobileNetV3 architecture from a MetaFormer perspective, introducing Rep-MMB, a new family of pure lightweight CNNs. By leveraging structural reparameterization, Rep-MMB decouples multi-branch training-time complexity from efficient single-branch inference, enabling high accuracy with low latency. Evaluations on four public HAR benchmarks show that Rep-MMB outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight models in accuracy and efficiency, with practical validation on embedded devices. We hope that Rep-MMB may serve as a strong baseline to inspire future edge-deployed HAR research.

title: Supraharmonic Measurement Based on Windowed Compressive Sensing and Orthogonal Matching Pursuit
authors: Chengbin Liang, Yuanda Liu, Guoping Zhang, Tenghua Yin, Zhaosheng Teng, Jianmin Li, Shiyan Hu
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy sources and widespread application of high-frequency power electronic devices, the issue of supraharmonics in power systems has become increasingly prominent, which imposes a significant challenge to power quality. To mitigate the impact of spectral leakage and the picket-fence effect on the accuracy of supraharmonic disturbance parameter measurements, and reduce the sampling pressure of supraharmonic signal, this article proposes a supraharmonic measurement method based on windowed compressed sensing (CS) and the orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) algorithm. The four-term third-order Nuttall window function is integrated into CS technology, where a windowed sparse measurement matrix is constructed to enable windowed compressed sampling of supraharmonic signals. Subsequently, the OMP algorithm is used for frequency estimation of the supraharmonic signals, and the three-spectral-line interpolation technique is applied to reduce the picket-fence effect, improving the accuracy of supraharmonic disturbance frequency, amplitude, and phase measurements. Simulation experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly addresses the spectral leakage and the picket-fence effect, effectively reduces the data required for supraharmonic detection, and improves the measurement accuracy while improving noise resistance. In practical experiments, the absolute errors in frequency and amplitude measurement for three sets of supraharmonic components are 0.55, 0.36, and 1.09 Hz and 0.0006, 0.0008, and 0.001 V, respectively, validating the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method.

title: DPSCS-Net: Dynamic Path Selection and Compressed Sensing Unfolding Network for ISAR Imaging
authors: Shuaihua Tian, Jianwen Guo, Hongyin Shi, Ting Yang, Tianchi Zhao, Hui Liu, Fang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Traditional Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) imaging algorithms frequently struggle with low resolution and poor noise suppression when operating under sparse aperture (SA) conditions, primarily due to the inherent non-uniformity and incompleteness of the collected data. In recent years, unfolding networks have emerged as a promising solution, establishing a systematic connection between conventional iterative algorithms and deep learning to achieve fast and efficient ISAR imaging. However, a common limitation of these unfolding networks is their reliance on a fixed number of layers for parameter optimization, which inherently limits their adaptability across diverse scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose an ISAR imaging unfolding network, termed DPSCS-Net, which is based on dynamic path selection and compressed sensing (CS). Specifically, DPSCS-Net unfolds a CS optimization algorithm into a deep network while simultaneously integrating phase error estimation within the CS framework. This integration significantly enhances cross-range resolution. To overcome the fixed-layer constraint of traditional unfolding networks, DPSCS-Net innovatively incorporates a dynamic path selector. This selector generates a one-hot vector based on the input data, where elements (0 or 1) dictate the selection of a fast and appropriate processing path. This dynamic execution mechanism effectively mitigates the unnecessary computational burden associated with conventional deep unfolding networks (DUNs), where all sampled data must traverse every processing stage. Both simulated and measured experimental results demonstrate that our DPSCS-Net exhibits notable flexibility and delivers superior performance with dynamic adaptability. This allows for an optimal balance that meets the primary demands of practical ISAR imaging applications. The network consistently shows strong robustness across various scenarios, coupled with superior performance and focusing accuracy.

title: Graph Percolation as Decision Threshold for Risk Management in Cross-Country Thermal Soaring
authors: John J. Bird
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Long range flight by fixed-wing aircraft without propulsion systems can be accomplished by “soaring” – exploiting randomly located updrafts to gain altitude which is expended in gliding flight. As the location of updrafts is uncertain and cannot be determined except through in situ observation, aircraft exploiting this energy source are at risk of failing to find a subsequent updraft. Determining when an updraft must be exploited to continue flight is essential to managing risk and optimizing speed. Graph percolation offers a theoretical explanation for this risk, and a framework for evaluating it using information available to the operator of a soaring aircraft in flight. The utility of graph percolation as a risk measure is examined by analyzing flight logs from human soaring pilots. This analysis indicates that in sport soaring pilots rarely operate in a condition which does not satisfy graph percolation, identifies an apparent desired minimum node degree, and shows that pilots accept reduced climb rates in order to maintain percolation.

title: HSCKBase: A Robust Hierarchical Federated Learning Framework for Semantic Communication Knowledge Base in Regionalized Service Scenarios
authors: Liang Li, Nan Ma, Yuhang Zhu, Jinyi Zhao, Xiaoxuan Qi, Ping Zhang
abstract: Multi-user, real-time semantic communication is the key to applying next-generation mobile communication technologies to realize complex, multimedia interactive applications in regionalized service scenarios. However, two factors challenge existing model-structure-based semantic knowledge base (SKB) updating and aggregation approaches, including the stochastic nature of mobile devices' service participation and the gradient-based update of multimedia multidimensional attributes. Therefore, this paper proposes a hierarchical federated learning (HFL)- based service framework for SKB updating and aggregation. Specifically, we first propose a synchronized waiting mechanism and a dynamic batch-updating aggregation approach for HFL SKB updating to overcome the efficiency and accuracy challenges caused by uncertainty in the distribution of computational resources. Secondly, the HSCKBase framework proposes a forced-wait mechanism for sub-network gradient updates to address the unreliability of gradient computation during sub-network aggregation. Finally, the HSCKBase framework proposes a weighted-average algorithm to mitigate the impact of uneven sub-network distribution, caused by the randomness of mobile device service participation, on the global SKB convergence efficiency and accuracy. Experiments demonstrate that HSCKBase achieves higher global updating and aggregation accuracy, lower communication latency, and greater robustness than existing HFL-based SKB updating and aggregation approaches, while ensuring the availability of the source semantic coding.

title: How Secure is a High-Performance RISC-V Core? A Spectre V1 Case Study on XiangShan Open-Source CPU
authors: Rey Paraula, Roberto Petenzi, Alessandro Bertani, Davide Conficconi
publish: Proceedings of the 19th European Workshop on Systems Security
abstract: None

title: 2025 II International Congress of Engineering with a Social Sense (CIISS)
authors: N/A

title: Byzantine-Robust Asynchronous Federated Learning via Feature Fingerprinting
authors: Meng Shen, Bohan Peng, Yi Zhao, Yue Qin, Ming Li, Qi Li, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Asynchronous federated learning (AFL) accelerates collaborative training across heterogeneous devices compared to synchronous federated learning, but increases vulnerability to Byzantine attacks due to its asynchronous aggregation. Existing defenses rely on parametric similarity between models and temporal consistency of updates, which are compromised by data and device heterogeneity, leading to ineffective robustness. To address this limitation, we propose Belisa, a Byzantine-robust AFL framework that enhances fidelity, robustness, and efficiency under heterogeneous scenarios. Belisa introduces novel discrepancies between feature representations of local models to distinguish malicious models from benign ones. By leveraging a reference model trained on publicly available data, Belisa quantifies these discrepancies, referred to as feature fingerprints, and filters out malicious models through clustering. Extensive experiments on six datasets from three types of tasks under five advanced Byzantine attacks demonstrate Belisa’s superiority. Notably, Belisa consistently outperforms existing approaches across both attack and non-attack settings. Under attack scenarios, it lowers the average test error rate to 0.42× that of baseline methods. Furthermore, Belisa accelerates the aggregation process by an average of 12.3× compared to other methods. To the best of our knowledge, Belisa is the first Byzantine-robust AFL framework, which provides a broadly applicable countermeasure in heterogeneous scenarios which are more prevalent in real-world settings.

title: QoE-ensured DDoS Mitigation in NOMA-assisted Edge Computing: A Game-theoretical Approach
authors: Aiping Wang, Lin Liu, Guangming Cui, Yuanjiaoyang Li, Ruichen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: In Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) environments, Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) technology significantly improves spectrum efficiency and system access capacity through power-domain multiplexing. However, it also exposes edge servers to more complex resource contention and security risks. In particular, during Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks, conventional mitigation mechanisms often overlook users' quality of experience (QoE), which can lead to prolonged service degradation. To address this challenge, this paper investigates a QoE-guaranteed Edge DDoS Mitigation problem (QEDM) in NOMA-assisted MEC systems by jointly considering request scheduling and power allocation. First, the QEDM problem is modeled as a finite-strategy potential game, and a distributed game-theoretic algorithm, QEDMGame, is proposed. Then, through a multi-player best-response mechanism, the system converges to a Nash equilibrium, thereby achieving joint optimization of service rate and QoE. Next, experimental results on real-world base station deployment data demonstrate that QEDMGame outperforms several representative baseline methods. Additionally, theoretical analysis shows that QEDMGame exhibits strong convergence properties and performance guarantees.

title: A Polarized Thin-Film Optical Model Based on Dual-Beam Interference for Quantitative Characterization of Marine Oil Films
authors: Qintuan Xu, Zhenduo Zhang, Ying Li, Yong Wang, Zhen Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Marine oil spills require reliable quantitative characterization to support accurate detection and thickness retrieval in remote sensing applications. However, existing optical approaches often treat thin-film interference, polarization, and extinction as weakly coupled processes, limiting physical interpretability across oil types and thickness ranges. This study develops a polarized thin-film optical model for air-oil-water interfaces by explicitly coupling dual-beam interference, polarization, and experimentally constrained wavelength-dependent extinction. Laboratory measurements demonstrate that oil-layer extinction exhibits pronounced nonlinear spectral dependence that cannot be captured by conventional simplified formulations. Incorporating this experimentally constrained extinction into the polarized interference framework reveals distinct interference- and extinction-dominated optical regimes, with enhanced sensitivity to thin oil films in the ultraviolet-blue spectral region. For thicknesses below ∼50 μm, coherent thin-film interference dominates, producing pronounced wavelength-dependent oscillations, whereas at greater thicknesses increasing extinction within the oil layer suppresses secondary reflections and drives polarization signatures toward saturation. The model further demonstrates that the degree of linear polarization (DOLP) shows opposite monotonic trends with increasing thickness at incident angles of 35° and 65°, highlighting the intrinsic geometry dependence of polarization-based retrievals. Comparisons with laboratory measurements at 488-590 nm show excellent agreement, with near-zero mean residuals (|μ| = 0.0001). These results establish a physically rigorous and experimentally validated framework for polarization-based remote sensing of marine oil films.

title: Baseline Design Method of GB-TomoArcSAR Based on Coding Optimization
authors: Weiming Tian, Kai Yan, Yunkai Deng, Xin Xie, Yuanhao Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Due to the limitation of lacking elevation resolution, ground-based interferometric radar suffers from serious layover problem when applied to building deformation monitoring. Ground-based tomographic arc-scanning synthetic aperture ra dar (GB-TomoArcSAR) enables tomography by controlling the antenna to scan multiple times in the horizontal plane of different elevation angles to form an arc-shaped synthetic aperture in the elevation direction. However, when GB-TomoArcSAR uses a baseline with uniformly distributed elevation angles, the excessive number of baseline samples leads to the low efficiency of tomog raphy. To address this issue, this paper proposes a tomographic baseline design method based on coding optimization (TBDCO). Firstly, the sampling positions in the elevation direction are bina ry coded. Then, with the elevation resolution and peak side-lobe ratio (PSLR) of the antenna pointing pattern satisfying specific constraints, a quality evaluation model of the tomographic base line is constructed by weighted summation of the sampling num ber and the Cramer Rao bound (CRB) estimation accuracy in the elevation direction. Finally, the genetic algorithm is employed to iteratively solve the optimal coding to achieve sparse design of the tomographic baseline. Validation using both simulated and measured data demonstrates that the proposed method can re duce the number of elevation samples to 42% of the original before optimization while maintaining tomographic performance, substantially shortening the time for collecting measured data.

title: An Adaptive Global-Local Collaborative Encoding-Decoding Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Wenbin Tang, Xingming Xiao, Shuli Cheng, Anyu Du
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: With the rapid development of deep learning techniques, Transformer-based methods for remote sensing change detection (RSCD) have achieved significant breakthroughs. However, these approaches often lose subtle local change information when extracting multi-level feature maps, and the late-stage feature fusion lacks sufficient interaction between bi-temporal features. To address these limitations, we propose an Adaptive Global-Local Collaborative Encoder-Decoder Network.At each layer of the encoder, an Adaptive Global-Local Attention (AGLA) module is introduced, leveraging the powerful multi-head attention mechanism to capture global contextual information. Simultaneously, adaptive channel selection with different convolutional kernels is employed to focus on local change details, enabling multi-dimensional interaction between global and local information in the encoder.Furthermore, we propose a Multi-scale Large Kernel Channel Attention (MLCA) module, which utilizes different convolutional kernels to capture diverse spatial information while compressing feature width and height, thereby effectively fusing bi-temporal feature representations. This fusion mechanism integrates changing semantic information and enhances the collaborative representation of global and local features. Finally, the change map is generated through layer-by-layer decoding.Comparative experiments conducted on three remote sensing datasets demonstrate that the AGLEDNet model outperforms other state-of-the-art methods in detection performance.

title: IEEE TechDefense 2025 in Rome, Italy
authors: Pasquale Daponte, Alfonso Farina, Giovanni Savoldelli Pedrocchi
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine
abstract: This report deals with the activities carried out during the IEEE International Workshop on Technology for Defense and Security held in Rome, Italy on November 5-7, 2025.

title: Metaverse-New Platform for Digital Marketing to Target Generation Z
authors: Elena Zlatanova-Pazheva, Tanya Gigova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Generation Z are immersed in the virtual world, consuming online content on a daily basis. As a result, the boundary between the physical and the virtual world is getting shorter in their minds. In order to reach and engage Generation Z, marketers need to be able to offer them an exciting type of experience tailored to their digital nature. Significant opportunities for this provide the concept of the metaverse, which is discussed. The meaning, trends and types of the Metaverse are discussed. The results of both qualitative and quantitative research methods among representatives of Generation Z in Plovdiv, Bulgaria are presented. The goal of the conducted research is to find out what is the attitude towards the metaverse of a young, receptive to new technologies generation like Z, which makes them a potential consumer of the metaverse.

title: CNNs, Transformers, and Beyond: AI Models for Crop Disease Detection - A Review
authors: Deepak Kumar Pathak, Lavika Goel, Aishwarya Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Crop disease detection plays a pivotal role in precision agriculture, influencing both food security and farmer productivity. This survey reviews recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) models-particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Transformer-based architectures, and hybrid approaches-applied to image-based disease identification. Through a comparative analysis of over 20 studies from 2021 to 2025, we report classification accuracies ranging from 84 % to 99.8 % across real-world datasets including PlantVillage, RARI, and various crop-specific image collections. CNNs such as ResNet50 and MobileNetV2 have shown reliable performance, achieving over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8 \%}$</tex> accuracy in controlled datasets. Meanwhile, Transformer-based models and attention mechanisms are increasingly favored for their scalability and ability to capture long-range dependencies, especially in complex or noisy field data. Hybrid models combining CNNs with nature-inspired optimization or attention layers further enhance detection accuracy and model robustness. However, practical challenges remain, including dataset imbalance, model generalization to different geographies, explainability, and edge-device deployment.
