Existing research predominantly examines isolated components–such as self-attention mechanisms and feedforward networks–without thoroughly investigating the interdependencies between these components, especially when residual connections are present. In this paper, we aim to bridge this gap by analyzing the convergence behavior of a structurally complete yet single-layer Transformer, comprising self-attention, a feedforward network, and residual connections. We demonstrate that, under appropriate initialization, gradient descent exhibits a linear convergence rate, where the convergence speed is determined by the minimum and maximum singular values of the output matrix from the attention layer. Moreover, our convergence analysis establishes a theoretical characterization of residual connections by showing that they alleviate the ill-conditioning of the attention output matrix, which arises from the low-rank structure induced by the softmax operation, thereby improving optimization stability. Empirical results corroborate our theoretical insights, illustrating the beneficial role of residual connections in promoting convergence stability.

title: Digital Gender Divide Among Teenagers: Analysing Disparities in Access to Technology and Internet Connectivity
authors: Kochukrishna Kurup VS, Gayathri. G
abstract: The Sustainable Development 2030 Agenda emphasizes the importance of gender equality. This literature review explores the digital gender divide among teenagers(DGD), specifically concentrating on disparities in access to technology and internet connectivity. By analyzing 30 articles from diverse regions, this study aims to identify and understand the factors contributing to the digital divide among teenagers and how gender-based disparities affect their access to digital platforms. Additionally, it examines global strategies that address these disparities and offers insights into narrowing the digital gender divide among teenagers. The review highlights the need for a more inclusive digital landscape to ensure equal opportunities for all genders in educational and social contexts. Furthermore, this study supports efforts toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by informing initiatives aimed at bridging the digital gender divide, particularly in the context of teenagers. The findings encourage greater scholarly involvement in addressing overlooked components of the digital divide, guiding policymakers, institutions, and decision-makers in creating more effective strategies to reduce the gap.

title: Global Trends in Digital Financial Inclusion for Women: A Decade-Long Bibliometric Review (2014-2024)
authors: Anupama S, Angan Sengupta
abstract: Digital financial inclusion has emerged as a critical driver for economic empowerment, particularly for women, who historically have been marginalized in traditional financial systems. The aim of this study is to conduct a bibliometric review of scientific publications that have explored the relationship between Digital Financial Inclusion and women as examined by previous researchers. The research was carried out using 281 documents indexed in the Scopus database. The authors utilize scientific mapping tools and performance analysis, including VOS viewer and Biblioshiny in RStudio to do the review. The results obtained are in the period from 2014-2024. The study explores the present status of digital financial inclusion on women, occurrence of keywords, most influential countries, influential authors, trending topics related to the keywords. Five clusters were found through the network visualization of keywords using Vos viewer. In addition, the Biblioshiny program in RStudio provided insights into the most relevant sources, authors, global cited documents, local cited documents, country scientific production and average citations. The results of this study highlight essential elements of this research field. However, the study recognizes its limitations, including reliance on a single database and exclusion of certain keywords. It calls for a broader methodological approach to capture a more extensive understanding of the relevant literature in this emerging field.

title: A High-Power Frequency-Doubling Current-Source Resonant Inverter System Based on a Rectangular Resonant Circuit
authors: Qixiang Yang, Fujun Ma, Shanwei Fan, Junpan Wang, Quanjie Li, Zhengxiao Zhang, Zhiheng Tong, Yukun Li
abstract: A high-power four-port current-source induction heating system for industrial applications is proposed in this study, along with an integrated power regulation strategy. This system is capable of simultaneously achieving frequency doubling, power doubling, and high-efficiency output. It employs a four-branch LLCR rectangular resonant circuit. Although the resonant circuit has a complex structure, the load observed at the output port of each inverter can be equivalently represented as a third-order CLC resonant network. The four inverters can operate simultaneously, combining their output power to achieve high-power output. Alternatively, by adopting an alternating operating mode, they can synthesize a higher-frequency current across the load, thereby realizing frequency doubling. Furthermore, an integrated power regulation strategy is proposed, Adjusted Pulse Density Modulation (APDM) for heavy-load and improved Half-Cycle Pulse Density Modulation (HC-PDM) strategy for light-load, to address the significant voltage ripple and discontinuity inherent to conventional PDM. The HC-PDM strategy effectively suppresses voltage ripple and removes discontinuity. The proposed comprehensive strategy ensures Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS) turn-off throughout the operating range, thereby reducing switching losses. Finally, an 8 kW, 20 kHz experimental prototype was developed, and the experimental results are in agreement with the theoretical analysis, confirming the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed topology and control strategy.

title: Energy Efficient Power Control in 6G Networks for Smart Grid Wide Area Network Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ainul Husna Binti Zaidi, Fauzun A. Asuhaimi, Aneesa Irdina Binti Ismail, Muhamad Arif Izzudin Bin Mohd Razali, Nur Sabrina binti Suhaimi, Huda Adibah binti Ramli
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: The rapid evolution of modern energy systems has highlighted the limitations of current communication technologies. Existing smart grid, particularly Wide Area Networks (WANs) that rely on 5G or older technologies, may often struggle to meet the increasing demands for ultra-reliable, high-speed data transmission and energy-efficient communication. This limitation can affect the performance of critical smart grid functions such as real-time monitoring, fault detection and distributed energy resource management. This research explores the integration of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with emerging 6G cellular networks to enhance power control and resource allocation decision for WANs. The study aims to develop an adaptive DRL-based framework that dynamically optimizes energy usage while maintaining network performance. Specifically, investigate how DRL techniques are employed to facilitate decision-making in power control through smart grid WANs. Performance metrics such as energy efficiency will be analyzed to validate the effectiveness of the model.

title: Half Title Page: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Workshop on Computational Intelligence and Applications (IWCIA)

title: Deep Learning-Based Recommendation System to Address Challenges in Providing Electronic Services
authors: Turki Abdullah Masood AL Hinai, Juma Al Kulaibi, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: In the era of digital transformation, universities are increasingly challenged to deliver personalized electronic services to students, particularly in areas like academic advising, GPA forecasting, and early warning systems. This study proposes an advanced academic performance prediction system using a Support Vector Machine (SVM) model, trained on a comprehensive dataset from 1,734 university students enrolled in the Spring 2024 semester. The dataset includes real and simulated GPA scores, academic classifications, warning records, and course registration details, providing a rich foundation for predictive analytics. The SVM algorithm, known for its robust classification capabilities in high-dimensional spaces, was implemented using MATLAB R2023b and evaluated on a system with an Intel Core i7 (14th Gen), 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA RTX 4070 GPU. The model classified students into three performance categories. It achieved a remarkable overall accuracy of 99.25 %, correctly predicting 1,721 out of 1,734 instances. The precision for Class 1 and Class 2 was 100 %, while Class 3 had a precision of 96.25 %, with only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> misclassifications. Furthermore, 10-fold cross-validation was employed to assess model generalizability, yielding accuracy scores ranging from 0.88 to 0.92, with Fold 4 scoring 0.89623. These results confirm the model's robustness and suitability for real-world deployment in educational institutions.

title:
Design and Optimization of E-Mode AlN FETs Featuring p-NiO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Gate Stack and Junction Termination Extension
authors: Yinhe Wu, Yu Fang, Ming Xiao, Weihang Zhang, Xiufeng Song, Longyang Yu, Wenyu Huang, Haoyin Ke, Shenglei Zhao, Yue Hao, Jincheng Zhang
abstract: This work demonstrates a novel enhancement-mode (E-mode) AlN-channel FET utilizing a recessed p-NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> gate stack and an optimized junction termination extension (JTE). The electrons beneath the gate electrode are effectively depleted by the recessed p-NiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> gate. TCAD simulations reveal that the synergistic optimization of gate-recess geometry and JTE parameters enables a record-high breakdown voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {BR}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 6210 V and a remarkable power figure of merit (PFOM) of 5.51 GW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. These results significantly surpass reported <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and GaN-channel devices. Furthermore, the high thermal conductivity of AlN ensures superior thermal management, with a minimal lattice temperature rise of only 10 K at peak power. This study provides a critical design framework for next-generation kilovolt-class ultra-wide bandgap (UWBG) power electronics.

title: Monitoring Harmonic ELF Magnetic Fields in Data Centers with Sensor-Based Instrumentation
authors: Francisco Portillo, Manuel Fernandez-Ros, Nuria Novas, Rosa Maria Garcia, Eduardo Viciana, Jose Antonio Gazquez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Modern data centers concentrate substantial amounts of power-electronic equipment, generating complex low-frequency electromagnetic environments with implications for electromagnetic compatibility (EMC). This paper presents a one-month assessment of the extremely low-frequency (ELF) magnetic field in a data center using an air-core coil magnetometer as a non-intrusive measurement instrument. The sensor operated continuously, and the recorded field was segmented into 5-minute windows to compute the RMS magnetic flux density (B<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub>), the harmonic spectrum from 50 to 2000 Hz, and the total harmonic distortion of the magnetic field (THD<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</sub>). Statistics show moderate field magnitudes, with typical B<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub> around 0.5 μT and a maximum below 1 μT, but consistently high distortion levels, with median THD<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</sub> around 36% and occasional values above 300%. Diurnal and weekly analyses reveal that THD<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</sub> is strongly modulated by the operating regime, with twice the median distortion during working hours compared with night-time and weekends, while B<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</sub> varies much less. Harmonic spectra confirm the dominant contribution of low-order odd harmonics, particularly the 3rd, whose relative amplitudes increase under high-distortion conditions. A percentile-based detector identifies 41 high-THD<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</sub> events, most of them short-lived (5–10 min). The results illustrate how ELF magnetometry, as a non-intrusive instrumentation approach, can support EMC-oriented characterization of harmonic disturbances in data centers and complement conventional power-quality measurements.

title: Non-Contact High-Resolution Stress Measurement Based on Resonance Eddy Current Technique
authors: Dong Liu, Kun Zeng, Bin Gao, Geng Yang, Diyuan Zou, Mengyao Wang, Haoran Li, Qiuping Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Non-contact measurement techniques yield lower resolution, thus limiting their application in fields requiring high-precision stress measurement, such as the measurement of stress variation in pipelines during peak shaving operations at thermal power stations. The present paper proposes a non-contact, high-resolution stress measurement method based on the series-parallel capacitor-coordinated resonance eddy current (R-ECT) technique. A theoretical analysis was conducted to examine the manner in which capacitors regulate the stress sensitivity of resonance frequency and impedance. Therefore, the stress response sensitivity of R-ECT can be amplified. In addition, the employment of a vector network analyzer was undertaken for the purpose of narrowband filtering, with the objective of attenuating noise. It is evident that these factors contribute to an enhancement in the measurement signal-to-noise ratio and stress measurement resolution. The experimental investigation conducted on the silicon steel sheet stretching machine demonstrated that the proposed model exhibited a maximum prediction error of 1.48% and 13.72% for the stress response sensitivity of resonance frequency and resonance impedance, respectively. The experiment also demonstrated that the R-ECT probe, which had been designed with high-frequency and high-sensitivity specifications, was capable of achieving a stress measurement resolution of less than ±1.72MPa (less than 3.52% F.S., resonance frequency) and less than ±2.78MPa (less than 5.69% F.S., maximum point of the magnitude-frequency curve).

title: Guest Editorial Special Issue on Plenary, Invited, and Workshop on Pulsed Power for Fusion From the 25th IEEE Pulsed Power Conference (PPC) and the 52nd IEEE International Conference on Plasma (ICOPS)
authors: Bangdou Huang, Laurent Pecastaing, Igor Timoshkin

title: Thermal diffusivity characterization of impacted composites using evaporative cryocooling excitation and inverse physics-informed neural networks
authors: Pengfei Zhu, Hai Zhang, Stefano Sfarra, Fabrizio Sarasini, Rubén Usamentiaga, Gunther Steenackers, Clemente Ibarra-Castanedo, Xavier Maldague
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The thermal diffusivity measurement of impacted composites using pulsed methods presents an ill-posed inverse problem influenced by multiple factors such as sample thickness, cooling duration, and excitation energy. In this study, a novel excitation method—evaporative cryocooling—was introduced for measuring the thermal diffusivity of tested samples. Compared to conventional excitation modalities, evaporative cryocooling excitation is compact, portable, and low cost. However, evaporative cryocooling cannot be considered a pulsed method due to its prolonged excitation duration. In general, it is difficult to measure thermal diffusivity based on non-impulsive pulsed excitation at times commensurate with the pulse duration, often due to ill-defined pulse shape and width and the subsequent potentially complicated thermal response which may be subject to diffusive broadening. To address this challenge, inverse physics-informed neural networks (IPINNs) were introduced in this work and integrated with an evaporative cryocooling method. The Parker method combined with a photothermal method was employed as a reference. To improve the accuracy of both IPINNs and Parker methods, terahertz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) was employed for measuring the thickness of impacted composites. Simulations and experimental results demonstrated the feasibility and accuracy of the IPINN-based approach.

title: Generative AI for Cross-Technology Communication in Consumer Electronics
authors: Demin Gao, Xin Li, Weizheng Wang, Zhaoyang Han, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu
abstract: Cross-Technology Communication (CTC) enables interoperability between wireless technologies such asWiFi, BLE, LoRa, and ZigBee in Consumer Electronics (CE). However, existing methods face limitations in scalability and adaptability, particularly under real-world impairments like Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) and Adjacent-Channel Interference (ACI). To address these challenges, we propose GAI-CTC, a Generative AI-driven framework for CTC that optimizes waveform synthesis, spectrum adaptation, and semantic alignment in a unified, end-to-end neural architecture. GAI-CTC utilizes a multi-domain neural network that generates waveforms for multiple protocols while complying with spectral-mask, power, and latency constraints. The framework leverages a cyclic generative learning mechanism and semantic consistency loss to ensure efficient transmitter-receiver mapping. The model is trained through joint optimization of key loss components, including LCTC, Lmask, and Lsem. Simulation results show that GAI-CTC achieves up to 3 × higher goodput and 20-40% lower energy-per-bit compared to Baseline CTC. Additionally, GAI-CTC improves spectral mask compliance by 30-50 percentage points and enhances CFO tolerance by 20 ppm, demonstrating its potential to enable efficient and adaptive communication in multi-protocol wireless systems.

title: A Dual-Stage Surrogate-Assisted Differential Evolution for Expensive Multimodal Optimization
authors: Haoyu Zhang, Minwei Huang, Yaochu Jin, Ruyu Liu, Xu Wang, Qiqi Liu, Weiguo Sheng, Ran Cheng
abstract: Expensive multimodal optimization problems present significant challenges due to the need to locate multiple global optima with high accuracy under strict evaluation budgets. To address this issue, this paper proposes a dual-stage surrogate-assisted differential evolution algorithm. In the early stage of the search, an adaptive phase-based global search strategy guided by a global surrogate model is employed. This strategy dynamically switches between exploring uncertain regions and exploiting areas near the current best seed, aiming to discover diverse global optima. In the later stage, a potential-then-exploit-based local search strategy based on multiple local surrogate models is introduced. It first perturbs each potentially promising solution via mutation and then applies surrogate-guided exploitation to enhance convergence. Experimental results on the CEC2013 multimodal benchmark suite and a real-world multiple competitive facility location and design problem demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly outperforms four state-of-the-art multimodal optimizers and five surrogate-assisted algorithms, especially under tight evaluation budgets.

title: Policy and Technological Synergies for Climate-Resilient Power Systems in Asia
authors: Ian B. Benitez
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Climate change is intensifying climate-induced disruptions across Asia's power systems, leading to greater risks to reliability, stability, and energy security. This paper presents a thematic narrative review of technological and policy pathways for developing climate-resilient power systems in developing Asia. It synthesizes current evidence on climate risks, socio-economic vulnerabilities, and adaptation needs while examining the role of artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), digital twins, and hybrid renewable-storage systems in enhancing grid resilience. The study further evaluates national and regional frameworks, including the Philippine Energy Plan (PEP 2023-2050), Pakistan's Alternative Energy Policy (AEP 2019), and cooperative initiatives under ASEAN and SAARC. Findings highlight that the alignment of digital innovation, governance reform, and green finance is critical for enabling climate-adaptive energy transitions. The review concludes that integrating digitalization, policy coherence, and regional cooperation can accelerate the shift toward sustainable, inclusive, and resilient power systems across Asia.

title: Enhancing Digital KYC Processes with Blockchain-Based Self-Sovereign Identity
authors: Kishan D. Gajjar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Traditional Know Your Customer (KYC) processes in banking are frequently characterized by high costs, inefficiency, and difficulty for consumers, owing to the dependence on centralized storage of sensitive data such as Social Security numbers. This study presents a distinctive framework for digital KYC processes based on blockchain SelfSovereign Identity (SSI) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs). This framework intends to reduce the risks associated with data breaches and identity theft by utilizing cryptographic security and decentralized verification, while also assuring regulatory compliance and improving user privacy. In the proposed model, SSNs are handled as VCs, allowing banks to verify customer IDs without directly accessing or maintaining raw SSNs. This strategy not only simplifies the KYC process but also gives consumers more control over their personal data, solving significant issues in current financial rules and digital identity management.

title: Predefined-Time Distributed Optimization Algorithm for Disturbed MASs With Equality Constraints
authors: Wu Yang, Jin-Bin Wu, Yan-Wu Wang, Zhi-Wei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This study investigates first-order multi-agent systems under external disturbances and introduces two predefined-time algorithms designed to solve convex optimization problems with global equality constraints. First, a continuous-time disturbance-rejection optimization algorithm is proposed that suppresses disturbance signals and achieves the optimal solution under bounded external disturbances. The algorithm's convergence time does not depend on both the initial states and external disturbances, and can be arbitrarily predefined. Second, to reduce communication overhead among agents, a distributed optimization algorithm utilizing a event-triggered mechanism is further developed, where agent states are updated only at certain discrete time points. This algorithm effectively suppresses disturbance signals, prevents Zeno behavior, and ensures convergence to the optimal solution within a predefined time. Using convex optimization and Lyapunov theories, the convergence of both algorithms under bounded disturbances is rigorously proven. Finally, a case study on economic dispatch in smart grids validates the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithms.

title: A Cellular Automaton-Based Pool Fire Simulation Method Integrating Deep Learning
authors: Mo Su, Yu Liu, Yuming Shen, Xiaoyun Wang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Conventional cellular automata (CA) for pool-fire spread struggle to reconcile physical fidelity with adaptive behaviour. We present a CA scheme that embeds a meta-rule correction layer. A three-channel state-fuel, temperature, diffusion-defines each cell; a deep network trained on physics data continuously refines the transition rules. Across repeated trials the upgraded CA autonomously estimates local fuel loads and combustible states and propagates the flame accordingly. Runtime stays below 2 ms per million cells per step, the rate error drops from 19.3% to 4.7%, and the visual SSIM reaches 0.923. The framework offers an efficient, intelligent route for fire-effect simulation and real-time disaster modelling.

title: Exploring User Engagement in Social Commerce through Social and Technology Factors: A Pilot Test in the Indonesian Market
authors: Hanny Juwitasary, Norizan Anwar, Mohd Nasir Ismail, Yohannes Kurniawan, Violitta Yesmaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Technological advancements have driven a shift in e-commerce, where social media platforms, as venues for social interaction, play a significant role in consumer engagement, leading to the emergence of social commerce. This study investigates the influence of social factors (social support, social interaction, social presence) and technological factors (information quality, service quality, and system quality) on user engagement, with relationship quality and motivational factors acting as mediators. Based on the Information Systems Success Model, a pilot study was conducted with 37 social commerce users in Indonesia to validate the measurement model and assess the initial relationships between variables. The reliability analysis showed satisfactory internal consistency for most constructs (Cronbach's alpha <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.70$</tex>), while regression results revealed that affective engagement significantly influenced user engagement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(t=2.209, p<0.05)$</tex>. This study extends the Information Systems Success Model by integrating social and motivational factors into the social commerce context. The findings indicate that social and technological dimensions significantly influence motivational and relational factors, which in turn drive user engagement across affective, cognitive, and behavioral dimensions. Practically, these insights provide guidance for platform providers to design emotionally engaging features that foster user trust, motivation, and sustained participation.

title: Characterization and Analysis of LED Complex Impedance for Enhanced UHF-RFID Tag Design
authors: Riyadh I. Alqamati, Saad M. Alhossin, Muna M. Alhossin, Mezna M. Alhossin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Measuring The integration of Light-Emitting Diodes (LEDs) into radio frequency (RF) systems, such as UHF-RFID tags, necessitates a precise understanding of their highfrequency impedance characteristics. This paper presents a rigorous methodology for measuring the complex input impedance of five commercially available LED chips across the 200 MHz to 2 GHz frequency range. The results reveal a significant and previously un-quantified variance in impedance characteristics between different LED types, driven by differences in semiconductor materials and package parasitic. A key finding is that while LED impedance approaches a simplified, near- 50Ω resistive value below 900 MHz, it exhibits pronounced frequency-dependent behavior at higher frequencies, dominated by reactive components. This behavior makes simple resistor models insufficient and leads to severe antenna detuning and performance degradation if not accounted for. Consequently, this work establishes that LED selection for RF applications must be based on characterized impedance data rather than optical properties alone. The provided dataset serves as a critical reference for designers to select optimal components and simplify impedance matching networks. Furthermore, the observed impedance dispersion suggests a potential for novel applications in frequency sensing. This research provides the essential foundation for designing efficient LED-integrated wireless devices.

title: Transformer-Based ABSA for Sentiment Prediction on Indonesia's Economic Policy Discourse Using Youtube Data
authors: Royce William Mau Bere, Tuga Mauritsius
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Digital platforms increasingly shape how citizens express and negotiate public opinion about economic policy. In Indonesia, YouTube has emerged as a major arena where the public reacts to policy narratives related to taxation, subsidies, fuel prices, inflation, public debt, and government performance. While these comments provide valuable signals of public perception, they are unstructured, noisy, and difficult to analyze at scale using manual approaches. This paper presents a large-scale empirical study of Indonesian YouTube economic-policy discourse using a Transformer-based Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) pipeline. We collected 68,642 Indonesian-language comments from 42 YouTube videos, and 68,307 comments remained after preprocessing. Each comment is assigned one dominant policy aspect via zero-shot Natural Language Inference with XLM-RoBERTa over seven predefined aspects. Sentiment polarity is predicted with IndoBERT, fine-tuned with class-weighted loss on a validated silver-labeled dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5, 0 0 0}$</tex> comments. Silver labels were produced through AI-assisted labeling with confidence filtering and targeted manual review. Results show predominantly negative sentiment (62.10%), followed by positive (21.67%) and neutral <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(16.23 \%)$</tex>. The most discussed aspects are Subsidies and Social Assistance (39.54%) and Government Policies <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{2 8. 3 1 \%) }$</tex>. Aspect-level analysis indicates strong negativity toward Public Debt and the State Budget (APBN), Fuel and Energy, and Taxation (PPN, Indonesia's value-added tax), while Macroeconomic Conditions and Subsidies are more balanced. Overall, the study provides a practical benchmark-style workflow for producing interpretable, aspect-level insights to support policy communication and public opinion monitoring.

title: Uncertainty Modeling in Fault Diagnosis Using Multi-Layer 1D Convolutional Neural Networks Combined with Evidence Theory
authors: Deiwei Huang, Yanzhu Zhang, Fuyi Qu, Yuhan Ma
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: With the rapid development of deep neural network technologies, significant progress has been made in their application to fault diagnosis. Benefiting from the powerful feature extraction capability of convolutional neural networks (CNNs), these methods achieve end-to-end fault diagnosis with high accuracy. However, such data-driven approaches are heavily dependent on training datasets. When encountering out-of-distribution (OOD) data, they often exhibit overconfidence in incorrect diagnostic results, which may mislead decision-making. To address this issue, this paper integrates evidence theory with multi-layer one-dimensional convolutional neural networks, referred to as Evidence-based Multi-layer One-dimensional Convolutional Network (EMOCN), to mitigate the risk of overconfidence in fault diagnosis. Specifically, multi-layer one-dimensional Convolutional Networks are first employed as the backbone network to extract signal features. A Dirichlet distribution is then imposed on the network outputs, treating them as the initial parameters of this distribution, thereby transforming the problem into a probabilistic framework. Furthermore, by incorporating evidence theory, an uncertainty component is introduced into the loss function, forming an evidence loss function that enables the model to estimate the uncertainty of its diagnostic results. The proposed method is validated on the NASA battery dataset. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reduces the degree of overconfidence by 52.02% when the model encounters out-of-distribution data. Moreover, the model exhibits strong robustness under noisy interference, which is of great significance for practical applications.

title: An End-To-End Multitask Deep Learning Framework for Depth-Aware Nutritional Content Estimation from Food Images
authors: B. Tamilselvan, B. Karthikeyan, R. Prahadeeswaran, M. Pugazhendi, S. Sathish Kumar, A. Gokul Shanmugam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Reliable prediction of nutrient content from food images is essential for automatic diet monitoring, clinical nutritional evaluation, and emerging IoT- and edge-enabled health applications. In previous vision-based solutions either focus on single tasks, e.g., classification or calorie estimation, and thus do not provide accurate end-to-end nutrient prediction in real-world multi-item meal scenarios. In addition, estimating portion size and micronutrient content remains difficult due to scale variation, food heterogeneity, and the lack of annotated datasets. In this paper introduce a joint learning framework that integrates instance segmentation, food classification, depth-aware portion estimation, and macro and micronutrient prediction into a single multitask learning model. It unifies Mask R-CNN-based segmentation, EfficientNet-B4 classification, and monocular depth estimation (MiDaS) to calculate adjusted nutrient values based on standard, curated nutrition databases. A specialized multi-cuisine dataset is introduced, with sub-character-level annotations of portion size and nutrients, to evaluate the system comprehensively. Extensive experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms baselines and competing methodologies by a large margin, achieving 92.8% classification accuracy, a MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5.12 ~\mathrm{g}$</tex> for portion prediction, and a MAPE of 6.7 % for nutrient estimation. The developed system is a practical, scalable solution for automatic dietary monitoring and has excellent potential for use in mobile, healthcare, and smart-kitchen environments.

title: Better Data, Better Decisions: The Impact of Digitalisation and Big Data on Decision-Making
authors: Salim Akyurek, Nesrin M. Bahcelerli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Customer interactions, transaction procedures, and service experiences are the primary data sources used by today's tourism businesses. In a similar vein, big data and digitalization have emerged as crucial instruments for all decision-making procedures. The topic of digital transformation is growing in importance, but little information is available about how these technologies are methodically altering management decisions in the travel and tourism industry. By thoroughly examining the effects of tools like customer analysis platforms, reservation management systems, social media tracking tools, and big data processing structures on information flow, quality, and accessibility for local managers, this study seeks to close the current gap. The study reveals how digitalization affects decision-making processes at every stage, from determining the topic to evaluating it, increasing accuracy to speeding up data processing, reducing risks and developing more specific strategies. This research also provides evidence of the impact of big data and digital technologies on various stages of the decision-making process and a conceptual framework that the tourism sector can adopt to accelerate its digital transformation.

title: Heavy-Tail Risk in Traditional Versus Crypto Markets: A Q-Q Plot-Based Analysis
authors: Aubain Nzokem, Daniel Maposa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper investigates extreme risk in cryptocurrency markets by comparing Bitcoin and Ethereum daily returns with those of S&P 500 and SPY ETF. Using the Generalized Tempered Stable (GTS) distribution to model heavy tails and Quantile-Quantile (Q-Q) plots to assess fitness, we find that all assets deviate sharply from normal distribution. Within this framework, Ethereum exhibits a higher frequency of extreme returns than Bitcoin, highlighting differences in risk profiles even among leading cryptocurrencies.

title: Automated IFC-to-RDF Conversion for Semantic BIM Using IfcWall Entities
authors: Murat Aydın
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study presents a Python-based framework for converting IFC data into RDF triples, ensuring semantic integrity and interoperability in BIM environments. Using the SemanIFC library for parsing and RDFLib for graph generation, the workflow was applied to 45 IfcWall elements from a two-storey villa project, producing 540 RDF triples that integrate geometric, material, and performance attributes. SPARQL queries validated the dataset with 100% semantic coherence, confirming consistency across all wall entities. The automated process achieved a conversion time of 2.4 seconds, demonstrating efficiency and reproducibility through modular open-source scripts. The RDF output was fully aligned with the ifcOWL ontology and IFC4 EXPRESS schema, supporting compliance with buildingSMART standards. Contributions include modular automation, ontology alignment, reproducible validation, and extensibility for future integration of additional building components. This framework establishes a sustainable foundation for semantic BIM data management in both academic and industrial contexts.

title: Smart Health Tracking with IoT and Android App
authors: Mahadevaswamy U B, Rakesh M D, Sumanth P, M Baluchandra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The rise in respiratory diseases has amplified the need for efficient and deployable health monitoring solutions. This paper presents an IoT-based Multi-Parameter Health Monitoring System designed to continuously measure vital physiological and environmental parameters, including heart rate, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SpO}_{2}$</tex>, temperature, and humidity. The system utilizes the ESP32 microcontroller for wireless data transmission and integrates the MAX30102 sensor for accurate heart rate and oxygen saturation readings. Environmental data is captured using dedicated temperature and humidity sensors. All sensor data is transmitted to the ThingSpeak cloud for real-time visualization and historical analysis. An Android application provides users with live data access and trend monitoring, while a Telegram bot sends alerts when critical thresholds are exceeded. Testing confirms the system's accuracy, reliability, and suitability for remote health monitoring applications.

title: Particle-Based Hydraulic Erosion of Heightmap Data
authors: Micah D. Schuster
abstract: Hydraulic erosion models simulate the gradual wearing away of terrain by flowing water, a phenomenon critical to realistic landscape generation and geomorphological research. Among the various computational approaches, particle-based methods provide a simple yet powerful framework for modeling erosion dynamics on discrete heightmaps. In this article, we implement a two-dimensional particle-based hydraulic erosion algorithm that models the transport of sediment using simulated water droplets that traverse heightmap data. This process demonstrates how local particle-terrain interactions lead to large-scale landscape features such as valleys, gullies, and mountain ridges.

title: Surface Roughness Optimization in Additive Manufacturing Using Taguchi Method and 3D Optical Profilometry
authors: Efrem Olivio Gomes, Ilham Saputra, Hsu-Feng Chang, Shyang-Jye Chang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Additive manufacturing, or three-dimensional (3D) printing, is increasingly used in engineering applications, but surface roughness remains a critical limitation affecting the functional and aesthetic properties of printed components. This study investigates surface roughness optimization in fused deposition modeling (FDM) using the Taguchi method combined with high-resolution 3D optical profilometry. To obtain highquality and precise print results, the most influential factors in the 3D printing process are first identified, namely material type, print temperature, print speed, and plate temperature. Experiments are carried out using a special Orthogonal Array (OA): <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L 8}\left(\mathbf{2}^{\mathbf{7}}\right)$</tex> table to reduce the number of experiments, saving costs, time and effort. Due to the use of L8, each factor has 2 different levels that will be combined to obtain the best print results. Surface roughness is measured in triplicate for each experiment, and the signal-to-noise ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S} / \mathbf{N}$</tex>) with a “smaller is better” criterion is applied. The results of data analysis show that material type, print temperature, print speed, and plate temperature are the dominant parameters that affect the average surface roughness. The optimized parameter set significantly reduced roughness compared to the initial settings. These findings demonstrate that Taguchi-based optimization combined with 3D optical profilometry provides a reliable and efficient approach to improving surface finish in additive manufacturing, thereby reducing post-processing requirements and improving product quality.

title: Research of the Characteristics of a Thermoelectric Power Sensor Chip in the Frequency Range from 0,01 to 40 GHz
authors: Igor P. Chirkov, Ivan M. Malay, Alexey I. Matveev, Vladimir A. Perepelkin, Dmitry A. Shpagin, Polina I. Matveeva
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: The results of the development of an electrodynamic model of coplanar microstrip lines with a matched load and studies of thin-film DC resistors are presented. Experimental verification has confirmed the agreement between theoretical calculations and practical results, allowing the selection of suitable samples for thermoelectric microwave power converters. The research includes simulation modeling of various line topologies and experimental evaluation of their performance characteristics in the frequency range from 0,01 to 40 GHz. The study focuses on optimizing the design parameters to achieve high accuracy and reliability of power measurements. The investigation covers the following key aspects: Development of electrodynamic models for coplanar transmission lines Experimental testing of thin-film resistors Evaluation of voltage-current characteristics Measurement of VSWR parameters Analysis of thermal properties The obtained results provide a scientific basis for creating a high-precision power converter suitable for use in modern metrological systems.

title: A Hybrid YOLO-NAS-EfficientNet Framework for Automated Brain Tumor Detection and Classification From MRI Scans
authors: D. Ganesh, P. Venkateswarlu Reddy, Neeruganti Vidya Sree, Thotla Chakra Ganesh, Pudi Nikhil Surya Teja, Galam Dhanunjaneya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Brain tumor detection and classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans is a crucial task in the diagnosis process. Manual analysis of MRI scans is a time-consuming process prone to variability depending on the size, shape, and intensity of the tumor. To mitigate the above limitations, a hybrid framework is introduced in this paper combining YOLO-NAS for tumor localization with Efficient Net for tumor classification. YOLO-NAS optimized using Neural Architecture Search accelerates the single-stage detection process to detect tumors accurately in a single go on the MRI scans. The detected tumors are processed as Regions of Interest (ROI) for further analysis. EfficientNet then classifies the ROI values into Glioma, Meningioma, Pituitary Tumor, or Non-tumor. The results are compared on publicly available Brain MRI datasets such as BraTS, REMBRANDT, and BR35H. Experiments show that the combination outperforms CNN as well as VGG16-based models on the above-mentioned parameters. This combination offers a complete automated result with zero chances of error in ROI selection from MRI scans.

title: An Integrated Adaptive Security Framework for Data Security Posture Management with AI-Driven Orchestration and Quantum-Resistant Capabilities
authors: S Santhiya, S MadhanKumar, K Poongodi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Although cloud storage offers the much-needed scalability, flexibility, and economic efficacy, it also presents significant security and privacy issues, which are mostly caused by the fact that, since data are encrypted, the traditional plaintext search approaches are compromised. To overcome this shortcoming, we introduce the Enhanced Aware Record Maintenance (EARM) architecture, a compound, privacy-aware architecture that supports ranked, multi-keyword encrypted data retrieval in the cloud. The fundamental retrieval process of EARM is novel, incorporating ontology-based keyword mapping, enhanced association rule mining, and inner-product similarities to produce highly accurate and relevant search results. The framework deploys a strong multi-layer defense model, which integrates quantum-resistant cryptography (Zero-Knowledge Proofs/Homomorphic Encryption), high-grade cipher suites (AES/RSA), and granular Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) access controls (such as geofencing and decentralized identity management). Moreover, EARM also brings intelligent defensive layers of AI-based orchestration, which include ransomware, intrusion, and anomaly detection, behavioral biometrics, and a tamper-proof blockchain audit trail. Secure file versioning, rollback, and an encrypted trash bin are other ancillary features that improve the system's resilience. Empirical analyses performed on actual data sets indicate that EARM maintains high privacy and high retrieval rates and suffers only a few system overheads, hence making it an effective and highly secure encrypted cloud storage solution for modern challenges.

title: A Method for Assessing the Informativeness of Parkinson's Disease and Essential Tremor Features in EOG Signals Based on the AUC Diagrams
authors: Anastasia M. Vasilega, Olga S. Sushkova, Alexei A. Morozov, Alexander A. Vinarsky, Larisa A. Chigaleichick, Vsevolod V. Poleshchuk, Alexei V. Karabanov
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: A method for assessing the informativeness of Parkinson's disease (PD) and essential tremor (ET) features in electrooculographic (EOG) signals based on the AUC diagrams was developed. A distinctive feature of the method is the use of a visual representation of the feature space and simultaneous solving the problems of identifying PD and ET features and assessing their informativeness. New quantitative features were obtained using the developed method, that distinguish groups of patients with PD and ET with AUC of approximately 98 %.

title: Blockchain–Cloud Integration for Cross-Border Invoice Factoring: A Secure and Scalable Framework for Sustainable Trade Finance
authors: Palaniappan Sellappan, Kavitha Shanmugam, Varun Sarda, Vijay Arpudaraj Antonyaj
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Cross-border trade finance supports liquidity and global supply chain growth, especially for small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Traditional documentation and invoice factoring systems face delays, huge documentation, fraud risks, poor interoperability, and limited transparency which results in unsustainable action. Blockchain-based trade finance improves immutability, decentralized validation, and automation through smart contracts. However, it suffers from scalability limitations, fragmented data management, and regulatory integration issues. Academic literature lacks a unified framework combining blockchain and cloud to address these operational gaps. This study fills the gap by evaluating traditional, blockchain, and hybrid models in trade finance systems. The paper reviews major use cases to understand realworld blockchain implementations in trade finance. These include HSBC and ING's blockchain letter of credit, and Bank of Canada's Project Jasper. UBS's blockchain payment initiative is also analyzed for its real-time settlement and auditability benefits. The study compares traditional and blockchain systems across speed, security, transparency, and integration parameters. It identifies that blockchain alone is insufficient due to its lack of scalability and limited system compatibility. To address this, the paper proposes a Blockchain-Cloud Integrated Trade Finance (TF) Framework. This includes hybrid data storage, Application Programming Interface (API) compliance, digital identity verification, and stakeholder dashboards. Using a design science approach, the framework improves auditability, transparency, and regulatory alignment. Findings benefit regulators, banks, and FinTech's. The framework not only enhances efficiency and compliance but also contributes to sustainable trade finance

title: SDGs in Electrical Engineering Education: A Data-Driven Mixed-Methods Analysis of Student Perceptions
authors: Mark Anthony G. Teodoro, Ian B. Benitez
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Electrical Engineering (EE) has substantial potential to advance Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy), SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure), and SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities). Yet, limited evidence exists on how EE students perceive the alignment of their academic training with the SDG agenda, especially in the Global South. This study provides a mixed-methods, datadriven assessment of SDG awareness, curricular integration, and perceived professional relevance among EE students (n = 51) at a private university in the Philippines. The methodology integrates quantitative survey analytics using weighted scoring, descriptive statistics, and visualizations with qualitative thematic coding of open-ended responses. Results reveal a triangular gap: awareness is moderate across most goals, curricular integration is uneven and biased toward energy and infrastructure themes, while perceived professional relevance is consistently high. Students highlight contributions to renewable systems, grid modernization, and sustainable infrastructure, while also identifying gaps in equity, biodiversity, and circular economy integration. Qualitative responses reinforce these findings, pointing to isolated project-based engagement but limited systemic curricular embedding. The study demonstrates how data-driven mixedmethods analysis can inform curriculum design, highlighting both technical strengths and social blind spots, and provides baseline evidence for aligning EE education with global sustainability imperatives.

title: Estimation of Nitrogen Pool for Terrestrial Ecosystem Using Extended Kalman Filter
authors: Priyanka Sharma, A. Bhuvana Chandra, Rabindra Kumar Nayak, Pyari Mohan Pradhan
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Accurate estimation of nitrogen pools is essential for understanding land-atmosphere interactions and bio-geochemical feedback. However, it remains challenging due to strong nonlinearities, sparse observations, and large spatial heterogeneity. This paper proposes an extended Kalman filter (EKF) based approach for large-scale nitrogen pool estimation over India. Using full grid simulations over two years at a monthly resolution, ten nitrogen pools are estimated, comprising nine organic pools (plant, litter, and soil organic matter) and one inorganic soil mineral nitrogen pool. The nitrogen cycle model is driven by carbon pool dynamics from the Carnegie Ames Stanford Approach (CASA) model and satellite derived net primary productivity (NPP). Reference nitrogen states are derived from CASA carbon pools using an identified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}: \mathrm{N}$</tex> ratio, enabling controlled system simulation. The true nitrogen pools are perturbed with Gaussian noise to create synthetic observations, and assimilation is carried out using a Monte Carlo framework. Average root mean square error (ARMSE) is used to evaluate the performance of the proposed EKF based approach. Results show that the EKF consistently outperforms other nonlinear KF variants across most nitrogen pools, particularly for plant, litter, and soil organic matter components, highlighting the effectiveness of first-order linearization for large scale terrestrial data assimilation. The study provides practical insights into the selection of EKF for nitrogen cycle modeling in Earth system applications.

title: Optimization of the Parameters of Weight Functions Used in Range Sensors to Smooth the Sampling Error
authors: Vladimir Mikhaylovich Artyushenko, Vladimir Ivanovich Volovach
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Quite a lot of research has been devoted to determining the range to moving extended objects. As a rule, all engineering solutions dealing with such problems are based on using a radar range sensor. Their main objective is to obtain a minimum error in determining the distance to the target. The determination of the optimal method error of the discreteness of the range sensor is known to have its own features characteristic of digital signal processing. The paper studies and analyzes a method for local optimization of parameters of an arbitrary-shaped weight function used to smooth measurement errors in range sensors based on the principle of a frequency range-finder. The effect of additive noise on the accuracy of range determination is estimated using a weighted approach to reducing sampling error. It is established that at small measuring distances, the optimal coefficients of the weight function ensure effective suppression of the noise component. With large ranges, there is a monotone decrease in noise error, which, however, remains significantly higher than the method error. An analytical expression for the variance of measurement error is obtained. The presented results confirm the effectiveness and practical importance of optimizing weight functions used in remote measurement systems based on frequency rangefinders, and allow us to determine the maximum permissible noise level, ensuring the required measurement accuracy in each specific case.

title: Mesophilic Anaerobic Co-Digestion of Whey with Sewage Sludge: Process Stability and Methane Yield Enhancement
authors: Nassima Tirichine, Mohamed Khitous
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: In this study, we evaluates the methanogenic potential of biogas produced from the mesophilic digestion of cheese whey, using sewage sludge from the wastewater treatment plant (WWTP) as inoculum. The influence of key operational parameters, including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, total solids (TS), volatile solids (VS), and the substrate-to-inoculum ratio (S/I) were investigated. Process performance was assessed through regular control of pH, alkalinity (TA), volatile fatty acid (VFA) production, TS, VS, cumulative biogas production and biomethane, and the composition of the biogas. The digestion of whey with WWTP sludge, without external <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> adjustment, produced a methane content of up to 83% and a high methane yield of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$274 \mathrm{L} \text{CH}_{4} / \text{kg}$</tex> VS. Optimal performance was achieved under the following conditions: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H} \mathbf{6. 5 - 7, ~ 3. 4 \%}$</tex> TS, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$45.6 \% \text{VS}$</tex>, and a substrate mixture of 20% whey with 80% of sludge. These results demonstrate that mesophilic digestion of cheese whey offers a suitable approach for large-scale renewable methane production while contributing to effective biowaste management.

title: LexCAT: A Lexicon-Enhanced Transformer for Tagalog–English Code-Switched Sentiment Analysis
authors: Glenn Marcus D. Cinco, Madhavi Devaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Sentiment analysis of Tagalog-English codeswitched text (Taglish) remains a significant challenge in low-resource natural language processing due to informal expressions, intra-sentential sentiment shifts, and lexical ambiguity. To address the neutral bias of out-of-the-box transformers (e.g., XLM-RoBERTa) and the contextual limitations of monolingual lexicons (e.g., FilCon, SentiWordNet), this study introduces LexCAT, a lexicon-enhanced transformer integrating XLM-RoBERTa with LexiLiksik, a novel, sense-disambiguated bilingual sentiment lexicon built from 78,487 entries scraped exclusively from the Pinoy Dictionary. LexiLiksik resolves polysemy (e.g., “saya” = dress vs. happiness), enriches entries with part-of-speech tags and code-switching patterns, and anchors sentiment via native-speaker-validated disambiguation. LexCAT injects this knowledge via soft constraints, attention recalibration toward contrastive conjunctions (e.g., pero, kaso), and weighted pooling. Evaluated on FiReCS and SentiTaglish, LexCAT achieves 89.38 % accuracy and 0.8566 F1-score, outperforming monolingual baselines (XLMR-FilCon: 51.31%, XLMR-SentiWordNet: 45.87%) and correctly classifying intra-sentential shifts. To assess cross-lingual generalizability, pilot experiments were conducted on Hiligaynon-English (-28.14 pp) and Tamil-English (-34.65 pp), revealing performance degradation due to morphological and syntactic divergence, confirming that successful adaptation requires languagespecific lexicon construction.

title: Efficient Edge AI Inference for Real-Time Verification of Sudden Unintended Acceleration
authors: HeeSeok Choi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In recent years, the increasing number of Sudden Unintended Acceleration (SUA) incidents has underscored the need for reliable, real-time verification methods in vehicles. This paper proposes an efficient method for real-time edge AI inference to verify SUA events. To achieve this, we present an Edge AI framework that integrates sensor-derived pedal displacement data with visual evidence of driver pedal operations, deploying light-weight convolutional neural networks (CNNs) on Jetson-based edge devices. Unlike cloud-dependent systems, the proposed approach provides robust and low-latency inference under resource-constrained environments. Furthermore, class-granularity optimization is applied to balance recognition performance with clear decision boundaries, enhancing model stability in complex classification tasks. Experimental results show that the proposed framework achieves high accuracy with low latency, demonstrating its practicality as a consumer-oriented verification tool for SUA. This study contributes not only a technical Edge AI solution but also a societal perspective on accident liability and automotive safety.

title: DDoS Attack Detection in IoT Using Spotted Hyena Optimizer with Hyperparameter Optimized Artificial Neural Network
authors: S.B. Gopal, K.R Prasanna Kumar, D. Nanthiya, Ashwin V, Deeksha P, Ganika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The internet and cloud services have grown rapidly, thereby creating a greater risk of Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, defined here by the overwhelming of systems and services via malicious incoming traffic, or overflow. Using conventional techniques for DDoS detection pose a challenge if they break to stay even with the dynamically changing along with evolving nature of present cyberattacks. This paper presents research addressing this problem by proposing a machine learning-based method for efficient DDoS attack detection, specifically focused on Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Spotted Hyena Optimization (SHO). A proposed system is built and evaluated based on the CIC-IDS 2017/2019 dataset that contains quasi-realistic network traffic, and includes multiple types of benign and malicious flow. Preprocessing, cleaning, feature encoding, normalizing, and traintest splitting were conducted in order to prepare the dataset for learning. A baseline ANN was implemented using multiple hidden layers and multiple activation functions to capture the non-linear traffic patterns that emerge. The model was trained, and then validated, using categorization standard including accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. Further improvements in neural network efficiency are provided, by integrating the system with SHO for hyper-parameter tuning and optimization. Experimental results exhibit that a hybrid ANN-SHO system produces higher accuracy and a more robust detection model compared to the baseline.

title: Development of a Tool for Visual Comparison of Patients with Each Other and with Population Based on BIA Parameters
authors: Vladimir A. Kolesnikov, Svetlana P. Shchelykalina, Dmitry V. Nikolaev
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: In routine practice, the diagnostic process can be considered as a sequential comparison of values of a set of onedimensional parameters with their acceptable limits obtained from their prevalence in the healthy population. A more accurate approach considers data relating to an individual as a single object, with the data being multidimensional in terms of the analysed diagnostic parameters. The objective of this work is to develop a tool for visual assessment of patients' similarity to and comparison with population parameters within the corresponding age and sex range based on two-dimensional representations of body composition parameters. The developed tool for visual assessment of patients' similarity to and comparison with population parameters includes five components: a system of two-dimensional percentile curves of the reference Russian population for the selected age and sex range and each pair of BIA parameters based on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,083,275$</tex> examinations conducted at Health Centres between 2009 and 2015; delineation of the low-typicality ring region between the 70th and 95th two-dimensional percentiles into eight ring sectors of 45 degrees between the radial boundaries; visualisation of the centre of the patient group - the median and/or mean value; construction of a two-dimensional 95% confidence interval for the mean value of the patient group; construction of probability ellipses encompassing user-defined proportions of the patient group (default: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 {\%}, 75 {\%}$</tex> and 95%). This tool for two-dimensional reference analysis of human body composition, developed using data from large-scale examinations conducted in Health Centres, may allow the visual detection of patterns associated with body composition characteristics in various diseases and conditions without the use of complex statistical data processing methods. The tool can be effectively used both for individual assessments and for the analysis of group patterns.

title: Continuous-Time Identification Methods for Multi-Input-Single-Output LPV Systems
authors: Chayma Touiti, Noura Ben Moussa, Haifa Ethabet, Manel Chetoui, Mohamed Aoun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper investigates the continuous-time identification of Multi-Input-Single-Output (MISO) Linear-ParameterVarying (LPV) systems. Due to the inherent complexity of such systems, efficient methods are required to accurately estimate the parameters of the overall MISO model by treating it as a set of interconnected Single-Input-Single-Output (SISO) subsystems. Thus, three continuous-time identification methods are proposed: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS), Instrumental Variables (IV), and Iterative Instrumental Variables (IIV). Their performance is assessed through Monte Carlo simulations under two output noise scenarios, namely white noise and colored noise, providing a comparative evaluation of estimation accuracy and robustness.

title: IoT-Guard: AI-Powered Intrusion Detection for Securing IoT Networks
authors: K Murugesh, Madhumithan S, Mahesh R, Anand Kumar, Mala B A, Ankita I B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The IoT-Guard system seeks to develop an AIpowered intrusion detection system (IDS) to enhance security of increasingly vulnerable IoT networks. While the number of IoT devices grows exponentially in healthcare, industry, and smart homes, traditional security is typically inadequate due to the inherent limitations and heterogeneity of devices. The proposed IoT-Guard addresses this by employing machine learning methods to scan network logs in real time, detecting anomalies that can suggest cyber-attacks such as DoS, DDoS, brute force attacks, malware, or intrusion. Through learning from past data, the system becomes adapt at distinguishing between normal and suspicious behavior, enabling it to detect threats in advance and with precision. The IDS continuously adapts and learns through predictive analytics, enabling proactive defense and minimizing the likelihood of future attacks. The Novel approach of IoT-Guard also triggers realtime alerts and detailed reports, enabling administrators to respond suitably and in time. This intelligent, self-learning system is a quantum leap to making IoT environments secure and immune to new cyber-attacks.

title: Improving Anomaly Detection in Banking Transactions Through Feature-Enhanced and Bootstrap-Based Deep Learning Models
authors: Manafaddin Namazov, Musa Rahimov, Isa Sadigov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study uses deep learning models in conjunction with feature engineering and bootstrap-based uncertainty estimates to forecast and detect anomalies in monthly banking transactions. Real, anonymised monthly bank transaction counts from January 2016 to September 2025 make up the investigated dataset, which is a univariate time series with 117 observations. The models of Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) are assessed both under baseline conditions and following the application of feature engineering techniques, such as lag-based features, moving averages, and zscore normalization. Additionally, bootstrap-based resampling is used to increase the robustness of the model and use confidence intervals to measure forecast uncertainty. Results from experiments show significant gains in performance following data augmentation. Strong short-term predicted accuracy was demonstrated by the LSTM model, which decreased the Mean Absolute Error from 203.57 to 112.41 and raised the coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>) from 0.83 to 0.94 for the 6 -month forecasting horizon. With an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.89 as opposed to 0.87 for the LSTM model, the GRU model demonstrated better generalization capacity for longer-term forecasting over an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8}$</tex>-month horizon. The suggested approach allows for uncertainty-aware forecasts that go beyond point forecasting accuracy. This makes it possible to identify anomalous months where observed transaction counts differ significantly from projected ranges. These irregularities could be the result of unusual consumer behavior, seasonal disturbances, or problems with the banking system's operations. Overall, the findings demonstrate that combining deep recurrent neural networks with feature engineering and bootstrap-based uncertainty estimates offers a useful and efficient method for tracking, predicting, and identifying anomalies in time series of banking transactions.

title: Energy Management of a 6.6 kW PV-Battery DC Microgrid Using Incremental Conductance and Bidirectional Conversions
authors: P. Chandrasekar, N. Haricharan, A. Dhanush
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This work presents a standalone PV-battery DC micro grid controlled by an integrated MPPT-bidirectional power-flow scheme aimed at stable bus regulation under irradiance and load disturbances. A 6.6 kW PV array is interfaced through a boost stage with Incremental Conductance (INC) MPPT, while a bidirectional DC-DC converter manages charging and discharging of the battery as a function of state of charge (SOC), bus voltage error, and power balance. The controller coordinates operating modes (PV-dominant, battery-assist, and battery-only) and enforces protection against over voltage, over current, and load disconnects. MATLAB/ Simulink studies evaluate bus-voltage regulation, MPPT tracking quality, battery cycling, and dynamic recovery for step changes in irradiance and load. Results show fast convergence to the MPP with minimal oscillation and robust DC-bus regulation during large disturbances, demonstrating the suitability of the approach for rural DC microgrids. Recent developments in DC-microgrid control and improved INC variants motivate the chosen architecture and parameterization.

title: A Quality-Controlled Three-Level Cascading IoT Intrusion Detection Model with Hybrid Feature Selection
authors: Richard Kimanzi Mutisya, Gabriel Kamau, Juliet Moso
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has evolved over time, with many applications adopting its technologies to address modern challenges. The extensive interconnectivity and resource constraints of IoT devices introduce various security vulnerabilities, making them prime targets for cyber-attacks. This study introduces a hybrid feature selection method for a three-level cascaded intrusion detection system tailored for IoT networks, to enhance threat detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency. Quality-controlled sample filtering is introduced in the cascading hierarchy to reduce error propagation across detection levels and improve detection reliability. The proposed method combines feature correlation analysis, Recursive Feature Elimination, Gradient Boosting, and Random Forest to improve threat detection while maintaining computational efficiency. Using the CIC-IoT-2023 dataset, 21 optimal features were selected, achieving 99.11% accuracy and a 9.08% F1-score at the first detection level with the XGBoost algorithm. XGBoost continued to perform best at the second level. At the third level, a trade-off between computation time, detection speed, and accuracy resulted in 93.55% accuracy and 93.51% F1-score.

title: Experimental Comparative Study Between Classical PI Controller and Fractional PI Controller Applied to Photovoltaic System
authors: Abdelhalim Borni, Badreddine Bezza, Layachi Zaghba, Abdelhak Bouchakour, Thameur Abdelkrim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper proposes an experimental study of a photovoltaic system driven by two MPPT controllers: a conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P \& O-P I$</tex> and a fractional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P \& O-P I$</tex>, with the aim of improving the dynamic performance of the boost converter control used in photovoltaic production systems. To this end, a maximum power point tracking strategy has been implemented. The optimal current, obtained from the MPPT algorithm, is used as a reference for current regulation in the conventional and fractional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P I$</tex> controller. The tracking performance and dynamic capabilities of the proposed control method were verified through experimental tests. The dynamic behavior of the algorithm was evaluated under various irradiance conditions. The results obtained demonstrate that this approach offers excellent dynamic performance, particularly thanks to its efficiency in tracking the reference.

title: AI-Driven Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery: Foundations, Methods, and Emerging Innovations
authors: D. Loganayaki, Vendhan Duraisamy, Geetha Priya J, Kirubhanandan C G, M.G. Dinesh, M Ezhilvendan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Knowledge Discovery and Intelligent Data Mining have become key elements of contemporary data-driven systems, which allow organizations to extract meaningful information out of large, intricate, and heterogeneous data. The survey is an in-depth discussion of smart data mining models by looking at their underpinnings, machine learning approaches, computational intelligence, and improved deep learning models. It emphasizes the fact that intelligent approaches overcome the shortcomings of conventional data mining as it provides automated feature learning, adaptive reasoning, and sound optimization. The paper also covers the combination of evolutionary computation, swarm intelligence, fuzzy logic, neural networks, and hybrid models in order to improve their pattern discovery and decision-support capabilities. In practice, the use of intelligent mining methods in healthcare, cybersecurity, business analytics, IoT, scientific discovery, and personalized recommendations is discussed to show the transformative potential of intelligent mining methods. Moreover, the survey also determines the major issues associated with scalability, data quality, interpretability, bias, ethical issues, and privacy. Future research directions such as quantum-inspired mining, federated knowledge discovery, neuro-symbolic integration, and sustainable AI are the new trends that define the future intelligent mining systems.

title: AI-Powered Facial Recognition With Real-Time Sentiment Analysis
authors: Bhanu Priya S, Chanddraprakash S, Preetha Evangeline D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Facial emotion detection has emerged as an important application in artificial intelligence, enabling machines to interpret human emotions from facial expressions and improving modern systems across security, education, healthcare and entertainment. Within the “Face Recognition System Using AI Technologies,” the emotion recognition module acts as a core extension that goes beyond identity verification by classifying emotions such as happiness, anger, sadness, surprise, fear, and neutrality, allowing the system to better understand human behavior. This module relies on deep learning and computer vision methods, primarily Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) trained on large labeled datasets containing different emotional states. To ensure accuracy and generalization, preprocessing steps including grayscale conversion, face extraction using Haar cascades, normalization, and data augmentation are applied before training. The CNN then learns distinctive facial patterns corresponding to each emotion and predicts the most likely state in real time. The successful integration of this emotion detection module highlights the growing potential of AI-based facial analysis in intelligent decision-making systems and lays the foundation for advanced real-world applications such as smart monitoring, adaptive learning platforms, customer sentiment tracking, and mental health analytics, ultimately improving user interaction and overall system responsiveness.
