title: ProitMTA: A Multi-Target Model Poisoning Attack Framework for Federated Recommendation Systems with Proxy Items_supp1-3678755.pdf
authors: Qing LIAO
abstract: In federated recommendation systems, model poisoning attacks aim to manipulate the gradient information of multiple target items sent back from local clients to the central server, with the goal of abnormally increasing their exposure across the system. Existing multi-target attack approaches directly manipulate multiple target items and apply a uniform attack strategy to all target items, which may lead to suboptimal promotion effectiveness. To address this issue, we introduce ProitMTA, a novel multi-target model poisoning attack framework that introduces proxy items and provides tailored attack strategies for target items. ProitMTA employs a three-stage process that balances the promotion of multiple target items while preserving recommendation quality. First, proxy item generation uses a Gaussian Mixture Model to create proxy items that represent diverse attack strategies. Second, proxy attack construction designs customized gradient manipulation strategies for each proxy item. Finally, proxy-based target item attack transfers these strategies to actual target items, enhancing their promotion while minimizing the negative impact on system performance. Through comprehensive experiments on multiple base federated recommendation frameworks and diverse real-world datasets, we demonstrate that ProitMTA outperforms existing attack methods, achieving higher success rates in target item promotion with minimal system-wide performance degradation. Our research highlights the vulnerability of federated recommendation systems when facing multi-target poisoning attacks and underscores the importance of researching effective defense mechanisms We have released our code at https://github.com/zdy769243418/ProitMTA.

title: HoneyLLMd: A Large Language Model-Powered Adaptive Honeypot System
authors: Wenjun Fan, Fei Du, Zhihui Wang, Qiyu Cao, Lekun Liu, Kejing Chang, Sang-Yoon Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: A honeypot is a cybersecurity mechanism designed to lure attackers by emulating vulnerabilities and serving as a decoy, intentionally crafted to detect and analyze malicious activities. However, achieving a balance between the ability to engage attackers extensively and the need to minimize exposure of system resources remains an enduring challenge. High-interaction honeypots (HIHs) provide in-depth system-level observation through full operating systems, yet they inherently increase the risk of being compromised due to the extensive exposure of functionalities. In contrast, low- and medium-interaction honeypots (LIHs/MIHs) emulate only minimal or disguised services, which cannot spawn reverse shells or execute real command responses, but significantly reduce the risk of system intrusion. To overcome this limitation, we present a large language model (LLM)-powered adaptive honeypot system capable of supporting both bind and reverse shell interactions with authentic shell-like responses generated by the LLM rather than a real OS. This design enables convincing, continuous “request—response” interactions in which attackers unintentionally reveal behavioral and intent data. Furthermore, a hierarchical probabilistic automaton (HPA) is employed to model attack transitions and dynamically adapt honeypot strategies. Experimental validation on real-world attacks demonstrates that the proposed system achieves superior engagement and data capture while maintaining low security risk.

title: Mitigating Edge Heterogeneity for Hierarchical Federated Learning: Prototype System and Convergence Analysis
authors: Dapeng Wu, Changxing Lu, Zhiyuan Zhu, Tong Tang, Ruyan Wang, Honggang Wang
abstract: Hierarchical federated learning (HFL) emerges as a promising solution for distributed systems in real-world IoT, compensating for the limitations of client and training data through multilevel aggregation. However, severe system heterogeneity, such as data heterogeneity, differentiated communication and computing power, can cause model drift and negatively impact system performance, posing challenges to existing HFL frameworks. To address this issue, we propose a mitigating edge heterogeneity method for HFL (MEH-Fed) in this paper. First, we design a new HFL framework, where the key idea is to introduce an initial update of the edge server side to reduce the impact of heterogeneity on system performance. Edge servers use synthetic datasets to guide the update direction of client models, facilitating model training and accelerating convergence. Then, we conduct a convergence analysis of the proposed method, establish a theoretical upper bound, and analyze the effects of key parameters. Finally, we experimentally evaluate the performance of the proposed MEH-Fed in terms of system performance and convergence rate. Experimental results validate that MEH-Fed achieves optimal performance in both accuracy and efficiency, establishing a new paradigm in HFL architecture design for heterogeneous IoT environments.

title: PLC-CA: Phoneme-Level Component Convolutional Adversarial Attack with High Transferability
authors: Jiacheng Deng, Yuhong Liu, Dengpan Ye, Zhaolin Wei, Yuhan lin, Yunming Zhang, Zhe Ye, Yueyun Shang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Traditional additive distortion is prone to introduce human-perceivable auditory distortion, while simple convolutional adversarial methods have limited transferability between different model architectures. This paper proposes a universal and highly transferable adversarial attack method Phoneme Level Component Convolutional Adversarial Attack (PLC-CA), aiming to maximize the deviation in the time-frequency distribution of audio signals to improve transferability and maintain auditory naturalness. Specifically, PLC-CA decouples phoneme-level temporal boundaries via the Montreal Forced Aligner (MFA) and extracts phoneme-level components using Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD). Subsequently, deep deviation of speech features is achieved through two key operations: (1) phoneme-level component filtering: We selectively filter extremely low-energy phoneme components to retain primary frequency components and avoid significant degradation of auditory perception. (2) phoneme-level component convolutional perturbation: We apply echo-like convolutional noise tailored to the frequency band characteristics of phonemes to fine-grain regulate the time-frequency distribution. Experimental results on keyword recognition (KWS), speaker verification (ASV), and automatic speech recognition (ASR) tasks demonstrate that PLC-CA enhances performance through two keys: 1) High transferability: Compared with existing baseline methods, PLC-CA significantly improves transferability metrics by 32%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>76%(KWS), 19%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>146% (ASR), and 24%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>400%(ASV), respectively. 2) Perceived invisibility: In the case of similar signal-to-noise ratio, its average MOS score is much better than that of existing additive adversarial methods.

title: PV-Based Hydrogen Supply Chain Optimization: CAPEX Development Effect on the Future Trends of Hydrogen Cost
authors: Hammou Tebibel, Abdelhamid M'raoui, Khadidja Khodja
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: In this paper, a techno-economic optimization model is applied to address the photovoltaic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PV})$</tex> hydrogen <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{H}_{2}\right)$</tex> production and cost development through 2050. It is used for optimal capacity allocation of the multi-megawatt-scale off-grid <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PV}-\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> system to meet a daily demand of 1.2 t at lowest levelized cost of hydrogen (LCOH). The system consists of an off-grid PV power plant, a battery bank, a set of alkaline water electrolyzer unit and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> storage facility. The framework involves a smoothing control strategy. Projection cost analysis was performed based on the expected CAPEX decrease of all system components due to the effect of technological learning. It is found that the PV ratio which is the ratio between the size of the PV generator and the size of the electrolyzer should be always greater than 1.2 in off-grid configuration. The weak competitiveness of the off-grid solar <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> currently in Algeria should be enhanced by 2040. LCOH may decrease each decade by more than 0.7 $ / kg in case of system without <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> storage and by 1.1 $ / kg in case of the system including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> storage facilities. An optimistic scenario could lead to an LCOH lower than 1.57 $/kg by 2050, while a pessimistic scenario could lead to an LCOH greater than 2.9 $/kg.

title: Leveraging IndoRoBERTa and BERTopic for Comparative Multi-Platform Sentiment Analysis of PayLater Services in Indonesia
authors: Sharla Devrina Aurelliya, Alfian Akbar Gozali
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The swift expansion of Buy Now Pay Later (PayLater) services in Indonesia has instigated a multifaceted transformation in consumer behavior, encompassing financial inclusion, impulsive purchasing, and debt entrapment. Nevertheless, existing sentiment analysis studies predominantly focus on individual platforms or brand-specific metrics, thereby overlooking the cross-platform dynamics of public perception. This study addresses this gap by conducting a comparative multi-platform sentiment analysis across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{X}$</tex> (Twitter), YouTube, and TikTok, utilizing a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2, 6 3 1}$</tex> user interactions. A fine-tuned IndoRoBERTa model is employed to effectively handle the linguistic noise and code-mixing characteristics of Indonesian social media, while BERTopic is applied to uncover latent discussion themes. Experimental results demonstrate that IndoRoBERTa outperforms conventional baselines (SVM and Random Forest), achieving an average accuracy of 0.857. Notably, the model attains strong performance on YouTube (0.810), exceeding the best baseline by 13.1%, which highlights the effectiveness of dynamic masking in processing unstructured comments. Topic modeling reveals that negative sentiment is not solely driven by external factors such as debt collection practices; instead, dissatisfaction primarily arises from impulsive buying regret (25.5%), particularly related to vehicle purchases, while psychological distress associated with usury (riba) accounts for 20.9%. These findings suggest that the PayLater ecosystem requires platform-specific interventions that integrate behavioral financial literacy with technical regulation.

title: Technical Aspects Review of Integration Vehicle to Grid (V2G) Systems with Renewable Energy Sources for Enhancing Grid Resilience
authors: Tyas Kartika Sari, Dianing Novita Nurmala Putri, Maulasukma Widjaja, Chairul G Irianto
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The growing concern about greenhouse gas emissions and global warming have emerged the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles (EVs/HEV), together with the integration of renewable energy sources (RES) into power systems. Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology enables bidirectional power exchange between EVs and the grid, it becomes as a key component in enhancing grid stability and resilience. This technology not only supports the integration of EVs into grid but also provides auxiliary services such as grid stability, peak demand management and renewable integration. This paper presents technical challenges review of the integration V2G systems with renewable energy sources. The study highlights the strategies needed to integration V2G and RES into power grid through technical approach review. Furthermore, this study aims to review technical aspects for V2G systems including key components, configurations, charging stations, communication protocols, integration with renewable energy sources and simulation for V2G or G2V systems in matlab simulink. Overall, the integration of V2G and RES offers a strategic roadmap for future energy sustainability and grid power systems resilience.

title: IoT-Based Urban Agriculture Container Farm Design and Implementation for Localized Produce Supply
authors: P. Surendra Varma, Bhimavaram Kishan Reddy, Kancharla Chandra Sekhar, Pydimukkala Gnan Joel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Urban agriculture is faced with the big challenge of ensuring the best growing conditions and with minimal use of resources in a limited urban setting. The design and implementation of the IoT-Based Urban Agriculture Container Farm (IUACF) is a smart farming system based on the DHT-11, MQ-135, LDR, and ESP8266 microcontroller, which monitors air quality, light intensity, and temperature and humidity in real- time and provides alerts about the need to water plants and turn on lights, respectively. The information is sent to a cloud service, where it is supervised remotely with the help of automatic feedback in order to keep crops in the best possible state. The essence is to maximize the utilization of water and energy in urban container farming with the basic crops to include spinach, carrot, capsicum, and cucumber. The experimental outcomes indicate that IUACF realized 28 percent water and 15 percent increased crop production than traditional farming systems that used man power, and at the same time under 18 kWh/month of energy consumption is enough to operate a typical system. Modular design and wireless cloud integration enable the system to support smooth decision making that is data-driven and is scalable. These results show that the suggested IoT solution can significantly boost sustainability and efficiency in urban agriculture to offer a highquality framework on future Smart City food production programs.

title: Green Computing: Real-Time Carbon Footprint Optimization in Data Centers
authors: Navneet Jaswal, Bharat Yadav, Gagan Kadian, Chirag Khanna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The use of data centers absorbs 1-1.5 percent of the global electricity and is under strain to lower carbon emissions as the climate issue grows. The conventional energy optimization methods have used fixed strategies that cannot respond to dynamic changes in carbon intensity relating to modern power grids where renewable energy integration leads to large swings throughout the day. This paper introduces a new multi-agent reinforcement learning framework, which is CarbonOpt-MARL, to optimize carbon footprint in real-time within data centers. Our strategy to coordinate three specialized agents who deal with workload scheduling, cooling systems, and energy storage in order to react dynamically to real-time carbon intensity data provided by the grid. The model uses a decentralized implementation and centralized critic structure to provide coordinated decisionmaking and yet individual autonomy of the agents. The simulated experiments on Microsoft Azure workload traces and historical grid data of three geographical locations clearly indicate that CarbonOpt-MARL minimizes carbon emissions by an average rate of 27.3 percent relative to traditional fixed optimization methods and preserves 98.7 percent of the base level performance. This piece of work presents a feasible model of how carbonconscious optimization can be applied to current data center.

title: Blockchain Platform Performance Assessment for Livestock Traceability
authors: Bernardo J. R. Figueiredo, Marco P. M. Ferreira, Marco Cova, Francisco Faria, Miguel L. Pardal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Distributed Ledger Technologies (ICDLT)
abstract: Ensuring transparency and trust in the livestock supply chain is a significant business challenge in agro-food due to the involvement of multiple stakeholders with often conflicting interests. The complex life cycle of cattle requires reliable data sharing with the ability to verify the authenticity of records. To address this, we developed a livestock traceability application on the Hyperledger Fabric platform, which models livestock as assets and captures their life cycles through event records. This system provides a decentralized, shared ‘single source of truth’ for farmers, transporters, veterinarians, and regulators. As performance is paramount for adoption, we conducted an evaluation of the platform under various configurations. Our test suite comprises 10 runs, each executed on 5 independent peer instances, evaluating 100 assets per run. We compare two specific configurations: Configuration A, featuring singletransaction blocks of 99 MB; and Configuration B, using 49 MB multi-transaction blocks with 10 transactions per block. Both configurations were tested across 10 runs to thoroughly assess their impact on transaction throughput and overall system scalability. The study also included long-duration runs, seven times the length of the main runs, to evaluate sustained performance. Furthermore, we investigated how blocks are filled by testing configurations with a 49 MB block size and a 1000 transaction limit. The results in throughput, latency, storage efficiency, and memory usage show that Configuration B had superior performance in throughput and success rate, exhibiting lower and more consistent latencies. However, Configuration B also showed more pronounced storage growth and memory usage anomalies. Our analysis provides insights and actionable recommendations for optimizing smart contract payloads, implementing compression techniques, and rebalancing the load on orderer nodes, to achieve a sustainable and scalable solution.

title: Data Science Field Job Recommendations in Indonesia Using Content-Based Filtering
authors: Mutiara Indria Zainuddin, Lili Ayu Wulandhari, Abba Suganda Girsang
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Lack of career information remains a major challenge among Indonesian students, leading to frequent career mismatches and job transitions. To address this issue, this study proposes a job recommendation system that identifies suitable roles in the field of Data Science based on users' hard skills and job requirements. Data were collected through web scraping from JobStreet and Glints, covering 450 unique job postings across six categories: Data Scientist, Data Analyst, Data Engineer, Business Analyst, Machine Learning Engineer, and AI Engineer. A Content-Based Filtering (CBF) approach was implemented, utilizing multiple similarity and distance metrics-Cosine Similarity, Euclidean Distance, Manhattan Distance, and Haversine Distance-to compare user input with job profiles. The dataset underwent a series of preprocessing and feature engineering steps, including skill extraction, education and experience normalization, and encoding of categorical features. Seventeen combinations of similarity and distance metrics were tested and evaluated using Precision as the main performance metric. It was found that the best configuration in this study used Euclidean Distance for hard skills (weight =0.6), Manhattan Distance for other attributes (weight =0.2), and Haversine Distance for location (weight = 0.2), with a precision score of 0.6533. This result indicates that distance-based metrics tend to perform better than similaritybased ones, as they capture the differences between job profiles and user inputs more clearly. In general, the developed system can provide practical and personalized job recommendations, especially for users who want to explore Data Science-related careers based on their existing skills.

title: Image-to-Image-Based Wave Forecasting Using Pix2pix Approach, Case Study in Pacitan, Indonesia
authors: Hendri Bhirowo, Indra.A. Aditya, Didit Adytia, Andry Alamsyah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: Accurate wave forecasting is essential for supporting maritime operations, particularly in coastal regions where high waves often disrupt activities such as shipping, loading and unloading, and port accessibility. The southern coast of Java, including the waters around Pacitan, is especially vulnerable to high wave conditions influenced by wind seas and swells, posing challenges for operations such as coal transportation to PLTU Pacitan. To address these challenges, this study proposes a wave downscaling framework based on an image-to-image translation approach using the Pix2pix model. The model is trained on high-resolution SWAN simulations performed with a nested grid system, enabling the transformation of coarse-resolution global model outputs into high-resolution local forecasts. The experimental results demonstrate that the choice of grid resolution and training data length significantly affects model performance. Among the tested configurations, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> degree grid yielded the most accurate results, achieving the highest correlation coefficient, the lowest RMSE, superior CSI values, and the smallest FAR. Furthermore, extending the training dataset to 5 years provided the lowest RMSE and balanced predictive performance, despite only marginal differences in other evaluation metrics compared to shorter training periods. The findings confirm the effectiveness of the Pix2pix framework in enhancing coastal wave forecasting, offering improved accuracy and spatial representation in the Pacitan coastal domain. This study highlights the potential of image-to-image deep learning approaches in coastal oceanography and suggests that further research involving model tuning, hybrid architectures, or integration with additional physical constraints could further improve forecasting accuracy and reliability.

title: A Robust Access Control and Logging Mechanism for Sensitive Information Systems
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Srigitha S Nath, Ganapathy K, Salaudeen A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Sensitive data breaches and unauthorized document access continue to pose significant risks to modern organizations, leading to financial loss, regulatory penalties, and erosion of trust. To address these challenges, a secure web-based system for managing and monitoring access to sensitive data is presented in this work. The system is designed using JWT authentication and role-based access control to ensure that only authorized users are permitted to access protected documents. Encrypted credential storage is employed to enhance user authentication security, while fine-grained permission policies are enforced to prevent misuse of privileges. All file access attempts are continuously recorded through a comprehensive logging mechanism that captures detailed metadata, thereby ensuring complete traceability and accountability. Real-time alert generation is integrated to notify administrators of suspicious activities such as repeated unauthorized access attempts or abnormal download behavior. The performance of the proposed system is evaluated under multiple authorized and unauthorized access scenarios, focusing on access control accuracy, audit log completeness, alert detection efficiency, and system response time. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy in access enforcement, complete audit coverage, reliable threat detection, and low response latency under concurrent usage conditions. The proposed solution strengthens organizational data security, improves compliance readiness, and provides a scalable and reliable framework for safeguarding sensitive information in digital environments.

title: Comprehensive Solution and Implementation of a 4-Axis Selective Compliance Assembly Robot Arm (SCARA) for Automated Pick-and-Place of Integrated Circuit Components Onto Printed Circuit Boards
authors: A. Shaji George, Sampada Viraj Dravid, Pravin A. Dwaramwar, Syeda Meraj Bilfaqih, Jonnala Subba Reddy, T.C. Manjunath
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The SCARA robotic arm, recognized for its precise horizontal motion and reliable position control, serves as a flexible tool for assembly operations across numerous sectors, with particular relevance in the field of electronics production. The central aim of this work is to derive the forward kinematics equations necessary for accurately determining where the arm's end-effectors are located in how it was oriented, relying on their angles of its four joints. These equations are built by defining the Denavit-Hartenberg parameters and applying matrix transformations, which establish the relationships b/w their robotic arm's joints movements & their final positions & direction of their working tip. To validate this mathematical framework, the project proposes simulating and evaluating the predicted positions and orientations of the end-effector using both computational models and actual test data. The forward kinematics calculations will be carried out using original program code, enabling a thorough analysis of the robot's pick-and-place functions. Moreover, the paper briefly discusses broader applications and the impact of integrating SCARA robotic technology into industrial assembly tasks, emphasizing precise and efficient operations. A graphical user interface is also suggested, with the function of improving user interaction and supporting the live monitoring and control of pick-and-place activities. By systematically solving the direct kinematics problem for a 4-axis SCARA robot-in the specific context of placing integrated circuit components onto printed circuit boards-this study promotes advancements in automated assembly and underlines the potential of robotics to raise efficiency and accuracy in modern manufacturing environments.

title: A Decade of Progress: Machine Learning in Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using Imaging Data
authors: Zineb IDRISSI JANATI, Hakim EL FADILI
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper surveys machine learning advancements in breast cancer diagnosis from 2015 to 2025, focusing on ultrasound, mammography, histopathology, and MRI. It examines the transition from traditional feature-based models to deep learning, assessing their impact on accuracy, interpretability, and clinical adoption. Key challenges such as dataset limitations, model generalizability, and ethical concerns are discussed. By synthesizing a decade of progress, the study highlights emerging trends and future directions for improving ML-driven breast cancer diagnostics.

title: Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Algorithms in Predictive Maintenance Applications
authors: Ashish Kumar Sahu, Shilpi Nishant Tanwani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Predictive maintenance (PM) is an essential principle of modern industrial systems, which uses informationbased approaches to anticipate equipment breakdown much earlier than it may happen and, as a result, reduce downtime, maintenance costs, and safety concerns. Use of machine learning (ML) methods in PM has dramatically improved advanced sensor data analysis, fault identification, and exact forecasting of possible failure. In this work, an extensive performance comparison of some of the most commonly utilized machine learning algorithms applied in predictive maintenance is discussed, including Decision Trees (DT), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), and Gradient Boosting Machines (GBM). The study utilizes publicly available industrial system benchmark data sets, including vibration signals, temperature readings, and operating conditions, to determine the performance of algorithms. Evaluation metrics are accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, computation time, and model interpretability. The result confirms that ensemble-based classifier like RF and GBM are superior to traditional classifiers in accuracy and stability especially when imbalanced data and nonlinear relationships are involved. ANN is highly flexible to pick up complex features, and is extremely time consuming in terms of training, as well as data preprocessing. SVM and k -NN exhibit similar accuracy with low computational cost; however, these algorithms lack scalability and are unsuitable for real-time applications. It also examines the impact of feature engineering method and data imbalance correction procedures such as the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) on the effectiveness of the model. Also, it is mentioned that the choice of the algorithm must be problem-specific, and it depends on data size, sensor sampling rate, the necessity of prediction, and computing power. The findings reveal the importance of combination and integrative approaches that exploit the merits of a series of algorithms in improving predictability of PM systems. This comparative study provides practicing engineers, data scientists, and maintenance planners with hands-on experience in selecting the most suitable machine learning models for specific industrial applications. The future work is aimed at uniting real-time analytics, edge computing, and explainable AI to enhance predictive maintenance strategy by bringing more transparency and efficiency.

title: Sensor Fusion and AI-Based Depth Estimation for 3D Mapping on the LIMO ROS2 Robot
authors: Saad Mahmud, MengCheng Lau
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: 3D reconstruction, or mapping, plays a critical role in enabling autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) to perceive and navigate complex environments. Unlike traditional 2D methods, 3D mapping provides richer spatial context but requires the integration of multiple sensing modalities and computational models. This paper investigates the performance of 3D mapping on the LIMO ROS2 platform through sensor fusion techniques that combine LiDAR, camera, and wheel encoder data. The study compares two configurations: one using LiDAR, camera, and encoder readings, and another where LiDAR data is replaced with camera-generated point clouds. It also evaluates the quality of depth images obtained from the robot's camera against those generated by a deep learning model based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture. Experimental results demonstrate that replacing LiDAR with camera-derived point clouds leads to sparser reconstructions and missing environmental features. Furthermore, the AI-based depth estimation method produces more consistent and accurate depth representations than the onboard RGB-D camera. These findings highlight the importance of sensor fusion and AI-driven depth estimation to improve the reliability and fidelity of 3D mapping for autonomous robots.

title: Heart Disease Classification Using Tab Transformer: Attention-Enhanced Learning on Clinical Tabular Data
authors: Tamizhmalar D, R. Premkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Heart disease significantly contributes to global mortality, emphasizing the need for accurate, scalable, and interpretable clinical prediction systems. Traditional machine learning models often struggle to capture the complex inter-feature relationships vital for diagnosis. This study investigated the Tabtransformer-based deep learning architecture, to predict heart disease using the UCI Cleveland dataset. The model employs attention-based transformer encoder layers to create contextual embeddings for categorical features, enriching patient profile representations. After thorough preprocessing and training, the TabTransformer results an accuracy of 77.05%, an F1-score of 0.79, precision of 76.47%, recall of 81.25%, and a ROC-AUC score of 0.856. These results indicate competitive performance compared to recent ensemble and deep learning models, while maintaining manageable architectural complexity. The study highlights its suitability for static clinical datasets and recommends integrating Explainable AI (XAI) for better model visibility in healthcare.

title: Swift Spot Pay: Developing a Real-Time Userfriendly Location Based Parking Payments Web Application System
authors: Vijay Kumar Virigineni, Foster Yeboah, Jones Yeboah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This research introduces a web application that transforms the parking experience through real time locationbased pricing by developing parking zones using Leaflet, Geolocation API and also uses scalable MongoDB Atlas database to store user data. How can technology be leveraged to improve parking efficiency and reduce driver frustration? This system leverage on a technology based on an easy one-click payment and safe credit card transactions using Stripe API. This web application is simple for anyone, even those with limited tech skills, making parking management a breeze without any complicated steps. The application eliminates queueing at parking meters, providing a timesaving, queue free experience. Automatic time extensions further enhance convenience, reflecting a user-centric design that prioritizes efficiency, security, and smart technology integration. However, there are certain limitations like lack of real-time parking data and incorporating into current parking system. In summary, this web application not only addresses traditional parking challenges but also pioneers a more convenient and technologically advanced solution for parking.

title: Topos-Theoretic Framework for Verifiable Multi-Agent Coordination and Team Formation
authors: Manuel Hernández, Eduardo Sánchez-Soto
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: We introduce a formally verifiable framework for multi-agent coordination, grounded in the mathematical language of topos and sheaf theory. The proposal is to achieve robust and predictable collective behavior in complex, decentralized systems. The proposed framework models a multi-agent system as a cellular sheaf, where agents' local states and beliefs are represented as data on stalks, and inter-agent constraints are encoded in restriction maps. Information from the environment is assimilated by individual agents through the categorical operation of a pullback, creating a contextualized basis for reasoning. Deliberation is performed via constructive proof within the topos's internal intuitionistic logic, ensuring that all deductions are verifiable. These locally-derived “proof objects” are then synthesized into a globally consistent state of agreement-a global section of the sheaf-via distributed consensus dynamics. This approach offers significant advantages in formal verification, inherent support for agent heterogeneity, and explainability, paving the way for a new class of provably reliable autonomous systems.

title: Convenience Prediction based on Coffee Consumption Patterns using Machine Learning
authors: Tiari Juliani, Mindit Eriyadi, Bambang Pudjoatmodjo, Desy Puspa Rahayu, Nor Azuana Ramli, Agus Pratondo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: The increasing trend of coffee consumption among university students, driven by the need to manage academic pressure and maintain focus, has raised concerns regarding its impact on their physical well-being. While coffee is often used as a productivity aid, improper consumption patterns can lead to various health-related discomforts. This study aims to predict the perceived convenience or inconvenience of coffee consumption among university students using machine learning methods. In this context, convenience is defined as the absence of self-reported negative effects on comfort, while inconvenience indicates the presence of such effects. Data were collected from 200 Indonesian students, encompassing fourteen essential features including academic level, consumption frequency, and health complaints. Two machine learning algorithms, Random Forest and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), were implemented and evaluated using a 5-fold cross-validation method to ensure high predictive reliability. The experimental results reveal that the Random Forest model achieved a superior accuracy of 87%, slightly outperforming the XGBoost model which reached 80%. Feature importance analysis identified that health complaints were the most significant predictor, followed by academic stress and staying up late to study. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of machine learning in identifying behavioral patterns related to caffeine use. This research provides a data-driven foundation for developing wellness strategies to promote healthier lifestyle habits within the academic community.

title: Multi-Letter Analysis of Marginal Rate-Fisher Information Tradeoff: Unbounded Gain
authors: Yuan Xinjie, Tianren Peng, Haohao Fu, Shao-Lun Huang
publish: 2025 Asia Pacific Workshop on Data Science and Information Theory (APWDSIT)
abstract: This paper studies the marginal tradeoff between communication capacity and Fisher information in bistatic ISAC systems. Considering the non–Bayesian scenario where the sensing receiver lacks transmitted signal knowledge, it analyzes perturbations on communication–optimal input distributions. As blocklength <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex> grows, specific perturbations yield unbounded Fisher information gains (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(\sqrt{n})$</tex>) with negligible rate loss, revealing unique multi–letter advantages distinct from single-letter tradeoffs.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Attention-Based LSTM-Network With Residual Connection
authors: Daniil Krasnoproshin, Maxim Vashkevich
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: Speech emotion recognition is an impotant component of modern human-computer interaction systems. However, many state-of-the-art approaches rely on large pretrained models with high computational and memory requirements, limiting their applicability. This paper proposes ResLSTMSA, a lightweight architecture that integrates residual connections with soft attention within an LSTM-based framework. Evaluated on the RAVDESS dataset under strict speakerindependent partitioning, the proposed model outperforms conventional attention-based LSTM baselines and several previously reported CNN- and hybrid CNN-LSTM architectures in terms of unweighted average recall (UAR). The bestperforming variant (ResLSTM-SA-h64) achieves a maximum UAR of 0.6517 with only 46.8 k trainable parameters, delivering competitive accuracy with three orders of magnitude fewer parameters than large-scale self-supervised alternatives, thereby enabling efficient deployment on edge devices and real-time voice assistants.

title: Reevaluating Churn Labeling in E-Commerce: Empirical Evidence for the Effectiveness of Personalized Dynamic Approaches
authors: Gia-Bach Vo, Quoc-Khang Nguyen, Son-Ha Van, Duc-Ngo Van
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In today's data-driven retail and e-commerce landscape, business decisions must rely on analytical insights rather than intuition or past experience. Customer data reveal behavioral patterns and portfolio characteristics that enable firms to optimize revenue and reduce unnecessary costs. Accurately predicting churn is key to enhancing Customer Lifetime Value (CLV), as maintaining long-term relationships with current customers yields substantially higher returns than continuously pursuing new acquisitions. However, most existing studies emphasize model development while overlooking the critical prelabeling phase. Public datasets often lack churn labels or contain homogeneous labeling, and in real business contexts, raw data rarely include explicit churn indicators. Consequently, prelabeling plays a decisive yet underexplored role in determining model performance. This study conducts a systematic review of pre-labeling practices within the customer churn prediction literature, identifying the primary methodologies employed in previous research and their inherent limitations. Based on this analysis, the widely adopted 90-day static threshold method is selected as a representative baseline for comparison. Building upon these insights, the paper introduces the eCommerce Personalized Dynamic Churn Labeling (ePDCL) method, a flexible and behaviorally grounded pre-labeling framework. Experiments on the Brazilian E-Commerce Public Dataset by Olist validate the methodological advantages of ePDCL across multiple evaluation dimensions, highlighting its potential for practical deployment in real-world business environments.

title: Detection and Identification of Poisonous Devil Reef Crab (Zosimus Aeneus) using Deep CNN
authors: Alysa Ashley B. Manoguid, Meo Vincent C. Caya, Jocelyn F. Villaverde
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: This study presents a prototype device for detection and identification of poisonous devil reef crab (Zosimus aeneus) using deep CNN. The development of the device includes both the development of hardware and software component. The hardware component was used to collect and gather training data, while the software was used for the prediction. The study utilized a total of 400 training images in which for each class there were 200 training images. For model development, the study utilized the pre-trained VGG-16 CNN architecture. Transfer learning and fine-tuning were applied to ensure that the developed model could be effectively adapted for the new classification task. 25 testing samples for each class were used to test and evaluate the performance of the developed prototype. The result shows that the device yielded an accuracy rate of 94%. The results of the prototype testing and evaluation indicate that its performance is relatively satisfactory and suitable for the classification of crabs.

title: Robust Vehicle Classification, Counting, and Recording Using Automated Synthetic Dataset
authors: Leonard U. Ambata, Elmer Jose P. Dadios
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Traffic congestion and vehicle-related crimes are persistent issues in metropolitan areas of the Philippines. This paper presents the development and evaluation of a universal, robust vehicle identification system utilizing image processing and deep learning-specifically the YOLOv12 object detection model combined with DeepSORT tracking—to automatically detect, classify, and recognize vehicles from CCTV footage. The system operates across varied locations and conditions, providing vehicle counts, vehicle type and color classification. An initial synthetic dataset was used to pre-train the model, reducing manual annotation effort, followed by iterative fine-tuning with real-world video data. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves high accuracy in vehicle detection and classification (exceeding 90% after fine-tuning). The integrated color recognition and data recording features distinguish this work from prior studies.

title: Exploring Quantum-Inspired Control Strategies for Robotic Manipulation: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Mohamed Salah Dahassa, Mehdi Fazilat, Nadjet Zioui
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study evaluates quantum-inspired control strategies on classical hardware, specifically Quantum Sliding Mode Control (QSMC), Quantum Genetic Algorithm (QGA), and a Grover-based quantum search controller, to improve control of a six-degree-of-freedom robotic arm with nonlinear dynamics. Building upon quantum-analogous probabilistic principles, these methods offer significant potential across industries. To clarify the basis of these approaches, the analysis centers on quantum-computation concepts, such as probabilistic amplitude encoding and superposition, which can enhance robustness, trajectory smoothness, and energy efficiency in classical control systems. Consistent, noise-free simulation conditions and multiple independent trials ensured fairness and reproducibility throughout the study. Performance assessment relied on Integrated Absolute Error (IAE), which measures total error over time, and Average Control Energy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{E}_{\text{av}} \mathrm{g}$</tex>) for mean energy consumption. Compared with classical Sliding Mode Control (SMC), QSMC achieved an 11 % faster transient response. Additionally, QGA delivered 21 % lower IAE and 12.3 % lower average control energy, while the Grover-based controller provided balanced performance among convergence speed, tracking accuracy, and energy efficiency. Overall, these results suggest that quantum-inspired approaches can significantly advance trajectory precision, robustness, and energy efficiency, supporting the development of nextgeneration intelligent robotics.

title: AIDAS: An AI Dashboard for Accreditation and Standards in Higher Education
authors: Mohammed Gollapalli, Victor Viray, Fowzia Akhter, Md Badiuzzaman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: With the increasing demand for evidence-informed accreditation in higher education, institutions face significant challenges in collecting, analysing, and reporting student performance data in line with professional and regulatory standards. In Australia, Information and Communication Technology (ICT) programs accredited by the Australian Computer Society (ACS) must demonstrate coherent mapping across the Core Body of Knowledge (CBoK), the Skills Framework for the Information Age (SFIA), and the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF). Managing these multi-layered mappings from assessment rubrics to unit learning outcomes (ULOs), course learning outcomes (CLOs), graduate attributes (GAs), and professional frameworks remains a labour-intensive and error-prone process. This study introduces AIDAS (AI Dashboard for Accreditation and Standards), a design-oriented architecture intended to streamline the orchestration, visualisation, and analysis of rubric-level performance data in higher education. Informed by a structured review of existing literature and co-designed with an institutional accreditation working group, AIDAS addresses limitations of spreadsheet-based and fragmented systems. The platform integrates rule-based logic and AI-assisted analytics to support ongoing improvement of curriculum by identifying learning gaps, generating accreditation-ready reports, and providing diagnostic insights into prerequisite knowledge requirements. AIDAS is being developed as a staged prototype, with the potential to reduce faculty workload, enhance student learning visibility, and strengthen institutional readiness for maintaining accreditation and standards.

title: Measurement Signal Processing for Multi-Sensors Data Estimation: New Data
authors: Yeldos A. Altay, Abu-Alim E. Ayazbay, Raisa K. Uskenbayeva, Zhuldyz B. Kalpeyeva, Alexey V. Fedorov, Aizhan M. Anartayeva
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This article presents the results of processing measurement signals obtained at the output of a multi-sensor water quality monitoring system. Signal processing was implemented using a developed mathematical model designed for evaluating multi-parameter data. It was established that the developed model is feasible and allows for the identification of intersystem associative relationships between data from a multi-parameter water quality monitoring system. For the first time, positive and significant associative relationships were identified, characterizing processes unique to the aquatic environment. The developed mathematical model can be widely applied to processing measurement signals from a multi-sensor water quality monitoring system.

title: Smart Track Surveillance for Railway Safety Using Computer Vision
authors: Sherin Mithushiya P, Vibin Sri R, Shanker Shalini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: This research proposes integrated smart railway surveillance system to improve the road safety and avoid railway accidents by auto-detection of the defects on the railway tracks and obstacle surveillance. The architecture of this system comprises of dual model architecture using the YOLO based deep learning networks, customized YOLOv11 network for the detection of rail track defects and a YOLOv11n network for critical obstacles such as pedestrian, vehicle, etc., on or near the track. The framework offers both manual uploading of images as well as real-time simulation using random batch image selection and processing of images for continuous monitoring. An intelligent alert mechanism is used to aggregate the results of detections, calculate the severity levels and trigger automatically mail notification with annotated evidence for fast decisionmaking. Additional modules added for validation of images, images resizing, result logging and scheduled cleanup to make operational robust. Experimental evaluation introduces reliable identification of defects and categorising of obstacles with high responsiveness, so as to be able to confirm the suitability of the system for proactive railway safety management as well as its scalability to be deployed into real-world environments.

title: Application of Optimal Power Flow (OPF) Method for Power Generation Optimization at Nusantara Power Indramayu Steam Power Plant Based on IEEE 9-Bus
authors: Muhammad Syamsiar, Tasma Sucita
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The application of the Optimal Power Flow (OPF) method in this study aims to evaluate OPF performance in optimizing the power system of Indramayu steam power plant based on the IEEE 9-Bus network. Through generation cost optimization, the study integrates the IEEE 9-Bus standard network without disregarding the operational constraints of the steam power plant, which serve as the constraints in the OPF system. A quantitative simulation-based method was employed using MATLAB software and an OPF program that applies the Newton-Raphson algorithm in its computation. Two simulations were conducted: the first was based on the load demand of the IEEE 9-Bus standard, while the second was based on the maximum load demand that could be simulated by the modeled cost function. The findings indicate that generation costs were reduced—by 0.32% from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$149.27 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$148.79 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> in the first simulation, and by 0.0052% from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$189.11 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$189.10 \$ / \mathrm{h}$</tex> in the second—accompanied by a reduction in the total apparent power of the generators, from 342.76 MVA to 328.83 MVA in the first simulation and from 427.86 MVA to 362 MVA in the second. The optimization was computed with high program accuracy, achieving NewtonRaphson convergence values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2, 1 6 1 3} \times 10^{-5}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3, 5 0 0 1} \times 10^{-6}$</tex> in the first and second simulations, respectively. These results conclude that OPF performs effectively in optimizing the system, as shown by cost savings in generation, generation outputs remaining within operational limits, and very small convergence values in the computation.

title: 5G Network Planning Analysis on 2300 MHz: A Case Study of Indonesia's New Capital City
authors: Putri Rahmawati, Muhammad Adam Nugraha
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Indonesia's New Capital City (Nusantara) development provides an opportunity for greenfield <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> infrastructure deployment. This study analyses coverage planning requirements for 5 G New Radio (NR) at 2300 MHz in the Central Government Core Area (KIPP), covering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$66.71 ~\text{km}^{2}$</tex> and projected to serve 488,409 residents. We apply morphological classification, link-budget analysis, and 3 GPP TR 38.901 Urban Macro (UMa) propagation modelling to uplink and downlink scenarios. Population density analysis (7,321 people/ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex>) classified KIPP as an urban environment, enabling the application of the UMa model. Coverage planning calculations determined that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 4}$</tex> gNodeB sites are required for comprehensive Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) coverage. Atoll simulation validation yields an average SS-RSRP of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 6 1. 9 1 ~ d B m}$</tex> (in the Very Good category), an SS-SINR of 21.66 dB (in the Good category), and a throughput of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 5. 4 3 ~ M b p s}$</tex>. Coverage rates of 98.50% for signal strength, 92.80% for signal quality, and 96.75% for throughput demonstrate the feasibility of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> networks in urban-forest transition environments, providing practical insights for infrastructure planning in Indonesia's new capital development.

title: Adaptive Loop Filter for Point Cloud Compression
authors: Alexander Alshin, Stanislav Efremov
publish: 2026 28th International Conference on Digital Signal Processing and its Applications (DSPA)
abstract: This paper introduces a point cloud coding tool called the Region based Adaptive Loop filter (RALF). The goal of this filter is to improve the visual quality and compression efficiency of point cloud attribute coding. It does this by adaptively reducing artifacts based on local content, and enhancing reference frames to improve subsequent prediction and coding performance.

title: Research on Concrete Crack Detection Algorithm Based on Yolov8
authors: Junkang Wang, Qiang Jiang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Aiming at the current low efficiency of concrete crack detection and the problem of missed detection and misdetection that is prone to occur in the detection of small micro-cracks, a YOLOv8 algorithm, called YOLOv8-MNCE for short, is proposed, which combines MobileNet V3 module, CA(Coordinate Attention) attention mechanism and EIoU(Enhanced Intersection over Union Loss) loss function. Firstly, the backbone network is optimized using MobileNetV3, which reduces the number of parameters of the network and the amount of computation, improves the detection efficiency and realizes the lightweight; at the same time, an attention mechanism is introduced to enhance the feature extraction capability of the micro-cracks. Finally, the EIoU loss function is applied to solve the problem that the traditional loss function cannot fully capture the relative position and size difference between targets. The experimental results show that compared with the original model algorithm, the average pixel accuracy of YOLOv8-MNCE algorithm reaches 81.80% for mAP0.5 and 63.11% for mAP0.5-0.95, which is improved by 3.58% and 2.3%, respectively, and the size of the model parameter is 2.36 M, and the FPS reaches 92.34, while the GFLOPs is 5.7. It has the advantages of high accuracy, advantages of small computation and fast detection speed, it provides an effective solution for concrete crack detection.

title: Automated Academic Planning Using Machine Learning for College Students
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Srigitha S Nath, Vanitha M, Vishal S, Vijay Krishna M, Rajalingam S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: A personalised university guidance gadget named Student Ripples has been advanced to deal with the demanding situations college students face in making academic and career choices. The system has been designed as an internet-based totally platform that generates individualized educational roadmaps to help students in making plans their educational adventure effectively. A complete-stack architecture the usage of Python with the Django framework and React for the frontend has been applied to make certain scalability and responsiveness. Machine learning concepts were incorporated conceptually to allow destiny integration of a hybrid advice engine capable of suggesting internships, tasks, and optionally available guides primarily based on student statistics. The machine has been established to provide a visible roadmap where milestones constitute academic and expert development tiers. Through interactive features, college students are allowed to song their progress and get right of entry to relevant studying assets. The prototype has been evaluated as a useful proof of concept that promotes self-guided learning and informed selection-making. By applying records-pushed methodologies, the device has been meant to bridge the space among educational making plans and career education. The proposed method demonstrates how personalised digital tools may be applied to decorate scholar engagement, reduce academic uncertainty, and create a structured framework for non-stop academic and professional growth in better training.

title: Design and Implementation of YOLOv8-Based Object Defact Detection on PCB with Graphical User Interface for Automated Optical Inspection
authors: Jaja Kustija, Resa Pramudita, Ibnu Hartopo, Ghayrie Habsy Alghifary, Permana Sidiq Ginanjar, Wendi Rachyenda Rachman, Muhammad Naufal Al Ghazali, Diki Fahrizal
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Printed Circuit Boards (PCB) are essential components in electronic devices, where surface defects such as missing hole, mouse bite, open circuit, short, spur, and spurious copper can significantly affect product reliability and manufacturing quality. Conventional inspection methods, relying on manual visual inspection or rule-based image processing, often face limitations in accuracy, consistency, and scalability. To address this challenge, this study proposes a defect detection system based on the YOLOv8 deep learning algorithm integrated into a Graphical User Interface (GUI) for Automated Optical Inspection (AOI). The dataset used in this research consists of 693 PCB images augmented to 1,871 samples with 7,968 annotated defect instances. Five YOLOv8 variants (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n, s, m, 1, x$</tex>) were trained and evaluated using standard object detection metrics. The results show that YOLOv8x achieved the best performance, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m A P {@} 50$</tex> of 0.962, mAP@50:95 of 0.503, precision of 0.953, recall of 0.945, and F1-score of 0.949. Based on these results, YOLOv8x was selected as the baseline model and integrated into the GUI. The system achieved an average processing time of 9.3 seconds per image (7.2s inference + 2.1s post-process), providing accurate and user-friendly defect visualization suitable for semi real-time PCB inspection. This research demonstrates the potential of combining state-of-the-art object detection with practical user interfaces to enhance electronic manufacturing quality control. Future work will focus on optimizing inference speed through lightweight YOLOv8 variants and extending the GUI for realtime industrial AOI applications.

title: Performance Analysis and Security Evaluation of RFC 9783 PSA Attestation Tokens in Resource-Constrained IoT Environments
authors: Hambardzum Minasyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has introduced significant security and resiliency conformance challenges, particularly when working towards implementing continuous Software Bill of Material (SBOM) and Supply Chain Risk Management measures, as well as Zero Trust Network Access management systems and Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) principles. RFC 9783, published in June 2025, specifies the Platform Security Architecture (PSA) Attestation Token as a standardized framework for hardware- rooted device attestation. This paper presents a comprehensive performance analysis and security evaluation of RFC 9783 PSA attestation tokens implemented on resource-constrained IoT devices. We evaluate the computational overhead, memory footprint, and network transmission costs of PSA token generation and verification across multiple hardware platforms including ARM CortexM series microcontrollers. Our experimental results demonstrate that PSA attestation introduces an average overhead of 23.4 ms for token generation and 18.7 ms for verification on ARM Cortex-M33 devices, with a memory footprint of 4.2 KB for token processing. Security analysis reveals that PSA tokens provide robust protection against replay attacks, man-in-the-middle attacks, and device cloning while maintaining acceptable performance for real- time IoT applications. We identify optimization strategies that reduce attestation overhead by 31 % through caching mechanisms and propose enhancements to the privacy- preserving capabilities of the PSA framework.

title: A PMO-SAC Method for Balancing and Path Tracking Control of a Two-Wheeled Vehicle
authors: Haojie Li, Yingzi Wei
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: This paper deals with the problems of non-linear couplings and performance conflicts when controlling two wheeled vehicles for the purpose of balancing as well as precisely following complicated paths. We construct a deep reinforcement learning framework that integrates transfer learning and curriculum learning to solve this problem. A Phased Multi-Objective Soft Actor-Critic (PMO-SAC) method is proposed. The method begins with a pre-training stage, where the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm is applied to a simplified dynamic balancing task. The learned network weights are then partially transferred to the path tracking agent to accelerate initial learning. Following this, a phased, progressive curriculum learning is conducted, which dynamically adjusts the reward function and environmental constraints to enable the vehicle to acquire the complex skill of continuous directional changes. The complete PMO-SAC framework is thus constituted by transferring the policy from the balancing task to the main tracking network. Experimental validation was performed in the PyBullet simulation environment using a digital physics model of the vehicle. Compared to a baseline using only Soft Actor-Critic with Curriculum Learning (CL-SAC), the PMO-SAC algorithm reduces the number of episodes required for convergence by approximately 600. The results confirm that curriculum learning is crucial for acquiring an effective tracking policy. Our proposed framework delivered a substantial performance leap. When compared with a Curriculum Learning-Soft Actor-Critic (CL-SAC) benchmark, the PMO-SAC algorithm shortened the convergence period by roughly 600 episodes. Our results show that curriculum learning is necessary to get an effective policy. More impressively, when the standard SAC algorithm fails completely, our method improves the average task success rate by more than 40 percentage points. This result shows that using curriculum and transfer learning inside SAC is good way to make a control policy that does well at being stable and precise at the same time.

title: Graphormer Integrated with Swin Transformer for Demand Forecasting and Portfolio Optimization
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Yarlagadda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of demand and the balanced optimization of the collection are key aspects of resource planning and financial decision-making. Conventional forecasting often faces problems in managing the complex spatial and temporal necessities between market states. Usual time series and shallow neural network methods often fail to capture the complex interdependence between unstable and asset market signals, resulting in volatile collections and less reliable forecast returns. To handle these issues, this study proposes a hybrid design integrating the Graphormer (GH) and Swin transformer (ST) protocol for best prediction. The GH factors capture the relationship between several financial instruments by using a chart-based work to help the protocol understand the inter-relationships and global correlations between assets. Meanwhile, the ST processes the sequence and visual depictions of financial data using a shifted focus of the window, which allows for an efficient extraction of temporal and structural features. The combination of these algorithms creates a balanced representation of decision-making tasks and supports adaptive learning of both graph and sequence patterns with datasets. The testbeds were made using asset classes and time intervals-based datasets. The proposed work was tested using mean absolute error, slope ratio, root mean square error, maximum drawdown and turnover and these results showed stable collection returns and higher forecasting accuracy than to statistical and Deep Learning algorithms.

title: Utility Based Explainable AI for Heart Disease Diagnosis: A Comparative Study of CIU, SHAP and LIME
authors: Aadyoth Sreeram, Manasvi Bhargava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Heart disease remains a leading cause of death globally, often due to improper or delayed diagnosis. This creates a need for systems of diagnosis which are accurate, reliable, and interpretable. There is a tradeoff between performance and explainability. Most deep learning models developed and deployed are high performing and accurate, but their clinical adoption is limited due to opacity. Most existing methods of explainability are unable to assess the clinical relevance or utility of model predictions, which diminishes the interpretability of the model at its most crucial in healthcare. This work presents a decision-theoretic explainability framework using contextual importance and utility (CIU) applied to deep learning models for heart disease prediction. CIU quantifies feature importance and utility in patient-specific predictions, bridging the gap between model output and clinical reasoning. To check the reliability of CIU, we benchmark CIU explanations against Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) and Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) on both local and global interpretability metrics, using a supervised training setup. The results show that CIU maintains high fidelity to model decisions while offering more interpretable and clinically aligned explanations. This study provides a reproducible path toward transparent Artificial Intelligence (AI) diagnostics and can be extended to other critical domains in healthcare.

title: Efficient LiDAR Object Detection with DBSCAN and L-Shape Fitting
authors: Alejandro Toro-Ossaba, Santiago Velasquez, Daniel Sanin-Villa, Juan C. Tejada
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents a lidar object detection algorithm that integrates density-based clustering with geometric fitting. The proposed algorithm employs DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise) to partition lidar point clouds into clusters, followed by an efficient L-shape fitting procedure to approximate each cluster with a bounding rectangle and estimate its pose. The algorithm was implemented in ROS2 and evaluated in a simulation environment under multiple experimental conditions, including static and dynamic objects as well as open and closed spaces. Results show that the method effectively clusters objects of varying geometries, provides accurate l-shape fitting, and achieves reliable pose estimation. While this work primarily focused on static scenarios, the algorithm also demonstrated feasibility for dynamic object detection, making it a promising foundation for future research.

title: DeepTrustChain: A Blockchain-Anchored Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for High-Integrity Fake News Detection
authors: D. Sravanthi, O. Shanmukha Hari Prasad, J. Srinath Reddy, A. Chiru Vardhan Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The uncontrolled propagation of fake news on the digital frontiers continues to undermine the confidence of the populace, disorganizing supply chains and misled decision making on high stake regions. The existing fake news detectors are all largely text-based and are driven by machine learning or deep learning and do not pay much focus to integrity, provenance, and post-hoc verifiability of the model predictions. Moreover, the current procedures are usually trained and tested in controlled settings and do not facilitate adversarial manipulation of content and metadata, and are rarely provided to produce audit trails which cannot be modified by auditors of identified artifacts. It is in this respect that this paper introduces DeepTrustChain, a blockchain anchored hybrid deep learning framework in the detection of high-integrity fake news. The proposed system uses an encoder of text a transformer encoder with a Bidirectional recurrent module and an ensemble stacking layer to improve the robustness and generalization owing to the recent discoveries on stacking and ensemble methods of identifying fake news, and reliability-based methods of evaluation. At the same time, DeepTrustChain computes a trust score that represents a combination of model confidence and source level and propagation level features and anchors the prediction and proof on an authorized blockchain. Such a design provides verifiable, immutable records of the histories of the detection results and enables the decentralized checking of the news items by time. The conceptual evaluation of the framework in respect to multilingual and low resource states and can also leverage advancements in sequence modeling and representation learning in other applications such as music and affective computing. The key feature of DeepTrustChain is that it is an architecture that integrates integrity conscious hybrid deep learning with blockchain based anchoring, so that simultaneously achieves the high degree of detection accuracy, resistance to decision manipulation, and ultimate traceability of decision making regarding fake news.

title: Synthetic Data Augmentation for Robust Solar Flare Classification: A Comparative Analysis of Conditional DCGAN, VAE, and Diffusion Models
authors: Swetha S, Gladis Sam Prakash, Jestin Thomas, Kalpana P, Teena Jose, Sumathi P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Solar flares are extremely dangerous to the ground and space-based resources. Solar flares have to be classified properly and in good time to offer protection to assets in both the environments. Deep Learning-based flares have been divided into 3 classes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex>). The main problems with this kind of classification are that high impact M- or X-class solar flares are extremely rare, and cannot be well sampled, thus leading to a very unbalanced sample. This paper exemplifies a comparative analysis of three models of Conditional Generative Models (cDCGAN models, cVAE models and cDDPM) to produce realistic images of solar flares considering each of the low frequency and high-impact solar flare types. The research question will be how such models can be evaluated in terms of their capacity to create realistic, class-specific images (magnetograms, and EUV) and time-series data which could be used to create class-balanced training samples. The initial experiments make use of the cDCGAN, cVAE, cDDPM architecture and considering the generation of class-conditional solar flare images portray high levels of stability (convergence was stated in less than 600 epochs in the case of the cDCGAN, cVAE models and cDDPM) and the generation of images which could be considered as practically indistinguishable to real life images. The results indicate that cDDPM may be a possible solution to a high-fidelity production of solar features. We measured the efficiency of these models in quantitative terms (popular metrics, like the Fréchet Inception Distance, Structural Similarity Index) in a manner that we could determine the best manner of training model based solar flare classification systems using realistic data. This research is aligned with Sustainability Development Goals 9- Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure with focus on verticals 9.1 and 9.5.

title: Automated Threat Prediction with Rapid Pattern Verification in Continuous Deployment Systems Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ankur Mahida
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The contemporary software development processes are becoming more based on Continuous Deployment (CD) pipelines, in which the code changes are detected, merged, tested, and launched automatically. Traditional rule-based or signaturebased threat detection approaches cannot match the dynamic deployment environment. In this paper, a proposal is presented to develop an Automated Threat Prediction with Rapid Pattern Verification (ATP-RPV) system utilizing Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and a two-stage verification system to provide security assurance in real time. The DRA agent recognizes behavioral patterns based on deployment streams and system logs continuously to predict anomalous behavior, whereas the Rapid Pattern Verification (RPV) unit conducts similar scans on suspicious event occurrences at high speed against available threat metadata to approve or disapprove suspicious events. Experimental test outcomes done on benchmark deployment datasets show that ATP-RPV will have 92 % detection, 4 % false positive and 550 events/sec throughput, which is better than the state-of-the-art models like DeepAir and GTAC-IRS. The innovativeness of the framework is the hybrid optimizer reward and two stage verifying pipeline that allow adaptive decisionmaking with low latency and reduced computing cost. Generally, ATP-RPV provides resilience to security in DevSecOps environments through predictive, automated and real-time mitigation of threat.

title: Aquatic Health Surveillance: Improved Early Detection of Infected Fish Using YOLOv12
authors: Nazih Aloui, Rim Kilani, Ahmed Zouinkhi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: In densely populated aquaculture cages, real-time detection and recognition of fish skin lesions can effectively prevent large-scale outbreaks of fish diseases, reducing fish mortality and economic losses. Despite the previous versions of YOLO, from v3 to v11, are effective in detecting classic objects, but they have limitations in accuracy and sensitivity when it comes to identifying early signs of infection in fish, which are often discreet and not very visible, especially in complex aquatic environments so we will focus on using in our paper YOLOv12 which represents a major advancement in real-time object detection, combining an optimized architecture with enhanced attention mechanisms to deliver superior accuracy and speed, making it a scalable and efficient solution for diverse applications such as fish diseases early detection across various hardware platforms.
