Evaluation on presentation videos demonstrates 92-100% segment extraction accuracy with processing speeds of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.556 \times$</tex> real-time. The current implementation targets single-speaker, frontal-face recordings with controlled audio quality. This enables large-scale investigation of differences in audiovisual integration, which is particularly relevant to research on autism spectrum disorder, where altered temporal binding may characterize the condition.

title: Deep CNN vs. Transfer Learning: A Comparative Study on Multi-Script Identification
authors: Abdeljalil Gattal, Yahia Menassel, Faycel Abbas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This research presents a comparative analysis of deep learning models for script identification in word images. We investigate the efficacy of a purpose-built Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), “Modified YafNet,” trained from scratch, versus conventional transfer learning methodologies. The models were benchmarked using the ICDAR 2021 Script Identification in the Wild (SIW) dataset, which features a mix of handwritten and printed scripts. Our findings indicate that the Modified YafNet architecture achieves superior performance metrics, including high classification accuracy and ROC AUC scores. The study also demonstrates that performance is significantly enhanced through data augmentation and the inclusion of external data, highlighting the model's robustness for deployment in OCR and document processing pipelines.

title: Scientific Software in the Age of Vibe Coding
authors: Gabrielle O’Brien, Nasir U. Eisty
abstract: Large language model-based tools are rapidly becoming part of everyday scientific programming. Here, we discuss converging findings from surveys and focus groups of scientific programmers about usage trends, tool choices, and emerging concerns. We argue that rising adoption will strain existing infrastructure for ensuring the rigor and scientific validity of scientific software, and discuss approaches to meeting this urgent challenge.

title: Mamdani Fuzzy Logic-Based Speed Control for Coffee Bean Drum Dryers
authors: Ika Noer Syamsiana, Taufikhur Rohman, Rahma Nur Amalia, Arwin Datumaya Wahyudi Sumari, Nihayatun Nafisah, Harrij Mukti K
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: The moisture content of coffee beans produced from the drying process using a drum dryer-based rotary system is highly dependent on the rotating speed of the drum and the time in the drying process. The drum rotation speed also affects the integrity of the coffee beans. In this study, a drum speed control mechanism was designed to maintain the moisture content of the coffee beans according to the standard, which is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11-12.5 {\%}$</tex>. The drum speed control mechanism is designed using Fuzzy Logic Control (FLC) with Mamdani method to automatically adjust the speed of the motor based on the temperature inside the drum and the drying time measured in real-time. The results of the experiments show that the combination of the motor rotation speed of 25 RPM and the drying time of 90 minutes can produce coffee beans with a moisture content of 12%, which is the optimal number according to the Indonesian National Standard (SNI). On the other hand, the use of FLC can increase the efficiency in maintaining temperature stability compared to manual methods, thus reducing energy consumption by up to 15%. From these results, it is concluded that FLC Mamdani can be recommended as a reliable, effective, and efficient solution for drum dryer-based coffee bean drying systems and can be further scaled up for industrial-scale applications, especially for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).

title: Optimization of a Palmprint Recognition System Based on 3D Ultrasound Images Captured with Compound Plane Wave Imaging Techniques
authors: Antonio Iula, Alessandro Ramalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Palm-related biometric traits such as palmprints and vein patterns have been primarily studied using optical and infrared images. These approaches are low-cost and contactless but are restricted to 2D surface information. Conversely, ultrasound imaging provides 3D structural data, inherently detects liveness, and is immune to environmental conditions such as lighting and humidity. This study employs Compound Plane Wave Imaging (CPWI) for fast 3D acquisition of palmprints and palm veins to achieve enhanced recognition and shorter scanning times. Optimal contrast and resolution were obtained by transmitting 17 linearly steered plane waves. The aim of this work is to compare recognition performances of this system with those achieved with conventional phased array imaging techniques. Verification experiments carried out using both 2D and 3D templates have shown recognition accuracy at least comparable with that reported in previous works. The advantages of a shorter acquisition time achievable with the CPWI technique are finally highlighted and discussed.

title: A Comparative Note on Q-Learning and Expected Sarsa for Inventory Control
authors: I. Peña-Arenas, M. Orozco-Rodriguez, J. C. Cifuentes-Taborda
publish: 2025 II International Congress of Engineering with a Social Sense (CIISS)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of two reinforcement learning (RL) techniques, Q-learning and Expected Sarsa, applied to inventory control under both stationary and nonstationary demand environments. In industries where inventory management faces high levels of uncertainty and probabilistic fluctuations, traditional methods can be suboptimal. While RL techniques have been employed in various fields, no prior study has directly compared the performance of Q-learning and Expected Sarsa in the context of inventory control. This paper aims to fill that gap by evaluating these two methods' efficiency and adaptability across different demand scenarios. The results provide actionable insights and tools for businesses seeking to improve inventory control through advanced learning techniques, offering a robust framework that outperforms conventional approaches in highly stochastic environments. By understanding the comparative strengths and weaknesses of Q-learning and Expected Sarsa, decision-makers can better implement reinforcement learning solutions tailored to their operational needs.

title: MAS-LLaVA: Motion-Aware Adaptive Sampling for Training-Free Video Large Language Models
authors: Jialin Tang, Yu Bai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Recent advances in video large language models (VLLMs) have enabled strong zero-shot reasoning, yet most systems still rely on uniform frame sampling and fragile GPU execution pipelines. We propose MAS-LLaVA, a training-free enhancement to LLaVA that introduces uniform frame sampling, adaptive importance-based token sampling, and deviceconsistent inference. First, uniform frame sampling ensures balanced temporal coverage by selecting equidistant frames, maintaining compatibility with existing aggregation strategies. Second, an adaptive token selection module computes the feature magnitude and diversity of patch tokens, assigning probabilistic importance scores and sampling a compact subset of visual tokens under a fixed token budget. Third, a device-aware execution pipeline ensures that all intermediate tensors inherit the input frame's device and data type, allowing uniform and adaptive sampling strategies to run reliably across heterogeneous GPUs. Experiments on NExT-QA and IntentQA show that MAS-LLaVA improves accuracy and stability across diverse video inputs without any retraining. These findings demonstrate that smarter, training-free sampling and inference design can substantially improve both the practicality and robustness of VLLMs in realworld video-language understanding.

title: Detecting Virtual Currency-Based Money-Laundering Accounts in Online Social Networks
authors: Arrojula Shashank, Ganesh B Regulwar, Gajengi Sai Vardhan, Kanakati Gayathri, Sreenivasulu Gogula, Vivin Chandrra Paasam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Virtual currency has become integral to Online Social Networks (OSNs), supporting services such as gaming, online purchases, and currency exchange. However, its popularity has led to an increase in money-laundering activities, where attackers exploit networks of fake or compromised accounts to transfer and convert virtual currency illicitly. These activities not only cause substantial financial losses but also threaten the stability and trust of OSN ecosystems. To address this challenge, we analyze operational data from Tencent QQ, one of the largest OSNs, to study the behavioral characteristics of benign and malicious accounts. Our approach introduces a multi-feature detection framework that evaluates accounts based on three key dimensions: (i) spatial connectivity among accounts, (ii) transaction sequence patterns, and (iii) account viability metrics. These features are processed using an algorithm-based detection model that integrates behavioral analysis and anomaly detection techniques. Experimental evaluation on real-world datasets demonstrates that the proposed method achieves a detection accuracy of 94.2% and a false positive rate of only 0.97%, outperforming traditional rulebased systems. The results confirm the effectiveness and scalability of our approach for securing OSNs against virtual currency laundering.

title: Exploring the Transformation and Development Path of Teachers' Profession in the Era of Artificial Intelligence: A Quantitative Study Based on Dongguan, Guangdong Province
authors: Li Lu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study investigates how artificial intelligence reshapes teacher professional development, using Dongguan as a case study to construct a five-dimensional analytical framework encompassing “technology application, role transformation, competency alignment, human-machine collaboration, and career challenges.” Quantitative analysis of 723 valid questionnaires revealed that AI technology application is highly correlated with precision teaching practices (r=0.712). Vocational schools demonstrate significant leadership in VR/AR applications and industry-education integration. Teachers face challenges including role identity confusion, insufficient digital competencies, ethical risks, and uneven resource allocation. The study proposes transformation pathways involving role reshaping, tiered training, human-machine collaboration, and improved support systems. These recommendations are not only applicable to Dongguan but also provide valuable references for teacher team development in other manufacturing cities.

title: Forward Blockchain and AI-Based Smart System
authors: Halima Mhamdi, Ahmed Zouinkhi, Abdelkrim Mohamed Naceur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: In the age of digital transformation, smart (SS) systems have emerged as the foundation of modern cities, industries, and households, necessitating highly efficient, secure, and autonomous data management. The proliferation of connected objects (IoT) and the increasing complexity of automated decisions highlight the need for systems that not only manage information fast but also ensure their dependability and immutability. Two innovative technologies are emerging as key pillars in meeting this challenge: artificial intelligence (AI), which allows for real-time analysis, learning, and sophisticated decision-making, and Blockchain (BC), whose decentralized architecture provides decentralized, transparency and security for transaction and data management. Intelligent systems are becoming more selflearning and trustworthy as blockchain technology and artificial intelligence are integrated. They can operate autonomously and reliably in crucial environments such as smart grids, logistics, and connected health. In this context, this paper discusses the integration of BC and AI in smart systems. First, we present the fundamental characteristics of these two technologies. Next, we emphasize their importance and significance in various areas of intelligent systems.

title: High-Level Framework for AI-based Teaching and Learning in Construction Management
authors: Mohammed Mawlana, Gulbin Ozcan-Deniz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based teaching has great potential to transform the traditional education system and enhance students’ learning experience through more personalized learning. The potential of AI in improving construction management education has been promising; however, a broader interpretation and structured integration of AI is still needed. This study aims to utilize AI to develop a conceptual and systematic framework for online self-paced construction management courses. The proposed framework employs a Learning Management System (LMS) and multi-agent systems with AI to create a foundational database. Through continuous learner-data feedback loops, the framework is expected to generate adaptive learning pathways tailored to the individual needs of each student. Upon successful implementation of this framework, it is expected to effectively address the knowledge gap in construction management courses. Ultimately, the integration of AI-supported multi-agent systems could lead to more efficient and personalized construction management education, supporting both academic institutions and industry training programs.

title: A Review of Optical Communication and its Applications in IoT
authors: Gaurav Soni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Weather primarily impacts the performance of FSO lines, particularly absorption, scattering, and scintillation, which can significantly weaken the received signal. Carbon dioxide and water vapor are the major things that produce absorption; however, things like humidity and altitude can change how strong it is. These problems cause attenuation, which directly affects the system's availability and dependability. Diffractive optical components are crucial for improving FSO systems. They shape and route beams and include optical power array generators and connectivity holograms. This article is based on a fundamental review of FSO, DoE, the use of DoE in FSO, and the FSO application in IOT. This paper focuses on the function of DoE in shaping and routing optical beams to make links more reliable and scalable, unlike traditional assessments that simply look at channel modeling or weather attenuation. A comparative investigation of the behaviour of FSO connections under different atmospheric circumstances, attenuation that depends on wavelength, and the possibility of optical link adaptations for IoT networks that need cheap, high-bandwidth, and easily deployable connectivity are all part of the research focus. According to the literature, WoC has great potential as a foundation for the future generation of Internet of Things (IoT) networks since DoEs can greatly enhance system scalability and connection dependability. An innovative aspect of this study is the integration of FSO physical-layer design with DoE-based optical enhancement. This unifies the approach to creating high-capacity optical IoT networks that are both energy-efficient and new. The article also looks at how FSO systems might be modified for Internet of Things (IoT) applications, which would make it possible to have high-bandwidth, low-cost, and quickly deployable communication solutions.

title: Clinical Applications and Efficacy of Muscle Suit
authors: Tomoya Yoshida, Takuya Hashimoto, Hiroshi Kobayashi
publish: 2025 18th International Convention on Rehabilitation Engineering and Assistive Technology (i-CREATe)
abstract: Low back pain is a widespread occupational and global health problem. Therefore, exoskeleton-type lumbar support devices known as “Muscle Suits” have been developed, and new benefits have been discovered, including improved posture, increased hip joint range of motion, and core activation. It operates in two modes: Passive, providing moderate assistive force for long-term functional training, and Active, delivering stronger force for immediate posture correction. Nursing care home-based trials demonstrated improvements in sit-to-stand performance, balance, and gait. Muscle Suits have unprecedented functionality that allows it to be used by staff as well as the elderly, making it a tool that can be used widely in healthcare and rehabilitation.

title: IPO Performance Predictor: Using Pre-Listing Sentiment and Fundamentals
authors: R Janagi, Udhayagiri A, Varsith Pranav S S, S Saranya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The article is premised on a hybrid machine learning model predictive of the success of the First Day offering (IPO). The pre-listing sentiment in social media, financial blogs and news are mixed with the financial fundamentals of the companies to accomplish both the quantitative and the psychological. The results of the NLP generated text sentiment are combined with the normalized valuation and growth rates as basic company data. Machine learning models like Random Forests, Logistic Regression and Neural Networks can then be used to classify them into these multimodal properties. With this mixed method, at least 84 and 78 high and under performers can be identified respectively. This prediction capacity is factual and formalized decision assistance apparatus and by far, a lot improved to decision making that rests on crude guesses or financial models. It demonstrates that the framework is operating within a realistic setting of investment decision making specific in the emerging markets where IPO is more volatile.

title: Comparative Design of Vedic Multiplier Using CMOS GDI and SAPON-GDI Technology
authors: Sumalatha Madugula, Gandi Chinnari, K. Padma Vasavi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Multipliers are essential components in modern processors, as they directly impact performance parameters such as speed, power consumption, and efficiency. This work introduces an 8-bit Vedic multiplier designed using the Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) technique, targeting low-power VLSI applications. To improve computational speed, the Urdhva Tiryagbhyam (UT) sutra from Vedic mathematics is utilized. Compared to traditional CMOS logic, which occupies more area and consumes more power, the GDI approach offers reduced transistor usage and lower power dissipation. Additionally, the implementation is enhanced using the Stackly Arranged Low Power ON transistor (SAPON) method, which helps to minimize leakage currents and switching losses. Overall, the proposed design achieves notable reductions in power, delay, and area, making it highly suitable for portable devices and high-performance systems.

title: AirClick: A Smart Ring for Seamless Device Control
authors: Mohamed Asif I, Rohith G, Adlin Sheeba
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Mouse pointers just suck; they always needed to be used on flat surfaces. Touchpads ruin the wrist, wireless ones just out of the blue die within extremely critical situations. Some ring-type products offer a so-called solution, but they are either ugly or don't do all the things. Therefore, a Bluetooth- enabled smart ring that allows users to manipulate cursors with simple finger gestures. A low-energy MCU should be included that embeds IMU sensors with BLE for power efficiency and rapidity for gesture recognition. The experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system achieves a mean gesture recognition accuracy in excess of 95 percent with an end-to- end response latency below 50 ms ensuring smooth and real- time interaction. Uniquely, the ring recognizes contextual gestures that then elicit different actions attached to the currently active application to distinguish it from the others. Moreover, it works independently across nearly all major brands of PCs, laptops, etc., or smartphones. Careful hardware optimization has resulted in the design of much more compact and comfortable form factors with long-lasting battery life. This technology opens new paradigms in access solutions for mobile productivity and natural humancomputer interaction.

title: Epilepsy Simulation With AI-Driven Insights & Detection - EpiSimAiD
authors: Meenakshi, Hariharan, Kavyashree I Pattan, Renuka Devi M N, Praveen Kulkarni, P Naresh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Advanced Brain Wave Monitoring for Epilepsy aims to leverage cutting-edge EEG simulation and AI-based detection techniques to address critical health and safety concerns associated with seizure disorders. EpiSimAiD: Epilepsy Simulation with AI-Driven Insights & Detection aims to advance brainwave monitoring for epilepsy through EEG-based simulation and intelligent seizure detection. The system integrates real-time EEG analysis, AI-driven alert mechanisms, and confidence-based diagnostics to enable early seizure prediction and enhance patient safety. By combining healthcare collaboration, therapy engagement, and self-care awareness, the project promotes holistic epilepsy management. Extensive simulations demonstrate the system's potential to provide timely alerts, improving response to critical conditions. Beyond its technical innovation, EpiSimAiD reflects a humancentered commitment to leveraging technology for improved well-being and healthcare outcomes.

title: Determinants of User Trust and Intention to Use Food Delivery Apps in Vietnam: The Pivotal Role of AI Chatbots and E-Service Quality
authors: Dang Vo Gia Le, Tuan-Kiet H.V. Truong, Nam Hoang Dang
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: This study investigates four digital features ESecurity, E-Privacy, E-Payment, and AI Chatbot that influence Trust, which in turn impacts the intention to use a food delivery app. The study uses a convenient sampling method, collecting data from 340 food delivery app users, which is then analyzed using PLS-SEM. The analysis results show that ESecurity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.234)$</tex>, E-Payment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.124)$</tex>, E-Privacy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta$</tex> = 0.288), and AI Chatbot (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.347$</tex>) positively influence Trust. Trust positively predicts the Intention to use (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\beta}=\mathbf{0. 6 2 2}$</tex>), indicating its central role in users' adoption of food delivery apps. It is important to emphasize that AI chatbots have the greatest impact on trust, highlighting the crucial role of interactive AI technology in fostering user confidence. Practical implications suggest that app developers should focus on enhancing the user-friendliness and responsiveness of AI chatbot interfaces, while also strengthening security and privacy measures, to build trust and encourage the intention to use food delivery apps.

title: IPFS in Perspective Lightweight Positioning of Decentralized Storage Systems Toward Data Sovereignty
authors: Chou Yu-Kun, Chou Yu-Hsiu, Jia-Ning Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Decentralized storage plays a pivotal role in enabling verifiable sovereign data flows within AI and cloud native pipelines. While IPFS has gained broad adoption for content addressing and off chain data sharing, a coherent comparative view of its relative strengths and limitations versus alternative systems is lacking in a single source. In this short paper, we propose a transparent minimal comparison framework that rates five representative systems including IPFS, Filecoin, Sia, Storj, and Swarm across eight dimensions. These dimensions are content addressing and versioning, decentralization, security and access tooling, persistence and durability, ecosystem readiness, cost and incentives, edge and cloud friendliness, and on chain integration. Using a zero to two scoring rubric and publicly available documentation, we compile a concise scoring table and visualize results via a radar chart and weighted sensitivity analyses under three weighting profiles which are equal weight, security and persistence emphasis, and ecosystem and chain integration emphasis. The resulting positioning map highlights where IPFS leads in content addressing and on chain compatibility and where it is typically augmented by other components such as pinning services, incentive layers, and access control middleware. We publish the scoring sheet and plotting scripts under open source licenses to support reproducibility and extensibility. This framework aids practitioners in judging when IPFS alone suffices and when it should be paired with incentive or persistence mechanisms in end to end verifiable data pipelines.

title: Adoption of Blockchain Consortium Model to Enhance Transparency of Bond Investment
authors: Inayatulloh, Armanto Witjaksono, Dwi Budi Srisulistiowati, R Wisnu Prio Pamungkas, Dian Hartanti, Achmad Noeman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Bond investment is the process of purchasing and owning debt securities issued by the government or companies in the hope of obtaining returns in the form of coupons and potential capital gains. The bond investment mechanism involves several stages from issuance to trading in the secondary market. Bond issuance occurs in the primary market, where the issuer (the party issuing the bonds) offers bonds to investors through specific mechanisms. Although bonds are considered a relatively stable invefstment instrument, several transparency issues can affect investors, such as the risk of financial statement manipulation, which can lead to misjudgements of bond credit risk. Rating agencies sometimes face a conflict of interest because bond issuers pay them. The ratings given may not fully reflect the actual financial conditions. This research proposes a solution to the low transparency of transaction information for bond investments by leveraging blockchain technology. By applying blockchain technology to bond investments, each transaction will be validated by all stakeholders, ensuring that the information produced is transparent and valid. This research employs a blockchain consortium model to enhance data privacy and facilitate coordination among institutions involved in bond investments. The research uses R&D and prototype testing. The simulation results with Hyperledger show that blockchain technology can produce transparent, verifiable information for investors and bondholders.

title: Real-Time Data Processing in Business Communication of B2B Supply Chain
authors: Matthew Boota, Edit Süle
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The paper examines how real-time data integration enhances communication, coordination, and decision-making in business-to-business (B2B) supply chains. It focuses on the use of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), and cloud-based systems to support seamless information exchange between business-to-business partners. Despite advances in digitalization, many organizations struggle to implement a fully integrated communication due to inconsistent data, a lack of standardization, limited interoperability, and restrictions in realtime synchronization across partners. A qualitative pilot study was conducted using semi-structured interviews with seven industry professionals from the manufacturing, automotive, pharmaceutical, healthcare, energy, and logistics sectors, along with case studies of real-world examples from Walmart, Nestlé, and GS1, to understand how these organizations successfully practice integration. The study reveals that firms with consistent master-data governance and integrated platforms achieve higher visibility, improved collaboration, and fewer operational delays. The paper concludes that the stages of digital integration performance include a practical overview of challenges in integration, the technologies currently in use, and key innovations planned for future adoption.

title: Predicted Modelling of Traffic Flows Behaviours on Regular Networks with Roundabouts
authors: Marina V. Yashina, Alena I. Vasileva, Ivan A. Kuteynikov, Alla G. Garibyan, Marina A. Trapeznikova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In this paper, we consider a rectangular lattice on a torus as a model for urban traffic flows. The nodes are transformed into circles, thus we create a model of a rectangular road network with roundabouts. The goal of the predicted modelling study is to identify patterns and basic network parameters that influence traffic behavior. The Biham-Middleton-Levine (BML) traffic model is used as a classical model. Flow behavior is described in relation to the formation of traffic jams. One method for improving safety is the use of roundabouts instead of standard intersections. Continuing our research on the interpretation of BML as a special case of the Buslaev network, the main objective is to study the speed spectrum of the corresponding dynamic system. In this paper, we obtain the main characteristics and a model in cellular automata notation for finding the speed spectrum of a network with roundabouts. The obtained criteria are used for machine learning of a neural network that generates control decisions based on network video observations. The specific application of Neural Networks in this study involves using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to process Cellular Automata states as image-like grids, where each cell represents a vehicle's occupancy or velocity. This approach enables the automatic extraction of complex spatial-temporal patterns, such as congestion clusters, without the need for manual feature engineering. By learning to recognize recurring patterns, the network can classify traffic configurations into distinct states, ranging from free-flow to jamming, and detect critical phase transitions, ultimately providing a predictive tool for forecasting traffic behavior in networks with roundabouts.

title: Intelligent Hybrid Edge–Cloud AI Predictive Maintenance for Industrial Electrical Systems
authors: S. Brindha, Haripriya V, Ishvarya K, Pavithran M, Abishek M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The growing adoption of Industrial Internet of Things technologies has increased the need for intelligent and cost-efficient predictive maintenance in industrial electrical systems and equipment such as motors, transformers, capacitors, relays, and IGBTs. Traditional cloud-only approaches often suffer from high latency, heavy bandwidth usage, and reliance on stable connectivity. This paper presents a hybrid Edge–Cloud AI framework designed to overcome these limitations by enabling real-time anomaly detection at the edge and advanced predictive analysis in the cloud. The edge module performs immediate monitoring and generates compact fault signatures, while the cloud module analyses these signatures using historical data and AI-based prediction models to deliver actionable maintenance insights. By combining localized processing with selective cloud analysis, the framework significantly reduces data transfer requirements, operational costs, and response time. The proposed system offers a scalable and practical foundation for future deployment of intelligent fault prediction solutions across diverse industrial environments.

title: Augmented Reality Face Filters: Identity, Technical Challenges, and Ethical Implications
authors: Tegveer Singh Bhatia, Dulcie Suri, Avinash Kumar, Deepak kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) face filters have gained widespread adoption on social media, enabled by sophisticated image processing techniques that overlay digital effects on realtime video of faces. This paper presents a comprehensive study that integrates the technical foundations of AR face filters facial landmark detection, real-time face tracking, rendering, and occlusion handling—with an empirical survey of 410 users on issues of self-identity, privacy, and long-term psychological impacts. We review key engineering methods such as advanced facial feature tracking algorithms and occlusion-aware rendering, citing recent improvements that achieve high precision and speed. In parallel, we analyze survey results by gender and age group, examining Likert-scale responses about how AR filters affect users' self-perception and privacy attitudes. The results indicate nuanced user perspectives: while many enjoy the creativity of filters, a majority expressed concern about potential long-term effects on body image and authenticity. Notably, younger users reported greater awareness of privacy and psychological impacts compared to older users. A regression analysis suggests age is a significant predictor of perceived negative impacts (with users over 35 less concerned than teens, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05)$</tex>, whereas gender and usage frequency showed no significant effect. By bridging technical insights with user-centered findings, this work highlights the need for responsible AR filter design that accounts for both cutting-edge performance and user well-being.

title: Analysis of FreeRTOS and Contiki Scheduler Performance with Scalable Task Loads on a Uniform Platform
authors: Kavita Sharma, S.R.N. Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Schedulers play a pivotal role in process management in real-time operating systems. They ensure efficient and responsive handling of resources in IoT systems. FreeRTOS and Contiki are two popular real-time operating systems that manage cooperative, priority-based, and event-based processes. This paper develops a priority-based round-robin scheduler and compares the performance of cooperative and prioritybased round-robin scheduling under maximum task load, i.e., execution time and code size, on a uniform development platform. The implementation and behavioral study of schedulers in a lab environment offer insights into RTOS suitability for different IoT applications and help developers make informed decisions when choosing an operating system based on the target scheduler's performance. The implementation results show that Contiki-NG exhibits better performance in memory efficiency and execution speed under cooperative and event-driven scheduling.

title: Optimization of Solar Power Plant (PV-Battery) Using Hydrogen Energy Storage System (HESS) for Telecom Tower Application
authors: M Aldy Wildan Maulana, Kevin Marojahan Banjar-Nahor, Nanang Hariyanto
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Reliable electricity supply for remote Base Transceiver Stations (BTS) or telecom tower remains a challenge due to limited grid access and dependence on costly diesel generators. This study optimizes an off-grid photovoltaic (PV) system integrated with hydrogen energy storage system (HESS) to enhance reliability and sustainability. Using HOMER simulations with a case study in Tanjung Kiaok, East Java, Indonesia, the system configuration was evaluated based on Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE), reliability, and surplus energy utilization. The optimal design, combining PV, batteries, electrolyzer, fuel cell, and hydrogen tank, achieves 100% load coverage with no outages and an LCOE of 0.5263 USD/kWh, within the typical LCOE range for similar hybrid systems. Surplus hydrogen production also offers economic benefits through potential sales to nearby BTS or other users. Compared with diesel-based solutions, the proposed system reduces costs, lowers operational and maintenance needs, and expands access to clean energy in surrounding 3T (underdeveloped, frontier, and outermost) regions.

title: Modeling of the Technological Labor Market Under Markov Switching and Poisson Approximation
authors: A.V. Nikitin, V.A. Sachovska, O.V. Kvashuk, A.S. Pertsov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents a stochastic evolutionary model that describes the interaction between employment and automation within the technological labor market under the Poisson approximation. The model extends the classical Lotka–Volterra dynamics by introducing impulsive random perturbations that reflect the influence of rare but significant socio–technological events. The Poisson approximation emphasizes the average of jump intensities, where small fluctuations occur with high probability, while large jumps appear rarely but have substantial impact. Such events correspond to abrupt changes in employment caused by the rapid adoption of automation or artificial intelligence systems. The proposed formulation captures both deterministic trends and stochastic impulses through a system of differential equations driven by an ergodic Markov environment. Analytical results include the explicit construction of limit generators and convergence theorems for the impulsive process. The model provides an effective mathematical tool for studying discontinuous transformations in labor dynamics in technological sectors.

title: Classification of AI-Driven Job Growth Across Industries Using KNN Algorithm
authors: Narciso Angelo R. Teofilo, Jerald R. Tomboc, Rhoderick D. Malangsa, Lorelie J. Paloma, Joedizza May Noverra L. Pilapil, Janrey C. Elecito
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: AI is reshaping the job market by driving job creation in some sectors while reducing demand in others. As industries adopt AI at different rates, understanding job growth trends is essential for businesses, policymakers, and job seekers. This study applies the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm to classify job growth into Growth, Stable, and Decline categories based on industry type, company size, AI adoption, required skills, and salary. By comparing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=1, K=3$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=5$</tex>, we observe a tradeoff between accuracy and generalization. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=1$</tex> delivers the highest accuracy but is highly sensitive to noise, while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=3$</tex> provides a more balanced approach with reduced sensitivity but lower accuracy. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=5$</tex> offers the most generalized predictions but demonstrates the weakest performance. These findings offer valuable insights into AI-driven workforce trends, enabling businesses to refine hiring strategies, policymakers to develop data-driven labor policies, and job seekers to align their skills with emerging opportunities. As AI continues to reshape industries, understanding job growth trends will be essential for workforce planning and economic stability.

title: The Possibility and Feasibility of Artificial Intelligence Optimizing the Allocation of Rural Education Resources
authors: Jiajie Hu, Dongling Chen, Jiachao Wei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: In the context of rural revitalization and education digitalization, rural education faces the dilemma where “hardware investment is not equal to education quality.” Existing research often adopts an “external transfusion” perspective, overlooking endogenous characteristics and cultural context. While artificial intelligence (AI) offers new potential, its application frequently falls into the shallow pattern of “technology grafting.” To explore deeper integration, this study constructed a “resources-culture-subjects” trinity framework and conducted a questionnaire survey with 100 students and 100 teachers, collecting data on cultural identity, resource status, and technology acceptance. Results show that students hold deep affection for their hometown culture (81 % like it very much) and strong expectations for AI-enhanced learning (85%). Teachers, however, note a clear “urbanization” bias in existing digital resources (67%) and find local resources inconvenient to access (only 25 % find it convenient). Based on these findings, the study builds a “culturally adaptive AI educational resource allocation model,” redefining AI from a “resource allocator” to a “cultural activator” and “subject enabler.” We conclude that the deep integration of technology and humanities is key to leveraging AI for optimizing rural education resource allocation. A systematic implementation path is proposed across technological, organizational, and institutional dimensions to shift rural education from “passive reception” to “active construction,” thus supporting the transition from “external transfusion” to “endogenous hematopoiesis.”

title: Application of NARX Artificial Neural Networks in Control Systems of Hot Rolling DC Electric Drives
authors: Alibek Batyrbek, Valerii Tytiuk, Galina Sivyakova, Zoya Gelmanova, Abdul Walid Fayez Wazani, Tkalenko Oleksandr
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper explores the application of nonlinear autoregressive neural networks with exogenous inputs (NARX) in DC electric drive control systems for hot rolling mills. We consider a NARX network architecture adapted for solving armature current (SEDCM_IA) and angular velocity (SEDCM_WA) problems under significant transient loads and process variables. Mathematical modeling of the electric drive operation is conducted using two separate NARX networks, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach compared to traditional mathematical modeling methods. It is shown that the use of NARX networks significantly improves control accuracy by taking into account the nonlinear dynamic characteristics of the system and compensating for disturbances in real time. The results of the study are of practical interest for the modernization of rolling mill automation systems and can be used in the design of adaptive control systems for critical electric drives.

title: Analysis of the Eeg Signals on Carnatic Music Ragas Using Signal Processing Techniques
authors: Harshith C, Suja Palaniswamy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The relationship between music and brain function has attracted significant attention with the growth of cognitive neuroscience and neuroimaging technologies. Music engages both hemispheres of the brain and influences cognition and emotion by evoking specific memories and producing measurable physiological responses through neural communication and signal transmission. This study investigates the cognitive impact of music by monitoring participants' performance in structured memory tasks before and after music exposure. Comparative analysis with a control group demonstrates that individuals who listened to music achieved higher cognitive gains, particularly in short-term memory retention. These findings support the potential of music listening as an effective tool for enhancing cognitive function and provide evidence for its integration into future therapeutic and cognitive training applications.

title: WealthWise: An AI-Based Stock Analysis Tool
authors: Rishabh Barhate, Shubhangi Kharche, Dhawal Mahajan, Mukesh Prajapati, Ishaan Sansare, Ronit Chaudhari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: WealthWise is introduced as a smart stock market analysis tool that merges technical indicators with AI to aid in well-informed trading choices. The system collects both realtime and past financial information using the yfinance library, and uses the ta library for calculating indicators like EMA, Bollinger Bands, OBV, ADX, RSI, MACD, and ATR. In contrast to traditional tools, WealthWise uses Google's Gemini AI to decipher the technical signals along with the sentiment found in financial news through the News API. Sentiment is processed through VADER and TextBlob so that the tool can identify market psychology and narrow down trading recommendations, including Buy, Sell, or Hold signals with automatic stop-loss strategies. The application is deployed on Streamlit so that it remains an interactive and user-friendly interface for global investors of different market outlooks. Experimental testing proves its reliability, with the classification model achieving 90.22% accuracy, 91.23% precision, 92.86% recall, and a 92.04% F1-score as per the confusion matrix. These results prove the effectiveness of WealthWise as an assistive decision-support tool for traders, while its modular structure also offers flexibility in integrating cutting-edge predictive models in upcoming versions.

title: Lossless ECG Compression Technique for Low Power Devices
authors: K Ramana Reddy, G Nikhil Goud, B Venkatesh, Sangeeta Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The monitoring of biomedical signals such as Electrocardiograms (ECG) and their storage become increasingly significant with the advent of wearable health technologies and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). This paper describes a special lossless ECG compression strategy tailored for devices incorporating very low power VLSI technology which are portable and battery operated. The method described here applies a simple Adaptive Linear Predictor (ALP) model to reduce redundancy within the signal, followed by Golomb-Rice Coding (GRC) for entropy encoding. Implementation of the architecture was done using Verilog HDL for synthesis in Xilinx Vivado while simulations were performed on Cadence NC Launch tools. The system has been designed to operate with minimal power consumption of 26.125 nW whilst maintaining critical medical data lossless yielding over 2.25 Compression Ratio (CR). Such specifications enable real-time monitoring through wearable or implantable medical devices. Based on the results obtained, it seems feasible to overcome challenges posed in next generation platforms for biomedical signal processing in terms of low power requirements and lossless data compression.

title: Large-Scale Multi-Objective Sensor-Weapon-Target Assignment Optimization: Benchmark Suites and Knowledge-Driven Heuristics
authors: Nan Zheng, Rui Wang, Kaiwen Li, Wenhua Li, Tianyu Luo, Handing Wang
abstract: With the expanding deployment of unmanned swarms on the battlefield, the optimization of the Large-Scale Multi-Objective Sensor-Weapon-Target Assignment (LSMO-SWTA) problem has become increasingly critical to achieving effective defense operations. The core challenge lies in the combinatorial explosion inherent to this problem, resulting in a large-scale decision space where feasible regions satisfying stringent battlefield constraints are very sparse. However, existing studies often oversimplify the complex spatiotemporal constraints, revealing an urgent demand for specialized high-fidelity benchmark suites. To bridge this gap, we propose SWTA_MOP1-5, a benchmark suite capturing four configurable objectives (value, cost, timeliness, and risk) integrated with complex operational constraints, while supporting customizable problem scales. To address the challenges caused by large-scale decision spaces and complex constraints in LSMO-SWTA problems, we propose two knowledge-driven heuristics: sparse initialization and infeasible repair. By leveraging the target knowledge encoded in the interceptability matrices, the initialization strategy prunes the large-scale search space to quickly locate feasible regions, while the repair strategy intelligently rectifies infeasible offspring solutions prior to evaluation. To validate the effectiveness and generalization ability of the proposed strategies, we integrate them into eight state-of-the-art algorithms and conduct extensive experiments on 162 instances generated by SWTA MOP1-5. Experimental results demonstrate that the knowledge-driven variants outperform their original counterparts significantly, confirming that incorporating domain knowledge is pivotal for solving LSMO-SWTA problems.

title: Gaussian Process Regression of Steering Vectors With Physics-Aware Deep Composite Kernels for Augmented Listening
authors: Diego Di Carlo, Shoichi Koyama, Aditya Arie Nugraha, Mathieu Fontaine, Yoshiaki Bando, Kazuyoshi Yoshii
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: This paper investigates continuous representations of steering vectors over frequency and microphone/source positions for augmented listening (e.g., spatial filtering and binaural rendering), enabling user-parameterized control of the reproduced sound field. Steering vectors have typically been used for representing the spatial response of a microphone array as a function of the look-up direction. The basic algebraic representation of these quantities assuming an idealized environment cannot deal with the scattering effect of the sound field. One may thus collect a discrete set of real steering vectors measured in dedicated facilities and super-resolve (i.e., upsample) them. Recently, physics-aware deep learning methods have been effectively used for this purpose. Such deterministic super-resolution, however, suffers from the overfitting problem due to the non-uniform uncertainty over the measurement space. To solve this problem, we integrate an expressive representation based on the neural field (NF) into the principled probabilistic framework based on the Gaussian process (GP). Specifically, we propose a physics-aware composite kernel that models the directional incoming waves and the subsequent scattering effect. Our comprehensive comparative experiment showed the effectiveness of the proposed method under data insufficiency conditions. In downstream tasks such as speech enhancement and binaural rendering using the simulated data of the SPEAR challenge, the oracle performances were attained with less than ten times fewer measurements.

title: Nurse Rostering Constrained by Physiological-Psychological State Evolution
authors: Qin Song, Xin-Ya Chen, Peng-Wei Zhang, Mumtaz Karatas, Wei-Guo Sheng, Yu-Jun Zheng
abstract: Nurse rostering is a typical personnel scheduling problem that involves a variety of constraints. Existing rostering methods that aim to minimize the penalty of soft constraint violations suffer from several limitations including the use of crisp parameters, inflexibility to changes, and a lack of consideration for individual nurse states. This is because violations of the widely-used soft constraints cannot truly reflect the actual working states of nurses that determine the quality of schedules. In this paper, we reformulate the nurse rostering problem by using a new objective function that maximizes the overall reward for achieving good physiological and psychological states of nurses, which are estimated based on their personal characteristics and work assignments using long short-term memory. To solve the problem, we propose an evolutionary algorithm assisted by Q-learning to dynamically adjust the fitness function based on constraint violations to balance positive rewards to good states and negative rewards to bad states during the evolution process. Our results on benchmark and real-world instances demonstrate that our method obtains significantly better nurse states compared to selected popular rostering methods.

title: Decision Support: QR-Code Based Visitor Log System with Data Analytics
authors: Joel C. Trillo, Ervin Simon D. Uadan, Ronie A. Sajelan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Supervising a user's activities in school libraries is an important area that serves to control attendance, security, and data-oriented decision making. However, the logs in the form of books are susceptible to human error, are time consuming, and are generally ineffective. This study presents the design and development of a Decision Support QR-Code Based Visitor Log System with Data Analytics that automatically time stamps the entry and exit logs of library users and provides real time tracking of patterns of users for data analysis. The system utilized imaging techniques including dynamic-threshold binarization, contour detection, and pattern recognition to provide accurate QR code recognition and logging. The pattern analytics module provided insight into important information such as the hours and usage of the library on the highest level, the frequency of visits of departments, and the library users who provide the most activity in the library. The system was validated with respect to usability, functionality, efficiency, reliability, and user satisfaction by using User Acceptance Testing (UAT) as defined by the ISO/IEC 25010:2023 quality model, which produced high ratings in usability (4.57), reliability (4.07), and functionality (4.37). These studies indicate that the system developed fills an important need in conquering the weakness of the manual log recording data and provides the user with data analytics insight in improving library management and resource allocation.

title: Two-Stage Self-Supervised Graph Neural Networks and Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Power Grid Reconfiguration
authors: Veeraiah Thalagondpati, C Srinika Sharma, M Riddhi Aparajita, YS Mahati
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Modern power grids face unprecedented challenges from renewable integration, load variability, and extreme weather events, necessitating adaptive reconfiguration strategies. Traditional optimization methods struggle with nonconvexity and poor scalability, while supervised learning approaches are hampered by data scarcity. Reinforcement learning (RL) offers promise for adaptive control but suffers from sample inefficiency and often ignores underlying grid topology. We propose a novel two-stage framework that combines self-supervised graph neural networks (GNNs) with deep RL to address these limitations. In the first stage, we pretrain a temporal GNN using contrastive learning on unlabeled historical grid snapshots, learning rich representations that capture both spatial topology and temporal dynamics. The second stage fine-tunes an RL agent using these pretrained embeddings as state representations, enabling sample-efficient policy learning for discrete line-switching actions. Experimental results on the IEEE 14-bus system demonstrate that our approach achieves remarkable learning efficiency, with episode rewards progressing from −5.5 to +3.27 during training and stabilizing at a mean evaluation reward of +1.887 ± 0.276. The framework consistently produces positive rewards across all test episodes, indicating reliable grid performance improvement. Our method eliminates the need for labeled data while effectively handling physical constraints, providing a scalable, data-driven solution for adaptive power grid reconfiguration that significantly outperforms pure RL baselines and demonstrates suitability for real-time reconfiguration compared to optimization-based approaches.

title: Toporiskformer-GNN: A Topological Riskaware Graph Transformer for Cybersecurity Anomaly Detection and Risk Assessment
authors: C.Harriet Linda, Bediga Sharan, K. Vetrivel, V. Dyana Christilda, Ananthi. M, N. INDIRA PRIYADARSINI
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The rise in complexity of cyber threats in the enterprise data warehouses necessitates the development of a corresponding framework of detectable forces, which should not only ensure accuracy but also be transparent and accountable. The current paper proposes XAI-OracleNet, or Accenture deep learning, which is a hybrid neural network to be applied to detecting cybersecurity threats in Oracle-based data warehouses. The model combines Bi-directional LSTMbased sequential pattern-based learning approaches, attention mechanisms to feed prioritized features, and explainability of post-hoc decisions through SHAP values and IGAM based visualizations. UNSW-NB15 was used as the basis of empirical testing, a set of real-world network traffic fit to test Oracle-like environments. The experimental findings display that XAI-OracleNet approaches a classification accuracy of 97.4 % that is superior to CNN (94.1 %) and GCN + LIME (96.3 %). Its inference time per sample remained low at 2.1 ms, which was suitable to relatively real-time utilization. The explainability was 0.93, a big increase over the conventional models and adequate regulatory disclosure requirements. Analysis with SHAPbased methods outlined that temporal and port-based features play the biggest role in the threat classification, and IGAMs plots confirmed that risk patterns are monotonic and interpretable. With its implementation in a simulated Oracle Cloud, the quantized version of XAI-OracleNet continued to demonstrate unrivaled scalability and efficiency by keeping an average response time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 2 ~ m s}$</tex>. The proper integration of performance, interpretability, and deployment readiness makes XAI-OracleNet a strong platform of the twenty-first century governance of cybersecurity within the data-based enterprise environment. The given model can provide practical knowledge without failing to comply with explainability and auditability standards.

title: Machine Learning Approach for Pneumonia Detection from X-Rays Amid H3n2 Virus Spread
authors: Yamunadevi S, Ariharan. G, Balakumar K, Deepika P, Jose maxitha J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The leading causes of death in the world and those with considerable diagnostic problems are lung diseases such as Bacterial Pneumonia, Coronavirus (COVID-19), Tuberculosis, H3N2, and Viral Pneumonia, which have similar radiographic phenotypes. Conventional diagnostic methods are based on the physical examination of the chest X-ray or CT scans which are time-consuming and prone to human errors. In spite of the fact that the conventional machine learning models like Naive Bayes, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbor, Support Vector Machine, and Random Forest have been examined, they cannot be used to address the complexity and variability of medical imaging data. CNNs have enhanced the accuracy of the classifier, yet have challenges to do with sensitivity and generalization. In order to eliminate these shortcomings, this paper proposes a Hybrid Deep Learning Framework, which combines CNNs with transfer learning through previously trained models Mobile Net and VGG19. The model under discussion improves deep feature extraction and has a high level of adaptability to diverse imaging conditions. Applied to a real-life set of chest X-ray images, it recognized an accuracy of 92.98 percent which is greater than the conventional classifiers without compromising the sensitivity and false negative rates. This powerful framework offers a powerful, scalable, and automated system of classifying lung diseases, helping radiologists to diagnose the conditions at an earlier stage and encouraging the implementation of AI-powered image analysis as an integral part of the contemporary healthcare framework.

title: AI-Assisted Azimuth Optimization in Cellular Networks for IoT Applications Using Genetic Algorithms and Atoll Simulations
authors: Mohammed Oussama Benosman, Hicham Megnafi, Sidi Mohammed Meriah, Zoheir Karaouzene
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper proposes an innovative methodology for antenna azimuth optimization in a cellular network composed of six sites, deployed in the city of Ghazaouat (Tlemcen Province, Algeria). The method relies on prediction data generated by the Atoll radio planning software and an optimization algorithm based on artificial intelligence. The approach integrates geospatial Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) maps with the technical parameters of sites and transmitters, with the objective of simultaneously maximizing radio coverage and quality of service (QoS) indicators. The proposed optimization process enables the automatic generation of updated configuration files, directly importable into Atoll for re-simulation and validation phases. Experimental results, obtained in a representative scenario, show significant improvements in coverage and quality metrics. These findings confirm the relevance and feasibility of AI-assisted azimuth optimization for real-world cellular network deployments.

title: Nishkarsh: An AI-Based Multimodal Political Bias Detection System for Indian News Media
authors: Priyanshu Bhojwani, Shanmuga Priya R, J R Shruti, Suraj Teli, Swastik Sharma, Shreelakshmi Hegde
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Background-Media bias in news reporting, particularly political bias, undermines balanced discourse in India where diverse media ecosystems strongly influence public opinion. Methods-We present Nishkarsh, an AI-based multimodal system that accepts raw text or URLs, performs web scraping, and applies four modules- (1) a fine-tuned RoBERTa model for political leaning classification, (2) sentiment analysis using DistilBERT, (3) TF-IDF-based lexical scoring, and (4) contextual similarity with ideology embeddings. Outputs are combined via weighted fusion, and a BART-based summarizer produces biasneutral summaries. Results-On a custom Indian news dataset of approx. 13,000 articles, Nishkarsh achieved 87 percent accuracy in political leaning detection and 92.6 percent in sentiment analysis, validated through ROC-AUC metrics and expert review. Conclusion: The framework demonstrates accurate, interpretable, and India- focused political bias detection while supporting fact-centered summarization, thereby enhancing transparency and public trust.

title: Investigation of Microwave-Convective Drying Techniques for Cranberries
authors: Ya-Chi Ho, Ruei-Yu Lin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Cranberries (Vaccinium macrocarpon) are rich in bioactive compounds that provide significant health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. However, their high moisture content and seasonal availability necessitate drying to extend shelf life and inhibit microbial growth. Conventional hot-air drying is time-consuming, energy-intensive, and often leads to surface charring. This study experimentally investigates microwave-convective (hot-air) drying as an alternative approach for cranberry dehydration. An AI-assisted electromagnetic simulation was employed to support the design and analysis of the microwave chamber, aiming to improve energy distribution and process stability. Drying performance was primarily evaluated through experimental trials under various microwave power and hot-air operating conditions. The results demonstrate that the combined microwave-convective drying method reduced the processing time from 8 h (hot-air drying alone) to 3 h while achieving a final moisture content of 29.6%, which lies within the target range of 20–30%. Further reduction of drying time to 90 min resulted in a moisture content of 42.3%, exceeding the acceptable limit. These findings indicate that excessive acceleration of the drying process compromises moisture removal efficiency and product uniformity. Overall, microwaveconvective drying exhibits clear advantages in drying efficiency compared with conventional hot-air drying. The AI-assisted electromagnetic analysis provides supportive insight into system design but is not the primary determinant of experimental optimization. The proposed hybrid drying approach offers a practical and effective solution for improving cranberry drying performance while maintaining acceptable product quality.

title: Marketing Personalization in Hospitality: A K-Modes Clustering Approach
authors: I Made Dwija Arsana, I Putu Agus Eka Darma Udayana, Aniek Suryanti Kusuma
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: In the highly competitive hospitality sector, the ability to deliver personalized guest experiences has shifted from a premium offering to a fundamental requirement. Despite the wealth of data captured through Property Management Systems (PMS), a significant gap persists between data collection and the execution of targeted marketing strategies. Conventional approaches often utilize K-means clustering; however, hotel datasets are predominantly categorical, making K-means—which relies on Euclidean distance—suboptimal for this domain. To address this limitation, this paper employs the K-modes clustering algorithm, which is specifically designed for categorical data dissimilarity. By identifying latent patterns in guest attributes such as age groups, booking channels, and travel motivations, we establish robust ‘guest personas.' These personas then serve as the foundational input for a Generative AI engine. Unlike manual content creation, our proposed framework automates the generation of hyper-personalized promotional messages that align with the specific linguistic and behavioral nuances of each segment. The primary contribution of this work is the development of a streamlined, end-to-end workflow that bridges the gap between raw behavioral data and automated, high-relevance marketing communication.

title: Distributed RAG Framework for Enterprise Systems
authors: Aswathnarayan Muthukrishnan Kirubakaran, Vinoth Punniyamoorthy, Nachiappan Chockalingam, Balakrishna Pothineni, Ram Sekhar Bodala, Adithya Parthasarathy, Akshay Deshpande, Siva Kumar Chintham
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is widely adopted to ground large language models (LLMs) in enterprise knowledge, yet most deployments rely on centralized vector indexes and document stores that require consolidating sensitive content into a single environment. This centralization complicates data governance, regulatory compliance, data residency, and tenant isolation, especially when information is distributed across business units, regions, and clouds. This paper presents a distributed RAG framework that allows each domain to retain ownership of its documents and indexes while participating in a federated retrieval architecture. distributed RAG framework employs a three-tier design with local nodes, regional gateways, and a cloud-level routing and inference service, and exchanges only compressed and privacy-aware embedding summaries across boundaries. Experiments on partitioned enterprise-style corpora show that distributed RAG framework maintains retrieval quality within 1.3% points of a centralized RAG baseline (nDCG@10), while reducing cross-site communication by approximately 70% and improving end-to-end latency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 6 5 \%}$</tex>, depending on node placement. These results indicate that distributed retrieval over locally governed indexes is a viable alternative to fully centralized architectures for large-scale enterprise knowledge systems.

title: CyberSentinel: An AI-Powered Secure Browser with Parental Dashboard and Automated Website Blocking for Child Safety
authors: C. A. Daphine Desona Clemency, Akshya Kumar. N, Abhishek. K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: With the rapid increase in unsafe and phishing websites, ensuring child safety during internet browsing has become a critical challenge. This work proposes CyberSentinel, an AI-powered secure browser integrated with a parental control dashboard and automated website blocking mechanism. The system employs multiple machine learning models for threat detection and user behavior analysis. A Multinomial Naive Bayes classifier was trained on URL datasets to classify safe and unsafe links, achieving an accuracy of 94.34%, with a weighted F1-score of 0.94. A Random Forest classifier trained for phishing URL detection obtained 100% accuracy, demonstrating robust performance in identifying malicious domains. For behavioral security, a Random Forest-based keystroke intrusion detection model achieved 82.70% accuracy, effectively distinguishing legitimate users from intruders. The integration of these models within the secure browser enhances web safety, enabling automated parental monitoring and intelligent content restriction. Overall, CyberSentinel demonstrates a scalable AI-driven approach for real-time online safety and user protection.

title: A Transfer Learning Framework for Multimodal Emotion Detection using Wav2Vec, EfficientNet, and LSTM
authors: M. Jagadeesh, N. Duraimurugan, Karthikeyan. U, Saravanan Siddhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Human-computer interaction is based on emotion recognition, which allows the systems in artificial intelligence and robotics to react to users in a truly empathetic way. This paper proposes a powerful, multimodal system of recognizing a wide range of human emotions when communicating naturally. We optimize three state-of-the-art pre-trained audio, video, and text models based on Wav2Vec, EfficientNet, and LSTM respectively, to a fresh, annotated ad hoc collection of expressions of affect. Using a combination of the complementary cues of these modalities, our system is more accurate than any of the unimodal baselines, and the dataset itself is a useful resource in future affective computing research.

title: Intelligent Graph-Convolutional Cleaner Fish Attentive Network for Personalized E-Learning Recommendation in IT
authors: Yuvapriya P, Subramanian P, Surendran R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Online learning platforms are becoming very popular and so it requires an intelligence recommendation system that respond to the various behaviour and skill needs of the learners. This research proposes the Graph-Convolutional Cleaner Fish Attentive Network (GCCFANet) to provide IT professionals with relevant course recommendations. GCCFANet consists of three main elements, including graph convolutional networks (GCNet), which function to extract structural correlations among learners, courses, and skills, multi-head attention model (MHA), which are used to prioritize heterogeneous interactions, and Cleaner Fish Optimization (CleFO), to tune hyperparameters of GCCFANet. Moreover, the BERT-based embeddings in GCCFANet enrich semantic representations of course materials and profiles of learners. As experimental analysis of MARS dataset demonstrates, GCCFANet performs much better than traditional deep learning networks with higher accuracy of 98.92%. The findings confirm the usefulness of integrating graph based learning and evolutionary optimization to solve the problems of personalized e-learning recommendation.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering for Dukcapil SOPs: Synthetic-Context Fine-Tuning and Chunking Design
authors: Ahmad Rizki, Gabriel P. H. Panjaitan, Fadhlan Nazhif Azizy, Ayu Purwarianti, Nugraha Priya Utama
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers a practical approach for question answering over long and structured documents, yet its effectiveness for Indonesian publicservice Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) remains underexplored. This paper evaluates Indonesian Dukcapil SOP question answering through two linked design stages: (i) generator adaptation via supervised fine-tuning (SFT) using retrieval-like synthetic chunk contexts (Synthetic-only) and mixed IndoQA+synthetic variant, and (ii) retrieval configuration over chunking granularity and embedding choices. We scrape and preprocess an official Dukcapil SOP corpus (55 enriched SOP documents) and construct a stratified golden benchmark with six query types (simple, complex, explicit, implicit, yes/no, and unanswerable). We then run a factorial study over four chunking strategies, four multilingual/Indonesian embedding models, and the selected generator checkpoints, reporting retrieval metrics (Hit@k and MRR@10) and end-to-end answer quality (BERTScore by span categories, yes/no accuracy, and unanswerable abstention accuracy). Results show that retrieval design strongly influences end-to-end performance, while synthetic-context SFT improves controlled answering-especially robust yes/no handling and safe abstention when evidence is missing. We conclude with practical guidance for selecting chunk granularity in SOP QA deployments and discuss operational considerations for real-world integration.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Continuous Testing Framework for Automated Front-End Validation in Student Attendance Systems
authors: Novita Sabila Nugraha, Novian Anggis Suwastika, Isa Mulia Insan, Qori Qonita
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: The development of school attendance systems faces increasing challenges due to frequent updates and rapid deployment cycles, where maintaining consistent, error-resistant, and delay-free testing without affecting the production environment remains a critical issue. Addressing this problem, this paper presents the design and evaluation of an Automated Continuous Testing Framework (ACTF) for validating front-end functionalities in student attendance systems within a Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) environment. The framework integrates Cypress for end-to-end (E2E) automation and GitHub Actions for continuous execution, enabling traceable, reproducible, and scalable testing workflows. Following the Design Science Research (DSR) methodology, the study was conducted through five iterative stages: problem identification, framework design, implementation, demonstration, and evaluation. Functional requirements (FRs) were analyzed and translated into automated test scenarios using equivalence partitioning and boundary value techniques, while a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) was used to ensure comprehensive coverage between test cases and system requirements. A total of 48 automated test cases were executed across both local and cloud environments. The evaluation results demonstrated 100 % execution consistency, 100 % requirement coverage, and significantly reduced testing time compared to manual testing approaches. Furthermore, the integration of Cypress and GitHub Actions ensured continuous validation across code updates, minimizing human error and accelerating defect detection during the development process. These results confirm that the proposed framework effectively improves testing reliability, scalability, and traceability, providing a replicable model for integrating automated testing within CI/CD pipelines.

title: LOOL: Low-Overhead, Optimization-Log-Guided Compiler Fuzzing
authors: Felix Berlakovich, Florian Schwarcz, Gergö Barany, Hanspeter Mössenböck, Stefan Brunthaler
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: WiFi-based Activity Recognition Empowered Consumer Healthcare with Kolmogorov-Arnold Network
authors: Xu Xu, Xiaofei Cui, Ghulam Muhammad
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) provides a non-intrusive sensing solution for consumer healthcare and assisted-living services in smart homes and human-centric industrial environments. However, the non-stationary wireless channel and complex nonlinear distortions in channel state information (CSI) make robust feature extraction and reliable downstream decision making challenging. To address this, we propose a Kolmogorov-Arnold network (KAN) based HAR framework over WiFi CSI, tailored for consumer and industrial IoT scenarios. CSI data are processed in parallel by a convolutional branch that captures fine-grained spatial features across antennas and subcarriers and a Transformer branch that models long-range temporal motion patterns. The two feature streams are concatenated, temporally averaged, and then classified by a KAN module that combines affine mappings with high-order polynomial expansions to approximate complex decision boundaries under channel variations. We evaluate the model on four public datasets (SignFi, Widar3.0, UT-HAR, NTU-HAR) for both gesture and action recognition. Experimental results show that our model consistently improves over state-of-the-art methods, achieving a peak accuracy of 98.75% and a Cohen’s kappa (CK) of 93.10%. Moreover, it is deployment-oriented, enabling lightweight inference on resource-constrained smart-home gateways and introducing negligible secure-upload overhead, i.e., less than 50 bytes per CSI batch.

title: Message from the Chairman
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
