abstract: A very warm welcome to all participants of the 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering 2025 (ICCCE 2025), hosted by the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM).

title: Empowering Rural Women Through Sustainable Cooking Solutions: A Human-Centric Approach to Clean Energy in Kalakrai, India
authors: Maitreyee Awasthi, Suraksha Rajagopalan, Gopikrishna M, Abhinav Jayakumar, Pavan Kumar, Vipina Valsan, Renjith Mohan
abstract: Globally, approximately 2.6 billion people face significant challenges in accessing clean cooking energy, leading to severe health issues and environmental degradation. This study, conducted under the Live-in-Labs® program, focuses on addressing these challenges in Kalakrai, a remote village in Pune, Maharashtra, India. The village's reliance on firewood, particularly by women who spend hours gathering it, contributes to health risks such as respiratory diseases and environmental issues like deforestation. Using Human-Centric Design (HCD) principles and Participatory Rural Appraisal (PRA) tools such as resource mapping and problem tree analysis, this research proposes a sustainable solution-a community-based biogas plant. This intervention not only mitigates health and environmental risks but also reduces the burden on women, promoting gender equality by alleviating the disproportionate physical and health strain they endure. The project aligns with the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), specifically SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-Being), SDG 5 (Gender Equality), and SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy). The implementation of this biogas plant aims to improve the quality of life for rural women, foster gender equality, and create a more sustainable environment for the community.

title: Satellite Reliability Analysis Using Mathematical Prediction Models: A Case Study of Satellite Anomalies
authors: Muhammad Imran Haris Ahmad Mahmood, Nadirah Abdul Rahim, Abdul Mutholib, Mahayaudin M. Mansor
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: The importance of satellites in delivering critical services including communication systems, navigation, and earth observation heavily depends on system reliability. Satellite reliability is defined as the probability that a system is failurefree under specified conditions to ensure the satellites remain safe, efficient and long-lasting. Previous studies highlighted the impact of satellite anomalies as significant contributors to objective failures, cost inefficiencies, and satellite system degradation. However, these studies often lack a comprehensive analysis of reliability using structured mathematical models. Hence, this research addresses this gap by analyzing 87 satellite data from the Seradata database using different mathematical models, categorized into parametric and non-parametric models. The parametric models are Exponential, Weibull and Poisson, whereas the non-parametric models are Kaplan-Meir and Monte Carlo. A case study of satellite anomalies is conducted focusing on satellite communication subsystems of failed satellites due to anomalies. The detected anomalies vary from antenna, transponder, amplifier and battery subsystems. Then, the mathematical models are developed and compared to evaluate the study's objective in obtaining the best mathematical model based on the lowest Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). Based on the five mathematical models, the results reveal that the Exponential model exhibits the best overall performance with the lowest RMSE values for antenna, transponder, amplifier and battery of 0.22, 0.27, 0.30 and 0.30 respectively. The findings of this study demonstrate the advantages of having multiple mathematical model evaluations that enhance the selection of models in satellite reliability analysis for the development of satellite design and operation.

title: Preface: ICCBDCS 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: The 9th International Conference on Cloud, Big Data and Communication Systems (ICCBDCS 2025) was convened at the University of Manchester, Manchester, United Kingdom, from 14 to 16 August 2025. The conference brought together researchers, practitioners, and educators to exchange developments and insights in the interrelated domains of cloud computing, big data, and communication systems.

title: A Low Profile S-Band Antenna for 1U CubeSat Communication Systems
authors: Ghaith Al Makdami, Omar Al Alawi, Jasim Al Maharbi, Hassan Al Lawati
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance evaluation of a circularly polarized patch antenna intended for CubeSat applications. The initial design employs a square patch antenna with side truncations on a 3.2 mm FR4 substrate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{r}= 4.4$</tex>) and is optimized to operate at 3.1 GHz. To enhance the bandwidth and polarization performance, parasitic patches were incorporated around the main radiator. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> patch configuration demonstrated a wide impedance bandwidth of 57%, spanning from 2.9 GHz to 4.8 GHz, and achieved circular polarization within the 3.0-3.25 GHz range, with a 3-dB axial ratio bandwidth of 8%. The antenna gain at the center frequency was approximately 1.8 dBi. Further enhancement was achieved by increasing the array to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \times 5$</tex> configuration, which remained within the 1 U CubeSat form factor. This design extended the axial ratio bandwidth to 10.8% and improved the gain significantly to around 7 dBi. The results confirm the potential of using parasitic patch arrays to meet the compactness and performance requirements of small satellite communication systems

title: Empowering Women Through Next-Generation Connectivity: Analyzing the Impact of 5G Networks on Women's Economic and Social Development in Kerala
authors: Harisankar S, Akhil S, Suresh A
abstract: The introduction of 5G networks marks a significant leap in telecommunications, with the potential to drive economic growth, enhance social inclusion, and expand access to essential services. Despite Kerala's high literacy rates and progressive social indicators, disparities persist in access to next-generation technologies, particularly for women in rural and semi-rural areas. This study investigates the impact of 5G connectivity on women's economic and social empowerment in Kerala. Using data from 480 women, a logistic regression model was employed to identify key socioeconomic determinants of 5G accessibility. Multicollinearity checks and goodness-of-fit tests validated the model, while results highlight the critical roles of education, income, and digital literacy. Findings reveal that women in semi-rural areas, with higher education levels and incomes, are significantly more likely to access 5G. However, infrastructural gaps, high costs, and socio-cultural barriers hinder equitable access. The study underscores the need for targeted policy interventions, including investments in rural digital infrastructure, subsidized 5G devices, and tailored digital literacy programs. These measures can bridge the gender digital divide, fostering inclusive development and empowering women economically and socially. The implications extend beyond Kerala, providing a framework for leveraging 5G to achieve broader societal goals.

title: The Influence of Financial Performance on Women Representation in Films: A Supervised Machine Learning Approach to Analyzing Bechdel Test Outcomes
authors: Juby Thomas, Sateesh Kumar T K, Vishnu Achutha Menon, Lijo P Thomas, Romy Mathew
abstract: This study investigates the relationship between financial indicators of movies and their likelihood of passing the Bechdel Test using a supervised machine learning approach. A logistic regression model was employed to analyze features including budget, domestic gross, international gross, return on investment (ROI), and domestic ratio. The dataset, sourced from secondary data, underwent preprocessing steps such as SMOTE for class balancing and standard feature scaling. Model training and evaluation were conducted using precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. Key findings reveal that while budget distribution shows no significant difference between movies passing or failing the Bechdel Test, ROI tends to be slightly higher for passing movies, suggesting potential financial advantages of increased female representation. International gross appears higher for passing movies, indicating broader global appeal. Feature importance analysis highlights budget and domestic gross as the most influential predictors. However, the model's performance metrics indicate moderate accuracy, with room for improvement in identifying movies likely to pass the test.

title: Empirical Insights into Female Gen Z's Perception and the Factors Influencing the Adoption of AI-Powered Customer Service Chatbots
authors: Akhil Krishna J, Athira R, Itty Daniel Skaria, Ananthi Rajayya
abstract: The customer service sector across global businesses, particularly e-commerce, has transformed by adopting AI-powered customer service chatbots. As Kerala is considered to be the most digitally literate state in India a sample of 150 respondents was taken to examine those variables impacting adoption of AI-powered customer service chatbots among Female Gen Z. In this research, by utilizing the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) framework many key drivers were identified and these insights will help business organizations design more effective chatbot solutions tailored to these demographics. The AI chatbot technology is designed to be responsive to the needs of women, providing them with an easily accessible and user-friendly product that aligns with their digital preferences. This paper identifies privacy and trust issues as key vulnerabilities for Female Gen Z, highlighting the need to address gender-based barriers in AI chatbot adoption. It offers solutions such as enhancing trust and security while promoting gender-sensitive technology design.

title: Minimax-Type Band-Pass Filters with Full-Band Center-Frequency Tuning and Narrow Transition-Band
authors: Tian-Bo Deng
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: The paper investigates designing a recursive band-pass filter that has a full-band variable passband center frequency (CF), while its transition bands are extremely narrow. Furthermore, since the filter structure is recursive, the stability guarantee tops the overall design issues. This paper shows a stability-guarantee scheme using parameter transformations to meet the stability requirement. Based on this scheme, the minimax design criterion is adopted to design a bandpass filter with full-band CF tuning and sharpened transition bands. The minimax design suppresses the peak deviation of the filter's amplitude response and thus produces a minimax-type variable-CF bandpass filter with absolute stability guarantee. To validate this stability-guaranteed minimax design scheme, we conduct computer simulations to demonstrate that the minimax design can obtain a bandpass variable-CF filter with a full-band tunable passband CF and extremely narrow transition bands. Moreover, the variable-CF bandpass filter is not only stable, but also exhibits superior CF-tunable performance.

title: Optimal Load Level Adjustment Policy for Balanced Systems in Shock Environment
authors: Qinglai Dong, Mengmeng Bai, Qingan Qiu
abstract: Balanced systems refer to a class of system architectures composed of multiple components with similar or synergistic functions, whose normal operation depends on the coordination between the components in terms of position, quantity, states, etc. The reliability analysis and load level adjustment policies for balanced systems are vital for ensuring stable operations. Existing research predominantly focuses on the reliability modeling of the dependence between load levels and degradation, and there is a notable gap in optimizing load level adjustment policies that take system condition information into account. This paper addresses this gap by examining the optimal timing for load level adjustments for balanced systems in shock environments, aiming to minimize the average cost rate. We propose a condition-based load level adjustment policy that leverages component state information. Specifically, the load level of a component is dynamically adjusted in response to the number of shocks, thereby significantly enhancing the system's performance and reliability. Our study delves into the system reliability and optimization of load level adjustment policies. A comprehensive numerical example using a voltage regulator system is presented to demonstrate the proposed policy's effectiveness. To ensure the rigour of our research, we perform extensive robustness checks. The results demonstrate that the proposed policy remains robust and effective even under significant environmental stochasticity, providing a reliable decision-support tool for engineering managers.

title: Bias-Dependent Total Ionizing Dose Effects in p-GaN Gate HEMTs: Capacitance-Based Charge Mapping and Defect Identification
authors: Zhao Wang, Shuai Yuan, Ming Qiao, Bo Zhang
abstract: In this work, total ionizing dose (TID) effects on p-GaN gate HEMTs under different drain-bias conditions are studied. A capacitance-based approach is proposed to spatially resolve and quantify irradiation-induced charges in the gate-to-drain access region. The results indicate that positive charges are generated in the AlGaN barrier and are strongly correlated with high electric fields, while negative charges dominate in the buffer and drain-side p-GaN layer. Deep-level transient spectroscopy (DLTS) and electron energy-loss spectroscopy (EELS) further reveal that the positive charges originate from the dissociation of O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">N</sub>–H complexes into donor-like O<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {N}}^{+}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> states near the AlGaN surface. These findings provide direct physical insight into irradiation-induced defect processes and offer guidance for the design of radiation-hardened p-GaN gate HEMTs for space power applications.

title: A Sensor-Aware Phenomenological Framework for LiDAR Degradation Simulation and SLAM Robustness Evaluation
authors: Doumegna Mawuto Koudjo Felix, Xianjia Yu, Zhuo Zou, Tomi Westerlund
abstract: LiDAR-based SLAM systems are highly sensitive to adverse conditions such as occlusion, noise, and field-of-view (FoV) degradation, yet existing robustness evaluation methods either lack physical grounding or do not capture sensor-specific behavior. This paper presents a sensor-aware, phenomenological framework for simulating interpretable LiDAR degradations directly on real point clouds, enabling controlled and reproducible SLAM stress testing. Unlike image-derived corruption benchmarks (e.g., SemanticKITTI-C) or simulation-only approaches (e.g., lidarsim), the proposed system preserves per-point geometry, intensity, and temporal structure while applying structured dropout, FoV reduction, Gaussian noise, occlusion masking, sparsification, and motion distortion. The framework features autonomous topic and sensor detection, a modular configuration with four predefined severity tiers (light–extreme), and real-time performance ($< 5$ ms per frame for solid-state LiDAR and $< 20$ ms for dense, wide-FoV spinning LiDAR). The implementation is Docker-containerized and compatible with ROS workflows. Experimental validation across three LiDAR models and five state-of-the-art SLAM systems reveals distinct patterns of robustness shaped by sensor design and environmental context. The open-source implementation provides a practical foundation for benchmarking LiDAR-based SLAM under physically meaningful degradation scenarios.

title: A Heatmap-Guided Two-Stage Framework for Energy-Efficient UAV-assisted IoT Data Collection
authors: Pengfei Wu, Jinyi Wang, Chao Sha, Haiping Huang, Fu Xiao
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things devices in smart cities presents a massive data collection challenge, where Unmanned Aerial Vehicles offer a flexible but energy-limited solution. A fundamental tension exists within these systems between the goal of maximizing valuable, non-redundant information and the critical need to minimize flight time and energy consumption. However, existing learning-based solutions are often inefficient and fundamentally unscalable, requiring costly retraining to adapt when the network size changes. To resolve this challenge, this paper introduces <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">U</u>AV <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</u>rajectory <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">I</u>nference via <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>onvolutional <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>ttention (UTICA), a novel and scalable trajectory optimization algorithm built on a Training-Inference Separation principle. UTICA leverages a Graph Convolutional Network trained only once offline on small, fixed-size problem instances. To achieve scalability and generalization to larger networks, it employs a sampling, transformation, and merging mechanism to generate a powerful heuristic heatmap for any large-scale waypoint set. The resulting heatmap then guides an efficient Monte Carlo Tree Search to discover the optimal trajectory. UTICA serves as the core of our two-stage optimization framework, which is preceded by the <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>aximizing <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">I</u>nformation and <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>uppressing <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RED</u>undancy (MISRED) algorithm, an information-theoretic submodular maximization model that identifies the most valuable, redundancy-aware waypoints. Comprehensive simulations, validated with real-world sensor data, validate the efficacy of our framework. Our approach outperforms established baseline methods, reducing flight distance by up to 12.05% and total energy consumption accordingly. Crucially, the framework demonstrates robust generalization across networks scaling from 100 to 500 nodes, confirming its practical value for dynamic and large-scale applications.

title: Modulation Dynamics of Ion Acoustic Envelope Modes and Associated Solitary, Shock, and Rogue Waves in a Viscous Electron–Positron–Ion Plasma
authors: M. M. Hasan, S. Sultana, A. Mannan, A. A. Mamun
abstract: A nonlinear Schrödinger equation (NLSE) has been formulated using the multiple scales perturbation technique in a dissipative electron–positron–ion (e-p-i) plasma, which contains inertial ions, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$q$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-distributed electrons, and Maxwellian positrons to investigate the dynamics of ion acoustic waves (IAWs). This work examines dissipative solitary and shock structures, modulational instability (MI), and the generation of rogue waves (RWs). The amplitude and width of solitary and shock structures are greatly affected by ion viscosity, nonthermal electrons, and Maxwellian positrons. The conditions for MI associated with IAWs and the fundamental characteristics of ion acoustic RWs (IARWs) are examined. The influence of plasma characteristics on the transition between stable and unstable areas, as well as the first and second-order RWs, are investigated in the unstable region. The results of our study may aid in understanding the nonlinear dynamics in astrophysical and laboratory plasmas.

title: Single-Stage Three-Phase AC-DC DAB Converter With Y-Connected Transformer Winding
authors: Mafu Zhang, Huanghaohe Zou, Saleh Farzamkia, Zibo Chen, Chen Chen, Alex Q. Huang
abstract: Three-phase single-stage AC-DC dual-active-bridge (DAB)-type converters are attractive isolated power-factor-correction (PFC) solutions for grid-tied battery charging applications requiring galvanic isolation and high efficiency. However, existing converters of this type often suffer from a high device count and complex modulation and control. To address these issues, this paper proposes an AC-DC YAB converter that employs an AC-side Y-connected winding to reshape the winding current. Compared with the conventional AC-DC DAB converter, the proposed topology reduces the device count and control complexity while maintaining comparable efficiency. A numerical steady-state model is developed to analyze the proposed converter. Comparative results show that, under uncompensated DC-side sinusoidal phase shift (Sin-PS) modulation, the proposed converter achieves lower current total harmonic distortion (THD) and reduced current stress than the conventional AC-DC DAB converter. A 100 kHz, 6 kW prototype for 480 V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_\rm{AC}$</tex-math></inline-formula> to 200–300 V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_\rm{DC}$</tex-math></inline-formula> operation is built to validate the proposed converter, and experimental results demonstrate a peak efficiency of 97.1%.

title: Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation Based on Boundary Awareness and Multi-scale Spatial Descriptors
authors: Haoxue Jia, Xiaoli Liang, Yunxiang Liu, Zhiqiang Qin, Ke Liu, Cancong Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: 3D point cloud semantic segmentation is essential for 3D environment perception and scene understanding. A key challenge lies in enhancing object boundary segmentation accuracy and capturing global contextual features efficiently. However, most existing methods optimize boundaries only through local feature enhancement or explicit boundary optimization, and struggle to comprehensively capture global information. Therefore, we propose BAMS-Net, a novel point cloud semantic segmentation network, integrating boundary awareness and multi-scale spatial descriptors. Our model innovatively fuses local feature enhancement with explicit boundary optimization in depth, enabling improvement in boundary delineation. Specifically, candidate boundary points are first determined through a boundary prediction module based on local neighborhood semantic consistency, and an adaptive weight learning mechanism is adopted to suppress the feature interference from boundary points in their neighborhoods. Then the boundary segmentation performance is further improved through a boundary optimization aggregation loss that combines local semantic boundary loss and adaptive aggregation loss. To effectively capture global context, an adaptive calibration module is used to reweight the multi-level local features and strengthen the critical information, then the enhanced local features of each layer are converted into multi-scale global descriptor through cross-level fusion, thereby efficiently capturing global contextual information. Experimental results on the S3DIS and SensatUrban datasets demonstrate that BAMS-Net extracts richer point cloud features, delivers precise object boundary segmentation, and achieves superior overall performance in point cloud semantic segmentation.

title: A Bayesian Optimization Method for Design Space Exploration of Processors with Efficient Evaluation Budget Allocation
authors: Yuhang Zhu, Mingyuan Hou, Xiaoliang Lv, Qing-shan Jia, Xiaohong Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Design space exploration (DSE), which aims to optimize the parameter configurations for the processor with a limited evaluation budget, is an important but challenging problem. Processor performance for a given parameter configuration can only be obtained through expensive evaluations. A key characteristic of processor DSE is that the optimization objective is the benchmark score, which is typically computed as a weighted combination of multiple sub-benchmark scores. Each sub-benchmark score can only be obtained through a costly evaluation. Bayesian optimization (BO) is widely used for DSE through an iterative process, where a surrogate model is constructed and updated to guide the selection of parameter configurations in each iteration. However, during the optimization process, it is not always necessary to evaluate all subbenchmarks for every recommended parameter configuration. In some cases, evaluating only a subset of sub-benchmarks can still provide significant improvements to the surrogate model. This paper proposes a BO-based algorithm that effectively utilizes the evaluation budget for efficient optimization. In each iteration, the evaluation budget is divided into two parts: exploitation and exploration. For the exploitation part, we propose a diversity-aware horse racing selection rule and use ordinal optimization to identify configurations with a high likelihood of being optimal. They are evaluated on all sub-benchmarks to obtain the total scores. For the exploration part, we propose a conditional entropy-based greedy algorithm to allocate the evaluation budget across parameter configurations and their corresponding sub-benchmarks, which can effectively improve the surrogate model. We conduct a comprehensive numerical experiment to validate the effectiveness of both components of our algorithm. Furthermore, experiments on three real-world DSE problems demonstrate that our algorithm outperforms state-of-the-art Bayesian optimization methods.

title: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: A Constant-Gain Equation-Error Framework for Airliner Aerodynamic Monitoring Using QAR Data
authors: Ruiying Wen, Yuntao Dai, Hongyong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Monitoring in-service aerodynamic performance of airliners is critical for operational efficiency and safety, yet presents significant challenges when using operational Quick Access Recorder (QAR) data due to sensor noise, low excitation, and the absence of key model parameters like moments of inertia. These constraints render conventional state-propagation and recursive estimation methods unsuitable. To address these challenges, this paper proposes and validates the Constant-Gain Equation-Error Method (CG-EEM), a robust framework tailored for QAR data analysis. The CG-EEM employs a custom constant-gain estimator that avoids both the infeasibility of state-propagation filters and the premature convergence or instability issues of standard recursive algorithms. Extensive validation on a multi-fleet dataset of over 200 flights demonstrates that the framework produces highly consistent and physically meaningful aerodynamic parameters. Crucially, follow-up work has verified that this approach successfully resolves the fundamental thrust-drag ambiguity problem, ensuring the estimates are not just plausible, but physically unique and correct. This demonstrates that CG-EEM is a scalable and computationally efficient tool for reliable fleet-wide performance monitoring and early detection of airframe degradation.

title: Interpretable Trust Assessment of Early Warning for Driver-Assistance Systems Using EEG
authors: Xianghao Meng, Wenshuo Wang, Cheng Shao, Hui Yao, Ruizeng Zhang, Junqiang Xi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate assessment of human trust in driver assistance systems is crucial for enhancing user acceptance and system safety. While prior research has explored physiological signals like skin conductance and heart rate to gauge trust, the link between these signals and trust remains insufficiently understood. Here, we present a novel approach to interpreting driver trust in intelligent warning systems by integrating subjective measures from questionnaires with objective electroencephalography (EEG) data. We develop TrustNet, a model leveraging separable convolution to capture the spatiotemporal dynamics of EEG signals and class activation mapping (CAM) to identify trust-relevant features. TrustNet achieves superior performance in trust assessment and classification, with accuracy and F1 score both exceeding 87%. CAM analysis reveals that EEG beta- and gamma-wave changes in the occipital and frontal regions are strongly associated with trust dynamics. Misclassification analysis highlights sensor noise and individual differences in response variability as key factors affecting performance. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of EEG-based trust assessment, offering new avenues for adaptive driver assistance systems responsive to human trust.

title: Linear and Nonlinear Propagation Models for Coupled-Core Multi-Core Fibers
authors: Tianqi Cheng, Lin Ma, Mingjing Xu, Zuyuan He
abstract: We propose linear and nonlinear models for studying optical propagation characteristics in coupled-core multi-core fibers (CC-MCFs). In the linear regime, analysis reveals that the spatial modal dispersion (SMD) originates from the group velocity mismatch among core modes and the wavelength dependence of inter-core coupling coefficient. Meanwhile, appropriate bending can minimize the average group delay difference under different core pitch conditions. The presented linear propagation equation can be employed to investigate the impulse response of CC-MCFs for varying inter-core coupling strengths. In the nonlinear regime, we find that the Manakov equations derived from both core mode and super-mode frameworks have consistent forms when small inter-core overlap effects are neglected. Despite rapid variations in super-mode field distribution caused by bending and twisting, the effective nonlinear coefficient (ENC) is determined solely by the number of cores and the nonlinear coefficient of core mode. The method we proposed for generating group delays of eigenmodes within a segment during random mode coupling simulation can be applied to nonlinear transmission modeling. This work will contribute to the understanding of both linear and nonlinear propagation characteristics in CC-MCFs.

title: Attention Shaping for GenAI Security Risks
authors: David Kinnberg Hein, John Stouby Persson, Anders Rysholt Bruun, Martin Gilje Jaatun
abstract: The adoption of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in software development introduces new security risks that extend beyond traditional threat models. When facing these new risks, security managers need to deliberately manage their attention as a critical resource. To support this, we conducted a multi-case study with four software organizations that distilled ten key security risks related to GenAI. Instead of a static checklist, we offer a socio-technical analysis that reveals three distinct attention shapes for the security risks associated with GenAI, specifically regarding governance, operation, and implementation. These attention shapes unfold how security risks are entangled with a software organization’s structure, actors, technologies, and tasks. We explain how such attention shaping is suitable for security managers to uncover socio-technical blind spots in managing GenAI security risks.

title: Constrained Diffusion Models for Synthesizing Representative Power Flow Datasets
authors: Milad Hoseinpour, Vladimir Dvorkin
abstract: High-quality power flow datasets are essential for training machine learning models in power systems. However, security and privacy concerns restrict access to real-world data, making statistically accurate and physically consistent synthetic datasets a viable alternative. We develop a diffusion model for generating synthetic power flow datasets from real-world power grids that both replicate the statistical properties of the real-world data and ensure AC power flow feasibility. To enforce the constraints, we incorporate gradient guidance based on the power flow constraints to steer diffusion sampling toward feasible samples. To enhance learning efficiency and reduce model complexity, we further leverage insights from the fast decoupled power flow method and propose a variable decoupling strategy for the training and sampling of the diffusion model. These solutions lead to a physics-informed diffusion model, generating power flow datasets that outperform those from the standard diffusion in terms of feasibility and statistical similarity, as shown in experiments across IEEE benchmark systems.

title: Model Predictive Active Damping Control of Dual Active Bridge Converter With Input LC Filter
authors: Changming Zheng, Haorui Wu, Baowang Lu, Miao Xie, Zheng Gong, Tomislav Dragičević
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The integration of an input LC filter with a dual-active-bridge (DAB) converter improves the quality of the input current. However, the interaction between the filter and converter can induce resonant instability. To address this issue, this article proposes a model-predictive active-damping control (MPADC) scheme for stabilizing the LC-filtered DAB converter system. First, an improved cost function is developed by incorporating output-voltage regulation and virtual-admittance-based active damping, reshaping the DAB converter’s input impedance and effectively suppressing resonance. Then, an MPADC scheme with a continuous-control set is developed, which improves steady-state performance, enhances robustness to model mismatches, and reduces the number of physical sensors. Furthermore, theoretical analysis of the closed-loop impedance stability and robustness of the proposed MPADC is provided. Experimental results confirm the superiority of the proposed strategy over conventional MPC approaches.

title: Oscillation Mechanism and Cooperative Optimization Control Strategy for MMC-HVDC and DC Voltage Divider
authors: Yinghong Hu, Yuan Zhao, Pengfei Liu, Fangfang Zhang, Yu Li
abstract: To address the DC voltage instability caused by a single-component fault in the resistor-capacitor voltage divider (RCVD) of the MMC-HVDC system, this paper presents the oscillation mechanism under the coupling effect of MMC-HVDC's constant DC voltage control and RCVD, and develops the dynamic regulation strategy for voltage division ratio (VDR) and the VDR limiting control for RCVD. First, the impedance analysis method is adopted to construct the DC-side impedance model of the MMC-HVDC system with RCVD. Subsequently, stability analysis and numerical simulations reveal the mechanism of DC voltage oscillation instability induced by the interaction between MMC-HVDC and RCVD. An electromagnetic transient model is then developed and validated through RTDS hardware-in-the-loop experiments and comparisons with on-site fault recordings. To mitigate the instability, a dynamic regulation strategy and a limiting control strategy for the VDR are proposed. The former stabilizes the VDR around its reference value through dynamic correction, while the latter constrains it within an acceptable range in real time. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategies effectively suppress DC busbar voltage divergence during RCVD faults, providing support for fault-tolerant control of MMC-HVDC systems.

title: Multi-Tone Side-Channel Noise Mitigation in an SST
authors: Debotrinya Sur, Sudip K. Mazumder, Mateo D. Roig Greidanus, Shantanu Gupta, Luiz Fernando M. Arruda
abstract: In this work, a novel adaptive-sampling (AS)-aided spectral decomposition (SD) framework is proposed to mitigate the detrimental impact of multi-tone side-channel noise intrusion (SNI) on the dynamic performance of modular solid-state transformers (SSTs). The catastrophic consequences of SNI on SST control and protection have been extensively documented in recent literature, with most existing mitigation strategies relying heavily on precise converter models for noise-free signal estimation. A recent study introduced an SD-based approach for SNI mitigation in modular SSTs; however, its performance significantly deteriorates under multi-tone noise spectra, which are increasingly prevalent in practical grid environments. The proposed AS-SD method enhances closed-loop resiliency by dynamically adjusting the analog-to-digital converter (ADC) sampling frequency to achieve spectral separation between the fundamental signal and injected noise components. This separation minimizes leakage and allows accurate signal reconstruction without requiring detailed converter models. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is experimentally validated on a cascaded SST prototype subject to conductively injected multi-tone noise, demonstrating robust signal reconstruction and improved disturbance rejection under adverse side-channel intrusion scenarios.

title: PCS-UNETR: A Physically Constrained SwinUNETR Model for Robust Seismic First Arrival Picking
authors: Yang Li, Haiwei Guo, Xianfeng Gong, Yundong Guo, Duanshi Lv
abstract: To address the challenges of low accuracy, poor noise resistance, and inefficiency in seismic first arrival picking, this paper proposes Physical-Constraint SwinUNETR (PCS-UNETR). First, a SoftLabel dataset is constructed by converting seismic traces into single-band 2D images with Gaussian probability density labels to enrich gradient information. Second, a physical prior-constrained SwinUNETR model is designed. By incorporating an amplitude change rate penalty term into the loss function, the model is forced to identify arrivals on the rising edge of the first peak. Finally, morphological operations define a region of interest, where the pixel with the maximum predicted probability is selected as the final pick. Experiments on Sinopec Zhongyuan Oilfield's Puguang seismic data (10.24 million traces) demonstrate that PCS-UNETR effectively reduces outliers, significantly improves accuracy and noise resistance, and achieves full automation without manual intervention. The innovation lies in fusing physical priors with deep learning via a "global probability prediction + local morphological constraint" mechanism, offering a new approach for intelligent seismic processing with high application value.

title: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Forensic Sciences: A Comprehensive Dataset and Evaluation Framework
authors: Ashmaan Sohail, Om M. Patel, Jack C. S. Venditti, Jihwan Choi, Addison J. Wu
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated substantial progress in medical and legal domains in recent years; however, their capabilities from the lens of forensic science, a field at the intersection of complex medical reasoning and legal interpretation, remains largely unexplored. Forensic medicine uniquely depends on the accurate integration of often ambiguous text and visual information, yet systematic evaluations of MLLMs in this setting are lacking.We conducted a comprehensive benchmarking study of eleven state-of-the-art MLLMs, including proprietary (GPT-4o, Claude 4 Sonnet, Gemini 2.5 Flash) and open-source (Llama 4, Qwen 2.5-VL) models. Models were evaluated on 847 examination-style forensic questions drawn from various academic literature, case studies, and clinical assessments, covering nine forensic subdomains. Both text-only and image-based questions were included. Model performance was assessed using direct and chain-of-thought prompting, with automated scoring verified through manual revision. Performance improved consistently with newer model generations. Chain-of-thought prompting improved accuracy on text-based and multiple-choice tasks for most models, though this trend did not hold for image-based and open-ended questions. Visual reasoning and complex inference tasks revealed persistent limitations, with models underperforming in image interpretation and nuanced forensic scenarios. Model performance remained stable across forensic subdomains, suggesting topic type alone did not drive variability. These findings provide the first systematic baseline for MLLM performance in forensic science and inform pathways for their cautious integration into medico-legal workflows.

title: DistilBERT-Based Hybrid Architecture for Phishing URL Detection
authors: Ülkü Özmen, Esra Odabas Yildirim
abstract: Phishing continues to be a major and rapidly evolving challenge in cybersecurity. By disguising malicious links as legitimate ones, attackers trick users into revealing sensitive information such as login credentials and financial details. Detecting phishing URLs is difficult because modern attacks rely on heavy obfuscation and are often lexically very similar to benign domains. In this paper, we propose a hybrid late-fusion approach that combines deep semantic (word) representations with handcrafted statistical features for phishing URL detection. We select the fusion weight using AUCPR-driven optimization on a validation set and obtain an optimal value of α = 0.47, which balances the contribution of semantic and lexical signals. Experiments on a large-scale benchmark dataset show that the proposed fusion model is highly effective. It reaches an accuracy of 98.79% and a ROC–AUC of 0.9983, and it performs better than either feature stream on its own. Under a strict security setting (FPR ≤ 1%), the system still achieves a phishing detection rate of 97.58%. To evaluate real-world robustness, we additionally perform external validation on 150 verified phishing URLs from PhishTank collected between December 2025 and February 2026, well after the benchmark dataset was compiled. Without any retraining, the model achieves a Recall/TPR of 99.33%, correctly flagging 149 out of 150 previously unseen phishing URLs. Overall, these results indicate strong temporal generalization and highlight the practical promise of the proposed method for real-world cybersecurity deployments.

title: A Miniaturized, Wideband and High-Gain Omnidirectional Dielectric Filled Fabry–Pérot Cavity Antenna
authors: Mingxuan Liang, Qing-Yi Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a methodology for designing a wideband, high-gain omnidirectional antenna by utilizing the Fabry–Pérot cavity (FPC) effect. It is the first attempt to apply high dielectric constant materials to fill the cavity, forming the proposed dielectric filled cavity (DFC). It not only reduces the size of the antenna, but also provides intrinsic partially reflection at the interface between dielectric and air, contributing to the elimination of a separate PRS. Furthermore, the proposed FPC architecture is developed to be an omnidirectional structure while keeping wideband and high-gain radiation. The proposed antenna consists of the DFC, a modified omnidirectional stepped reflector (MOSR), and a horizontally polarized omnidirectional feeding source. The MOSR can be used to excite multiple resonant modes and manipulate the phase distribution on radiation aperture, enhancing bandwidth and gain. For verification, an antenna prototype based on the above operating principles is designed, fabricated and measured. It achieves an impedance bandwidth of 19.61% and a peak gain of 6.2 dBi. The gain fluctuation is less than 1 dB within the operating bandwidth. Compared with other omnidirectional antennas, the proposed design possesses the characteristics of wideband, high-gain, and a compact size. It is a good candidate for indoor coverage equipment with penetrability and corrosion-resistant outdoor cellular terminals.

title: Modulated Lapped Transform Based OFDM-IM with Low Complex Deep Neural Network Detector
authors: Ahmed Waheed, Smaya Moher
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper presents a novel modulated lapped transform-based orthogonal frequency division multiplexing with index modulation (MLT-OFDM-IM) scheme combined with a low-complexity deep neural network (DNN) detector. The main objective is to meet the stringent demands of future industrial wireless networks, including ultra-reliability and low-latency communication. The proposed MLT-OFDM-IM exploits the time-frequency localization properties of the modulated lapped transform (MLT) to show robustness against carrier frequency offset (CFO) and inter-carrier interference (ICI). A closed-form mathematical expression based on pair-wise error probability is derived to estimate the bit error probability (BEP) of MLT-OFDM-IM for performance analysis. Moreover, a low-complexity DNN-based detector for MLT-based OFDM-IM is proposed that can achieves superior performance compared to traditional maximum likelihood (ML) detection under perfect channel state information (CSI) and imperfect CSI. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms existing competitive benchmark schemes, such as FFT based OFDM-IM and OFDM. Specifically, at SE of 1.25 bps/Hz and BEP of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup>, our proposed scheme achieves an additional gain of 3 dB.

title: A Piezoelectric MFC Enabled Active Micro-Force Sensor with High Range-to-Resolution Ratio for Cross-Scale Robotic Cell Micromanipulation
authors: Shengzheng Kang, Xiaolong Yang, Yao Li, Jiafeng Yao, Tao Li
abstract: During robotic cell manipulation tasks, micro-force sensors play an important role to provide real-time force feedback for precise interaction between the robotic manipulator and the environmental cells. In order to accommodate a larger cross-scale range of forces, this paper presents an active micro-force sensing strategy based on a macro-fiber composite (MFC) enabled piezoelectric cantilever operating in a closed-loop force-balancing mode. Unlike conventional passive piezoelectric sensors whose usable range is limited by structural nonlinearity and stiffness, the proposed sensor employs a symmetric MFC sandwich beam, in which one MFC layer performs force sensing while the other provides counteracting actuation under a robust model predictive control scheme. By actively maintaining near-zero tip deflection, the applied micro-force is inferred directly from the balancing actuation voltage rather than from the structural deflection. A distributed-parameter dynamic model of the composite beam is derived using modal superposition, enabling precise controller synthesis and state estimation via a Kalman observer. Calibration experiments demonstrate a sensing resolution of 10.12 μN over a measurement range of 21.53 mN, resulting in a range-to-resolution ratio of 2127.5, significantly exceeding that of existing piezoelectric micro-force sensors. After that, the effectiveness of the developed sensor is further validated in robotic manipulation of zebrafish embryos and onion cells. The results confirm that the active MFC enabled force sensor provides a practical route to wide-range, high-resolution force sensing for delicate biological micromanipulation tasks.

title: Uncooled Poisson Bolometer for High-Speed Event-Based Long-wave Thermal Imaging
authors: Mohamed A. Mousa, Leif Bauer, Utkarsh Singh, Ziyi Yang, Angshuman Deka, Zubin Jacob
abstract: Event-based vision provides high-speed, energy-efficient sensing for applications such as autonomous navigation and motion tracking. However, implementing this technology in the long-wave infrared remains a significant challenge. Traditional infrared sensors are limited by either slow thermal response times or the high power demands of cryogenic cooling. Here, we introduce the first event-based infrared detector operating in a Poisson-counting regime. This is realized with a spintronic Poisson bolometer capable of experimentally validated broadband detection from 3–14 μm across the MWIR and LWIR atmospheric transmission windows, with sensitivity estimated to extend into the SWIR regime down to 0.8 μm based on near-infrared transduction layer characterization. In this regime, infrared signals are detected through statistically resolvable changes in stochastic switching events. This approach enables room-temperature operation with high timing resolution. Our device achieves a maximum event rate of 1,250 Hz, exceeding the maximum detectable event rate of conventional uncooled microbolometers by a factor of 3.6. Power consumption is kept low at 0.2μW per pixel. These results demonstrate a pathway toward high-speed, energy-efficient, event-driven thermal imaging.

title: Text-guided Vision Token Reduction with Low-rank Adaptation for Efficient Visual Grounding
authors: Liangtao Shi, Ting Liu, Jinxia Xie, Ning Li, Bineng Zhong, Richang Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Transformer-based pretrained models have advanced Visual Grounding (VG) significantly, but their scaling up has caused soaring training and inference costs. While efforts adapting Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning to VG have cut training expenses to some extent, inference costs are over-looked, stemming from Transformer’s computation costs growing quadratically with input token length. To address the above challenge, we propose a text-guided token prune method based on a one-stream architecture for VG, named OneSVG. Specifically, OneSVG transfers low-rank adaptation LoRA to VG, and calculates the relevance between each vision token and text semantics in multiple stages, and gradually prunes the vision tokens with low relevance. In addition, traditional VG methods use a single [REG] token to predict bounding boxes, and the [REG] token relies on complete vision tokens during training, pruning large number of vision tokens will disrupt the spatial orientation information of images. Therefore, the prediction head in OneSVG first restores the square structure of images by padding the missing area, and then accepts complete vision tokens. Experimental results on three widely-used benchmarks demonstrate that our OneSVG achieves state-of-the-art real-time speed while maintaining the best accuracy with only 34.3% tokens. Codes and models are available at https://github.com/ltShi/OneSVG.

title: A Wireless Microwave Monitoring System for Measuring the Pollutants of Triclosan and Glyphosate in Water
authors: Wen-Jing Wu, Hao Xie, Wen-Sheng Zhao, Wensong Wang
abstract: A wireless microwave monitoring system for measuring the pollutants of triclosan and glyphosate in water is proposed in this manuscript. The proposed wireless microwave monitoring system is a long-range, low-power internet of thighs (IoT) system, which can serve as a sensing node for monitoring water quality in a large-scale water area. The microwave sensing system and the LoRa-based wireless communication module constitute the real-time online wireless microwave monitoring network. The microwave sensing system is composed of the multiple stepped impedance transformer (MSIT) with an open-ended stub, radio frequency (RF) oscillator, and frequency demodulation circuit. To suppress the external interference, the microwave sensing system is designed as the differential configuration. The test channel of microwave sensing system outputs the pollutant concentration-dependent DC voltage, transmits this DC voltage to a laptop terminal via LoRa transceiver module, and the host computer system on the laptop terminal can display the concentrations of triclosan and glyphosate in distilled water by the trained BP-NN model. In measurement, a sensitivity of 1.33553 mV/(μg/mL) and a limit of detection (LOD) of 1.001 μg/mL are calculated for water-triclosan mixture. In contrast, a sensitivity of 0.87638 mV/(μg/mL) and a LOD of 1.525 μg/mL are obtained for water-glyphosate mixture. The measured results demonstrate that the proposed wireless microwave monitoring system is an attractive role for industrial application.

title: When Does Dialogue Flourish? A Comparative Study of Dialogue Motivation Depending on Negative Attitudes toward Robots
authors: Takahisa Uchida, Midori Ban, Takashi Minato, Hiroshi Ishiguro
abstract: In recent years, dialogue-support systems involving robots and AI have been spreading. However, the psychological factors that underpin people’s willingness to engage in dialogue remain unclear. We conducted an online vignette-based survey in which participants imagined having non-task-oriented conversations with three interlocutors (a robot, a voice-only AI agent, and a human; within-participant design). We examined how six psychological factors (trust, social presence, enjoyment, self-disclosure, anthropomorphism, and likeability) were associated with motivation to continue the dialogue, while treating negative attitudes toward robots (NARS) as a continuous moderator and controlling for age and gender. Linear mixed-effects modeling showed that enjoyment was the strongest predictor of dialogue motivation, followed by likeability and self-disclosure. Trust was also positively associated with motivation. NARS did not show a direct effect after controlling for these proximal factors, but it significantly moderated the effects of enjoyment and self-disclosure. Adjusted means differed slightly across interlocutors (AI > human), suggesting limited but detectable partner-type differences once perceptions were accounted for. We discuss design-relevant prioritization of interaction qualities while noting the constraints of vignette-based, self-reported intentions.

title: AS-RAM: Automated Search with Rich Attention Modules for Network Traffic Prediction
authors: Jongkyung Im, Sung Oh, Myungjun Oh, Ji-yeon Park, Joung-Sik Kim, Na-rae Yi, Myungho Kim, Sung-Ho Bae
abstract: By effectively forecasting network traffic patterns, future cellular networks have the potential to manage themselves and incorporate intelligent, streamlined operations autonomously. Existing methods have relied on handcrafted architectures to predict network traffic, which still have two main limitations. First, human-designed models may have sub-optimality in modeling. Second, existing handcrafted designs did not take spatio-temporal long-term dependencies into account explicitly even though they play crucial roles in network traffic prediction. To address these issues, we propose a method using NAS (Neural Architecture Search) to find more optimized architectures than handcrafted ones for network traffic prediction. Specifically, we introduce a new search space that integrates attention modules enabling better extraction and reflection of the unique characteristics of network traffic data. These attention modules are seamlessly incorporated into the architecture from an optimization perspective. To verify the effectiveness of the proposed method, we validate the performance of the proposed framework using the Italia Telecom dataset. Experimental results show that our framework significantly improves the performance compared to the baseline in terms of root mean squared error between predicted value and ground-truth value. Specifically, our method improves the performance by 9.5%, 9.8%, and 10.2% on SMS, CALL, and INTERNET traffic datasets, respectively.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on Synthetic Network Traffic Generation
authors: Nirhoshan Sivaroopan, Kaushitha Silva, Chamara Madarasingha, Thilini Dahanayaka, Guillaume Jourjon, Anura Jayasumana, Kanchana Thilakarathna
abstract: Synthetic network traffic generation has emerged as a promising alternative for various data-driven applications in the networking domain. It enables the creation of synthetic data that preserves real-world characteristics while addressing key challenges such as data scarcity, privacy concerns, and purity constraints associated with real data. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive review of synthetic network traffic generation approaches, covering essential aspects such as data types and generation models. With the rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), we focus particularly on deep learning (DL)-based techniques while also providing a detailed discussion of statistical methods and their extensions, including commercially available tools. We present a comprehensive comparison of generation approaches and provide an AI tool to apply this comparison for any network traffic generation papers. Furthermore, we highlight open challenges in this domain and discuss potential future directions for further research and development. This survey serves as a foundational resource for researchers and practitioners, offering a structured analysis of existing methods, challenges, and opportunities in synthetic network traffic generation.

title: Improved Harris Hawk Optimized Multi-Feature-Based Generative Adversarial Network for Effective Video Summarization
authors: Vidya M Bharde, Zahir Aalam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Video summarization involves interpreting the video contents and extracting the keyframes to create a summarized video that accurately conveys the real content. The summarization allows users to rapidly grasp the core information. However, the prevailing methods have complexities in dealing with longer videos and noises in graphical elements, leading to the generation of redundant content. Therefore, the research proposes an Improved Harris Hawk optimized multi-feature-based Generative adversarial Network (ImH2-MFGN) for addressing the complexities and to achieve rapid summarization. The Improved Harris Hawk optimization (HHO) algorithm includes the updation of random parameters using the Levy mechanism to precisely acquire the targeted regions, resulting in effective weight tuning. Moreover, the Multi-frame Feature Extractor (MuFE) provides a better representation of the video frame by acquiring the intensity differences, direction of object flow, as well as granular-level features. Furthermore, the frame refinement using the Enhanced Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (ESRGAN) deals with the missing fragments and conserves the semantic continuity of the frames. The evaluation of ImH2-MFGN with the conventional approaches exhibits the potential in video summarization by acquiring a lower Mean absolute error of 0.74 with a higher structural similarity index measure of 0.91 and peak signal-to-noise ratio of 58.13 decibels.

title: Virtual 2-Phase Flow Metering Using Multitask Learning
authors: Kehinde Owoeye
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Flow meters are useful in the water, manufacturing and oil and gas industries to measure the flow rate of fluids, including gas and oil. Virtual flow meters (VFMs) on the other hand are cost effective software based intelligent systems for estimating flow rates given previous sensor readings and process conditions such as temperature and pressure leveraging computational methods. This helps reduce overall operational and maintenance costs for maintaining or replacing flow meter hardware and also acts as a backup when there is hardware failure. Several machine learning methods have been proposed in the past to estimate flow rates for virtual flow meters, but these methods either do not scale in some cases or do not utilize effectively the inherent dependencies in the data, especially for multiphase flow meters. In this work therefore, we show first, the limitations of previous methods for multiphase virtual flow metering and then, we propose a framework for overcoming these challenges. More specifically, we cast the virtual flow metering problem as a regression task, leveraging an attention network where we augment the learning of one phase with the learning of another phase of the flow meter. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on a real world production dataset and consequently show that it outperforms several baselines in the literature.

title: Vulnerabilities of IllusionCAPTCHA: An Empirical Analysis and a Stronger Human-Easy AI-Hard Design
authors: Nuthan B, Abhinav L G, Chethohaar J M, Pranav A, Minal Moharir, Sebastian Biedermann, Aaron Kutzner
publish: Proceedings of the 19th European Workshop on Systems Security
abstract: None

title: Satellite Imagery and AI-Based Detection of Common Waterhemp (AMATU) Infestation in Soybean Fields
authors: Nourhene Aloui, Sandra Flores-Mejia, Lokman Sboui
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: Recent advances in Earth Observation (EO) and artificial intelligence (AI) have enabled scalable solutions for sustainable crop management. In Quebec soybean fields, weeds such as common waterhemp (Amaranthus tuberculatus, AMATU) are spreading rapidly, showing strong resistance to herbicides and threatening crop productivity. To address this challenge, we propose an operational pipeline that combines very highresolution (0.5 m) multispectral Pleiades imagery, ground-verified infestation data, and supervised machine learning. Spectral indices are extracted from four bands, with Random Forest emerging as the most robust model (Accuracy: 0.818, F1-score: 0.821) compared to Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, KNN, SVM, and Linear Regression. SHAP analysis shows that near-infrared (NIR) based measurements are the most important for the model's decisions. The final output is a probability heatmap that highlights true infestation zones with minimal false negatives and limited false positives, mainly in bare soil or dense soybean areas. Results demonstrate that this approach provides a cost-effective, scalable tool for early detection and targeted management of AMATU. These results contribute to reducing herbicide inputs, production costs, and also promote more sustainable agricultural practices.

title: A Systematic Review of Metaheuristic MPPT Algorithms for PV Systems
authors: Yohanes Leonaldo Sinaga, Elya Syafa'atun Ni'mah, Muhammad Daffa Pratama, Arvina Rizqi Nurul'aini, Mohammad Mahruf Alam, Rizky Ajie Aprilianto
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Materials and Electrical Engineering (ISMEE)
abstract: Global energy demand has intensified with industrialization, electrification of transportation, and digital transformation, positioning solar photovoltaic (PV) technology as the fastest-growing renewable source. Nevertheless, the effective performance of PV systems is constrained by irradiance variability, temperature fluctuations, and partial shading conditions, which disrupt maximum power point (MPP) operation. Conventional MPP tracking methods, while simple, suffer from slow convergence and steady-state oscillations, reducing their effectiveness in large-scale gridconnected applications. To address these challenges, recent studies have explored metaheuristic algorithms. This paper provides a systematic review of metaheuristic-based Maximum Power Point Tracking for PV systems, conducted using the population, intervention, comparison, outcome, and context (PICOC) framework and preferred reporting items for systematic reviews and meta-analyses (PRISMA) methodology. A total of 52 articles published between 2021 and 2025 are analysed, focusing on four thresholds, efficiency, convergence time, oscillation, and computational complexity. The results show trends for Research Question (RQ1-RQ2), Particle Swarm Optimizer appears most frequently, followed by Genetic Algorithm. Simulation studies still dominate over hardware validation. For comparative performance, RQ2 highlighted the top 5 algorithms in the review, including PSObased sensorless backstepping with Immersion & Invariance tuning, Marine Predator and Mayfly Optimization duty-cycle search, Growth Optimization Algorithm with Deep Neural Network and Fractional Order Proportional-IntegralDerivative tuning, Reptile Search Algorithm two-stage duty optimization, and Snake Optimizer Algorithm. These top 5 algorithms surpassed the predefined thresholds across the four evaluation dimensions (efficiency, convergence time, oscillation, and algorithmic complexity) and thus merit priority for follow-up hardware validation and deployment.

title: Proposal of a Transform for the Detection of Cardiac Components in the Phonocardiogram of a Patient
authors: Cosme Dotonou, Roland Houessouvo, Patrick Sotindjo, Daton Medenou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Cardiovascular health surveillance is now benefiting from the rise of smart, connected technologies, which simplify the recording of biomedical signals outside the hospital setting. Consumer-accessible devices such as smart watches, smartphones and tablets are now able to collect physiological data and even contribute to diagnostic analyses through the integration of embedded algorithms. In this context, this paper proposes a method of treating pathological phonocardiograms (PCG) in the time domain, to accurately detect the heart sounds B1 and B2. The approach is based on the combination of the transform of the quadratic energy and the central derivative of the signal, providing better discrimination of heart sounds according to their amplitudes, The experimental results obtained with this new method show superior performance in terms of SNR (43.95 dB), PBR (1796.75).) and MEN (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.80 \times 10^{-5}$</tex>), in comparison with several approaches from the literature. This enables more accurate localization and differentiation of B1 and B2 heart sounds, even in the presence of pathological noises.

title: Third Eye: A Real-Time Assistive System for the Visually Impaired Using Speech and Object Detection
authors: S Sruti, Siddhi Jadhav, Sanika Karmarkar, Dynaeshwari Shinde, Saniya Patil, Uma Gurav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: This paper introduces Third Eye, an innovative assistive system tailored to support visually impaired individuals in navigating their surroundings. The system integrates advanced yet lightweight technologies, including Whisper for speech recognition and YOLOv8 for object detection, to deliver real-time auditory feedback about the environment. Designed to run entirely offline on a Raspberry Pi, the solution prioritizes affordability, portability, and hardware efficiency, making it accessible to a broader audience. Third Eye enables users to receive detailed descriptions of objects and scenes, enhancing situational awareness and promoting independence. Rigorous testing in real-world scenarios confirms the system's reliability in accurately identifying objects and converting speech to text. Its offline functionality eliminates the need for constant internet access, addressing connectivity challenges while keeping hardware costs low. This work establishes a robust foundation for developing comprehensive assistive tools, with future potential to incorporate advanced navigation and path finding capabilities, further empowering visually impaired individuals in their daily lives.

title: Encrypted Vector Operations for Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning and Data Retrieval
authors: Deepthi V S, Sahana B, Mangala L, Venkat Chavan Nagabhushana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: The growth of machine learning and advanced information retrieval raises serious privacy concerns when sensitive data is shared with untrusted parties. This work enables similar computations directly on encrypted data without revealing sensitive information. Partially Homomorphic Encryption with the Paillier cryptosystem is used to perform vector operations such as dot product, cosine similarity, and Euclidean distance securely in the encrypted domain. Experiments show near-plaintext accuracy while significantly reducing computational overhead compared to Fully Homomorphic Encryption. The results demonstrate that Partially Homomorphic Encryption provides a practical balance between security and efficiency, making it suitable for applications in healthcare, biometric verification, recommender systems, and federated learning.

title: Trust-Aware Ensemble Framework for Chronic Kidney Disease Severity Prediction Using Entropy and Clinical Rules
authors: Subashini. N. J, Subashini. B, K. Venkatesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) remains challenging to classify accurately, particularly when medical datasets are limited or imbalanced. This study presents a trustaware learning framework designed to enhance the reliability and interpretability of CKD stage prediction. The framework combines three key ideas: an entropy-based confidence measure to estimate prediction uncertainty, a local switching mechanism that selects the most reliable decision path for each patient record, and a clinical rule validation step that ensures predictions conform to nephrology standards. Real clinical data from 750 patients were refined through preprocessing, outlier removal, and class balancing to produce a dataset of 500 balanced samples containing 27 relevant features. The entropyguided design achieved near-perfect accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99-100 \%$</tex>) with low uncertainty. However, the rule-validation process indicated that about 56 % of the predictions required clinical correction, emphasizing the importance of domain-level reasoning. Feature explanation using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) identified Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR), serum creatinine, and blood urea as the most influential indicators. By integrating uncertainty modeling, clinical rule checks, and explainable AI, the framework offers a reliable and transparent solution for CKD severity assessment in practical healthcare settings.

title: Design of an Optimized Four-Port MIMO Antenna with Enhanced Isolation for 5G Applications
authors: Abdelilah Mansour, Wyssem Fathallah, Bassem Ben Salah, Hedi Sakli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper introduces a compact rectangular patch antenna design featuring a partial ground plane on a low-cost FR4-epoxy substrate, suitable for wireless communication systems. Simulations confirm excellent single-element performance at 5.9 GHz, achieving a reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>) below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-57 d B$</tex>, a peak gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 d B i$</tex>, and efficiency exceeding 90%. Following a systematic optimization process, a 4-port MIMO configuration based on this element was developed. This multi-antenna system demonstrates strong performance, resonating at 5.9 GHz with a wide 4 GHz bandwidth (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.5-8.5 \text{GHz}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}<-10 d B$</tex>) and excellent inter-port isolation exceeding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 d B$</tex>. The design maintains high gain and radiation efficiency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>90 {\%}$</tex>). Analysis of standard MIMO metrics (ECC, TARC, MEG, CCL) confirmed acceptable performance. In summary, the optimized 4-port design successfully reduces mutual coupling by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 d B$</tex> while preserving high gain, efficiency, and bandwidth.

title: Reliability Analysis of a Single-Chip Microcontrollerdriven Laser Ignition Circuit System
authors: Zechun Tian, Shuhong Ba, Fengsheng Lu, Aifeng He
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: To investigate the application reliability of laser ignition devices in aerospace systems, an intelligent laser ignition device circuit system with a single-chip microcontroller as the core was designed. The component stress analysis method and the GO method were used to calculate the failure rates of individual components and the overall laser ignition device circuit system, respectively. The calculation results were compared and analyzed against the requirements of GJB. The results show that: the failure rate of the signal control module in the designed intelligent laser ignition device circuit system is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.7105120 \times 10^{-5} ~\mathrm{h}^{-1}$</tex>; the failure rate of the signal amplification module is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.500389 \times 10^{-6} ~\mathrm{h}^{-1}$</tex>; the failure rate of the laser ignition drive module is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.201276 \times 10^{-6} ~\mathrm{h}^{-1}$</tex>; and the overall failure rate of the circuit system is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.7806785 \times 10^{-5} ~\mathrm{h}^{-1}$</tex>. After 58 consecutive hours of operation, the device still meets the aerospace initiator reliability index requirement of reliability > 0.999. This study provides design support and reliability data basis for the subsequent application of this technology, and lays a theoretical foundation for the application of the GO method in the reliability analysis of intelligent systems.

title: Deep Learning for Agricultural Weed Recognition: A Two-Phase Transfer Learning Strategy with Model Efficiency Evaluation
authors: Torana Kamble, Sheetal Rathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Weed management is one of the most difficult and costly issues that modern agriculture faces. The Integration of AI-based weed detection and robotic weeding systems is making farming more precise and sustainable. Still, to differentiate crop and weed at various growth stages is difficult as they share the same color and texture characteristics, which disturbs both productivity and profits. This study compares four lightweight CNN architectures, MobileNetV2, ShuffleNetV2, EfficientNetB0, and DenseNet121, for soybean weed detection through two-phase transfer learning on 15,336 annotated segments across four classes. Our multi-dimensional evaluation integrates classification metrics with computational profiling (inference time, throughput, model size) and training dynamics. All models performed exceptionally well, with EfficientNetB0 hitting 100% accuracy. While ShuffleNetV2 and MobileNetV2 demonstrated higher speed and resource efficiency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>99 \%$</tex> accuracy, <1% overfitting), whereas DenseNet121 was the heaviest model. This validates the twophase transfer learning approach for deployment on UAV and IoT platforms, with real-device testing needed to confirm practical performance.

title: A Novel Framework for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis from Eye-Tracking using Knowledge Graph Neural Network with Hiking Optimization Algorithm
authors: Senthil Kumar D, R Pugalenthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that impairs memory, cognitive functions, and daily activities. Early detection remains challenging due to subtle behavioral changes and the limitations of conventional diagnostic methods. To address these challenges, a novel framework for Alzheimer's disease diagnosis from eye-tracking using a Knowledge Graph Neural Network with Hiking Optimization Algorithm (KGNN-HOA) is introduced. Initially, eye-tracking data is collected from the MPIIFaceGaze dataset, providing rich information on gaze patterns, fixation durations, and saccades. This raw data is fed into the Cubature Kalman Filter (CubKF), which preprocesses the signals by removing noise and smoothing gaze trajectories. The preprocessed signals are then fed into the Quadratic Phase Quaternion Domain Fourier Transform (QP-QDoFT) to extract discriminative features that capture intricate eye movement dynamics. The extracted features are fed into a Knowledge Graph Neural Network (KGNN), where they are represented as nodes and inter-feature relationships are modeled for accurate Alzheimer's disease diagnosis. Finally, the KGNN outputs are fed into the Hiking Optimization Algorithm (HOA), which optimizes the model by minimizing classification loss, ensuring robust and precise detection. With accuracy of 99.8 %, recall of 99.4 %, F1-score of 99.6 % precision of 99.3 % and specificity of 99.5 %, experimental assessments show better performance than state-of-the-art techniques. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining graph-based feature modeling with advanced optimization for eye-tracking-based AD detection. This method is a great advance, a non-invasive way for early diagnosis, probably allowing quick actions and better patient outcomes. The suggested system integrates behavioral biomarkers with computational intelligence, offering an easily expandable solution for both clinical and research applications in Alzheimer's detection

title: HIRE-ML: Hiring Intelligence and Representative Evaluation via Machine Learning
authors: İbrahim Umut Bozlar, Yiğit Ateş, Alperen Sayar, Seyit Ertuğrul, Boran Ertuğrul
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study aims to predict the potential success of customer representatives in the factoring industry during the hiring process using machine learning models based on pre-employment survey. The target variable was derived from each employee's achievement of sales premium goals over the last six months.
