The dataset included demographic features such as age, gender, marital status, military service, and education level, in addition to 56 survey questions covering behavioral and industry-specific factors. To prevent data leakage, survey responses were collected before employment, ensuring that the model only used information available at the hiring stage. Among the tested models, only the LightGBM model achieved fair performance, with a test ROC-AUC of 0.7001, while all other models remained below 0.70. The decision threshold was optimized to achieve a precision of 0.75 for the positive class, aligning with the company's goal of achieving 75% successful hires. The results demonstrate the potential of data-driven hiring to improve decision-making and reduce turnover in sales-based roles.

title: OFDM Based Communication Model Incorporating Precoding and PSK Modulation Over a Regularized Zero Forcing (RZF) Channel
authors: N. Saranya, J. Friska, R. Sushmitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: In this paper we suggest an OFDM based massive MIMO communication system with Phase Shift Keying (PSK) modulation and Regularized Zero-Forcing (RZF) precoding in order to overcome the high computational cost and noise enhancement factors of traditional Zero-Forcing (ZF) systems. The main aim of the study is to improve the interference suppression, the Bit Error Rate (BER) performance, and the spectral efficiency in the large scale wireless systems. In the suggested approach, the PSK-modulated symbols are carried on the OFDM subcarriers and the RZF precoding is carried out at the transmitter to stabilize the channel inversion and reduce the multi-user interference. The system performance is measured with the help of MATLAB-based simulation with the variable Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), modulation parameter, and channel conditions. The results of the simulation obtained prove that the offered RZF-assisted OFDM system has a better BER behaviour, better signal quality and less computational load than traditional ZF-based systems with OFDM. The findings confirm the efficiency of the given methodology and emphasize its applicability to the next generation of the high-capacity wireless communication networks.

title: Integration of Symptoms Notions on Failure Causes for Diagnostic Process Enhancement
authors: Walid Mechri, Christophe Simon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This paper presents a probabilistic approach to enhance failure cause diagnostic, with a focus on the integration of symptom based analysis to improve isolation accuracy. The proposed method utilizes a Bayesian Network (BN) model, whose structure is derived from the transformation of a deterministic tree provided by a system expert. The BN approach enables continuous probability updates based on dataset inputs, allowing the model to adapt over time. Through the integration of expert knowledge, experience feedback, and symptom-based reason, this method enhances the precision and efficiency of failure isolation. The methodology is demonstrated through a case study, presented in a simplified format for clarity. The results highlight the potential to support informed maintenance decisions, with a contribution to improvement in the availability of the monitored system.

title: On-Device Thermal Regulation in Embedded Systems via Deep Q-Learning
authors: Oleh Yatskiv
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper presents an on-device inference Deep QLearning (DQL) approach for predictive thermal management in resource-constrained embedded systems. Traditional thresholdbased fan control methods often result in energy inefficiency and suboptimal thermal regulation, while existing reinforcement learning models either rely on offline inference or require substantial computational resources. To address this, we design and deploy a lightweight DQL agent on a Raspberry Pi Zero W, using only system temperature and prior fan state as input features. The proposed system learns an optimal cooling policy in real time without relying on cloud access, meta-training, or external sensors. Experimental results show a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23. 7 \%$</tex> reduction in fan activation while maintaining CPU temperature below 60°C for over 99% of the operational time. The method outperforms classical control strategies and several recent RL-based models in energy efficiency, responsiveness, and deployability. This work demonstrates the feasibility of deploying reinforcement learning for edge thermal control under tight hardware constraints and lays the foundation for scalable, adaptive cooling in the Internet of Things (IoT) and embedded AI ecosystems.

title: IoT-Enabled Virtual Doctor Robot
authors: S Kishore, Sumukh G, Sunitha S V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies in Electronics and Green Energy (ICETEG)
abstract: Doctors are commonly required in a variety of medical settings and during emergencies, yet their availability is sometimes limited. To address this, an IoT-based virtual doctor robot that enables remote consultations. This robot enables physicians to navigate remotely, interact with patients, and review medical records. The system is built on a mobile robotic platform that includes video calling, vital sign monitoring, and self-navigating capabilities. This technology provides various benefits, including improved access to healthcare, more efficient patient monitoring, and increased flexibility for medical personnel. The robot's capabilities help to streamline healthcare processes and improve patient care, especially in settings when prompt access to doctors is problematic.

title: Local Dimension Enhancement Representation Learning for Skeleton-Based Action Segmentation_supp1-3682105.pdf
authors: Jiaying Liu
abstract: Most existing self-supervised learning methods for skeleton-based temporal action segmentation (TAS) fail to capture the short-term motion semantics essential for dense framelevel prediction, as they typically learn representations that are either too coarse or motion-insensitive. This issue is reflected in local dimension collapse, which highlights the limitations of current approaches and suggests directions for improvement. Specifically, to address the issue of local dimension collapse for self-supervised learning in TAS, we propose the Local Dimension Enhancement (LoDE) framework, which introduces the local effective rank (LER) as a metric to measure and a learning objective to reduce this collapse. A new fine-grained representation scale, termed a motion unit, is defined as a temporal clip of consecutive skeleton frames to model skeleton data. Centered on this representation scale, we analyze existing methods (sequence-scale and frame-scale learning) with the tool of LER and theoretically demonstrate that introducing motion unit-scale learning is essential to alleviate local dimension collapse. Inspired by our theoretical insights, we design a multi-scale semantics module that integrates frame-, sequence-, and motion unit-scale learning, with LER-based regularization to enrich local representation diversity. These designs effectively alleviate local dimension collapse and lead to significant improvements in TAS, as evidenced by LoDE’s superior performance over state-of-the-art methods on three large-scale untrimmed datasets: PKUMMD, TSU, and BABEL. Our project website is available at https://carefreesun.github.io/LoDE TIP 2026/.

title: SimuNEX: A 3D Simulation Framework for Teaching Control Systems
authors: Lee Bissessar, Craig J. Ramlal, Arvind Singh
abstract: Applying project-based learning (PBL) for teaching control systems often involves significant complexities related to hardware costs, safety, and preparation. SimuNEX is an open-source and interoperable simulation framework designed to facilitate the teaching and learning of controls engineering in a safe, engaging, and cost-effective manner. SimuNEX provides a 3D environment which enhances students' understanding of spatial dynamics and system interactions, often not apparent from current signal and scope based visual tools. This allows for a quicker and more intuitive debugging and iterative development of control algorithms. The platform supports realistic scenario-based learning and offers immediate visual feedback. This paper discusses the design and implementation of SimuNEX, along with two application examples in an educational course on modern control theory.

title: Efficient Simulation and Verification of Local Specific Absorption Rate for Complex Signals
authors: Feng Fang, Xin Zhou, Shichen Yang, Haining Zhao, Xujun Zhang, Yongjiu Zhao, Lei Xing, Qian Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: With the development of the mobile communication technology, radiated signals from wireless devices have become increasingly complex. Waveform duration can extend to millisecond range, making conventional full-wave (FW) specific absorption rate (SAR) simulations computationally intensive and time-consuming. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a two-step method for local SAR evaluation under complex excitations. First, the magnitude of the transfer function |H(f)| between the input signal and the E-field at a specified location is extracted. Second, the input power spectrum Pin(f) is combined with |H(f)| to reconstruct the E-field and calculate the local SAR. For a given scenario, |H(f)| can be efficiently obtained using a Gaussian pulse excitation and then reused to evaluate local SAR for different modulated signals without repeating FW simulations. For other configurations, |H(f)| is re-extracted once for the new scenario. Dipole and two-peak antennas are used to simulate |H(f)| and demonstrate signal reusability. Measurements are performed at 2.45 GHz using a two-peak antenna excited by modulated signals (M5, M6 and M10). Measured peak local SAR agrees well with the proposed method, with a mean absolute error of 3%-10%. Overall, the proposed method provides an accurate and efficient approach for estimating the local SAR for different complex excitations.

title: Nurse Rostering Constrained by Physiological-Psychological State Evolution_supp1-3683922.pdf
authors: Yujun Zheng
abstract: Nurse rostering is a typical personnel scheduling problem that involves a variety of constraints. Existing rostering methods that aim to minimize the penalty of soft constraint violations suffer from several limitations including the use of crisp parameters, inflexibility to changes, and a lack of consideration for individual nurse states. This is because violations of the widely-used soft constraints cannot truly reflect the actual working states of nurses that determine the quality of schedules. In this paper, we reformulate the nurse rostering problem by using a new objective function that maximizes the overall reward for achieving good physiological and psychological states of nurses, which are estimated based on their personal characteristics and work assignments using long short-term memory. To solve the problem, we propose an evolutionary algorithm assisted by Q-learning to dynamically adjust the fitness function based on constraint violations to balance positive rewards to good states and negative rewards to bad states during the evolution process. Our results on benchmark and real-world instances demonstrate that our method obtains significantly better nurse states compared to selected popular rostering methods.

title: SMDGS: Scale-aligned Monocular Depth-guided 3D Gaussian Splatting for Rendering and Surface Reconstruction_supp1-3682676.pdf
authors: Jian Yao
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has been explored for surface reconstruction, however unstructured and discontinuous Gaussian point clouds lead to uneven surface reconstruction accuracy as well as frequent loss of Novel View Synthesis (NVS) quality. To address this problem, we propose a scale-aligned monocular depth-guided 3DGS, a promising novel framework that combines geometric prior regularization and consistency supervision to achieve high-quality rendering and surface reconstruction. Specifically, monocular depth, estimated by some recently proposed monocular depth estimation models, contain implicitly abundant valuable geometric cues, but scale ambiguity limits its application. Therefore we first propose a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Nearest Neighbor (KNN)-based depth alignment framework that utilizes the full-domain gradient at monocular depth map to align to the sparse point cloud obtained during the Structure from Motion (SfM), which is employed for regularization to enhance geometric representation. Then a pseudo-mesh-based multi-view consistency module is introduced to fine-tune and guide the model to recover the accurate surface. Finally, a pixel-level isotropic gradient aware method guides the appropriate growth of the Gaussians to further improve the surface and rendering quality. Experiments on dozens of indoor, outdoor, and object-centered/non-object-centered datasets demonstrate that our method achieves accurate surface reconstruction with excellent NVS performance. The code will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://versewei.github.io/SMDGS/</uri>.

title: ProSE: Incorporating Prior Knowledge For Noise Robust Speaker Embedding Neural Network
authors: Yi Ma, Kong Aik Lee, Ville Hautamäki, Meng Ge, Haizhou Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Despite the remarkable progress in deep speaker embedding, noise interference remains a significant challenge. Although data-guided methods have shown promise in suppressing noise, they often rely on large paired datasets and struggle to generalize to unseen noise conditions. Inspired by human cognitive abilities in noisy environments, we propose the Prior Knowledge-Guided Robust Speaker Embedding (ProSE) network. This framework incorporates the Cohort Activation Map (Cohort-CAM) to capture the speaker-specific information in the given feature. With this prior knowledge, a task-specific enhancement model generates a noise-filtering mask, effectively improving speaker verification by isolating the target speaker's voice. We first conduct experiments to analyze the effectiveness of Cohort-CAM in capturing speaker-specific features. Subsequently, the ProSE network was evaluated in speaker verification tasks using the SITW and VoxCeleb1 datasets under a range of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the Cohort-CAM consistently benefits the robustness of speaker embedding. In addition, visualization analysis are provided offering deeper insights into the proposed framework and its underlying mechanisms.

title: A Cognitive Decline Screening System Using iWakka: Quantification of Motor Learning Through Finger Dexterity Tasks
authors: Kazuya Toshima, Takamasa Kume, Yoshifumi Morita, Tsukasa Tamaru
publish: 2025 18th International Convention on Rehabilitation Engineering and Assistive Technology (i-CREATe)
abstract: In our previous study, we developed a model to classify mild cognitive impairment (MCI) by quantifying motor learning through finger dexterity tasks using iWakka. However, the model required 30 minutes of measurement, which limited its practical applicability. In this study, we modified the model to reduce the measurement time to 15 minutes while maintaining high classification performance. Based on this model, we further developed a cognitive decline screening system implemented as an application. The system provides automated data input, classification, and result management, and can be used in community health screenings and telemedicine settings.

title: Alert-Driven Pattern Mining in Action – Reducing Alert Floods in Road Traffic Management
authors: Johannes Schönböck, David Graf, Werner Retschitzegger, Wieland Schwinger, Elisabeth Kapsammer, Birgit Pröll, Marianne Lechner
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The immense flood of alerts continuously generated in large-scale control systems of critical infrastructures, such as energy or traffic networks, poses a substantial challenge to their efficient and safe operation. Despite ongoing research efforts, mining alert patterns as a means to cope with alert floods remains particularly difficult, since relationships between alerts are often unknown due to the complexity of systems and limitations in log data. In pursuit of a robust solution to this problem, this paper introduces our alert-driven pattern mining approach, which is based on a hybrid, multi-objective evolutionary algorithm. The approach is unique in that it simultaneously maximizes pattern coverage, enabling reasoning about the full range of underlying relationships, and leverages pattern frequency to identify both rare and frequent patterns. This dual focus supports alert flood reduction in regular as well as exceptional, potentially critical situations. The applicability and effectiveness of the proposed approach are demonstrated using real-world log data from the domain of road traffic management, with a particular focus on the characteristics of the discovered patterns.

title: Synchro Store: AI- Powered Voice and Icon based Billing System for Retail Operations
authors: Balaji S, Kavitha A, Vishnupriya T S, Kabil C, Ramya R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Small and local grocery stores struggle with operational problems which include slow checkout procedures and inaccurate billing, manual stock control systems and limited product availability that leads to customer dissatisfaction and financial losses. The digital billing system resolves these issues while it also enhances inventory management and store operations through its predictive analytics capabilities. The system provides two billing options which enable vendors to generate bills through voice commands or by selecting predefined icons for product addition. The system processes information through Tamil and English languages while it allows users to make payments through various methods and provides instant inventory updates. The system keeps all bills in digital form, so vendors can quickly pull up past transactions when customers come back for returns-no more digging through piles of paper. It also offers a vendor dashboard that shows current inventory levels and automatically sends alerts when stock falls below set minimums, helping vendors stay on top of their supplies. Basic validation tests at the billing stage confirm that both the product selection and the quantity ordered are correct. The integration of ARIMA with AI models enables vendors to optimize inventory management which results in waste reduction and enhanced customer satisfaction through demand forecasting and upselling identification.

title: Modern Payment Security Cryptographic Algorithm Performance Analysis and PCI DSS V4.0.1 Compliance Evaluation on ColdFusion 2025
authors: Samet Diri, Özgür Barış Kayapınar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: With the enactment of version v4.0.1 of the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS), it has become mandatory for organizations conducting financial transactions to review their cryptographic solutions. This study aims to comparatively analyze the performance of different encryption algorithms, such as AES, DESEDE, and BLOWFISH, used to secure cardholder data on the ColdFusion 2025 platform, which is a server-side technology. In the study, load tests involving thousands of iterations were conducted using ColdFusion's builtin Encrypt() and Decrypt() functions, comparing modern modes recommended by PCI DSS, such as AES/GCM/NoPadding and AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding, against older and weak algorithms like RC4 and DESEDE. The findings revealed that the modes of AES were faster than insecure algorithms like RC4 or RC2, both in encryption and in the average decryption time. The AES/GCM/NoPadding mode, which offers the highest level of security, was observed to incur a slight performance cost due to providing integrity control, executing the process in 150 ms for encryption and 63 ms for decryption. In conclusion, for ColdFusion 2025 users seeking PCI DSS v4.0.1 compliance, AES/GCM/NoPadding is suggested as the most appropriate solution due to its integrity guarantee, although AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding offers an acceptable alternative in high-performance scenarios. The study provides evidence-based recommendations to prevent organizations from risking the use of deprecated algorithms like RC4 or older ones like DESEDE due to performance concerns, guiding them toward adopting solutions that are both secure and efficient.

title: Denoising Diffusion for Enhanced Representation Learning in Sequential Recommendation
authors: Tingting Zheng, Jiesheng Huang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Computing (SPIC)
abstract: Sequential recommendation seeks to predict users' next actions from their historical interactions. Despite the success of self-attention-based models, their performance often degrades under sparse data due to insufficient representation learning. To target these challenges, we propose a diffusion-augmented sequential recommendation model that enhances representation learning through a denoising process. By incorporating a diffusion-based denoising mechanism as an auxiliary training objective, our model effectively strengthens sequence embeddings, making them more discriminative and robust, especially in data-sparse environments. Experiments conducted on real-world datasets show the effectiveness of our model. This work presents an effective framework that leverages diffusion models for representation enhancement to offer a robust solution for sequential recommendation, especially in data-sparse scenarios.

title: Design and Experimental Evaluation of a Multirotor UAV Based on the Number of Propellers
authors: Lotfi Lakhdhar, Abdallah Bouabidi, Ali Snoussi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Multi-Conference on Smart Systems &amp; Green Process (IMC-SSGP)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive analysis of multirotor UAV configurations based on the number of propellers, focusing on the relationship between total takeoff weight and required thrust per motor, and rotational speed (RPM). By combining theoretical modeling with experimental validation, key parameters such as minimum and maximum allowable masses for tricopters, quadcopters, hexacopters, and octocopters are established and compared. The results demonstrate that configurations with more motors, such as hexacopters and octocopters, require lower thrust per motor and support higher payload capacities compared to tricopters and quadcopters. This translates to improved flight stability and operational flexibility. Experimental data also emphasize the importance of empirical testing, as theoretical maximum loads can exceed practical motor capabilities. The proposed methodology offers a practical framework for optimizing multirotor design, balancing performance, energy efficiency, and mission reliability. Future work includes real-world testing of each configuration to further validate and refine the design approach.

title: IoT and Vision in Disaster Monitoring: Toward Resilient Infrastructure
authors: Nino U. Pilueta, Ian B. Benitez, Jamie Adrian D. Jamasali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The increasing frequency and intensity of natural hazards highlight the limitations of traditional disaster monitoring systems that rely on static sensors and delayed reporting. Advances in the Internet of Things and computer vision now enable distributed, real-time observation across multiple hazards. IoT networks provide fine-scale measurements of environmental and structural parameters, while vision systems using cameras, drones, and satellite imagery deliver spatial verification and automated impact assessment through artificial intelligence. When integrated, these technologies improve detection accuracy, shorten response times, and strengthen situational awareness. This review synthesizes recent global developments across three dimensions: technology, resilience, and governance. The analysis examines hybrid architectures that merge IoT and vision systems, evaluates continuity strategies such as renewable-powered microgrids and UAV-based communications, and identifies governance challenges involving interoperability, privacy, and institutional coordination. A layered conceptual framework is proposed to link sensing, analytics, alerting, and policy mechanisms. Findings reveal persistent gaps in endurance during extended outages, the need for generalizable multihazard fusion models, and the importance of ethical data governance. The synthesis provides guidance for integrating IoT-vision systems into resilient, scalable, and inclusive early warning infrastructures aligned with global sustainability and disaster-risk-reduction goals.

title: Residential Power Consumption Forecasting Model Using Integrated Big Data Technologies and Support Vector Machine
authors: Kristian Diego R. Dimapindan, Chih-Chieh T. Ho, Federick Leonard A. Vitug, Mariz A. Chua
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Monitoring residential energy use offers 5-15% cost reduction, highlighting the value of accurate power consumption prediction. This study developed a residential power consumption forecasting model integrating Big Data technologies with the Support Vector Machine (SVM) model. While SVM excels at longterm prediction, its computational load increases significantly with large datasets and numerous parameters. To address this, Big Data technologies such as Data Store, Mapping and Reducing were used for efficient preprocessing and handling of large datasets before inputting them into the SVM. This approach was investigated as an initial step to optimize the SVM's demanding computing process. The resulting forecasting model, formulated in MATLAB, was compared against a conventional SVM model. From the result, integrating Big Data technologies with SVM dramatically improved performance. Training and prediction times were reduced by 46%, and memory allocation decreased by 98.2%. Error metrics also showed significant improvement as the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) dropped by 10.59%, the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) by 8.88%, and the Mean Squared Error (MSE) by 12.63%. This combined approach yields a more reliable and less resource-intensive energy management tool, easing the burden on computer systems while enhancing the accuracy of consumption forecasts. 98.2%. Error metrics also showed significant improvement as the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) dropped by 10.59%, the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) by 8.88%, and the Mean Squared Error (MSE) by 12.63%. This combined approach yields a more reliable and less resource-intensive energy management tool, easing the burden on computer systems while enhancing the accuracy of consumption forecasts. 98.2%. Error metrics also showed significant improvement as the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) dropped by 10.59%, the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) by 8.88%, and the Mean Squared Error (MSE) by 12.63%. This combined approach yields a more reliable and less resource-intensive energy management tool, easing the burden on computer systems while enhancing the accuracy of consumption forecasts.

title: Efficient LLM Inference via Activation-Aware Weight Quantization: System Integration and Performance Analysis
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Gajendra Babu Thokala, Sandeep Shivam, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Vinay R Soni, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Isan Sahoo, Chaitanya Kulkarni
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The rapid scaling of large language models (LLMs) has driven extraordinary gains in natural language understanding and generation, but at the cost of substantial compute and memory demands. Efficient deployment of such models remains a central challenge for both academic and industrial systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of activation-aware weight quantization (AWQ) applied to modern transformer architectures within the vLLM serving framework. We benchmark FP16 and 4-bit quantized inference for the OPT and Mistral model families, analyzing latency, throughput, and memory utilization under varying prompt lengths and batch sizes. Results demonstrate that AWQ achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 0 \%}$</tex> GPU memory reduction and approximately 60% throughput improvement with minimal impact on output quality, validating AWQ as a practical compression strategy for real-world deployment. The integration of quantization with efficient serving engines like vLLM enables research-grade LLMs on mid-range GPUs without accuracy compromise, highlighting the potential for sustainable, accessible, and scalable LLM inference across hardware tiers.

title: Dynamic Video Summarization Based on Objects of Interest Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Manasa Yarrarapu, Dedeepya Pulletikurthy, Tatiparthi Tej Mahendra Reddy, Tummuru Vimala Reddy, Parasa Kamal Siddardha, Ruksana shaik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: The advancement of digital video technology transformed surveillance footage into a treasure in the maintenance of security and protection. Security protocols lead the analysis of huge video data streams generated by surveillance systems which monitor everyday operations in retail spaces and transport systems and public safety sectors. Video analysis stands out as a process which requires extensive time, human effort along with large amounts of computer processing power. The proposed deep learning method develops customized video summarization functions which enables users to create summary content based on their defined Object of Interest such as vehicles, subjects or specific items. A cutting-edge object detection model named YOLOv7 operates as part of the proposed system to perform real-time detection of essential objects which reduces redundancy in video content. The experimental results generated by SumMe and TVSum show successful efficiency of the proposed method which achieved 82% accuracy and 90.4% accuracy with summary rates of 80.42 % and 85.02 % respectively. The model demonstrates compatibility with various applications including security monitoring along with social media content generation and sports analytics because of its adaptable design. Our approach demonstrates improved performance compared to existing methods which confirms its improved ability for producing effective video summaries. These results show the potential offered by the proposed approach to increase the efficiency, accuracy, and adaptability to the specific user of video surveillance systems as well as personal video analysis.

title: Adaptive Multi-Criteria Re-Ranking for Hybrid Semantic Information Retrieval in Metadata-Rich Collections
authors: Eric Julianto, Raihan Evanza, Arinal Dzikrul Haqqy Amir, Muhammad Irfan Karim, Muhammad Thohir, Wahyu Purnama Magribi, Muhammad Fazly Qusyairy, Budi Tjahjono
publish: 2026 International Seminar on Intelligent Business and Edge-Computing Research (ISIBER)
abstract: The rapid explosion of digital document collections necessitates more sophisticated retrieval systems that go beyond simple keyword matching and basic semantic search. Modern document repositories are frequently rich in structured metadata, which are often underutilized in standard information retrieval (IR) pipelines, leading to suboptimal relevance in complex search scenarios. This paper proposes an Explainable Hybrid Semantic Information Retrieval framework that integrates high-dimensional dense vector retrieval with traditional sparse keyword search (TF-IDF and BM25) to effectively capture both broad semantic context and precise term matches. Furthermore, we introduce an adaptive Multi-Criteria Re-ranking (MCR) module that leverages document-specific metadata, such as quality signals and categorical constraints, to dynamically refine search results based on detected user intent. A key innovation of our framework is the incorporation of an Explainable AI (XAI) layer using SHAP (Shapley Additive exPlanations) analysis, which provides real-time transparency and interpretability into the system's ranking decisions. Experimental results on a curated e-commerce catalog benchmark, comprising 50 diverse and challenging queries, demonstrate that our adaptive hybrid approach significantly outperforms standard baseline models. The system achieves a Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) of 0.91 and maintains high precision across complex descriptive queries, offering a robust and transparent solution for modern metadata-driven discovery applications.

title: Demand Forecasting in Retail Using Prophet and Deep Learning
authors: Valaiyapathy N, Vishal J, P Uma Maheshwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Visual Analytics and Data Visualization (ICVADV)
abstract: Effective inventory management continues to pose a significant challenge for businesses, primarily due to unpredictable demand, varying costs, and unstable market trends, which frequently lead to stockouts or excessive holding costs. In order to mitigate this issue, the present research proposes a smart inventory management system that amalgamates demand forecasting, financial planning, and market-driven decision-making. The innovation resides in synchronizing inventory control with real-time market insights and financial strategies, thereby reconciling operational and accounting perspectives. The proposed system, which is implemented in MATLAB, forecasts monthly product demand by utilizing historical sales data, seasonal fluctuations, and cost patterns, while also incorporating stock market price forecasting to inform procurement strategies. Real-time inventory levels are perpetually monitored and contrasted against predictions, facilitating automated replenishment and diminishing inefficiencies. The novelty of the framework is to improve accuracy, adaptability, and cost-effectiveness throughout supply chain operations. Experimental validation substantiates its scalability, robustness, and potential to enhance intelligent decision-making in dynamic business environments.

title: Large Language Models as MT Evaluators: Statistical Evidence from a Low-Resource Setting
authors: Dasa Munkova, Michal Munk, Martin Chotar, Petr Hajek
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) can serve as reliable evaluators of machine translation (MT) quality for a low-resource, morphologically rich language-Slovak. Using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> English-Slovak segments, we compare the fluency, adequacy, and usability ratings produced by seven human annotators and five LLM configurations (GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, and GPT-5 under multiple temperature settings). Statistical analyses reveal significant differences between human and LLM ratings across all criteria, but also show that advanced LLMs - particularly GPT-5 - exhibit strong internal consistency and form homogeneous groups with human annotators in fluency and usability assessment. Adequacy ratings demonstrate weaker alignment, reflecting both human disagreement and linguistic complexity. Overall, our results suggest that LLMs offer a scalable and reproducible complement to human MT evaluation, with near-human parity emerging in specific dimensions.

title: Wirachain: Blockchain and Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources for Clinical Data Interoperability
authors: Leonardo Grau, Joao Urrunaga, Jose Santisteban
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: Interoperability challenges in healthcare often result in fragmented patient records and duplicated procedures, affecting continuity of care and operational efficiency. While numerous theoretical frameworks and standards exist, practical implementations remain limited, with few complete applications enabling secure and controlled data exchange. Motivated by this gap, we developed Wirachain, a decentralized application that integrates blockchain technology with the HL7 FHIR standard to provide a functional environment for electronic health record management. The system supports patient-controlled permission granting and revocation, allowing healthcare providers to access clinical data only when authorized. It incorporates authentication, role-based access, and persistent storage in both FHIR-compliant and traditional databases, enabling practical testing of clinical workflows. Validation was performed using synthetically generated data and stress tests to evaluate system performance under load. Results demonstrate that the application can reliably manage permissions and data access, supporting interoperable clinical operations while maintaining efficiency and security. This work illustrates that fully implemented, blockchain-based solutions can bridge the gap between conceptual frameworks and practical healthcare interoperability.

title: Business Cycle Dynamics with Initial Conditions Inherited from a Fuzzy Logic Model
authors: Carlos S. Ramos Medina, Luis E. Mendiola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: This paper constructs a dynamical framework for predicting inflationary peaks and business cycle dynamics from linguistically communicated macroeconomic conditions. We employ the Mamdani-Assilian fuzzy inference model to define a language of economic variables and policy messages whose semantic interactions determine inflation levels. The induced fuzzy valuation space inherits a metrizable topology to parameterize a multidimensional logistic dynamical system. The system exhibits semi-stable equilibria corresponding to cyclical peaks, allowing a topological characterization of business cycle semi-stable fixed points. We provide rigorous bifurcation analysis demonstrating period-doubling routes to chaos and establish conditions for the emergence of complex inflationary dynamics.

title: Fake News Detection System for Textual Data Using Sequential Deep Learning Model
authors: Mohd Shahid Husain, Umar Sathic, Nawazish Naveed, Hemalatha Gunasekaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)
abstract: The penetration of social media has caused a surge in the circulation of intentionally verifiable false news. This new phenomenon called as fake news, has been posing a serious threat to the prosperity and security of people across the world. The problem of dealing with the online fake news has attracted the research community, the people in news agencies and government bodies in disseminating the news stories in online platforms. The mainstream social media giants such as face book, and tweeter are increasingly being used to circulate misinformation or unreliable textual stories in the form of news articles, videos and posts. It results in the steering bodies in government sector and news agencies to overcome this social evil with the help of automated software applications. These software applications can help the people in the government sector to accelerate the verification process of fake content that has been spread in the social media. Inspired by the societal impact and evil menace of such fake news at individuals, this research work tries to bring in the development of text-based state-of-art fake news detection system and evaluation of developed model with other models in order to study the scope for improvement in this area of research. The results of the proposed model were compared with traditional machine learning methods. It has been found that the proposed model achieved supreme performance over other approaches in terms of training and test set accuracy.

title: Used Car Prices Prediction using RNN
authors: Sara Abou Chaar, Manuel Aouad, George Chamoun, Gaby Abou Haidar, Michel Owayjan, Roger Achkar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, and Data Analytics (ICoABCD)
abstract: In a world pushing for sustainable consumption (UN SDG 12), the used car market-over 40 million U.S. sales yearly, 50% cheaper than new cars-cuts waste and emissions by keeping vehicles on the road longer. But pricing swings from mileage, brand, fuel type, and crises like Lebanon's make fair deals hard for buyers and sellers. This study uses Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) to predict used car prices accurately. It trains on 83,888 vehicles' data (brand, mileage, fuel, transmission, year) after cleaning, scaling, and adding depreciation features. The model has four LSTM layers with dropout, plus dense layers, trained 500 epochs on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 / 20$</tex> split with k-fold checks. It hits 85-88% accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\$ 1, 2 0 0}$</tex> MAE, 12-15% MAPE-better than KNN, deep nets, or linear regression. Loss curves show quick convergence after 20 epochs, with training and validation aligning closely to avoid overfitting. The model captures time-based patterns like mileage impact on value, enabling reliable forecasts amid market shifts. Visualizations reveal skewed price distributions (most under $ 50,000) and clear inverse mileage-price trends, validating key insights. This robust RNN advances data-driven valuation for global auto markets, especially volatile ones like Lebanon.
